  • Luxury Boat Rental Marbella: Premium Fleet with Skipper
  • Speedboat Charter Marbella: Fast Adventures on the Costa del Sol
  • Yacht Rental Benalmadena: Marina Charters
  • Boat Hire Fuengirola Port: Easy Booking with Skipper

faqs.q: Do I need a licence to hire a boat in Fuengirola?
faqs.a: No. All our charters include a licensed skipper who handles the boat. You just relax and enjoy the trip.

faqs.q: How much does boat hire Fuengirola cost?
faqs.a: Prices start from around EUR 300 for a 2-hour RIB trip, EUR 500 for a half-day motor yacht, and EUR 800 for a half-day catamaran. Full-day rates are higher.

faqs.q: What is the closest port to Fuengirola for boat hire?
faqs.a: Cabopino Marina is the closest, about 10 minutes east of Fuengirola. Puerto Banús is 20 minutes west and has the largest selection.

faqs.q: Can I bring my own food and drink?
faqs.a: Yes. Most boats have a cooler and ice. You can bring your own picnic and drinks. Some charters offer catering for an extra fee.

faqs.q: What happens if the weather is bad?
faqs.a: The skipper cancels the trip if conditions are unsafe. You get a full refund or can reschedule. We monitor weather forecasts closely.

faqs.q: How many people can fit on a boat?
faqs.a: Capacity depends on the boat size. Small RIBs take 6-8 guests, motor yachts up to 12, catamarans up to 12, and superyachts up to 30 or more.

faqs.q: Can I book a boat for a party or event?
faqs.a: Yes. Many boats allow parties with music and decorations. Check with the skipper beforehand. Some charge extra for cleaning or damage deposits.

title: Luxury Yacht Charter Marbella: Premium Experiences
metaDescription: Book a luxury yacht charter Marbella with a licensed skipper. Explore Puerto Banús, Costa del Sol, and beyond. Premium motor yachts, catamarans, and superyachts. Reserve now.
h1: Luxury Yacht Charter Marbella: Premium Experiences
intro:
You want a luxury yacht charter Marbella, and you want it without the fluff. You are looking at a day on the water with a licensed skipper, top-tier service, and a boat that turns heads. Marbella is the place for that, and we are the platform that connects you with the best boats available. No hidden fees, no nonsense. You pick the boat, we handle the rest.

Forget the brochures and the sales pitch. Here is what you actually need to know: you can charter a motor yacht, a catamaran, a sailing yacht, or even a superyacht from ports like Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona, Sotogrande, or Benalmádena. Prices start from around EUR 1,500 for a half-day on a quality motor yacht, and go up to EUR 50,000 or more for a week on a superyacht. Every charter includes a skipper, so you just relax. No licence required. You pay, you board, you enjoy.

This page is your guide to making that happen. We cover what is included, the best ports, boat options, pricing, seasons, and how to book. Read it, pick your boat, and get on the water.
bodyHtml:
What a Luxury Yacht Charter Marbella Actually Includes

A luxury yacht charter Marbella is not just a boat ride. It is a fully managed experience. You get a professional skipper who knows the coast, the weather, and the best spots. The boat is cleaned, fuelled, and stocked with basic safety gear. Some charters include a hostess, a chef, or water toys like jet skis and paddleboards. But read the fine print. Not everything is included. Fuel, food, drinks, and dockage fees are often extra. Ask before you book.

Typical inclusions: a licensed skipper, insurance, basic safety equipment, and a standard itinerary. Exclusions: fuel (expect EUR 200-600 for a half-day on a motor yacht), food and drink (catering from around EUR 50 per person), and any special requests like a DJ or extra crew. Always confirm with the provider. We list the details on each boat page on BoatHire24.

Departure Ports for Luxury Yacht Charter Marbella

You have options. Six ports serve the Costa del Sol, each with its own vibe. Puerto Banús is the glitzy one, home to superyachts and designer boutiques. Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo) is more laid-back, closer to the old town. Cabopino is a quiet gem with a sandy beach. Estepona offers a charming old town and calmer waters. Sotogrande is exclusive, with a golf and polo scene. Benalmádena is lively, with a marina full of restaurants and bars.

Your choice affects the itinerary. From Puerto Banús, you can cruise to Cala del Faro or the Marbellan coast in 15-20 minutes. From Estepona, you are closer to the Strait of Gibraltar and dolphin spotting. From Sotogrande, you can head to Gibraltar or the African coast. We cover the distances and times on each port page. Use our search to filter by port.

Boat Types for a Premium Charter

Motor yachts are the most common for luxury charters. They offer speed, space, and style. A 15-metre motor yacht can hold 8-12 guests, with a flybridge, sun pads, and a cabin. Prices from around EUR 1,500 for a half-day. Catamarans are more stable, with more deck space and shallower drafts. They are ideal for groups up to 12, with prices from EUR 2,000 for a half-day. Sailing yachts offer a quieter, more traditional experience. You can charter a 14-metre sailing yacht for around EUR 1,200 for a half-day. RIBs and speedboats are faster, smaller, and more adrenaline-focused. Prices from EUR 600 for a half-day. Fishing boats are equipped with rods and gear, from EUR 800 for a half-day. Licence-free day boats (under 6 metres) are for the independent, no skipper required, from EUR 300 for a half-day. Superyachts are the top end, over 24 metres, with full crew, from EUR 10,000 per day. Each type is listed on BoatHire24 with specs and photos.

Pricing Guidance for Luxury Yacht Charter Marbella

Prices vary by boat size, age, season, and inclusions. A half-day (4 hours) on a 12-metre motor yacht in June might cost EUR 1,800. A full day (8 hours) on a 20-metre catamaran could be EUR 4,500. A week on a 30-metre superyacht can exceed EUR 50,000. Always check the season. July and August are peak, so prices are 20-30% higher. May, June, September, and October are shoulder, with better availability and lower rates. November to March is low season, but still possible for a short trip.

Fuel is a major variable. For a half-day on a motor yacht, budget EUR 200-400. For a full day on a catamaran, EUR 150-300. For a superyacht, EUR 1,000-3,000. Catering: EUR 50-150 per person for a buffet or BBQ. Drinks: EUR 30-100 per person. Dockage fees at Puerto Banús can be EUR 100-300 for a day. Always ask for a full quote before booking. We provide a price range on each boat page, but you must confirm with the provider.

Best Seasons for a Luxury Yacht Charter Marbella

The Costa del Sol has a Mediterranean climate. Summer (June to August) is hot, 28-35 degrees C, with calm seas and long days. This is the peak season, so book months ahead. Spring (April to May) and autumn (September to October) are milder, 20-28 degrees C, with fewer crowds. Winter (November to March) is cooler, 15-20 degrees C, but still pleasant for a short cruise. Dolphin sightings are good year-round, especially near the Strait of Gibraltar. The best time for a luxury charter is May to October, when the sea temperature is above 20 degrees C and the wind is light.

How to Book a Luxury Yacht Charter Marbella

Booking is straightforward. You browse boats on BoatHire24, filter by port, boat type, and price, then send an enquiry. The provider responds with availability, a full quote, and a contract. You pay a deposit (usually 30-50%) to confirm. The balance is due before departure. You must provide a guest list and any special requests. On the day, you arrive at the port, meet the skipper, and board. The skipper handles the safety briefing, navigation, and itinerary. You just enjoy. No hidden steps. We have a guide on how to book a yacht charter if you need more detail.

Who Is a Luxury Yacht Charter Marbella For

This is for anyone who wants a private, high-end day on the water. Couples celebrating an anniversary, families with older children, corporate groups for a client event, or friends on a bachelorette party. It is not for budget travellers or large groups without a clear plan. The boats have capacity limits, usually 8-12 guests for most charters, up to 20 or more for superyachts. If you want a party boat with loud music and unlimited drinks, ask for that specifically. If you want a quiet sunset cruise, confirm the vibe. The skipper can customise the itinerary to your preferences, within reason.

Local Highlights on a Luxury Yacht Charter Marbella

You can see the Costa del Sol from the water. The La Concha mountain rises behind Marbella, visible from the coast. The Río Verde estuary near Puerto Banús is a wildlife spot. Cala del Faro is a secluded cove for swimming. Estepona old town has colourful flower pots and tapas bars. The Strait of Gibraltar is a dolphin and whale hotspot. Nerja caves are a longer trip, 60 NM east, but doable on a full-day charter. Your skipper knows the best anchorages and lunch spots. Just ask.

Why Choose BoatHire24 for Your Luxury Yacht Charter Marbella

We are a marketplace, not a charter company. We list boats from multiple local providers, so you compare options. Every boat is checked for safety and licensing. We do not add fake reviews or hidden fees. You book directly with the provider, and we are here if there is a problem. We cover all six ports and every boat type. No other platform gives you this range for Marbella. Start your search on our search page.

Puerto Banús: The Epicentre of Luxury Yacht Charter Marbella

Puerto Banús is where the luxury yacht charter Marbella scene really lives. This marina was built in 1970 and it has not calmed down since. You will see 30-metre superyachts moored next to 50-metre megayachts, and the docks are lined with designer shops and champagne bars. If you want to be seen, this is your port. Departing from here, you can reach the Marbella coastline in 10 minutes, the Cala del Faro cove in 20, and the Cabopino dunes in 30. The marina itself charges premium dockage fees, so expect your charter cost to reflect that. But the convenience is unmatched. You step off the yacht and straight into a restaurant. For a true luxury experience, Puerto Banús is the default choice.

Marbella Marina: The Local Alternative for a Premium Charter

Marbella Marina, also called Puerto Deportivo, is a quieter, more authentic option for a luxury yacht charter Marbella. It sits right next to the old town, so you can combine a day on the water with a walk through the orange-tree-lined streets and tapas bars. The marina is smaller than Puerto Banús, but it has all the facilities. You will find fewer superyachts and more motor yachts and catamarans in the 12-20 metre range. The vibe is less flashy, more relaxed. From here, you can cruise east to Cabopino or west to Puerto Banús in 15 minutes. The dockage fees are lower, so the charter price might be slightly less. If you want luxury without the paparazzi, this is your port.

Cabopino: The Hidden Gem for a Private Luxury Charter

Cabopino is a small marina east of Marbella, near the Artola sand dunes. It is not as famous as Puerto Banús, but that is the point. For a luxury yacht charter Marbella that feels private, Cabopino delivers. The marina has a handful of restaurants and a direct beach, so you can anchor off the sand or walk ashore. The boats here are mostly motor yachts and catamarans, with a few sailing yachts. The water is calm, and the coast is less developed, so you get a more natural view. From Cabopino, you can head east to Calahonda or west to Marbella. The journey to Puerto Banús is about 25 minutes. If you want to escape the crowds, book from Cabopino.

Estepona: The Western Gateway for Dolphin Spotting

Estepona is the westernmost port on our list, and it offers something unique for a luxury yacht charter Marbella: proximity to the Strait of Gibraltar. The water here is deeper and the currents are stronger, but that brings dolphins. Common and striped dolphins are almost guaranteed on a half-day trip. You might also see pilot whales or even orcas in season. The marina is modern, with good facilities and a short walk to the old town, which is famous for its flower-covered balconies. Boats here tend to be slightly cheaper than in Puerto Banús, because the demand is lower. A 15-metre motor yacht might cost from EUR 1,200 for a half-day. If you want wildlife with your luxury, Estepona is the choice.

Sotogrande: The Exclusive Option for a Superyacht Charter

Sotogrande is not just a port, it is a lifestyle. This gated community is home to golf courses, polo fields, and some of the largest private villas in Spain. For a luxury yacht charter Marbella that is truly exclusive, Sotogrande is where you go. The marina is small but pristine, with a handful of high-end restaurants and a direct view of Gibraltar. The boats here are mostly superyachts and premium motor yachts, from 20 metres upwards. Prices reflect the exclusivity. A 25-metre motor yacht might cost from EUR 6,000 for a half-day. From Sotogrande, you can cross the Strait to Africa in 45 minutes, or cruise east to Estepona in 30. If you want privacy and prestige, this is your port.

Benalmádena: The Lively Port for a Fun Luxury Charter

Benalmádena is the most eastern port we cover, and it is the liveliest. The marina is full of restaurants, bars, and shops, and the atmosphere is more party than quiet luxury. For a luxury yacht charter Marbella that includes a stop for lunch or a sunset drink, Benalmádena works well. The boats here are varied, from speedboats to catamarans, and prices are competitive. A 14-metre motor yacht might cost from EUR 1,000 for a half-day. From Benalmádena, you can cruise east to Fuengirola or west to Cabopino. The coastline is built-up, but the water is calm. If you want luxury with a side of nightlife, Benalmádena is your port.

What a Skipper Does on a Luxury Yacht Charter Marbella

Every luxury yacht charter Marbella includes a licensed skipper. That is not optional. The skipper is not just a driver. They know the local waters, the weather patterns, and the best anchorages. They handle navigation, mooring, and safety. They also act as a guide, pointing out landmarks like the La Concha mountain or the Río Verde estuary. Most skippers speak English and Spanish. Some speak French or German. They will customise the itinerary based on your preferences, within safety limits. If you want to swim at Cala del Faro, they will find the best spot. If you want to see dolphins, they will head to the Strait. The skipper is your host on the water. Tip them 10-15% of the charter fee if the service is good.

Water Toys and Extras for a Premium Experience

A luxury yacht charter Marbella can be upgraded with extras. Jet skis are common, from EUR 200 per hour. Paddleboards and kayaks are cheaper, often included in the charter. Snorkel gear is standard on most boats. Some providers offer a DJ or a sound system for a party vibe. Catering can range from a simple tapas platter (EUR 30 per person) to a full BBQ with paella (EUR 80 per person). Drinks are usually BYO or provided by the caterer. Champagne, wine, and beer are typical. If you want a specific brand, bring it yourself. Always confirm what is included before you book. We list the extras on each boat page on BoatHire24.

How to Choose the Right Boat for Your Group

The boat type matters for the experience. For a luxury yacht charter Marbella with a group of 6-8, a 12-15 metre motor yacht is ideal. You get a flybridge for sunbathing, a cabin for changing, and a saloon for dining. For a group of 10-12, a catamaran offers more deck space and stability. No one gets seasick. For a couple, a 10-metre sailing yacht is romantic and quiet. For a party, a 20-metre motor yacht with a sound system and a DJ. For a fishing trip, a 9-metre fishing boat with rods and a fighting chair. For a quick adrenaline hit, a 7-metre RIB with a 250hp engine. The choice depends on your group size, budget, and activity preference. Use our search filters on BoatHire24 to narrow it down.

What to Wear and Bring on a Luxury Yacht Charter Marbella

You are on a luxury yacht charter Marbella, so dress accordingly. Swimwear, a light cover-up, and non-marking shoes are essential. The deck gets hot, so bring flip-flops or bare feet. A hat, sunglasses, and sunscreen are non-negotiable. The sun reflects off the water, so SPF 50 is recommended. A light jacket or windbreaker is useful, even in summer, because the breeze can be cool. Towels are usually provided, but check. If you want to swim, bring a dry bag for your phone and wallet. No high heels on the deck, they damage the teak. No black-soled shoes, they mark the white gelcoat. Keep it simple and comfortable.

How to Customise Your Itinerary for a Luxury Yacht Charter Marbella

The standard itinerary for a luxury yacht charter Marbella is a cruise along the coast with a swim stop. But you can customise it. If you want to visit the Nerja caves, that is a 60 NM trip east, so you need a full day and a fast boat. If you want to cross the Strait to Africa, that is 30 NM from Sotogrande, doable in a half-day on a motor yacht. If you want to anchor for lunch at a chiringuito, your skipper knows the best beach clubs. If you want to watch the sunset from the water, a 4-hour evening charter works. The key is to communicate your preferences when you book. The skipper can adjust the route, but they need time to plan. We cover itinerary ideas on our blog.

Related guides

  • Catamaran Rental Fuengirola: Group Deals with Skipper

  • Catamaran Rental Puerto Banus Hourly: Book by the Hour
  • Luxury Motor Yacht Hire Marbella: Premium Charter Deals

faqs.q: Do I need a licence for a luxury yacht charter Marbella?
faqs.a: No. Every charter includes a licensed skipper who handles navigation, safety, and docking. You just relax. The only exception is licence-free day boats under 6 metres, which you can drive without a licence, but those are not luxury charters.

faqs.q: What is the typical cost of a luxury yacht charter Marbella?
faqs.a: Prices start from around EUR 1,500 for a half-day on a 12-metre motor yacht in shoulder season. A full day on a 20-metre catamaran might cost EUR 4,500. Superyachts start at EUR 10,000 per day. Fuel, food, and drinks are extra. Always get a full quote.

faqs.q: Which port is best for a luxury yacht charter Marbella?
faqs.a: Puerto Banús is the most glamorous, with superyachts and designer shops. Marbella Marina is closer to the old town. Cabopino is quieter. Estepona offers calmer waters. Sotogrande is exclusive. Benalmádena is lively. Choose based on your itinerary and vibe.

faqs.q: Can I customise the itinerary on a luxury yacht charter Marbella?
faqs.a: Yes. You discuss the route with the skipper before departure. Typical stops include Cala del Faro, the Marbellan coast, or dolphin spotting near the Strait of Gibraltar. The skipper knows the best anchorages and lunch spots. Just state your preferences.

faqs.q: What is the cancellation policy for a luxury yacht charter Marbella?
faqs.a: It varies by provider. Most require a 30-50% deposit, with the balance due 2-3 weeks before. Cancellations within 7-14 days may forfeit the deposit. Bad weather cancellations are usually free, with a reschedule or refund. Check the contract before booking.

faqs.q: How many guests can a luxury yacht charter Marbella accommodate?
faqs.a: Most motor yachts and catamarans hold 8-12 guests for a day charter. Superyachts can hold 20 or more. The capacity is legally limited by the boat's safety certificate. Do not exceed the limit. The skipper will enforce it.

faqs.q: What should I bring on a luxury yacht charter Marbella?
faqs.a: Bring swimwear, sunscreen, sunglasses, a hat, and a light jacket for the wind. Towels and snorkel gear may be provided, but confirm. No hard-soled shoes on deck. Leave valuables at home. The skipper will provide a safety briefing at the start.

title: Speedboat Hire Marbella: Fast Rentals for Thrill Seekers
metaDescription: Book speedboat hire Marbella with BoatHire24. Fast RIBs and speedboats from Puerto Banús. Licensed skipper included. Prices from around EUR 400. Reserve today.
h1: Speedboat Hire Marbella: Fast Rentals for Thrill Seekers
intro:
You want to feel the wind in your hair and the roar of an outboard behind you. Speedboat hire Marbella is exactly that: a direct, no-fuss way to blast along the Costa del Sol coast, with a licensed skipper handling the controls so you can focus on the fun. At BoatHire24, we list the fastest RIBs and speedboats available for charter from Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, and other ports along the coast. No hidden fees, no nonsense.

Expect a rigid inflatable boat (RIB) or a sleek bowrider, both capable of hitting 40 knots or more. Your skipper knows the local waters, the best spots for a quick dip, and the best route to show off the coastline. You just turn up, climb aboard, and hold on.
bodyHtml:
What You Get with Speedboat Hire Marbella

Every speedboat charter on BoatHire24 includes a licensed skipper. That means you don't need a boating licence, and you don't have to worry about navigation, fuel, or docking. The skipper handles everything. You get a private charter for your group, typically up to 8 or 12 passengers depending on the boat. The price covers the boat, the skipper, fuel, and insurance. Some charters include a cooler with ice, snorkelling gear, or a Bluetooth speaker. Check the listing for specifics.

Typical durations are 2 hours, 4 hours, or full day (8 hours). A 2-hour blast is enough to get out, open the throttle, and return. A 4-hour trip lets you stop for a swim at a cove like Cala del Faro or drop anchor off the coast of Estepona. Full day charters allow for a longer run, maybe towards the Strait of Gibraltar to spot dolphins, or a relaxed cruise past the Marbella coastline with a lunch stop.

Where You Can Depart From

Speedboat hire Marbella is not limited to one marina. BoatHire24 lists boats from several ports, so you can choose the one closest to your accommodation or the area you want to explore. The main departure points are:

  • Puerto Banús: The most glamorous port, right next to the designer boutiques and restaurants. Ideal if you are staying in Nueva Andalucía or San Pedro Alcántara. Quick access to the open sea and the famous Marbella coastline.
  • Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo): Central Marbella, near the old town. Good if you are staying in Marbella centre or the Golden Mile. Slightly less crowded than Banús.
  • Cabopino: A small, pretty marina near the dunes of Artola. Quieter, with easy access to the Cabopino beach and the natural park.
  • Estepona: West of Marbella, near the charming old town. A good base for exploring the coast towards Gibraltar.
  • Sotogrande: A luxury residential area further west, close to the Gibraltar border. Ideal if you are staying in Sotogrande or Almenara.
  • Benalmádena: East of Marbella, near Fuengirola and Torremolinos. A convenient option if you are staying in the eastern Costa del Sol.

Each port has its own character. Puerto Banús is the flashiest, but all offer easy parking and access to the boats.

Types of Speedboats Available

When you search for speedboat hire Marbella on BoatHire24, you will find two main types of fast boats:

Rigid Inflatable Boats (RIBs): These are the most common. A RIB has a solid hull (usually fibreglass) and inflatable tubes around the sides. They are stable, fast, and handle rough seas well. Most RIBs for charter are between 6 and 10 metres long, with outboard engines ranging from 150 HP to 300 HP or more. Speeds of 35 to 50 knots are standard. RIBs are great for adrenaline seekers and families alike. They seat 6 to 12 people comfortably.

Bowriders and Open Speedboats: These are fibreglass runabouts with a pointed bow and an open cockpit. They look more like a classic speedboat. They are slightly less stable than RIBs but offer a smoother ride in calm conditions. Bowriders typically have a single outboard or sterndrive engine, reaching 30 to 45 knots. They seat 4 to 8 people. They are more about style and comfort than outright speed, but still plenty fast for a thrilling ride.

Some listings also include centre-console fishing boats that are fast enough for speedboat hire, but they are primarily designed for angling. Check the description to confirm the boat type and speed.

Pricing and What It Costs

Prices for speedboat hire Marbella vary depending on the boat size, engine power, duration, and season. As a rough guide:

  • 2-hour charter: from around EUR 400 to EUR 700
  • 4-hour charter: from around EUR 700 to EUR 1,200
  • Full day (8 hours): from around EUR 1,200 to EUR 2,500 or more

These prices include the skipper, fuel, and basic insurance. Some high-end RIBs with twin engines or larger capacity cost more. Always check the listing for exact inclusions. You can filter by price on the search page to find something within your budget.

There are no hidden fees. The price you see on BoatHire24 is the total charter cost. You may need to pay a refundable deposit (typically EUR 200 to EUR 500) on the day, which is returned after the charter if there is no damage. Tipping the skipper is not mandatory but appreciated if you had a good time.

Best Seasons for Speedboat Hire Marbella

The Costa del Sol has a Mediterranean climate. The boating season runs from April to October, with peak season in July and August. Here is what to expect:

  • April to June: Spring. Air temperatures 20-25°C. Sea temperatures 16-20°C. Fewer crowds. Good for a fast ride without the heat. You might need a light jacket on the water.
  • July to August: High summer. Air temperatures 28-35°C. Sea temperatures 24-26°C. Busy ports and crowded anchorages. Book well in advance. The water is perfect for swimming.
  • September to October: Autumn. Air temperatures 25-30°C. Sea temperatures 22-24°C. Still warm, with fewer tourists. Excellent conditions for a speedboat charter.
  • November to March: Winter. Cooler and windier. Some boats are still available, but the sea can be rough. Not ideal for speedboat hire unless you are a hardcore enthusiast. Prices are lower.

The wind can pick up in the afternoon, especially in summer. Morning charters often offer calmer seas. The skipper will advise on the best time for your trip.

How to Book Your Speedboat Charter

Booking speedboat hire Marbella on BoatHire24 is straightforward. Follow these steps:

  1. Go to the BoatHire24 homepage and enter your preferred date, duration, and number of guests.
  2. Browse the results. Use filters for boat type (RIB, speedboat), price, and departure port.
  3. Click on a listing to see full details: photos, specifications, skipper profile, and the exact itinerary.
  4. Check the availability calendar. Select your date and time.
  5. Complete the booking. You pay a deposit (usually 30-50%) online via secure payment. The balance is paid on the day of the charter.
  6. Receive a confirmation email with the meeting point and skipper contact details.

You can also contact the skipper directly through the platform if you have questions before booking. Cancellations are subject to the individual boat owner's policy, usually free cancellation up to 48 or 72 hours before the charter.

Who Is Speedboat Hire Marbella For?

Speedboat hire Marbella suits a range of people:

  • Thrill seekers: If you want speed, a RIB or fast speedboat delivers. Hold on and enjoy the acceleration.
  • Families with older kids: A RIB is safe and stable. Kids love the speed and the chance to spot dolphins. Most skippers are great with children.
  • Groups of friends: A 4-hour charter with a stop for swimming and music is a perfect day out. Many boats have Bluetooth speakers.
  • Couples: A private speedboat charter for two is a romantic and exciting way to see the coastline.
  • Corporate groups: Team building or client entertainment. A fast boat adds a memorable element to any event.

It is not ideal for very young children or people with mobility issues, as boarding and moving around a small boat can be tricky. Check with the skipper if you have specific needs.

What to Bring and Wear

For a speedboat charter, pack light and practical. Essential items:

  • Sunscreen (SPF 50+), sunglasses, and a hat. The sun reflects off the water.
  • A towel and swimwear if you plan to swim.
  • A light windbreaker or jacket, even in summer. The boat creates its own wind.
  • Non-slip shoes or bare feet. No high heels on the boat.
  • Your phone or camera in a waterproof case.
  • Cash or card for tips, drinks, or any extras.

The skipper usually provides life jackets for all passengers. Some boats have a small fridge, but bring your own drinks and snacks if you want something specific. Alcohol is allowed, but the skipper cannot serve it. You can bring your own.

Local Highlights You Might See

On a speedboat charter from Marbella, you can cover a lot of coastline. Depending on your route, you might see:

  • Puerto Banús: The superyacht harbour and the famous Marbella Club Hotel from the sea.
  • La Concha mountain: The iconic peak behind Marbella. Visible from the water.
  • Río Verde: The river mouth near Puerto Banús, a popular anchoring spot.
  • Cala del Faro: A small cove near Cabopino, good for swimming.
  • Estepona old town: The white houses and flower-filled streets visible from the coast.
  • Strait of Gibraltar: On a longer charter, you can head west towards Tarifa and Gibraltar. Dolphin and whale sightings are common here.
  • Nerja caves: Too far for a speedboat from Marbella in a half-day trip, but possible on a full-day charter if you head east.

Your skipper will suggest a route based on your interests and the conditions.

Safety and Regulations

All boats listed on BoatHire24 are licensed and insured. The skipper holds a valid Spanish commercial boating licence. Life jackets are provided and must be worn at the skipper's discretion, especially in rough conditions or for children. The skipper has the final say on the itinerary based on weather and sea state. Safety is always the priority. You are advised to listen to the skipper's briefing at the start of the charter.

Alcohol is permitted on board, but the skipper cannot allow passengers to operate the boat. The skipper remains in control at all times. If you have any medical conditions, inform the skipper before departure.

Frequently Asked Questions

How Speedboat Hire Marbella Compares to Other Boat Types

You might wonder whether a speedboat is the right choice compared to a motor yacht or catamaran. Speedboats are faster and more agile. A typical RIB can hit 40 knots, while a motor yacht of the same length might max out at 25 knots. The trade-off is comfort. Speedboats have less shade, smaller cabins (if any), and a bumpier ride in choppy seas. Catamarans are stable and spacious but slow. If your priority is covering distance quickly or feeling the wind in your face, speedboat hire Marbella wins. If you want lounging space and a toilet, go for a motor yacht. For a party with a group of 12, a catamaran might be better. Speedboats are best for short, active trips.

Specific Routes for a 4-Hour Speedboat Charter from Puerto Banús

A 4-hour charter from Puerto Banús can cover a lot of ground. One popular route heads west: out of the port, past the San Pedro Alcántara beach, and along the coast to Cala del Faro. This takes about 20 minutes at cruising speed. You stop for a swim in the cove, then continue to the Cabopino dunes. Total distance is around 8 nautical miles one way. Another route goes east: past the Marbella Marina and the Golden Mile, towards the Río Real beach. You can stop at the small bay near the Marbella Club Hotel. That is about 5 nautical miles. For a longer run, the skipper might take you to the Estepona coastline, 12 nautical miles west. You see the Sierra Bermeja mountains in the background. The skipper will adjust based on wind and sea state.

What to Expect from a 2-Hour Speedboat Blast

A 2-hour speedboat hire Marbella charter is a short, intense experience. You meet the skipper at the port, board the boat, and get a safety briefing. Within 10 minutes, you are out of the marina. The skipper opens the throttle once you clear the no-wake zone. You accelerate to 30-40 knots. The boat skims across the water. You might do a few high-speed turns if the conditions are calm. The skipper will point out landmarks like La Concha mountain or the Puerto Banús skyline. After 45 minutes of fast cruising, you slow down for a 15-minute swim stop in a sheltered cove. Then you head back. Total time on the water is around 1 hour 45 minutes of active boating. It is enough to get the adrenaline fix without committing to a half day.

How the Sea Conditions Affect Your Speedboat Experience

The Mediterranean is generally calm, but the Costa del Sol can have a choppy sea state, especially in the afternoon. The wind pattern is called the Poniente (west wind) or Levante (east wind). In summer, the Levante can create short, steep waves of 1 to 2 metres. A RIB handles this well because of the inflatable tubes. A fibreglass speedboat will slam more. If the forecast shows wind above 15 knots, the skipper may suggest a morning departure when the sea is flatter. In winter, the sea can be rougher, with waves over 2 metres. Speedboat hire Marbella is still possible, but you will get wet and the ride will be jarring. Check the marine forecast on Windy or ask the skipper before booking. Most skippers will cancel or reschedule if conditions are unsafe, and you get a full refund.

Fuel Costs and Range: What You Should Know

Fuel is included in the price of speedboat hire Marbella on BoatHire24. That is a key advantage. You do not have to worry about refuelling or paying extra. However, the skipper will plan the route to stay within the fuel budget. A typical RIB with a 150 HP outboard burns around 20 to 30 litres per hour at cruising speed (25-30 knots). At full throttle (40 knots), consumption can double. For a 2-hour charter, the fuel cost is roughly EUR 60 to EUR 100, which the owner covers. The range depends on the fuel tank size. Most RIBs have tanks of 100 to 200 litres, giving a range of 60 to 100 nautical miles at cruising speed. That is enough for a trip to Estepona and back from Puerto Banús (24 nautical miles round trip) with plenty of reserve.

How to Choose the Right Speedboat for Your Group Size

Speedboats on BoatHire24 come in different sizes. For 2 to 4 people, a 6-metre RIB or a bowrider is fine. You get individual seats and a small bow area. For 5 to 8 people, look for an 8-metre RIB with two rows of seating. Some have a small sunpad at the front. For 9 to 12 people, you need a 10-metre RIB or a larger centre-console boat. These have more deck space and a toilet compartment. Check the maximum passenger capacity on the listing. Also consider the seating layout. Bench seats are better for groups, while bucket seats are more comfortable for couples. If you want to stand while the boat is moving, a RIB with a grab bar is ideal. The skipper can advise on which boat suits your group size and activity level.

Additional Costs: Deposits, Extras, and Tips

While the charter price is all-inclusive, there are a few potential extra costs. A refundable deposit is common, usually EUR 200 to EUR 500, paid in cash on the day. You get it back if there is no damage. Some skippers offer extras like a GoPro rental (EUR 50), a professional photographer (EUR 150), or a catering package with tapas and drinks (EUR 30 per person). These are optional. Tipping the skipper is not required but is standard in Spain. A tip of 10-15% of the charter price is appreciated if the service was good. For a EUR 600 charter, that is EUR 60 to EUR 90. You can give cash at the end. Parking at the marinas can cost EUR 10 to EUR 20 for a few hours. Bring cash for these incidentals.

Why Book Through BoatHire24 Instead of Directly

Booking speedboat hire Marbella through BoatHire24 gives you several advantages over contacting a boat owner directly. You see all available boats in one place, with transparent pricing and verified photos. The platform handles the payment securely, so you are protected if something goes wrong. You can read the skipper's profile and reviews from previous guests. The booking system sends automatic reminders and the meeting point details. If you need to cancel, the platform enforces the cancellation policy. Direct bookings sometimes involve language barriers or unclear terms. BoatHire24 also offers customer support in English and Spanish. You can browse our blog for tips on what to do in Marbella and the best times to book.

faqs.q: Do I need a boating licence for speedboat hire Marbella?
faqs.a: No. Every speedboat charter on BoatHire24 includes a licensed skipper. You just relax and enjoy the ride.

faqs.q: How many people can fit on a speedboat?
faqs.a: Most RIBs and speedboats seat 6 to 12 passengers. Check the specific listing for the maximum capacity. Larger groups may need a bigger boat.

faqs.q: Can I choose the departure time?
faqs.a: Yes. When you book, you select a start time. Morning charters often have calmer seas. Afternoon charters are warmer. The skipper can confirm the best time.

faqs.q: How far in advance should I book?
faqs.a: In peak season (July-August), book at least a week ahead. In other months, a few days is usually enough. Last-minute bookings are sometimes available.

title: Catamaran Rental Puerto Banus: Group Charters Deals
metaDescription: Looking for catamaran rental Puerto Banus? Compare group charter deals with skipper, food & drinks included. Book your luxury catamaran today.
h1: Catamaran Rental Puerto Banus: Group Charters & Deals
intro:
You want to cruise the Costa del Sol on a catamaran from Puerto Banus without the hassle of haggling with half a dozen charter companies. So do we. BoatHire24 compares the best catamaran rental deals in Puerto Banus, all with a licensed skipper, fuel, and extras like food and drinks included. No hidden fees, no language barriers, just a straightforward booking process.

Whether it's a birthday bash, a corporate jolly, or a family day out, we've got the biggest catamarans on the Med. From 12-metre day cruisers to 30-metre party platforms, you can book direct with the owner or a vetted operator. Prices start from around EUR 1,200 for a half-day charter for up to 12 guests, and go up to EUR 8,000 for a full-day luxury experience with a crew of four.
bodyHtml:
What's Included in a Catamaran Rental in Puerto Banus

Every catamaran charter through BoatHire24 includes a licensed skipper who knows the local waters inside out. You won't need a boating licence or any experience. The skipper will handle navigation, mooring, and safety briefings. Most charters also include fuel for up to 6 hours of cruising, a welcome bottle of cava or sangria, soft drinks, water, and ice. Some premium packages add a full catering menu, snorkelling gear, paddleboards, and a hostess. Always check the listing details, but expect at least basic refreshments and insurance.

Puerto Banus: The Ideal Departure Point

Puerto Banus is the glitziest marina on the Costa del Sol, and for good reason. It's a 15-minute drive from Marbella town, 45 minutes from Malaga airport, and right next to the golden beaches of San Pedro Alcántara. Departing from here means you're minutes away from the famous Marbellan coastline, with views of La Concha mountain to the north and the African coast on a clear day. The marina itself has parking (expensive but convenient), restaurants, and shops. You can also depart from Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona, or Sotogrande if you prefer a quieter vibe.

Types of Catamarans Available

We list three main categories. Day catamarans (12-18 metres) are the most popular for groups of 10-20 people. They have a flybridge, a sun deck, a shaded cockpit, and a galley. Party catamarans (18-25 metres) come with a sound system, a bar, a dance floor, and often a BBQ. Luxury catamarans (25-35 metres) have cabins, a jacuzzi, a crew of 3-4, and full catering. All are motor or sailing catamarans with stabilisers, so you won't get seasick. If you want to sail, we have sailing catamarans too, but most clients prefer the spacious motor catamarans for stability and comfort.

Group Charters and Deals

Booking a catamaran rental Puerto Banus for a group is where the savings kick in. A half-day charter for 12 people might cost EUR 1,200, which works out at EUR 100 per person. That's cheaper than a day at a water park and infinitely more glamorous. For larger groups, we have catamarans that hold up to 60 guests. The per-person price drops to around EUR 80 for a full-day charter including lunch and drinks. Look for deals that include a photographer, a DJ, or a custom cake. We also offer discounts for mid-week bookings and last-minute cancellations (check the search page for current offers).

Best Time of Year for Catamaran Rental

The Costa del Sol has over 300 sunny days a year, so you can charter a catamaran from March to November. July and August are peak season, with temperatures hitting 35 degrees C and water temps at 24 degrees C. Book at least 3 weeks ahead in summer. May, June, September, and October are ideal: 25-30 degrees C, fewer crowds, and lower prices. Even in April and November, you can enjoy a sunny cruise with a light jacket. Winter charters (December-February) are rare but possible for special events; expect 18 degrees C and a chance of rain.

What to Expect on the Day

You arrive at the marina 15 minutes before departure. The skipper greets you, does a safety briefing, and shows you the facilities. You bring your own sunscreen, towels, and swimwear. The catamaran has a toilet, a shower (freshwater), and a sound system. You can bring your own food and drink if the charter doesn't include catering, but most packages include at least a light lunch. The skipper will take you along the coast to Cala del Faro, a secluded cove for swimming and snorkelling. You can also request a stop at the Benabola beach club or a cruise past the Marbella yacht club. The return is usually at 6 PM, but sunset charters are popular too.

How to Book a Catamaran in Puerto Banus

Go to the search page, select Puerto Banus as the departure port, choose your date and group size, and browse the options. Each listing has photos, a detailed description, and a price. You can book directly online with a deposit (usually 30-50%). The balance is paid on the day. We recommend reading the cancellation policy carefully, especially for large groups. If you need help, email us or use the live chat. We can also arrange a custom charter with a specific boat or itinerary. For more tips, check our blog on what to pack and how to haggle.

Who Is a Catamaran Charter For?

Families with kids love catamarans because they're stable and have plenty of shade. Groups of friends use them for birthdays, bachelor parties, and reunions. Corporate clients book them for team-building events, client entertainment, or product launches. Couples sometimes charter a catamaran for a private sunset cruise with a bottle of champagne. Even solo travellers can join a shared charter (look for 'join a group' options). Basically, if you want to be on the water with a group, a catamaran is the best choice.

Local Highlights Near Puerto Banus

From the water, you'll see the iconic La Concha mountain, the pine-covered hills of Sierra Blanca, and the luxury villas of the Golden Mile. The skipper might point out the Río Verde, where the river meets the sea. You can anchor at Cala del Faro, a small beach with turquoise water, or head towards Estepona to see the old town from the sea. If you're lucky, you'll spot dolphins in the Strait of Gibraltar area, though that's a longer trip (2-3 hours from Puerto Banus). For a full-day charter, you can even go as far as Nerja caves, but that's a 4-hour cruise each way.

Why Book Through BoatHire24

We're a marketplace, not a charter company. That means we list boats from multiple owners, so you can compare prices, sizes, and inclusions. We have over 50 catamarans available in Puerto Banus alone, from budget-friendly to ultra-luxury. Every charter includes a skipper and insurance. We don't charge booking fees, and you pay the balance directly to the operator. We also provide a local contact number in case of last-minute changes. Start your search on the BoatHire24 homepage and filter by Puerto Banus.

Comparing Catamaran Sizes for Your Group

Choosing the right catamaran size is about balancing space and budget. A 12-metre catamaran typically accommodates 10-12 guests comfortably. That means a flybridge for sunbathing, a main deck with seating, and a small galley. For 15-20 people, step up to a 15-metre catamaran. You get a larger flybridge, a separate bow sun deck, and more shade. The 18-metre party catamarans handle 25-35 guests with ease, featuring a bar, a BBQ, and a sound system. For 40-60 guests, you need a 22-25 metre catamaran with two decks and a crew of at least three. The price difference between a 12-metre and a 22-metre catamaran is roughly EUR 800 to EUR 3,000 for a full day. Work out the per-person cost before you book, because a bigger boat often works out cheaper per head.

Half-Day vs Full-Day Charters: Which is Better

A half-day catamaran rental in Puerto Banus lasts 4 hours, usually 10 AM to 2 PM or 2 PM to 6 PM. That's enough time for a coastal cruise, a swim stop at Cala del Faro, and a light lunch. It costs from around EUR 800 for a 12-metre catamaran for up to 12 guests. A full-day charter runs 8 hours, typically 10 AM to 6 PM. You get two swim stops, a proper lunch (often grilled fish or paella), and time to explore further, like cruising to Estepona or even the Marbella coast. Full-day rates start at EUR 1,500 for the same boat. For groups of 15 or more, the full-day option makes sense because you can split the cost and get more value. Sunset charters (6 PM to 10 PM) are popular for romantic evenings, with prices similar to half-day but including a dinner option.

Departure Port Alternatives to Puerto Banus

If you're staying in Estepona, the marina there is quieter and cheaper to park at. Estepona's old town is a 5-minute walk from the marina, and the catamaran can take you past the Sierra Bermeja mountains. Cabopino Marina, near Marbella, is smaller but has a lovely dune beach and a nature reserve. It's ideal if you want a less crowded departure point. Sotogrande is 30 minutes south of Puerto Banus, closer to Gibraltar. It's a luxury resort with a golf course and a private marina. Catamarans from Sotogrande can reach the Strait of Gibraltar faster, so you're more likely to see dolphins. Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo) is in the town centre, near the old town and the beachfront promenade. Each port has its own vibe, and BoatHire24 lists catamarans at all of them. Check the search page to compare departure points.

Catering Options and What to Expect

Most catamaran charters include a basic menu: a selection of sandwiches, salads, fruit, and a bottle of cava or sangria per 4 guests. Premium packages add a three-course lunch with fresh seafood, grilled meats, and desserts. You can also request a custom menu for an extra EUR 30-50 per person. For a birthday or anniversary, ask for a cake, a fruit platter, or a champagne tower. Some catamarans have a full galley where the crew can cook paella on board. Others require a pre-ordered catering delivery from a local restaurant. Always confirm the menu when you book, especially if you have dietary restrictions. Vegetarian, vegan, and gluten-free options are available if you ask in advance. If you prefer to bring your own food, most charters allow it, but check the listing details.

Weather and Sea Conditions on the Costa del Sol

The Mediterranean is generally calm, but conditions vary. In summer (June to September), the sea is flat, with waves under 0.5 metres. The wind is light, usually a thermal breeze from the west in the afternoon. That's perfect for catamarans, which are stable even in 1-metre swells. In spring and autumn, you might get a Levante wind from the east, which can create choppy seas of 1-2 metres. Catamarans handle this well, but the skipper may adjust the route to stay in sheltered waters. Winter can bring storms, but charters are rare then. Always check the forecast on the day. If the sea is too rough, the operator will cancel or reschedule, and you'll get a full refund. The water temperature is 22-26 degrees C from June to October, so swimming is comfortable. In April and May, it's 18-20 degrees C, still doable with a wetsuit.

How to Get the Best Deal on a Catamaran Rental

Book early for peak season, but look for last-minute deals in shoulder months. Mid-week charters (Monday to Thursday) are 20-30% cheaper than weekends. Shared charters are the cheapest option, where you join a group of strangers on a scheduled trip. Prices start at EUR 80 per person for a half-day. For private charters, negotiate directly with the operator through BoatHire24. If you book a full-day charter for 20+ guests, ask for a discount on the catering or a free upgrade to a larger boat. Some operators offer a free hour if you book a 6-hour charter. Check the blog for seasonal promotions and coupon codes. Also, consider booking a catamaran with a hostess included, as it saves you EUR 100-150 for a separate hostess hire.

Skipper and Crew Details

Every catamaran charter includes a licensed skipper. The skipper is responsible for navigation, safety, and anchoring. They also act as a guide, pointing out landmarks like La Concha mountain and the luxury villas of the Golden Mile. Some skippers speak English, Spanish, and German. If you want a hostess, it costs an extra EUR 100-200 per day. The hostess serves drinks, prepares food, and keeps the boat clean. For larger catamarans (20+ guests), a crew of two or three is standard: a skipper, a hostess, and a deckhand. The crew works on tips, so budget EUR 50-100 per person for a full day if you're happy with the service. You don't have to tip, but it's customary in Spain for good service.

Safety and Insurance on Catamaran Charters

All catamarans listed on BoatHire24 have valid insurance covering third-party liability and passenger accidents. The skipper will do a safety briefing before departure, showing you where life jackets, fire extinguishers, and first aid kits are stored. Life jackets are mandatory for children under 12 and non-swimmers. The catamaran has a VHF radio and a GPS for emergencies. In case of bad weather, the skipper has the authority to cancel the trip. You'll get a full refund if the operator cancels. If you cancel within 48 hours, you may lose your deposit. Read the cancellation policy on each listing before you book. For peace of mind, consider travel insurance that covers charter cancellations.

What to Bring and What to Leave at Home

Bring sunscreen (SPF 50+), a hat, sunglasses, a towel, and swimwear. A light jacket or windbreaker is useful for the evening, even in summer. Leave high heels at home, as catamarans have non-slip decks and heels can damage the teak. No glass bottles on most boats, so bring plastic cups or cans. If you're prone to seasickness, take a motion sickness pill an hour before departure. Catamarans are stable, but if the sea is choppy, you might feel it. The crew will have water and soft drinks, but bring your own if you want specific brands. A waterproof phone case is handy for photos. Don't bring large coolers unless the charter specifies it's allowed. Most catamarans have a built-in fridge and ice maker.

Special Events on Catamarans in Puerto Banus

Catamaran charters are popular for birthdays, bachelor parties, and anniversaries. For a bachelor party, you can add a DJ, a photobooth, and a champagne tower. Some catamarans have a built-in sound system with Bluetooth. Corporate events include product launches, client dinners, and team-building activities. You can request a projector for presentations or a whiteboard for brainstorming. Weddings are possible, but you need a catamaran with a large deck for a ceremony and a reception. The crew can arrange a wedding cake, flowers, and a photographer. Prices for special events start at EUR 2,000 for a half-day charter for 20 guests. For a full-day with catering and entertainment, budget EUR 5,000-10,000. Contact BoatHire24 for a custom quote on special events.

Nearby Attractions Accessible by Catamaran

From Puerto Banus, you can cruise to the Benabola beach club for lunch or a swim. The beach club has a restaurant, sun loungers, and a pool. Further south, Cala del Faro is a secluded cove with crystal-clear water, ideal for snorkelling. If you head east, you'll pass the Marbella yacht club and the Golden Mile villas. Estepona's old town is visible from the sea, with its whitewashed houses and colourful flower pots. For a longer trip, the Strait of Gibraltar is 45 minutes by catamaran from Sotogrande. You'll see dolphins, and sometimes whales, in the strait. The Nerja caves are 4 hours east, so that's a full-day charter with an overnight stay. The skipper can tailor the itinerary to your interests.

Related guides

  • Fishing Charter Marbella Half Day: Morning or Afternoon

faqs.q: Do I need a boating licence to rent a catamaran in Puerto Banus?
faqs.a: No. Every catamaran charter includes a licensed skipper who handles all navigation. You just relax and enjoy the cruise.

faqs.a: Most day catamarans hold 10-20 guests. Party catamarans can take up to 60. Check the listing for max capacity. Prices vary by size.

faqs.a: Half-day charters start from around EUR 1,200 for 12 guests. Full-day luxury catamarans cost EUR 4,000-8,000. Per person, that's EUR 80-100.

faqs.a: Yes, most charters allow you to bring your own. Many packages include a basic selection of drinks and snacks. Check the listing details.

faqs.a: May, June, September, and October offer warm weather and lower prices. July and August are peak season with higher demand and prices.

faqs.a: Use the search page to filter by Puerto Banus, date, and group size. Browse listings, read details, and book online with a deposit.
