  • Luxury Yacht Charter Puerto Banus Day: All Inclusive
  • Private Catamaran Hire Marbella Sunset
  • Motor Yacht Rental Estepona Day: Luxury on Water
  • Catamaran Charter Marbella Birthday: Party Package

faqs.q: Do I need a boat licence to rent a boat in Estepona port?
faqs.a: No. All charters on BoatHire24 include a licensed skipper. You can also rent a licence-free day boat (under 6m, low power) without a skipper, but you must pass a basic safety check at the marina.

faqs.q: How much does a boat rental Estepona port cost?
faqs.a: Prices start from around EUR 350 for a half-day on a RIB, EUR 500-800 for a motor yacht, and EUR 900-1,200 for a catamaran. Superyachts cost EUR 4,000+. Fuel is usually included.

faqs.q: What is the best time of year for a boat charter from Estepona?
faqs.a: May, June, September, and October offer warm weather, calm seas, and fewer crowds. July and August are hotter and busier but still good. Winter charters are possible but weather is unpredictable.

faqs.q: Can I bring my own food and drink on the boat?
faqs.a: Yes. You can bring a cooler with your own food and drinks. The skipper will have a fridge on board. Some operators offer catering packages from marina restaurants.

faqs.q: How long does it take to get from Estepona to Gibraltar by boat?
faqs.a: About 1 hour 15 minutes at cruising speed (25 NM). You can see dolphins in the Strait. You cannot land in Gibraltar without a passport and customs check.

faqs.q: What happens if the weather is bad on the day of my charter?
faqs.a: The skipper decides if it’s safe to sail. If they cancel due to weather, you get a full refund or a reschedule. We recommend checking the forecast the day before.

faqs.q: Can I book a boat for a sunset cruise from Estepona port?
faqs.a: Yes. Many operators offer 3-hour sunset charters from around EUR 300-400. You leave at 6pm in summer, cruise, anchor, and watch the sunset over the Sierra Bermeja.

title: Luxury Catamaran Rental Marbella: Premium Comfort
metaDescription: Book a luxury catamaran rental in Marbella with a licensed skipper. Explore the Costa del Sol from Puerto Banús. Check prices and availability now.
h1: Luxury Catamaran Rental Marbella: Premium Comfort on the Sea
intro:
If you want a day on the water that’s less about adrenaline and more about lounging in style, a luxury catamaran rental in Marbella is the obvious choice. Catamarans are the SUVs of the sea: stable, spacious, and equipped with enough deck space to host a small party or just stretch out with a book. You get two hulls, a massive sunbathing area up front, and usually a flybridge for panoramic views of the coast. Every charter includes a licensed skipper, so you don’t need to know anything about navigation. You just decide where to go and what to drink.

BoatHire24 lists dozens of catamarans based in Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona, Sotogrande, and Benalmádena. The boats range from 40 feet up to 70 feet, with capacities from 8 to 30 guests. Prices start from around EUR 1,500 for a half-day on a mid-sized model and go up to EUR 8,000 for a full-day on a 70-footer with all the extras. You can see the full inventory on our search page.
bodyHtml:
What You Get with a Luxury Catamaran Rental in Marbella

A luxury catamaran rental in Marbella is not just a boat. It is a floating lounge with a galley, a bathroom, and often a small cabin for changing. The key features are stability (you barely feel the swell), shallow draft (you can anchor close to beaches like Cala del Faro), and a wide beam that gives you a huge deck. Most catamarans have a trampoline net at the bow where you can sunbathe or sit with your feet dangling over the water. The flybridge usually has a second helm station and a seating area with a bimini top for shade.

Every charter includes a licensed skipper who knows the local waters. You can ask them to take you past the Marbella coastline, stop for a swim at a quiet cove, or head towards the Strait of Gibraltar to spot dolphins. You can also bring your own food and drink, or order catering through the charter company. Some catamarans come with paddleboards, snorkelling gear, and even a small inflatable dinghy. The skipper will handle the anchor, the lines, and the safety briefing. You just relax.

Departure Ports for Catamaran Charters

The most popular starting point is Puerto Banús. This is the glitzy marina with the designer shops and the superyachts. From here, you can head east towards Marbella town and the Marbella Marina, or west towards Estepona and the lighthouse at Punta Doncella. The typical route is a coastal cruise with a stop for lunch at a beach club or a swim in the clear water off Cabopino.

If you are staying in Sotogrande, you can depart from the marina there and explore the coast towards Gibraltar. The advantage of Sotogrande is that you are closer to the dolphin pods that gather in the Strait of Gibraltar. From Benalmádena, you can go east to the Nerja caves or west to Marbella. The choice of port affects the cruise time and the scenery. You can filter by port on our search page.

Boat Options: Which Catamaran Is Right for You

The catamarans available for luxury rental in Marbella include models from Lagoon, Fountaine Pajot, Bali, and Leopard. A 40-foot Lagoon 42 sleeps six in three cabins, but for a day charter the cabins are used for changing and storage. A 50-foot catamaran like the Lagoon 52 has a large flybridge, a foredeck lounge, and a spacious saloon with sliding doors. The biggest catamarans, such as the Lagoon 620 or Sunreef 60, have multiple seating areas, a bar, and sometimes a jacuzzi on the flybridge.

For a group of 8 to 12, a 45- to 50-foot catamaran is ideal. For 12 to 18, you want a 55- to 60-footer. For anything above 20 guests, you are looking at a 65- to 70-foot catamaran or a superyacht. The price increases with size and age of the boat. Older models (2015-2018) cost less than new ones (2022-2024). You can also choose between sailing catamarans and power catamarans. Power catamarans are faster (around 20 knots cruising) but use more fuel. Sailing catamarans are quieter and more eco-friendly, but slower (around 8 knots).

Pricing Guidance for Luxury Catamaran Rental Marbella

Prices for a luxury catamaran rental in Marbella vary by boat size, age, season, and duration. As a rough guide, a half-day (4 hours) on a 42-foot catamaran in the low season costs from around EUR 1,200 to EUR 1,800. A full day (8 hours) on the same boat is EUR 1,800 to EUR 2,500. For a 50-foot catamaran, half-day starts at EUR 2,000 and full day at EUR 3,000. For a 60-footer, expect EUR 3,500 to EUR 5,000 for a full day. The top-end 70-foot catamarans can cost EUR 6,000 to EUR 8,000 for a full day.

High season (June to September) adds 20-30% to these prices. Also note that fuel is usually not included and costs around EUR 200 to EUR 500 depending on the distance and speed. Food and drink are extra, unless you choose a package with catering. Some charter companies require a security deposit, typically EUR 500 to EUR 2,000, refunded after the charter. You can check exact prices for specific dates on each boat listing.

Best Time for a Catamaran Charter in Marbella

The Costa del Sol has a Mediterranean climate with mild winters and hot summers. The best months for a luxury catamaran rental in Marbella are May, June, September, and October. In these months the air temperature is 22-28 degrees C, the water is 18-24 degrees C, and the wind is moderate. July and August are hotter (30-35 degrees C) and more crowded, but the water is warm (24-26 degrees C) and the sea is calm in the mornings. The afternoon sea breeze can pick up, but catamarans handle it well.

Winter charters (November to March) are possible but less popular. The air temperature is 15-20 degrees C, and the water is colder (14-16 degrees C). You can still cruise if you bring warm clothes, but swimming is less pleasant. The advantage is lower prices and fewer boats on the water. Many catamarans are laid up for winter maintenance, so availability is limited. Spring and autumn offer the best balance of comfort and value.

How to Book a Luxury Catamaran Rental in Marbella

Booking through BoatHire24 is straightforward. You go to our BoatHire24 homepage, choose catamaran as the boat type, select your preferred port, and enter the date and duration. The search results show available boats with photos, specifications, and prices. You can compare several options and then click through to the charter company’s booking page. Some listings allow instant online booking, while others require a request for a quote.

When you book, you will need to provide the number of guests, any special requests (catering, extra activities), and your contact details. The charter company will confirm availability and send a contract. Payment is usually by bank transfer or credit card. A deposit of 30-50% is common, with the balance due a few weeks before the charter. Cancellation policies vary, so read the terms carefully. We recommend booking at least two weeks in advance in high season.

Who Should Rent a Luxury Catamaran in Marbella

A luxury catamaran rental in Marbella is ideal for families with children (the stability is a plus), groups of friends celebrating a birthday or bachelor party, corporate groups for a team-building day, and couples wanting a romantic cruise. The wide deck and shallow draft make catamarans great for anchoring close to beaches, so you can swim, snorkel, or paddleboard. The skipper can also take you to quieter spots like Cala del Faro or the coves near Cabopino.

If you are a group of 10 or more, a catamaran is often more cost-effective than a smaller monohull because you get more space per person. For a private event like a wedding reception or a sunset cocktail party, a catamaran with a flybridge offers a great setting. You can also combine a catamaran charter with a visit to Estepona old town or a trip to the Strait of Gibraltar to see dolphins. The skipper can tailor the itinerary to your preferences.

Local Attractions and Itineraries

From Puerto Banús, a common itinerary is to cruise east towards the Marbella Marina, then continue to Cabopino for a swim stop. Cabopino has a sandy beach and a dune system that is a protected area. If you have more time, you can go further east to Cala del Faro, a rocky cove with clear water. Another route is west from Puerto Banús to Estepona, where you can see the old town from the sea and stop at the Estepona Marina for lunch.

For a longer charter (full day or more), you can head towards the Strait of Gibraltar. This area is known for pilot whales, striped dolphins, and common dolphins. The crossing from Marbella to the strait is about 40 nautical miles, so it takes 4-5 hours one way on a sailing catamaran or 2-3 hours on a power catamaran. You can also go north to the Nerja caves, but that is a long trip (60 NM) and better done as a two-day charter. For more ideas, read our blog on Costa del Sol itineraries.

What to Bring and What to Expect

On a luxury catamaran rental in Marbella, you should bring swimwear, sunscreen (reef-safe is recommended), a hat, sunglasses, a light jacket or windbreaker for the afternoon breeze, and a camera. Most catamarans have a cooler with ice, but you can bring your own drinks and snacks. The skipper will provide safety equipment and a briefing. You can also bring music via Bluetooth if the boat has a sound system.

Expect a relaxed atmosphere. The skipper will handle all the navigation and anchoring. You can help with the sails if you want, but it is not required. The catamaran will have a toilet (usually one or two), and some have a shower on the back deck. The galley has a stove, fridge, and sink. If you order catering, it will be delivered to the boat before departure. The charter usually includes cleaning and fuel for a standard itinerary. Extra fuel for long distances is charged separately.

Safety and Regulations

All catamarans on BoatHire24 are licensed for commercial charter and have the required safety equipment: life jackets, fire extinguishers, flares, first aid kit, and a VHF radio. The skipper is licensed by the Spanish authorities and has experience in the local waters. The boat is insured for liability and damage. Before departure, the skipper will give a safety briefing and explain the location of emergency equipment.

In Spanish waters, the maximum number of passengers on a charter catamaran is determined by the boat’s registration and is typically 12 for a 40-footer, 18 for a 50-footer, and 30 for a 70-footer. Children count as passengers. The skipper has the final say on the itinerary based on weather conditions. If the sea state is too rough, the charter may be cancelled or rescheduled. You can check the weather forecast a few days before your charter.

Why Choose a Catamaran Over a Motor Yacht for Luxury in Marbella

You might wonder why you would pick a catamaran instead of a sleek motor yacht for your Marbella charter. The answer is space and stability. A 45-foot catamaran has about the same deck area as a 60-foot motor yacht because the beam is wider. You get two hulls instead of one, which means no heeling over when sailing and almost no rolling at anchor. If you have guests who get seasick easily, a catamaran is the better choice. The shallow draft also lets you anchor in spots where a deep-keel motor yacht cannot go, such as the sandbar off Cabopino where the water is only 2 metres deep. The trade-off is speed: a motor yacht can do 25-30 knots, while a sailing catamaran cruises at 8-10 knots. But if the goal is to lounge, swim, and eat, the slower pace is fine.

Specific Catamaran Models You Will Find in Marbella

The charter fleet in Marbella includes a range of catamarans, each with a different feel. The Lagoon 42 is the most common mid-size option. It has four cabins and a large saloon with panoramic windows. The Fountaine Pajot Astrea 42 is similar but has a more open galley layout. If you want something newer, the Bali 4.6 has a hard-top bimini that covers the entire cockpit, giving you shade without losing the outdoor feel. For a bigger group, the Lagoon 52 has a flybridge that seats 10 people around a table, plus a sun pad area on the foredeck. The Leopard 50 is another popular choice with a walk-through transom that makes boarding from the water easy. At the top end, the Sunreef 60 is a luxury model with a fully enclosed flybridge, air conditioning throughout, and a jacuzzi. You can see photos and deck plans on each listing to compare.

How the Season Affects Your Catamaran Experience

The time of year changes not just the price but also the feel of the charter. In May, the water is around 18 degrees C and the beaches are quiet. You can anchor at Cala del Faro without fighting for space. In June, the water warms to 22 degrees C and the sea breeze starts around 2 PM, which is perfect for sailing. July and August are peak season: the water is 24-26 degrees C, but the anchorages near Puerto Banús and Marbella Marina get crowded. You might need to anchor further out and take the dinghy to shore. September is a sweet spot: the water is still warm (22-24 degrees C), the crowds thin out after the first week, and the evening light is golden for photos. October is cooler (air 22-26 degrees C, water 20-22 degrees C) but still pleasant for a day cruise. If you book in July or August, aim for a morning departure (10 AM) to avoid the afternoon wind and the heat.

Extra Costs You Should Budget For

The base price of a luxury catamaran rental in Marbella covers the boat, the skipper, and basic fuel for a standard itinerary. But there are extras. Fuel surcharges apply if you go beyond the typical 20-30 nautical mile range. For example, a trip from Puerto Banús to the Strait of Gibraltar and back is about 80 nautical miles, which adds EUR 300 to EUR 600 in fuel for a 50-foot catamaran. Mooring fees at beach clubs like Ocean Club in Marbella or Nikki Beach in Estepona cost EUR 50 to EUR 150 for a lunch stop. If you want a water toys package with a Seabob, jet ski, or inflatable slide, that adds EUR 200 to EUR 500. Catering from a local caterer costs EUR 30 to EUR 60 per person for a lunch menu with paella, salads, and drinks. Some charter companies charge a cleaning fee of EUR 100 to EUR 200 if you leave the boat messy. Always ask for a full breakdown before you confirm the booking.

How to Choose the Right Skipper for Your Catamaran Charter

Every catamaran charter includes a skipper, but the experience level varies. A good skipper for a luxury catamaran rental in Marbella should speak English and Spanish, know the local anchorages, and be able to suggest a flexible itinerary. Some skippers are also certified in first aid and have experience with large groups. If you want a more interactive trip, ask for a skipper who is happy to explain the navigation and let you help with the sails. If you prefer privacy, request a skipper who stays on the flybridge and only interacts when needed. You can read the skipper’s profile on the charter company’s page, including their years of experience and languages spoken. For a corporate event or a wedding proposal, you might want a skipper who can coordinate with the beach club or the photographer. The skipper is not a tour guide, but most are happy to point out landmarks like La Concha mountain or the Marbella Castle.

faqs.q: Do I need a licence to rent a luxury catamaran in Marbella?
faqs.a: No. Every charter through BoatHire24 includes a licensed skipper who handles the navigation. You just enjoy the ride.

faqs.q: How many people can a luxury catamaran hold?
faqs.a: It depends on the boat size. A 40-foot catamaran holds up to 12 guests. A 50-footer holds up to 18. A 60-footer holds up to 24. A 70-footer holds up to 30.

faqs.q: What is the cost of a luxury catamaran rental in Marbella?
faqs.a: Prices start from around EUR 1,200 for a half-day on a 42-foot catamaran in low season, up to EUR 8,000 for a full day on a 70-footer in high season. Fuel and catering are extra.

faqs.q: Can I bring my own food and drink?
faqs.a: Yes, you can bring your own. Most catamarans have a cooler and a galley. You can also order catering through the charter company at an additional cost.

faqs.q: What is the best time of year for a catamaran charter?
faqs.a: May, June, September, and October offer warm weather, calm seas, and fewer crowds. July and August are hotter but still good. Winter charters are possible but cooler.

faqs.q: Where can I go from Marbella on a catamaran?
faqs.a: Popular destinations include Cabopino, Cala del Faro, Estepona, the Strait of Gibraltar for dolphin watching, and the Nerja caves for a longer trip.

faqs.q: How do I book a luxury catamaran rental in Marbella?
faqs.a: Go to BoatHire24, select catamaran as the boat type, choose your port and date, and compare available boats. Click through to book or request a quote.

title: Speedboat Rental Puerto Banus: Fast & Fun
metaDescription: Looking for speedboat rental Puerto Banus? Fast, fun rides with a licensed skipper. Compare RIBs and speedboats from EUR 300. Book your adventure now.
h1: Speedboat Rental Puerto Banus: Fast & Fun
intro:
If you want to get out on the water fast, a speedboat rental in Puerto Banus is your best bet. No waiting around, no complicated sailing lessons, just you, the wind, and a powerful engine. Every rental comes with a licensed skipper, so you can sit back and enjoy the ride, or take the helm if you fancy it. We’re talking about RIBs, speedboats, and small motor yachts that hit 30-50 knots and get you to the best spots in minutes.

Puerto Banus is the obvious starting point. You leave the marina, pass the golden beach of San Pedro, and within 15 minutes you’re at Cala del Faro or the cliffs of Cabopino. Want to see dolphins in the Strait of Gibraltar? That’s a longer trip, but doable in a fast boat. The point is, speedboat rental in Puerto Banus gives you flexibility and thrills without the hassle. At BoatHire24, we compare options from local operators so you can pick the boat that fits your group and budget.
bodyHtml:
What You Get with a Speedboat Rental in Puerto Banus

A speedboat rental in Puerto Banus includes the boat, fuel for the trip, and a professional skipper. Most boats are RIBs (rigid inflatable boats) or small motor yachts with outboard engines. You get life jackets, safety equipment, and basic insurance. The skipper handles navigation and docking, but you can drive if you have the right licence. Typical trips last 2, 4, or 8 hours. You can stop for swimming, snorkeling, or lunch at a beach club. Prices start from around EUR 300 for a 2-hour spin in a 6-metre RIB, up to EUR 1,200 for a full day on a 10-metre speedboat.

Puerto Banus, The Launch Point for Speed

Puerto Banus is the glitzy marina in Marbella, packed with superyachts and designer shops. For speedboat rental, it’s ideal because you’re close to the open sea. You pass the breakwater and you’re already in the Mediterranean. The marina has plenty of parking, restaurants, and a fuel dock. Most operators are based here, so you can walk from your hotel to the boat. The departure is straightforward: you meet the skipper at the charter office, do a quick safety briefing, and you’re off. If you’re staying in Marbella, Estepona, or Sotogrande, Puerto Banus is a short drive and worth the trip for the variety of boats available.

Types of Speedboats Available

The term “speedboat” covers a few different craft. RIBs are the most common: a rigid hull with inflatable tubes, light and fast. They seat 6 to 12 people and hit 40-50 knots. Perfect for a short adrenaline rush. Then there are classic speedboats with a deep-V hull, more stable at speed, often with a cuddy cabin for storage. These seat 8 to 14 people and do 30-40 knots. Some operators offer small motor yachts with twin engines, which are slower (25-30 knots) but more comfortable for longer trips. All include a skipper. You can search by boat type on our search page to see what’s available on your dates.

How Much Does a Speedboat Rental in Puerto Banus Cost?

Prices vary by boat size, engine power, and trip duration. A 6-metre RIB for 2 hours is around EUR 300-400. A 9-metre speedboat for 4 hours is EUR 600-800. Full-day rentals (8 hours) on a 10-metre boat are EUR 1,000-1,500. High season (June to September) costs 20-30% more. Fuel is usually included, but check with the operator. Some boats have a fuel surcharge if you go beyond a certain distance. You can also add extras like water toys, snorkeling gear, or a photographer. For exact prices, use the search page to get quotes from multiple charter companies.

Best Routes from Puerto Banus

With a speedboat, you can cover a lot of ground. A 2-hour trip might go to Cala del Faro (15 minutes east), a rocky cove with clear water for swimming. Or head west to the beaches of Estepona, past the new marina. A 4-hour trip can reach the Strait of Gibraltar, where you might see dolphins and pilot whales. You can also go east to Cabopino and the dunes of Artola, or south to the coast of Africa on a clear day. For a full day, you can go to the Nerja caves (1.5 hours east) or the port of Sotogrande. Your skipper will advise based on weather and sea conditions.

Who Is Speedboat Rental For?

Speedboat rental in Puerto Banus suits groups who want excitement, not a lazy cruise. It’s good for stag and hen parties, birthday celebrations, or corporate team-building. Families with older kids (8+) enjoy the thrill and the chance to swim in secluded spots. Couples might prefer a private sunset trip with champagne. If you’re after a more relaxed day, a catamaran or sailing yacht might be better. Speedboats are louder and bumpier, but they get you there fast. You can read our blog for tips on choosing the right boat for your group.

Licence-Free Options

Some speedboats in Puerto Banus are available without a licence, but only if you take a skipper. That’s the default on BoatHire24, every rental includes a licensed skipper. If you have a boating licence (ICC or equivalent), you can ask to drive under the skipper’s supervision. Operators may require a test drive first. For licence-free day boats (smaller, slower), you can rent without a skipper, but those are usually 4-6 metre boats with 15-30 hp engines, not speedboats. Speedboats require a skipper for insurance reasons. So you get the speed without the responsibility.

When to Rent a Speedboat in Puerto Banus

The best time is May to October, when sea temperatures are 18-26 degrees C and the wind is light. July and August are peak, with prices higher and marinas busy. Morning trips (9am-1pm) have calm seas and the best light. Afternoon trips can be choppy due to the Levante wind. Sunset trips (6pm-9pm) are popular for the golden hour. Winter (November to April) is still possible, but the sea can be rough and some operators reduce services. Book at least a week in advance in summer, especially for weekends.

How to Book a Speedboat in Puerto Banus

Booking through BoatHire24 is simple. Go to the search page, enter your dates, group size, and preferences. You’ll see available speedboats with prices, photos, and skipper profiles. Choose one, pay a deposit (usually 30-50%), and the rest on the day. You’ll get a confirmation with the meeting point and skipper contact. Cancel up to 48 hours before for a full refund. On the day, arrive 15 minutes early at the charter office in Puerto Banus. The skipper will do a safety briefing, and you’re off. No hidden fees, no surprises.

What to Bring on Your Speedboat Trip

Bring swimwear, a towel, sunscreen (reef-safe if possible), sunglasses, a hat, and a waterproof phone case. The boat has a small cooler for drinks, but you can bring your own. Most operators supply water and soft drinks. If you’re prone to seasickness, take a pill an hour before, speedboats are bumpy. Wear non-slip shoes. Leave valuables at the hotel; the boat has limited dry storage. A GoPro or action camera is great for video. Your skipper will have a first aid kit and basic tools.

Safety and Skipper Qualifications

All skippers on BoatHire24 hold a valid Spanish boating licence (Patrón de Navegación Básica or higher) and have experience in local waters. They know the hazards: rocks near Cala del Faro, shallow areas at Cabopino, and traffic in the Strait of Gibraltar. The boat is inspected annually and has life jackets for all passengers, a VHF radio, and emergency equipment. In rough weather, the skipper may cancel or reroute. Your safety is the priority. If you have any concerns, ask the skipper during the briefing. They’re there to ensure you have fun without incident.

Comparing Speedboat Rental to Other Options

Speedboats are the fastest way to explore the coast, but they’re not for everyone. Catamarans are more stable and spacious, better for large groups wanting a party. Sailing yachts offer a quieter, more romantic experience. Fishing boats come with gear and a guide. Licence-free day boats are cheaper but slower and limited to calm waters. For thrill-seekers, a speedboat rental in Puerto Banus is the best value per hour of fun. You cover more distance, see more spots, and get an adrenaline kick. Check our blog for a full comparison of boat types.

Local Attractions Accessible by Speedboat

From Puerto Banus, you can reach several landmarks. Cala del Faro is a small cove with a lighthouse, great for photos. The Río Verde estuary is a nature spot with birds and quiet beaches. Estepona old town, visible from the sea, has a colourful marina and tapas bars. The Strait of Gibraltar is a 40-minute run west, where you can see the Rock of Gibraltar and maybe a pod of dolphins. Nerja caves are 1.5 hours east, but worth the trip for the dramatic cliffs and sea caves. Your skipper can tailor the route to your interests.

Why Puerto Banus Works Better for Speedboats Than Other Ports

Puerto Banus has a direct channel to the open sea, no winding river or narrow harbour to navigate. From the moment you leave the marina, you’re in deep water within 5 minutes. Compare that to Estepona, where you spend 10 minutes motoring through the port channel, or Sotogrande, where the Guadiaro River mouth can be tricky at low tide. Benalmádena is close to the open sea too, but the marina is smaller and has fewer speedboat options. Cabopino is a tiny port with limited charter operators, mostly fishing boats. For pure speed, Puerto Banus gives you the fastest exit and the widest choice of high-performance RIBs. You can be doing 40 knots past La Concha mountain before the other ports have cleared their breakwaters.

Fuel Costs and Range: What to Expect

Fuel is included in most speedboat rentals in Puerto Banus, but not all. If it’s not included, expect to pay around EUR 80-150 per hour for a 200-300 hp outboard, depending on how hard you push the throttle. A 2-hour trip at cruising speed (25-30 knots) burns roughly 40-60 litres of petrol. At full throttle (40+ knots), that doubles. With fuel included, the operator factors in a standard route. If you ask to go further, say to Nerja or Tarifa, they may charge a fuel supplement. The range of a typical 7-metre RIB with a 250 hp engine is about 80-100 nautical miles at cruising speed. That’s enough to reach Gibraltar and back with some margin. Always confirm fuel policy before booking. Our BoatHire24 listings show whether fuel is included or not.

Speedboat Rental for Fishing Trips from Puerto Banus

A speedboat is not ideal for serious fishing, but it works for light trolling or jigging. RIBs with 200+ hp engines can reach the fishing grounds off Cabopino or the Estepona reef in 15 minutes. You can catch mackerel, bonito, and small tuna in summer. The skipper will have basic rods and lures, but serious anglers should bring their own gear. Speedboats lack the space and rod holders of dedicated fishing boats. For a proper fishing charter, look at the fishing boats on our search page. But if you want a quick session between swim stops, a speedboat rental in Puerto Banus does the job. The shallow banks off the Río Verde are a good spot, 10 minutes from the marina, with 10-15 metre depths and plenty of baitfish.

Speedboat Rental for Sunset Cruises

A sunset speedboat trip from Puerto Banus is a different experience from the daytime adrenaline run. The sea calms in the evening, and the light turns the La Concha mountain pink. A 2-hour sunset rental (usually 7pm-9pm in summer) costs around EUR 400-600 for a 7-metre RIB. You can stop at Cala del Faro for a swim, watch the sun drop behind the Strait of Gibraltar, and cruise back under the stars. The skipper will have a cooler with drinks. Some operators offer a champagne package for an extra EUR 50-100. It’s quieter than a day trip, but the boat still has power if you want a fast run back to the marina. Sunset bookings are popular, so reserve at least a week ahead in July and August.

Speedboat Rental for Large Groups: Seating Capacity and Comfort

Speedboats in Puerto Banus seat 6 to 14 passengers, but comfort drops above 10. A 6-metre RIB fits 6 people comfortably, with bench seats around the console. An 8-metre boat seats 8-10, with a small bow seating area and a rear sunpad. A 10-metre speedboat can take 12-14, with a cuddy cabin and a proper head (toilet). For groups larger than 10, consider a catamaran instead, they have more deck space and a toilet. Speedboats have limited shade; bring a hat and sunscreen. The ride is bumpy at speed, so passengers prone to seasickness should sit in the middle. The skipper controls the throttle, so if someone is uncomfortable, they can slow down. For stag or hen groups, a 2-hour trip with a champagne stop is enough. For corporate groups, a 4-hour trip with a lunch at a beach club works better.

Speedboat Rental in Winter: What Changes

Speedboat rental in Puerto Banus is available year-round, but winter (November to March) brings challenges. Sea temperatures drop to 15-17 degrees C, so swimming is brief. The wind is stronger, often 15-25 knots, making the sea choppy. Many operators reduce their fleet or only run in the morning when the wind is lighter. Prices drop by 20-30% compared to summer. A 2-hour RIB rental in December costs around EUR 250-350. You still get the scenic coast, but the trip is more about sightseeing than swimming. The Strait of Gibraltar is rougher, so stick to the Marbella-Estepona stretch. Book with a flexible cancellation policy, as weather cancellations are common. The upside is the marinas are quiet, and you can often get a last-minute booking. Check the blog for winter boating tips.

How to Choose the Right Speedboat for Your Group

Start with group size. 6 people or fewer: a 6-metre RIB is fine. 7-10 people: an 8-metre speedboat with a wider beam. 11-14 people: a 10-metre boat with a cabin. Next, consider the purpose. For pure speed, pick a RIB with a 250+ hp outboard, it will hit 45 knots. For comfort, choose a deep-V hull with twin engines, it will be more stable at 30 knots. For a mix of speed and amenities, a centre-console speedboat with a T-top for shade works well. Check the boat’s age; newer boats (2020 or later) have better suspension seats and less engine noise. Read the listing details on search page for specs. If you’re unsure, message the operator through BoatHire24, they’ll recommend the best boat for your group.

Speedboat Rental and the Environment: Responsible Boating

Speedboats burn more fuel per mile than other boats, so your carbon footprint is higher. To reduce impact, choose a 2-hour trip instead of 4, or cruise at 20 knots instead of 40. Some operators in Puerto Banus are switching to four-stroke outboards, which are cleaner and quieter. Avoid anchoring on seagrass meadows, common near Cabopino and the Río Verde. Use reef-safe sunscreen, as chemicals damage marine life. Don’t feed the dolphins if you see them in the Strait. The skipper will know the local regulations, including no-go zones and speed limits near the coast. Responsible boating keeps the Costa del Sol beautiful for everyone. BoatHire24 encourages operators to follow the Blue Flag guidelines for sustainable tourism.

Related guides

  • What to Pack for a Boat Day on the Costa del Sol
  • Catamaran vs Motor Yacht: Which to Charter in Marbella
  • The Best Anchorages and Swimming Spots near Marbella
  • Do You Need a Licence to Rent a Boat in Spain?
  • Stag & Hen Boat Party Ideas in Marbella

faqs.q: Do I need a licence to rent a speedboat in Puerto Banus?
faqs.a: No, because every rental includes a licensed skipper. You can drive under their supervision if you have an ICC or equivalent, but it's not required.

faqs.q: How many people can fit on a speedboat?
faqs.a: Most speedboats seat 6 to 12 passengers. Check the boat's capacity on the listing. Larger groups may need two boats or a catamaran.

faqs.q: Can we swim and snorkel during the trip?
faqs.a: Yes, the skipper will stop at safe spots like Cala del Faro or Cabopino. Snorkeling gear is usually provided. Just ask in advance.

faqs.q: What happens if the weather is bad?
faqs.a: The skipper may cancel or postpone for safety. You'll get a full refund or reschedule. Contact us if you're unsure about conditions.

faqs.q: Is fuel included in the price?
faqs.a: Most rentals include fuel for the trip. Some operators charge extra for long distances. Confirm with the charter before booking.

faqs.q: Can we bring our own food and drinks?
faqs.a: Yes, you can bring a cooler with snacks and drinks. Alcohol is allowed, but the skipper must remain sober. Glass bottles are discouraged.

faqs.q: How far in advance should I book?
faqs.a: In summer, book at least a week ahead. In other seasons, 2-3 days is fine. Last-minute bookings are possible if boats are available.

title: Private Boat Charter Marbella: Customized Itineraries
metaDescription: Book a private boat charter Marbella with a licensed skipper. Custom itineraries from EUR 450. Motor yachts, catamarans, RIBs. Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina. Reserve online.
h1: Private Boat Charter Marbella: Your Own Itinerary, Your Way
intro:
You want a day on the water that isn't just a standard tour. You want to choose where you go, when you leave, and what you do. That is what a private boat charter Marbella gives you. No fixed routes, no crowds, just you and your group on a boat with a professional skipper who follows your plan.

BoatHire24 connects you with over 200 private boats available for charter in Marbella and the Costa del Sol. Every booking includes a licensed skipper, so you don't need a licence or experience. You pick the boat type, the departure port, and the itinerary. The boat is yours for the day, half-day, or evening.
bodyHtml:
What a Private Boat Charter Marbella Includes

A private charter means you have exclusive use of the boat and crew. There are no other passengers. The skipper handles navigation, safety, and mooring. You handle the drinks, the music, and the swimming stops. Most charters include fuel, insurance, and basic safety equipment. Some include a host or hostess, paddleboards, snorkelling gear, or a BBQ. Check the boat listing on BoatHire24 for exact inclusions.

You can bring your own food and drink unless the charter specifies a catering package. Many boats have a cooler, ice, and a sound system. You decide the pace. If you want to spend two hours anchored off a quiet beach, that is fine. If you want to cruise to Estepona and back, the skipper will plot the course.

Customising Your Itinerary

The main advantage of a private charter is the flexibility. You are not restricted to a set route. Popular options include a run along the coast from Marbella to Puerto Banús, a longer trip to the Strait of Gibraltar to see dolphins, or a day trip east to the cliffs of Cala del Faro. You can also combine a beach stop with a visit to a chiringuito for lunch. The skipper will advise on distances and timings based on your chosen boat's speed and the weather.

For a half-day (4 hours), you can comfortably cruise 10-15 nautical miles from the port. For a full day (8 hours), you can reach Estepona from Marbella or go as far as the Nerja caves, though that is a long day. The skipper will suggest a realistic plan. You can change it on the day if the wind picks up or you find a spot you like.

Departure Ports for Your Charter

You choose the departure port when you book. The main options are Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo), Cabopino, Estepona, Sotogrande, and Benalmádena. Puerto Banús is the most popular for luxury motor yachts and catamarans. Marbella Marina is central and close to the old town. Cabopino is quieter and closer to the dunes. Estepona gives you quick access to the western coast and the Strait. Sotogrande is a good base for a day around Gibraltar. Benalmádena is east of Marbella, near Málaga airport.

Each port has its own character. Puerto Banús is full of superyachts and designer shops. Marbella Marina has a relaxed vibe with seafood restaurants. If you want to avoid crowds, Cabopino or Estepona are better. The skipper will meet you at the port and help you board. Parking is available at all ports, though it can be expensive in Puerto Banús.

Boat Types for Private Charter

The boat you choose affects the experience. Motor yachts are fast and stable, good for covering distance. Catamarans offer more deck space and a shallow draft, ideal for anchoring close to beaches. Sailing yachts are quieter and more traditional, but slower. RIBs and speedboats are small and fast, perfect for a short, adrenaline-filled trip. Fishing boats come with fishing gear and a skipper who knows the spots. Licence-free day boats are available for those who want to drive themselves, but they are limited to 5 knots and a small area.

Superyachts are available for full-day or multi-day charters. These come with a full crew, including a chef and stewardess. Prices start from around EUR 3,000 per day and go up significantly. For most groups, a 40-50 foot motor yacht or a 35-45 foot catamaran is the best balance of space and cost. Browse all options on the boat search page.

Pricing Guidance for Private Charters

Prices vary by boat size, age, and season. In low season (October to April), a half-day private charter on a 35-foot motor yacht starts from around EUR 450. A full day is from EUR 800. Catamarans are slightly more, from EUR 600 for a half-day. RIBs and speedboats are cheaper, from EUR 300 for 2-3 hours. Superyachts are price on request.

In high season (June to September), prices increase by 20-40 percent. Book at least two weeks ahead for summer weekends. Deposits are typically 30-50 percent, with the balance due on the day. Most operators accept bank transfer or card. Fuel is usually included, but check. Some boats charge extra for fuel if you do a long trip. Tips for the skipper are not included but are customary, around 10-15 percent of the charter fee.

Best Time of Year for a Private Boat Charter Marbella

The Costa del Sol has over 300 days of sunshine a year, so you can charter a boat in any month. The best weather is from May to October, with sea temperatures above 20 degrees Celsius and air temperatures from 25 to 35 degrees. July and August are the hottest and busiest. The sea is calmest in the mornings, with a light breeze picking up in the afternoon.

Spring (March to May) is good for whale watching, as migrating whales pass through the Strait of Gibraltar. Autumn (September to November) has fewer crowds and still warm water. Winter (December to February) is cooler, around 15-18 degrees, but you can still enjoy a sunny day on a yacht with a heated cabin. The skipper will adjust the itinerary based on the conditions.

Who Is a Private Charter For

Private charters work for couples, families, groups of friends, and corporate events. Couples often book a sunset cruise with dinner. Families with children prefer a half-day with swimming stops. Groups of friends charter a catamaran for a party. Corporate clients use it for team-building or client entertainment. The boat is private, so you can talk business or relax without strangers.

If you are celebrating a birthday, anniversary, or wedding, the crew can arrange decorations, a cake, or a photographer. Some boats allow small events, but check with the operator first. The skipper will keep the boat safe and within legal limits for passenger numbers.

How to Book a Private Boat Charter Marbella

Booking through BoatHire24 is straightforward. You search by date, port, and boat type. Each listing shows photos, specifications, and the skipper's profile. You send a booking request with your preferred itinerary. The operator confirms availability and sends a contract. You pay the deposit online. On the day, you meet the skipper at the port, and they handle the rest.

You can also contact us for help choosing a boat. We know the fleet and the skippers. Tell us your group size, budget, and what you want to do, and we will recommend three options. Read our blog for tips on planning your itinerary and what to bring.

Local Colour and Highlights

The coast between Marbella and Estepona is dotted with coves, cliffs, and beaches. Cala del Faro is a popular spot for swimming and snorkelling. The water is clear and calm. Near Puerto Banús, you can see the luxury villas and the marina from the water. La Concha mountain looms behind Marbella, visible from miles out. The Río Verde estuary is a quiet spot for birdwatching.

Further west, the Strait of Gibraltar is one of the best places in Europe for dolphin and whale watching. Common dolphins, striped dolphins, and pilot whales are frequent. You might also see fin whales or orcas in summer. The skipper will know the best areas. East of Marbella, the coast leads to the Nerja caves, which you can visit from the sea. The caves are 4.5 million years old and have dramatic stalactites.

Estepona old town is worth a stop for lunch. The streets are whitewashed and full of flower pots. The marina has good restaurants. If you want a longer trip, you can cross to Gibraltar and see the Rock from the water. That requires a full day and a boat with a fast cruising speed.

Skipper and Safety

Every private charter includes a licensed skipper. They have commercial endorsements, local knowledge, and first aid training. They are responsible for the safety of the boat and passengers. They will brief you on safety procedures at the start. Life jackets are available for all passengers, including children. The boat has a VHF radio, flares, and a first aid kit.

The skipper will not take risks with weather. If the forecast is bad, they may suggest a different route or reschedule. You are not charged if the charter is cancelled due to unsafe conditions. The skipper is also a good source of local tips. They know the best beaches, the quietest coves, and the best chiringuitos for lunch.

What to Bring and Wear

Bring sunscreen, a hat, sunglasses, and a swimsuit. A light jacket is useful for evening charters, as the wind can pick up. Shoes should be non-marking, no black soles. You can bring your own snorkel gear, but many boats provide it. A waterproof bag for your phone and wallet is a good idea. The skipper will have a cooler for your drinks and food.

If you are prone to seasickness, take a tablet an hour before departure. The Costa del Sol is generally calm, but the Strait can have swell. The skipper will adjust the speed and route to keep everyone comfortable.

Ready to Book

A private boat charter Marbella gives you control over your day. No fixed routes, no strangers, just your group and the sea. Browse the boats on BoatHire24, choose your port and boat type, and send a booking request. The skipper will confirm the details and meet you at the port. It is that simple.

Choosing Between Puerto Banús and Marbella Marina for Departure

Your choice of departure port affects the first hour of your trip. From Puerto Banús, you motor past the superyacht row and the golden-sand beach of Puerto Banús itself. The harbour is a spectacle, but leaving it can take 15 minutes at idle speed. Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo) is less flashy but quicker to exit. You are in open water within 5 minutes, heading west past the Marbella Club Hotel beach or east toward Cabopino. If you want a fast start to your charter, choose Marbella Marina. If you want the full Costa del Sol glamour experience, Puerto Banús is the one. Both ports have plenty of parking, but Puerto Banús charges around EUR 30 for a day, while Marbella Marina is closer to EUR 15.

Fuel Costs and Distance Planning

Fuel is usually included in the charter price, but you need to understand the limits. A 40-foot motor yacht burns roughly 100-150 litres per hour at cruising speed (20-25 knots). At EUR 1.50 per litre, that is EUR 150-225 per hour. For a half-day covering 15 nautical miles, you might burn 60-80 litres total. The charter operator factors this into the base price. If you request a long trip to the Strait of Gibraltar, which is 35 nautical miles from Marbella, the fuel cost doubles. Some operators will add a fuel surcharge for trips beyond 20 nautical miles. Always ask before booking. A catamaran burns half the fuel of a motor yacht, so longer trips are more economical on a cat. Sailing yachts use almost no fuel if the wind cooperates, but you lose speed and predictability.

Beach and Cove Stops on the Marbella Coast

The best swimming spots are not the main beaches. Cala de las Tortugas, near Cabopino, is a small cove with clear water and few boats. The skipper can anchor 50 metres from the shore. Playa de la Vibora, east of Marbella, has a rocky seabed and good snorkelling. Playa de la Perla, near Estepona, is quiet on weekdays. For lunch, the chiringuitos at Playa de la Rada in Estepona serve fresh grilled fish. You can moor the boat and take a dinghy to shore, or the skipper can drop you at the jetty. Avoid anchoring near the mouth of the Río Guadalmina, where the water is murky. The skipper will know the best spots for the day's wind and tide.

What Happens if the Weather Turns

The Costa del Sol is generally calm, but the Levante wind can blow from the east at 20-30 knots, creating choppy seas. In summer, this happens maybe 5-10 days a month. The skipper will monitor the forecast and may suggest a protected route along the coast instead of crossing open water. If the wind is too strong, the charter can be rescheduled or cancelled with a full refund. Do not push the skipper to go out in bad weather. The boat will be uncomfortable, and you will not enjoy it. Spring and autumn have more variable conditions, with occasional thunderstorms. Winter is the calmest season for sea state, despite cooler air temperatures. Check the blog for monthly weather averages and sea temperature data.

How Many People Can You Bring

The legal passenger limit on a private charter depends on the boat's licence. Most motor yachts up to 12 metres carry 8-10 passengers plus the skipper. Catamarans of similar length can carry 10-12 passengers because they have more deck space. RIBs and speedboats are limited to 6-8 passengers. Superyachts over 24 metres can carry 12-16 passengers, but the crew size increases. The boat listing on BoatHire24 shows the maximum capacity. Do not exceed it. The port authority checks, and the skipper will refuse to sail if you try to bring extra people. For a group of 6-8, a 40-foot motor yacht is ideal. For 10-12, go for a 45-foot catamaran. For 2-4, a 30-foot motor yacht or a RIB is more cost-effective.

What to Do if You Have Never Been on a Boat Before

If you are new to boating, tell the skipper when you book. They will adjust the itinerary to keep the ride smooth. Avoid a RIB or speedboat for your first trip, as they bounce on chop. Choose a motor yacht or catamaran, which are more stable. The skipper will show you where to sit and how to move around the boat safely. Bring motion sickness tablets as a precaution. The first hour is usually the worst if you are prone to nausea. Once you are anchored and swimming, the feeling passes. Most first-timers love it. The Costa del Sol is a forgiving place to start, with calm waters and short distances between ports.

Booking Last Minute: Is It Possible

Yes, but with caveats. In high season, boats are booked weeks ahead. You might find a cancellation, but do not rely on it. In low season, you can often book 24-48 hours in advance. The boat search page shows real-time availability. If you see a boat you like, send a booking request immediately. The operator will respond within a few hours. For same-day bookings, call the number on the listing. Some operators keep a boat ready for walk-ins at the port, especially in Puerto Banús. Expect to pay a premium for last-minute bookings, as the operator loses the chance to plan fuel and crew. A half-day charter booked on the day might cost 10-15 percent more than one booked a week ahead.

faqs.q: Do I need a boat licence for a private charter in Marbella?
faqs.a: No. Every private charter includes a licensed skipper who handles the boat. You just relax and enjoy. The only exception is licence-free day boats, which you can drive yourself but are limited to 5 knots and a small area.

faqs.q: Can I choose my own itinerary on a private charter?
faqs.a: Yes. The itinerary is customisable. You tell the skipper where you want to go and for how long. They will advise on distances and timings based on the boat and weather. Popular stops include Cala del Faro, Puerto Banús, and the Strait of Gibraltar.

faqs.q: How much does a private boat charter Marbella cost?
faqs.a: Prices start from around EUR 450 for a half-day on a 35-foot motor yacht in low season. Catamarans from EUR 600. RIBs from EUR 300. Superyachts are price on request. High season is 20-40 percent more. Fuel is usually included.

faqs.q: What is the best time of year for a private charter?
faqs.a: May to October has the warmest weather and sea temperatures above 20 degrees Celsius. July and August are busiest. Spring is good for whale watching. Autumn has fewer crowds. Winter is cooler but still pleasant on a yacht with a cabin.

faqs.q: Can I bring my own food and drink?
faqs.a: Yes. Most charters allow you to bring your own food and drink. The boat has a cooler and ice. Some boats offer catering packages with BBQ or tapas. Check the listing on BoatHire24 for details.

faqs.q: What happens if the weather is bad?
faqs.a: The skipper will assess the conditions. If the forecast is unsafe, they will suggest a different route or reschedule. You are not charged if the charter is cancelled due to bad weather. Safety is the priority.

faqs.q: How many people can fit on a private charter?
faqs.a: It depends on the boat. A 35-foot motor yacht typically holds 8-10 passengers. Catamarans can hold 12-16. RIBs hold 6-8. Superyachts can hold 20 or more. The skipper will enforce the legal passenger limit for safety.

title: Yacht Hire Puerto Banus: Book Your Dream Charter
metaDescription: Book yacht hire Puerto Banus with a licensed skipper. Compare motor yachts, catamarans & RIBs. Instant online quotes for Marbella & Costa del Sol charters.
h1: Yacht Hire Puerto Banus: Your Charter Starts Here
intro:
You want a day on the water in Puerto Banus. You want to see the superyacht marina from the sea, anchor off a quiet cove, and have someone else handle the boat. That is exactly what BoatHire24 does. We are a marketplace for boat and yacht hire in Puerto Banus and all along the Costa del Sol. Every listing includes a licensed skipper, so you can relax with a drink instead of worrying about navigation.

Puerto Banus is the obvious starting point. The marina is packed with boats of every size, from compact RIBs to multi-deck superyachts. You can pick a half-day cruise to Cala del Faro, a sunset trip past the Marbella skyline, or a full-day crossing to the Strait of Gibraltar to watch the dolphins. The choice is yours, and the booking process is straightforward.
bodyHtml:
What Is Included in a Yacht Hire Puerto Banus Charter

Every charter on BoatHire24 comes with a licensed skipper as standard. That means you do not need a boating licence yourself. The skipper handles the mooring, the navigation, and any safety requirements. You just turn up with your group and enjoy the day.

Most charters include fuel for a standard itinerary, though longer trips or high-speed runs may incur a surcharge. Check the listing details for fuel policy. You will also find that the boat is cleaned before you board, and basic safety equipment is provided. Some operators offer extras such as a paddleboard, snorkel gear, or a Bluetooth speaker. If you want food and drink, you can either bring your own or request a catering package when you book.

What is not included is your own food, drinks, and any port fees for stopping at a beach club or marina. Those are usually paid locally. Also, the skipper's tip is not included, though it is customary to tip 10-15% if you are happy with the service.

Departure Ports for Your Yacht Hire Puerto Banus Trip

Puerto Banus is the most famous departure point on the Costa del Sol, but it is not the only one. Depending on where you are staying and what you want to see, you can also start from Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo), Cabopino, Estepona, Sotogrande, or Benalmádena.

Puerto Banus itself is ideal if you want to cruise past the superyachts and head straight for the open sea. The marina is well-served by taxis and has parking nearby. If you are staying in Marbella centre, the Marbella Marina is a shorter drive and gives you quick access to the coast east towards Cabopino and the dunes. Estepona is better for trips west towards Gibraltar and the dolphin-watching zones. Sotogrande is a quieter option, and Benalmádena works well if you are based near Fuengirola or Torremolinos.

You can search all available boats by departure port on the BoatHire24 site. Just select your preferred location and dates, and the system will show you what is available.

Boat Types Available for Yacht Hire Puerto Banus

The range of boats in Puerto Banus is wide. You can choose from motor yachts, catamarans, sailing yachts, RIBs and speedboats, fishing boats, licence-free day boats, and superyachts. Each type suits a different kind of day out.

Motor yachts are the most popular for luxury charters. They offer speed, space, and comfort. A typical 12-metre motor yacht can take 8-10 guests and cruise at around 20 knots. Prices for a half-day start from around EUR 800. For a bigger group, a 20-metre motor yacht with a flybridge and sun pads costs from EUR 2,500 for a full day.

Catamarans are stable and spacious, making them a good choice if anyone in your group is prone to seasickness. They have a wide beam, so you get more deck space. A 40-foot catamaran can carry 12 guests and costs from about EUR 1,500 for a half-day. Sailing yachts are quieter and more traditional. They are slower than motor yachts, but the experience is more relaxing. A 40-foot sailing yacht with a skipper costs from EUR 600 for a half-day.

RIBs and speedboats are for thrill-seekers. They are fast, nimble, and great for watersports. A 6-metre RIB with a 200hp engine can hit 40 knots and costs from EUR 350 for a two-hour blast. Fishing boats are equipped with rods and tackle, and the skipper knows the best spots. A half-day fishing charter costs from EUR 500. Licence-free day boats are small powerboats you can drive yourself without a licence, but they are limited to inshore waters. They cost from EUR 200 for a half-day. Superyachts are the top end, with prices on request and a full crew including stewardesses.

Pricing Guidance for Yacht Hire Puerto Banus

Prices vary by boat size, age, season, and duration. As a rough guide, here are typical price ranges for Puerto Banus charters in 2025.

Half-day (4 hours) on a motor yacht up to 10 metres: from EUR 600. Full day (8 hours) on the same boat: from EUR 1,100. A 15-metre motor yacht for a full day: from EUR 2,200. A catamaran for a full day: from EUR 1,800. A RIB for a half-day: from EUR 400. A superyacht over 30 metres: price on request, typically EUR 8,000 upwards for a day.

High season (June to September) sees prices increase by 20-30%. Low season (November to February) is cheaper, and you may find last-minute deals. Always check the listing for exact pricing and any extras like fuel, food, or port fees.

Best Seasons for Yacht Hire Puerto Banus

The Costa del Sol has a Mediterranean climate with mild winters and hot summers. The best months for a boat charter are May through October. In May, temperatures are around 22 degrees C, and the sea is calm. July and August can hit 30 degrees C, with strong sunshine and occasional afternoon breezes. September is still warm, around 26 degrees C, and the crowds thin out.

Winter charters are possible if you want a quiet trip. The sea can be choppier, and temperatures drop to 15 degrees C in January. But you will have the coast almost to yourself, and prices are lower. The Strait of Gibraltar is a year-round destination for dolphin watching, as the resident pod is always there.

How to Book Yacht Hire Puerto Banus on BoatHire24

Booking is done entirely online. Go to the BoatHire24 homepage and enter Puerto Banus as the departure port, along with your date and group size. The search results will display available boats with photos, specifications, and prices. You can filter by boat type, capacity, and price range.

Once you find a boat you like, click through to the listing page. There you will see the full itinerary options, cancellation policy, and what is included. You can send a booking request, and the owner or charter company will confirm availability. Payment is handled via secure online transfer. You will receive a confirmation email with the skipper's contact details and meeting point instructions.

If you are unsure which boat to choose, read the BoatHire24 blog for guides on picking the right charter for your group.

Who Is Yacht Hire Puerto Banus For

This is for anyone who wants a day on the sea without the hassle of owning a boat. Families with children will find catamarans and motor yachts with cabins comfortable. Groups of friends can hire a RIB for a high-speed adventure or a sailing yacht for a lazy afternoon. Couples looking for a romantic sunset cruise can book a smaller motor yacht with a dinner package. Corporate groups use the larger yachts for client entertainment or team-building days.

If you cannot swim or are nervous about the sea, that is fine. The skipper will keep the boat steady, and life jackets are provided. You do not need any boating experience at all.

Typical Itineraries from Puerto Banus

A standard half-day trip from Puerto Banus goes east to Cala del Faro, a small cove with clear water. The skipper will anchor, and you can swim or use the paddleboard. The return trip takes you past the Marbella coastline and back into the marina.

A full-day trip can go west to Estepona, where you can stop for lunch at a beach club or explore the old town. Alternatively, head south-east towards the Strait of Gibraltar. The crossing takes about 90 minutes from Puerto Banus, and you will almost certainly see dolphins. You can anchor off Tarifa for lunch before returning.

For a longer charter, some boats offer trips to Nerja, where you can visit the famous caves from the sea. That requires a full day and a faster boat, but it is a memorable experience.

Local Attractions Near Puerto Banus

Puerto Banus itself is worth exploring. The marina is full of luxury boutiques, restaurants, and bars. You can walk from the boat to the main square in two minutes. Behind the marina, the La Concha mountain provides a dramatic backdrop. The Rio Verde runs into the sea nearby, and you can see the river mouth from the boat.

If you have time before or after the charter, visit the old town of Marbella, a short taxi ride away. It has narrow streets, whitewashed buildings, and a 16th-century church. Estepona old town is also charming, with its flower-filled balconies and traditional tapas bars.

Why Use BoatHire24 for Yacht Hire Puerto Banus

BoatHire24 aggregates listings from multiple charter companies in one place. You can compare prices, boat types, and availability without contacting each company separately. The platform is free to use, and you pay the same price as booking direct. We do not add a markup. The booking process is transparent, with no hidden fees.

We also provide detailed information on each boat, including photos, specifications, and reviews from previous guests. You can see exactly what you are getting before you commit. If you have questions, you can message the owner through the platform.

How Far Can You Go on a Yacht Hire Puerto Banus Charter

Distance depends on the boat's speed and how long you have. From Puerto Banus, a motor yacht cruising at 20 knots can cover about 40 nautical miles in a two-hour run. That puts Gibraltar within reach for a full-day trip. A slower sailing yacht at 6 knots covers roughly 12 nautical miles in two hours, so you are limited to the coast between Estepona and Cabopino. The skipper will plan the route based on your preferences and the weather. If you want to go further, like to the Nerja caves (about 35 nautical miles east), you need a fast boat and a full day. Expect to pay more for fuel on longer trips.

Fuel Costs and Surcharges on Yacht Hire Puerto Banus

Fuel is usually included for a standard itinerary of 4-6 hours within 15 nautical miles of the port. If you push further, the skipper may charge a fuel surcharge. A typical motor yacht burns 60-100 litres per hour at cruising speed. At around EUR 1.50 per litre of marine diesel, that adds up. For a RIB with a 200hp outboard, consumption is higher, around 30-50 litres per hour at high speed. Always ask the owner about the fuel policy before booking. Some listings specify "fuel included for 4 hours" or "fuel extra at cost." If you are on a tight budget, stick to the standard itinerary to avoid surprises.

What to Wear and Bring on a Yacht Hire Puerto Banus Trip

Bring swimwear, a towel, sunscreen (SPF 50, as the sun reflects off the water), and a light jacket or windbreaker. Even in summer, the sea breeze can feel cool at 20 knots. A hat and sunglasses are essential. Non-slip shoes are a good idea, though many boats ask you to remove shoes on deck. If you are prone to seasickness, take a motion sickness tablet an hour before departure. The skipper will have basic first aid, but bring any personal medication. For a full-day trip, pack a change of clothes for the evening. Most boats have a small fridge, so you can bring your own drinks and snacks. Glass bottles are often banned on boats, so use plastic or cans.

How Many People Can You Take on a Yacht Hire Puerto Banus Charter

Capacity is regulated by the boat's licence, not just how many seats there are. A 10-metre motor yacht typically carries 8-10 passengers plus the skipper. A 15-metre catamaran can take 12-14 guests. RIBs are smaller, usually 6-8 passengers. Superyachts over 30 metres can carry 20-30 guests but require a full crew. The BoatHire24 listing shows the maximum number of passengers. Do not exceed this, as the skipper will refuse to sail. If you have a large group, consider hiring two boats or a larger catamaran. For a wedding party or corporate event, contact the owner directly to arrange multiple boats.

Can You Anchor and Swim on a Yacht Hire Puerto Banus Charter

Yes, that is the main point of a charter. The skipper will anchor in a calm cove like Cala del Faro, Cala de la Maestra, or off the coast of Estepona. The water is clear and usually calm in the morning. Most boats have a swim ladder and a freshwater shower on the back deck. Some provide a paddleboard or snorkel gear. If you want to use a seabob or jet ski, check with the owner first, as not all boats allow them. The skipper will advise on safe swimming areas and watch for other boats. Do not swim near the marina entrance or in busy shipping lanes.

What Happens if the Weather Is Bad on Your Yacht Hire Puerto Banus Date

The skipper has the final say on whether it is safe to sail. If the wind is over 25 knots or there is a storm warning, the charter will be cancelled or postponed. In that case, you can reschedule for another day or get a full refund. Many operators have a weather policy in their terms. If the weather is marginal, the skipper may suggest a shorter trip or a different route. Check the forecast before your booking and stay in touch with the owner. BoatHire24 recommends booking in the morning during summer, as afternoon winds often pick up. If you are flexible, choose a date with a calm forecast.

How to Get to Puerto Banus for Your Yacht Hire Charter

Puerto Banus is about 10 minutes by taxi from Marbella centre, 45 minutes from Malaga Airport, and 90 minutes from Gibraltar Airport. Parking is available at the Puerto Banus underground car park, but it costs around EUR 30 for a day. Alternatively, take a bus from Marbella to the Puerto Banus stop. If you are staying in a nearby hotel, ask if they offer a shuttle. The meeting point is usually at the charter company's office in the marina. Arrive 15 minutes early to check in. The skipper will meet you there and walk you to the boat. If you are late, the charter time still counts from the booked start time.

  • Starlite Festival Boat Trips: Combine Music & Sea

faqs.q: Do I need a boating licence to hire a yacht in Puerto Banus?
faqs.a: No. Every charter on BoatHire24 includes a licensed skipper, so you can enjoy the day without a licence. The skipper handles all navigation and safety.

faqs.q: How much does yacht hire in Puerto Banus cost?
faqs.a: Prices vary by boat size and season. A half-day on a motor yacht starts from around EUR 600. A full day on a catamaran starts from EUR 1,800. Superyachts are price on request.

faqs.q: Can I bring my own food and drink?
faqs.a: Yes, most charters allow you to bring your own food and drink. Some operators offer catering packages if you prefer not to carry supplies.

faqs.q: What is the best time of year for a boat charter in Puerto Banus?
faqs.a: May to October is the best period, with warm weather and calm seas. July and August are the busiest. Winter charters are cheaper but cooler and choppier.

faqs.a: It depends on the boat. A typical motor yacht holds 8-12 guests. Catamarans can take up to 12. RIBs are smaller, usually 4-8. Check the listing for maximum capacity.

faqs.q: Can I cancel my booking?
faqs.a: Cancellation policies vary by operator. Most offer a full refund if you cancel at least 48 hours in advance. Check the specific policy on the listing page before booking.
