faqs.q: How much does a luxury yacht rental Puerto Banus cost?
faqs.a: Prices start from around EUR 2,500 for a half-day on a 40-foot motor yacht in low season. Full-day rates on larger vessels can reach EUR 6,000 or more. Superyachts are price on request.

faqs.q: Do I need a boating license to rent a luxury yacht in Puerto Banus?
faqs.a: No. Every charter includes a licensed skipper who handles navigation and docking. You can relax or take the helm under supervision if you want.

faqs.q: Can I bring my own food and drinks on a luxury yacht rental?
faqs.a: Yes, most charters allow you to bring your own catering. Some offer onboard catering at an extra cost. Check the listing details before booking.

faqs.q: What is the maximum number of guests for a luxury yacht rental Puerto Banus?
faqs.a: It depends on the vessel and its safety certificate. Motor yachts typically carry 6 to 12 guests. Catamarans can handle up to 20. Superyachts may host 12 to 30.

faqs.q: Is fuel included in the price?
faqs.a: Some charters include fuel for a standard itinerary, while others charge actual consumption. Always confirm the fuel policy in the booking details.

faqs.q: Can I charter a luxury yacht for just a few hours?
faqs.a: Yes, half-day charters (4 hours) are common. Full-day (8 hours) and multi-day charters are also available. Minimum booking time is usually 4 hours.

faqs.q: What happens if the weather is bad on my charter date?
faqs.a: The skipper has the final say on safety. If conditions are unsafe, the charter may be rescheduled or cancelled with a full refund. Check the cancellation policy before booking.

title: Speedboat Hire Estepona: Fast Rentals
metaDescription: Looking for speedboat hire Estepona? Compare fast RIBs and speedboats with skipper. Depart from Estepona port. Book online with BoatHire24.
h1: Speedboat Hire Estepona: Fast Rentals with Skipper
intro:
You want to blast across the Mediterranean, wind in your hair, sun on your back, and Estepona’s coastline sliding past at 30 knots. Speedboat hire Estepona is the quickest way to get that rush, and BoatHire24 has the boats and the skippers to make it happen. No licence needed, no hidden costs, just a fast RIB or speedboat ready to go from Estepona port.

Estepona’s a smart choice for a speedboat trip. The marina is compact, the old town is ten minutes walk away, and you’re right on the edge of the Strait of Gibraltar. That means you can be dolphin-watching off the coast of Gibraltar in under 20 minutes, or bombing along the Costa del Sol towards Marbella. With a licensed skipper included, you just turn up and enjoy the ride.
bodyHtml:
What You Get with Speedboat Hire Estepona

Every speedboat hire Estepona booking through BoatHire24 includes a professional skipper. That’s not optional. It’s the law in Spain for rental boats over a certain power, and it’s a good thing too. You don’t need a boating licence, you don’t need to know the local navigation rules, and you don’t have to worry about fuel or mooring. The skipper handles everything. You just sit back, or stand up and hold on, as the boat accelerates.

The boats are RIBs (rigid inflatable boats) and purpose-built speedboats. Typical sizes range from 6 metres to 9 metres, with outboard or inboard engines that push 200 to 400 horsepower. Speeds of 30 to 45 knots are normal, depending on sea conditions and the specific boat. Most have a T-top or Bimini shade, a stereo system, and a small cooler for drinks. Some have a sunpad at the bow or a small cabin for storing bags.

What you don’t get is a party deck or a full galley. These are fast, functional boats designed for day trips and adrenaline. If you want a lounging platform with a barbecue, you’re looking at a catamaran. But for pure speed, a RIB is hard to beat.

Estepona Port: Your Starting Point

Estepona Marina (Puerto Deportivo de Estepona) is a modern, sheltered harbour with around 500 berths. It’s located just west of Marbella, about 15 minutes by car from Puerto Banús. The marina has a fuel dock, restaurants, and a small beach. For speedboat hire Estepona, you’ll meet the skipper at the port office or the boat’s berth. Parking is available nearby, but it can fill up in summer, so arrive early.

The departure point matters because it dictates your first few minutes of the trip. From Estepona, you head south-west towards the Strait of Gibraltar, or east towards Marbella and Cabopino. The water is usually calm in the morning, with a light sea breeze picking up after noon. Summer water temperatures hit 22 to 26 degrees C, so you can swim comfortably from May to October.

Boat Options for Speedboat Hire Estepona

We list several types of fast boats available for speedboat hire Estepona. Here’s a breakdown of what you can expect.

RIBs (Rigid Inflatable Boats)

RIBs are the most common choice for speedboat hire Estepona. They’re light, stable, and fast. The hull is fibreglass, the tubes are heavy-duty PVC or Hypalon. A 6.5-metre RIB with a 200 hp outboard will do around 35 knots and seat up to 8 people. For a smaller group of 4, a 5-metre RIB with a 150 hp engine is plenty. RIBs are also the best option if you want to do watersports like towable bananas or wakeboarding, because the engine can handle the pull.

Speedboats

These are more traditional fibreglass runabouts, often with a deeper V-hull for cutting through chop. A 7-metre speedboat with a 300 hp inboard engine might top out at 40 knots. They tend to have more comfortable seating and a smoother ride than a RIB in rougher water. Some models have a small cabin with a toilet, which can be useful for longer trips.

Fishing Boats (Fast)

If you want to combine speed with fishing, some of the local fishing boats are converted for charter. These are usually 7 to 8 metres, with outboard engines, and they’re set up with rod holders and a live bait well. They’re not as fast as a pure RIB, but they’ll still do 25 to 30 knots. You can hire one for a half-day fishing trip, then ask the skipper to open it up on the way back.

Pricing Guidance for Speedboat Hire Estepona

Prices vary by boat size, engine power, season, and trip duration. Here’s a rough guide based on typical listings on BoatHire24. These are estimates only; check the actual listing for exact prices.

A half-day (4 hours) hire of a 6-metre RIB starts from around EUR 350. A full day (8 hours) with a 7.5-metre speedboat costs from EUR 600 to EUR 900. For a top-end 9-metre RIB with twin engines, expect from EUR 1,200 per day. Fuel is usually included in the price, but confirm with the skipper. Some operators charge extra for fuel if you do a long run to Gibraltar or Marbella.

Peak season (July and August) prices are 20 to 30 percent higher. Book early in summer. Off-peak (April, May, June, September, October) you can often negotiate a discount, especially for weekday bookings. BoatHire24 shows live availability and prices, so you can compare options.

Best Seasons for Speedboat Hire Estepona

The Costa del Sol has a Mediterranean climate with mild winters and hot summers. For speedboat hire Estepona, the best months are May through October. June and September are ideal: warm air (25 to 30 degrees C), warm sea (20 to 24 degrees C), and fewer crowds than July and August. July and August are hot (30 to 35 degrees C) and busy, but the sea is at its warmest (24 to 26 degrees C).

April and November are marginal. You might get a perfect day, but the water is cooler (16 to 18 degrees C) and the wind can pick up. December to March is generally too cold for comfortable speedboat trips, though some hardy locals go out on sunny days. If you’re visiting in winter, consider a larger yacht with a heated cabin.

Routes and Destinations from Estepona

With speedboat hire Estepona, you have several options for where to go.

Dolphin Watching in the Strait of Gibraltar

The Strait of Gibraltar is a migration corridor for dolphins and whales. From Estepona, it’s about 10 nautical miles to the area where common dolphins, striped dolphins, and occasionally pilot whales are seen. The trip takes 20 to 30 minutes at speed. Skippers know the hotspots. You can spend an hour watching the dolphins, then head back. Total trip: 2 to 3 hours.

Coastal Cruise to Marbella and Puerto Banús

Heading east from Estepona, you pass the beaches of San Pedro Alcántara, then Puerto Banús and Marbella. The distance is about 12 nautical miles, so a 20-minute blast at 35 knots. You can stop for a swim at Cala del Faro, a small cove near Marbella, or have lunch at a beach club in Puerto Banús. The skipper will drop you at the marina and pick you up later. This works well for a full-day trip.

Gibraltar and the Rock

Gibraltar is about 15 nautical miles south of Estepona. You can see the Rock from the port. A speedboat can make the crossing in 25 to 30 minutes, but you need to check with the skipper about customs and immigration. Some skippers are licensed to take passengers to Gibraltar; others are not. If you want to go, book a boat that specifically offers Gibraltar trips.

Estepona Coastline and Beaches

You don’t have to go far. The coast between Estepona and the Guadalmansa River has several small coves and beaches accessible only by boat. Playa del Cristo is a popular spot for anchoring and swimming. The skipper can show you the best spots for snorkelling, with rocky areas that hold sea bream and octopus.

How to Book Speedboat Hire Estepona on BoatHire24

Booking is straightforward. Go to the BoatHire24 homepage, select Estepona as the departure port, and filter by boat type “Speedboat / RIB”. You’ll see a list of available boats with photos, specifications, and prices. Each listing shows the maximum number of passengers, the length of the boat, the engine power, and what’s included (skipper, fuel, insurance, sometimes snorkel gear).

Select a boat, choose your date and time, and complete the booking. Payment is secure online. You’ll receive a confirmation with the skipper’s contact details and meeting instructions. Cancel up to 48 hours before departure for a full refund. For last-minute bookings, search our live availability to find boats that are free today or tomorrow.

If you’re unsure which boat suits your group, read our blog posts about speedboat trips on the Costa del Sol. We cover tips for first-timers, what to bring, and the best routes for different interests.

Who Is Speedboat Hire Estepona For

Speedboat hire Estepona is for anyone who wants a fast, fun day on the water without the hassle of driving a boat. It works for couples, small groups of friends, and families with older children (skippers usually set a minimum age of 4 to 6 years for safety). It’s not ideal for large groups of 10 or more, because speedboats have limited space. For bigger parties, look at catamarans or motor yachts.

It’s also not for people who get seasick easily. At 30 knots, the ride can be bumpy in choppy water. If you’re prone to motion sickness, take medication an hour before departure, or choose a larger displacement boat that moves more slowly.

What to Bring and What to Expect

Bring swimwear, a towel, sunscreen (factor 50, you will burn), a hat, and sunglasses. A windbreaker or light jacket is useful, because the wind chill at speed can be significant, even on a hot day. The skipper provides life jackets for everyone, but you can bring your own if you prefer. No glass bottles on board, as broken glass is a hazard. Use plastic cups or cans.

Expect to get wet. The spray from the bow will soak the front seats, and if you stop for a swim, you’ll be in the water. Leave your phone and valuables in a waterproof bag or with the skipper in a dry locker.

The skipper will do a safety briefing before departure. Listen carefully. They’ll show you where the life jackets are, how to use the radio in an emergency, and what to do if someone falls overboard (it’s rare, but it happens). Then they’ll fire up the engine, cast off, and you’re away.

Why Book Through BoatHire24

BoatHire24 is a marketplace that connects you with local boat owners and skippers. We vet each listing for safety and legality. All skippers have the required Spanish licences and insurance. You book directly through the platform, so you have payment protection and a clear cancellation policy. No cash handovers, no uncertainty.

We cover all the main ports on the Costa del Sol: Puerto Banús, Marbella, Cabopino, Estepona, Sotogrande, and Benalmádena. If you’re staying in a different area, search by your preferred port to find the closest speedboat hire option. For more detailed information on the local area and boat types, check our blog.

Speedboat Hire Estepona for Watersports

If you want more than just a fast ride, some speedboats available for speedboat hire Estepona are set up for watersports. RIBs with a towing eye on the transom can pull a wakeboard, a towable banana, or a donut. The skipper will handle the driving and the rope. You just hold on. Typical extras cost from EUR 50 to EUR 100 per hour for the equipment hire. Wakeboarding requires a bit of practice, but a banana ride is easy for everyone. Ask the skipper when you book whether they have the gear.

Fuel Costs and What They Mean for Your Trip

Fuel is the biggest variable in speedboat hire Estepona pricing. A 6-metre RIB with a 200 hp outboard burns roughly 30 to 40 litres per hour at cruising speed. At current fuel prices in Spain (around EUR 1.50 per litre for petrol), that’s EUR 45 to EUR 60 per hour. For a 4-hour trip, fuel alone costs EUR 180 to EUR 240. Most listings on BoatHire24 include fuel in the total price, but a few operators charge extra based on hours used or distance covered. Always check the listing details. If fuel is not included, ask for an estimate based on your planned route.

Speedboat Hire from Nearby Ports

If Estepona is not your base, you can find speedboat hire at other Costa del Sol ports. Puerto Banús, 15 minutes east, has a wide selection of RIBs and speedboats, often with larger engines and higher prices. Marbella Marina is a quieter option with good access to the same coastal routes. Cabopino, near Calahonda, is smaller but closer to the Cabopino dunes and the Cala del Faro cove. Sotogrande, further west, offers a more exclusive setting with less traffic. Benalmádena, near Fuengirola, has a large marina and is a good choice if you are staying on the western Costa del Sol. Each port has its own character, but the boats and routes overlap.

Group Size and Boat Capacity

Speedboat hire Estepona is best for small groups. A typical 6-metre RIB seats up to 6 passengers plus the skipper. An 8-metre speedboat might take 8 or 9 passengers. The legal maximum is set by the boat’s licence plate, and the skipper will not exceed it. For a group of 10 or more, you need a catamaran or a motor yacht. If you have a group of 4, you can hire a smaller, cheaper boat and have more room. Check the passenger capacity on each listing before booking.

Weather and Sea Conditions for Speedboat Trips

The Costa del Sol is generally calm, but the sea can get choppy. The Levante wind, blowing from the east, can create short, steep waves that make a speedboat ride uncomfortable. The Poniente wind from the west is lighter and brings clearer water. Skippers monitor the forecast and will cancel or reschedule if conditions are unsafe. You get a full refund if the skipper cancels. For the smoothest ride, book a morning departure when the wind is usually lighter. Afternoon trips in July and August can be bumpier due to thermal winds building up.

Related guides

  • Sunset Champagne Boat Marbella: Romantic Ideas
  • Marbella Boat Weather Guide: Best Conditions
  • Snorkeling Tours Marbella: Guided Adventures
  • Marbella Boat Photography: Capture the Coast

faqs.q: How many people can fit on a speedboat in Estepona?
faqs.a: Most speedboats and RIBs seat 6 to 8 passengers. Some larger models can take up to 10. Check the specific listing for maximum capacity. The skipper counts as crew, not a passenger.

faqs.a: No. Spanish law requires a professional skipper on board for rental boats over a certain power. The skipper is included in the price. You are not allowed to take the helm.

title: Private Yacht Rental Marbella: Exclusive Deals
metaDescription: Book a private yacht rental Marbella with a licensed skipper. Compare motor yachts, catamarans & sailing yachts from Puerto Banús. Deals from EUR 1,200. Reserve now.
h1: Private Yacht Rental Marbella: Exclusive Deals with Skipper
intro:
You want a private yacht rental in Marbella, not a crowded ferry or a generic booze cruise. You want your own vessel, your own itinerary, and a licensed skipper who knows the best coves between Puerto Banús and Estepona. That is exactly what BoatHire24 offers. We are a marketplace for private yacht charters on the Costa del Sol, and every single booking includes a professional skipper, fuel, and insurance. No hidden fees, no surprises, just you, your group, and the Mediterranean.

The primary keyword here is private yacht rental Marbella, and the answer is simple: you can book a private yacht from around EUR 1,200 for a half-day trip, depending on the boat type and season. Prices vary, but you always get a dedicated crew, a clean vessel, and the freedom to explore the coast on your terms. Whether you want to party off Puerto Banús, swim at Cala del Faro, or spot dolphins near Gibraltar, a private charter is the way to go.
bodyHtml:
What a Private Yacht Rental Marbella Includes

When you book a private yacht rental Marbella through BoatHire24, you are not just renting a boat. You are hiring a complete experience. Every charter comes with a licensed skipper who holds the relevant Spanish qualifications (Patrón de Yate or similar). The skipper handles navigation, docking, and safety, so you can relax and enjoy the day. Fuel is included in the price, as is basic insurance (third-party liability and damage cover). Most boats also include a hostess or stewardess for larger groups, but that is optional and comes at an extra cost.

You also get a clean, well-maintained vessel with a full tank of water, life jackets, a first aid kit, and a VHF radio. If you book a catamaran or a motor yacht, you typically get a sound system (Bluetooth or AUX), a fridge, and a sun deck with cushions. Snorkelling gear, stand-up paddleboards, and inflatable water toys are often available for a small supplement. Check the boat listing for exact inclusions.

Ports for Your Private Yacht Charter

Your private yacht rental Marbella departs from one of six ports along the Costa del Sol. The most popular is Puerto Banús, the glitzy marina in Nueva Andalucía, known for its superyachts, designer boutiques, and celebrity spotting. From here, you can cruise west to the dunes of Cabopino or east to Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo) and the old town. Other departure ports include Estepona (with its charming old town and flower-filled streets), Sotogrande (an exclusive residential marina near Gibraltar), Benalmádena (close to Málaga airport), and Cabopino (a quieter, more natural harbour).

Each port offers a different starting point for your itinerary. If you want to see dolphins in the Strait of Gibraltar, depart from Sotogrande or Estepona. If you prefer the Marbella coastline with views of La Concha mountain, start from Puerto Banús or Marbella Marina. For a day trip to the Nerja caves (by sea, you can anchor near the cliffs), Benalmádena is a good option. The skipper will advise on the best route based on weather and sea conditions.

Boat Types for Private Yacht Rental Marbella

The term private yacht rental Marbella covers a range of vessels. Here is what you can choose from:

  • Motor yachts (from 40 to 80 feet): Fast, luxurious, with cabins and a flybridge. Ideal for day trips or overnight charters. Prices from around EUR 1,500 for half a day.
  • Catamarans (from 38 to 55 feet): Stable, spacious, with a shallow draft for anchoring close to beaches. Great for families and groups who want space. Prices from EUR 1,200 for half a day.
  • Sailing yachts (from 35 to 55 feet): Classic, quiet, and eco-friendly. You can help the skipper with the sails if you want. Prices from EUR 900 for half a day.
  • RIBs and speedboats (from 20 to 35 feet): Open, fast, and fun. Perfect for a short blast to Cala del Faro or a fishing trip. Prices from EUR 600 for half a day.
  • Licence-free day boats (up to 6 metres): No boating licence required, but still with a skipper. Ideal for beginners. Prices from EUR 400 for half a day.
  • Superyachts (over 80 feet): Ultra-luxury with a full crew. Price on request.

Pricing Guidance for Private Yacht Rental Marbella

Prices for a private yacht rental Marbella depend on boat size, type, season, and duration. As a rough guide, expect to pay from EUR 1,200 to EUR 3,000 for a half-day (4 hours) on a motor yacht or catamaran. Full-day (8 hours) rates range from EUR 2,000 to EUR 6,000. Superyachts and overnight charters are price on request. The high season (June to September) commands premium rates, while spring and autumn offer better value. Winter charters are possible but less popular due to cooler weather (average 15-18 degrees C in January).

All prices include the skipper, fuel, insurance, and basic amenities. Extras like food, drinks, water toys, and port mooring fees (if you stop at a marina) are not included. You can bring your own catering or order from a local caterer; the crew will serve it. Tipping the crew is customary but not mandatory, typically 10-15% of the charter fee.

Best Seasons for a Private Yacht Charter

The best time for a private yacht rental Marbella is from May to October. In May and June, the weather is warm (25-30 degrees C) and the sea is calm, with fewer tourists. July and August are peak season: hot (30-35 degrees C), busy, and expensive. September and October are still pleasant (25-28 degrees C) and quieter. November to April is the low season, with cooler temperatures and occasional rain, but you can still sail if you dress warmly and check the forecast. The Strait of Gibraltar has strong winds (Levante and Poniente) that can affect sailing yachts more than motor yachts.

If you want to see dolphins, the best months are April to October, when the sea is warmer and the dolphins feed near the coast. Whale watching (pilot whales and orcas) is possible from July to September off Tarifa, but that is a longer trip from Marbella (about 2 hours by motor yacht).

How to Book a Private Yacht Rental Marbella

Booking a private yacht rental Marbella through BoatHire24 is straightforward. Visit our search page to filter by boat type, port, date, and group size. Each listing shows the boat's specifications, photos, and a price range. Once you find a boat you like, click 'Book Now' to send an enquiry. We will confirm availability within 24 hours and send you a secure payment link. A deposit of 50% is required to secure the booking, with the balance due 7 days before departure. Cancellation policies vary by boat owner, but most offer a full refund if you cancel more than 14 days in advance.

You can also call or email us for a personalised quote. We speak English, Spanish, and German. For groups larger than 12 passengers, we can arrange multiple boats or a superyacht. Children are welcome on most boats, but life jackets for kids must be requested in advance. Pets are allowed on some boats, but check the listing first.

Who Should Book a Private Yacht Rental Marbella

Private yacht rental Marbella is for anyone who values privacy, flexibility, and quality. Couples celebrating an anniversary or honeymoon often book a sailing yacht or a small motor yacht for a romantic sunset cruise. Families with children prefer catamarans for their stability and space. Corporate groups use charters for team-building, client entertainment, or product launches. Groups of friends rent motor yachts for parties with music and swimming. Fishing enthusiasts can book a dedicated fishing boat with a skipper who knows the best spots for sea bass, bream, and tuna.

If you are a solo traveller, you can join a shared charter (not private) for a lower price, but that is not covered under private yacht rental. For private, you need a minimum of 2 passengers, but most boats are designed for 6 to 12 guests. Larger groups up to 150 can charter a superyacht or a large catamaran.

Local Attractions and Activities During Your Charter

A private yacht rental Marbella allows you to visit places inaccessible by land. Anchor off Cala del Faro near Estepona, a rocky cove with clear water and a small lighthouse. Swim in the Río Verde estuary (the river meets the sea near Marbella), where you can see birds and sometimes turtles. Cruise past the Strait of Gibraltar for dolphin sightings; the skipper will slow down so you can take photos. For a longer trip, head to Nerja and anchor near the cliffs below the Balcón de Europa; the caves are a short walk from the beach.

On board, you can sunbathe, eat, drink, and listen to music. Many charters include a stop at a beachside chiringuito (beach bar) for lunch. The skipper will recommend a spot based on your preferences. Popular chiringuitos include El Ancla in Marbella, La Terraza in Estepona, and Chiringuito Pepe in Cabopino. You can also bring your own food and have a picnic on the boat. Some boats have a grill for barbecuing.

Safety and Regulations for Private Yacht Charters

All private yacht rentals Marbella through BoatHire24 comply with Spanish maritime regulations. The skipper holds a valid licence and the boat has the required safety equipment (life jackets, flares, fire extinguisher, first aid kit). The skipper is responsible for your safety and will give a safety briefing at the start of the charter. You must follow their instructions, especially when anchoring or in rough seas. Alcohol is allowed on board, but the skipper will not tolerate excessive drinking that endangers the crew or vessel. If you plan to swim, the skipper will anchor in a safe area and monitor the tide and currents.

Insurance covers damage to the boat and third-party liability. Personal accident insurance is not included, so we recommend you have your own travel insurance. In case of bad weather, the skipper may cancel or alter the itinerary; you will receive a full refund or a rescheduled date.

Why Book Through BoatHire24

BoatHire24 is a marketplace that connects you directly with boat owners and charter companies on the Costa del Sol. We do not own the boats; we list them and handle the booking process. This means you get a wide selection of vessels, from budget-friendly RIBs to luxury superyachts. Our team vets every boat for safety and quality. We also provide a secure payment system and 24/7 customer support during your charter. You can read our blog for tips on choosing the right boat, planning your itinerary, and understanding local regulations.

We are based in Marbella, so we know the local conditions, ports, and crew. When you book through us, you get a local perspective, not a generic call centre. For more information, visit our BoatHire24 homepage or contact us directly. We are happy to help you find the perfect private yacht rental Marbella for your trip.

Choosing the Right Port for Your Private Yacht Rental Marbella

The port you choose for your private yacht rental Marbella shapes the entire day. If you want the full celebrity treatment, start from Puerto Banús. You will see the superyachts of the rich and famous, and the marina itself is a spectacle with its luxury car showrooms and designer shops. From here, you can cruise east to Marbella Marina in 20 minutes or west to Cabopino in 30 minutes. For a quieter departure, Cabopino is a small, natural harbour with a sandy beach and a few chiringuitos. It is ideal if you want to avoid the crowds and head straight to the dunes of Dunas de Artola. Estepona offers a more laid-back vibe, with its old town just a 10-minute walk from the marina. You can combine a morning exploring the flower-covered streets with an afternoon on the water. Sotogrande is the best choice if you plan to cruise towards Gibraltar and the Strait; it is the closest port to the dolphin hotspots. Benalmádena is convenient for those flying into Málaga airport (15 minutes by taxi) and offers easy access to the eastern Costa del Sol, including Fuengirola and the Nerja cliffs. Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo) is right in the centre of Marbella, close to the old town and the Golden Mile. It is a good base for a short half-day charter focusing on the Marbella coastline.

What to Expect on the Water: Weather and Sea Conditions

The Mediterranean off Marbella is generally calm, but conditions vary by season and location. In summer (June to September), the sea is flat and the wind is light, especially in the morning. The water temperature reaches 24-26 degrees C, perfect for swimming. The prevailing wind is the Levante (easterly), which can pick up in the afternoon, creating a slight chop, but rarely dangerous for motor yachts and catamarans. The Poniente (westerly) is less common but brings clearer skies and cooler air. In spring and autumn, you may encounter stronger winds, especially near the Strait of Gibraltar. If you book a sailing yacht, the skipper will use the wind to your advantage, but motor yachts are less affected. Always check the forecast the day before your charter; the skipper will make the final call on safety. If the weather is poor, you can reschedule or get a refund. For a private yacht rental Marbella, the best time to be on the water is between 10:00 and 16:00, when the sun is high and the wind is at its calmest.

Customising Your Private Yacht Charter: Food, Drinks, and Extras

Most private yacht rental Marbella charters allow you to bring your own food and drinks. This saves money and gives you control over the menu. However, many clients prefer to order catering from a local company. Popular options include a Spanish paella cooked on board, a sushi platter, or a barbecue with fresh fish and meat. The crew will serve the food and clean up. Drinks are usually BYO, but some boats have a bar with soft drinks and beer at an extra cost. Champagne and wine are common for sunset cruises. Water toys like a jet ski, a banana boat, or a paddleboard can be added for an extra fee, usually EUR 50-150 per item. Snorkelling gear is often included for free. If you want a photographer or a DJ on board, that can be arranged for an additional charge. The key is to discuss your preferences with the boat owner or our team when booking, so everything is ready on the day.

Private Yacht Rental Marbella for Special Occasions

A private yacht rental Marbella is a popular choice for celebrations. Birthdays, anniversaries, and proposals are common. For a proposal, many clients book a sunset charter on a motor yacht or catamaran, with a bottle of cava and a private corner of the deck. The skipper can anchor off Cala del Faro or near the Marbella coastline for a dramatic backdrop. For a birthday party, a catamaran is ideal because of the large sun deck and stable platform. You can bring a speaker, decorations, and a cake. Some boats allow a small DJ setup. Corporate events on a private yacht rental Marbella are also popular. Companies use charters for client entertainment, team-building, or product launches. The yacht provides a private, exclusive environment away from distractions. For larger groups, we can arrange a superyacht with a professional crew, including a chef and waitstaff. The cost for a superyacht is price on request, but expect to pay from EUR 10,000 for a half-day for a 100-foot vessel.

Practical Tips for Your Private Yacht Charter Day

To make the most of your private yacht rental Marbella, arrive at the port 15 minutes before departure. Bring sunscreen, a hat, sunglasses, and a light jacket (the breeze can be cool at sea). Swimwear and a towel are essential if you plan to swim. Seasickness is rare in the calm Mediterranean, but if you are prone to motion sickness, take a tablet an hour before boarding. The skipper will provide a safety briefing at the start, so listen carefully. You can use the onboard toilet (heads), but do not flush anything other than toilet paper. Smoking is allowed on the deck, but not inside the cabin. If you bring a pet, confirm with the owner first, as not all boats allow animals. Tipping the crew is customary but not expected; 10-15% of the charter fee is standard if you are happy with the service. Finally, leave your valuables at home or in a secure bag; the skipper is not responsible for lost items.

Related guides

h1: Boat Hire Marbella Port: Instant Booking & Best Prices
intro:
You want to get on the water from Marbella port, and you want it sorted now. No endless email chains, no waiting 48 hours for a reply. BoatHire24 lets you browse real availability from Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona, Sotogrande and Benalmádena, compare prices and book instantly. Every charter includes a licensed skipper, so you just turn up with your swimmers and suncream.

Marbella has two main ports for boat hire: the glitzy Puerto Banús and the more laid-back Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo). Both give you direct access to the Costa del Sol coastline, La Concha mountain views and the chance to spot dolphins in the Strait of Gibraltar. This page covers what you get, what it costs and how to lock in your booking without the hassle.
bodyHtml:
What Is Included with Boat Hire from Marbella Port

When you book through BoatHire24, every charter comes with a licensed skipper who knows the local waters. That means you don't need a boating licence, and you can relax while someone else handles the navigation, mooring and safety. Fuel is usually included for half-day or full-day trips, but check the listing because some longer charters charge extra. You also get snorkelling gear, towels and a cooler box on most boats. Food and drinks are not included unless you select a catering package, but you can bring your own or order from the marina shops before departure.

The skipper speaks English and Spanish, and they will point out landmarks like the Río Verde, the sandbanks at Cabopino and the rocky coves near Cala del Faro. They also know the best spots to anchor for a swim. If you want to steer the boat yourself under supervision, just ask. Many skippers are happy to let you take the helm for a few minutes.

Which Marbella Ports Can You Hire From

Puerto Banús is the famous one. It is the marina with the superyachts, the designer shops and the champagne bars. You depart from the main dock, cruise past the luxury boats and head out to sea. It is a 10-minute walk from the centre of Nueva Andalucía, and there is paid parking nearby. Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo) is closer to the old town, a 5-minute walk from the Avenida del Mar. It is less flashy but still well-equipped, with restaurants and a small beach. Both ports offer the same range of boats, but prices at Marbella Marina can be slightly lower because the mooring fees are cheaper.

If you want a quieter departure, consider Cabopino or Estepona. Cabopino has a small marina next to the Artola sand dunes, great for a relaxed start. Estepona port is 20 minutes west of Marbella and gives you quicker access to the dolphin-watching areas near Gibraltar. Sotogrande and Benalmádena are further afield but still within a 30-minute drive from Marbella. BoatHire24 covers all these ports, so you can choose the one that suits your plans.

Types of Boats Available for Instant Booking

Motor yachts are the most popular choice for Marbella port hire. You get speed, comfort and a sun deck. Typical models range from 30 to 50 feet, with a flybridge, a cabin and a bathroom. Prices start from around EUR 600 for a half day on a 35-foot yacht. Catamarans are better for groups. They have more deck space, a shallower draft for anchoring close to shore and a steady ride. A 40-foot catamaran for a full day costs from around EUR 1,200.

Sailing yachts are quieter and more hands-on. You can help the skipper with the sails if you want, or just lie on the foredeck. A 40-foot sailing yacht costs from around EUR 500 for a half day. RIBs and speedboats are for the thrill seekers. They are fast, open and ideal for a quick blast to Cala del Faro or a wakeboarding session. Prices start from EUR 350 for two hours. Licence-free day boats are also available if you want to drive yourself, but you still need a skipper for insurance reasons on BoatHire24 charters. Fishing boats come with rods, bait and a skipper who knows the local marks. Superyachts are price on request, but you can view them on the site and send an enquiry.

Pricing Guidance for Boat Hire Marbella Port

Prices vary by boat size, season and duration. In low season (November to March), a half-day charter on a motor yacht starts from around EUR 450. In high season (June to September), the same boat jumps to EUR 700 or more. Full-day charters are roughly double the half-day rate, but you get more value because the fuel cost is spread over a longer trip. Catamarans and sailing yachts follow a similar pattern. RIBs and speedboats are cheaper because they use less fuel and have a simpler setup.

BoatHire24 shows the exact price for each listing, so you can compare without sending a request. There are no hidden fees. The price you see includes the skipper, fuel (unless stated), VAT and insurance. Deposits are handled through the platform, and you pay the balance on the day. If you book more than a week in advance, you can often get a 10% early-bird discount. Look for the 'instant book' filter on the search page to see only the boats that can be confirmed immediately.

Best Time of Year for Boat Hire from Marbella Port

The Costa del Sol has a Mediterranean climate with mild winters and hot summers. The best months for boat hire are May to October, when the sea temperature is above 20 degrees C and the wind is light. July and August are peak season. The water is warm, the sun is strong and the evenings are long, but the ports are busy and prices are highest. If you want quieter conditions, try June or September. The sea is still warm, the crowds have thinned and the prices drop by about 20%.

Winter charters are possible but less popular. The sea can be choppy, and the air temperature rarely exceeds 18 degrees C. You will still get sunny days, but bring a jacket. The advantage is availability. You can often book a superyacht at a fraction of the summer rate. Just check the weather forecast before you go.

How to Book a Boat at Marbella Port Instantly

Go to the BoatHire24 homepage and enter Marbella as the location. Select your date, group size and preferred boat type. The search results will show you all available boats with prices, photos and a description. Use the filters to narrow by port (Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, etc.) and by instant book availability. When you find a boat you like, click 'Book Now'. You will be asked for your contact details and payment. The booking is confirmed immediately, and you receive a confirmation email with the skipper's contact number and the meeting point.

If you need help choosing, use the search page to compare multiple options side by side. You can also read our blog for tips on what to look for in a charter. The platform supports cancellations up to 48 hours before departure for a full refund, minus a small processing fee.

Who Is Boat Hire from Marbella Port For

This is for anyone who wants a day on the water without the stress of organising it. Families with kids appreciate the stability of a catamaran and the shallow anchor spots. Groups of friends go for motor yachts with a sound system and a sun deck. Couples often book a sailing yacht for a romantic sunset cruise. Business clients use the charters for client entertainment or team-building days. The skipper handles everything, so you can focus on enjoying the views of La Concha, the Gibraltar coastline and the occasional dolphin pod.

If you are a first-time visitor, start with a half-day trip from Puerto Banús. You get the full Marbella experience without committing to a full day. If you are a regular, try a full-day charter to Estepona or even across the Strait to Tangier (visa required). The platform covers all these options.

What to Expect on the Day of Your Charter

Arrive at the designated port 15 minutes before departure. The skipper will meet you at the dock, show you the safety briefing and explain the itinerary. You can suggest changes if you want to visit a specific cove or restaurant. The boat will have a toilet, a shaded area and a sun deck. Bring sunscreen, a hat and a towel. If you booked a catering package, the food will be ready on board. Otherwise, bring your own snacks and drinks. The skipper will anchor at a quiet spot for swimming, usually near Cala del Faro or the Cabopino sandbanks. You will return to the same port at the end of the charter.

If the weather turns bad, the skipper may cancel for safety. In that case, you get a full refund or a rescheduled date. BoatHire24 monitors the conditions and will contact you if necessary.

How to Get the Best Price on Instant Book Boat Hire in Marbella Port

If you want the lowest rate, book a half-day trip on a weekday in May or September. A 35-foot motor yacht from Marbella Marina costs around EUR 480 on a Tuesday morning in May, compared to EUR 750 for the same boat on a Saturday in August. Use the 'instant book' filter on the search page to lock in that price without waiting for the owner to confirm. Another trick is to split a full-day charter with another group. If you have six people and the boat holds eight, you can advertise the spare spots on social media or local expat forums. That brings the per-person cost down to around EUR 100 for a full day on a catamaran.

For last-minute bookings, check 24 to 48 hours before your desired date. Owners often drop prices to fill empty slots. You can find a half-day RIB trip for as little as EUR 250 if you book the night before. Just make sure the weather forecast is clear, because last-minute cancellations due to wind are not refundable if you cancel on the same day.

Hidden Costs to Watch Out for When Hiring a Boat from Marbella Port

Most listings on BoatHire24 include fuel, but some half-day charters cap the fuel at 50 litres. If you blast out to Cala del Faro at full throttle, you might exceed that. The skipper will ask for extra cash to top up the tank. Ask before you book whether fuel is unlimited or capped. The same applies to food and drinks. Some boats offer a catering package for EUR 30 to EUR 50 per person, but if you bring your own, you might need to pay a corkage fee for glass bottles on board. Check the listing notes or message the owner through the platform.

Another cost is the dock fee for the port. Puerto Banús charges a EUR 10 to EUR 20 fee for boats that moor after the charter, but this is usually included in the price you see. Estepona and Cabopino have no extra fee. If you want a skipper who speaks a specific language other than English or Spanish, request it at booking. Some skippers charge an extra EUR 50 for French or German speakers.

Which Marbella Port Is Best for Different Types of Trips

For a party trip, depart from Puerto Banús. You are already in the centre of the nightlife, and you can walk to the bars and clubs after the charter. The marina has a fuel station and a supermarket right on the dock, so stocking up is easy. For a family day out, choose Marbella Marina. It is quieter, the parking is cheaper (EUR 15 for the day versus EUR 30 at Banús), and you are a short walk from the old town for a post-charter ice cream. Cabopino is best for a nature-focused trip. The Artola dunes are protected, and the sea is usually calmer because the port is smaller. You can anchor near the sandbanks and see flamingos in the summer.

For dolphin watching, depart from Estepona. The Strait of Gibraltar is 15 NM south, and the dolphins are almost guaranteed. A half-day trip from Estepona costs around EUR 400 on a 30-foot motor yacht, and you can combine it with a stop at Punta Chullera for a swim. Sotogrande is for luxury seekers. The marina is exclusive, and the boats tend to be larger (50 feet plus). You can cruise to the mouth of the Guadiaro River and see the polo fields from the water.

Benalmádena is the least popular for Marbella visitors because it is 30 km east, but it has a good selection of RIBs and fishing boats. If you want a cheap half-day fishing trip, Benalmádena is your port. Prices start from EUR 200 for a three-hour session with rods and bait included.

How the Weather Affects Boat Hire from Marbella Port

The Costa del Sol is sheltered by the mountains, so the wind is usually light in the morning. By 2pm, the Levante wind can pick up to 15 to 20 knots, making the sea choppy. If you are on a RIB or a small speedboat, book a morning slot. The water is flatter, and you get more speed. For catamarans and motor yachts over 40 feet, afternoon trips are fine because the hull handles the chop better. In July and August, the sea breeze is predictable, and the swell rarely exceeds 1 metre.

In winter, the Poniente wind from the west can bring rain and big waves. Check the AEMET marine forecast the day before. If the wind is above 20 knots, most skippers will cancel for safety. BoatHire24 has a weather policy that gives you a full refund if the skipper cancels. If you decide to cancel because you do not like the forecast, you need to do it 48 hours before departure for a refund. That is why booking in advance during winter is risky. Stick to last-minute bookings when the forecast is stable.

Specific Routes and Itineraries for Boat Hire from Marbella Port

A popular half-day route from Puerto Banús is to head west to Cala del Faro, a rocky cove with clear water 6 NM away. The skipper anchors for 45 minutes, and you swim in the calm bay. Then you cruise back past the sandbanks at Cabopino, where you might see kite surfers. Total distance is about 12 NM, and you are back at the dock by 2pm. For a full day, go east to Nerja. It is 25 NM from Marbella Marina, so you need a fast motor yacht. You stop at the Nerja caves for a swim, then have lunch at a beach club in Burriana beach. The return trip takes 90 minutes at 20 knots.

Another full-day route is south to the Strait of Gibraltar. From Estepona, you cross the shipping lanes to the Tarifa area, where the wind is stronger. You see dolphins, sometimes pilot whales, and on a clear day you can see the coast of Africa. The skipper will not take you into the busiest shipping lanes, but you get close enough for photos. This route is best on a 40-foot motor yacht with a flybridge for spotting. A full-day charter from Estepona to the Strait costs from around EUR 1,000 in high season.

For a sunset trip, depart from Puerto Banús at 6pm. Cruise to the Río Verde, anchor near the mouth, and watch the sun go down behind La Concha. The skipper will serve sparkling wine and olives if you book the sundowner package (EUR 25 per person). You are back by 9pm, just in time for dinner at one of the Banús restaurants.

What to Bring and Not Bring on a Boat Hire from Marbella Port

Bring a soft-sided cooler bag, not a hard one. Hard coolers scratch the deck and take up too much space. Bring reef-safe sunscreen because regular sunscreen damages the boat's upholstery and the marine environment. The skipper will provide a towel, but bring your own if you want a thick beach towel. Do not bring glass bottles. Many skippers ban them because broken glass on the deck is a hazard. Transfer your drinks into plastic or metal containers. Do not bring inflatable floats unless the skipper agrees. Some boats have limited deck space, and a large unicorn float blocks the walkways.

If you are prone to seasickness, take a motion sickness pill 30 minutes before departure. The pharmacist in Puerto Banús sells Biodramina for around EUR 5. Do not eat a heavy breakfast. Stick to toast and fruit. The skipper will have sick bags on board, but it is better to avoid the need.

faqs.q: What is the cheapest boat hire from Marbella port?
faqs.a: The cheapest option is a RIB or speedboat for two hours, starting from around EUR 350. For a half-day on a motor yacht, prices start from EUR 450 in low season. Use the instant book filter on the search page to see the lowest prices available for your date.

faqs.q: How far in advance should I book a boat at Marbella port?
faqs.a: In high season, book at least two weeks ahead to get the best choice. In low season, a few days is usually enough. Instant book listings can be confirmed immediately, even for same-day charters, but availability is limited.

faqs.a: The skipper will assess the conditions and may cancel for safety. You get a full refund or a rescheduled date at no extra cost. BoatHire24 monitors the forecast and will contact you if a cancellation is needed.

faqs.q: Can I charter a boat from Marbella port for a party or event?
faqs.a: Yes, many motor yachts and catamarans are suitable for parties, with sound systems and sun decks. You can book a full-day charter for celebrations. Check the listing for maximum guest capacity and noise restrictions. Some ports have rules about amplified music.

title: Catamaran Rental Marbella: Group Fun
metaDescription: Book a catamaran rental Marbella for your group. Motor or sailing catamarans with skipper from Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino. Prices from around EUR 1,200. Check availability.
h1: Catamaran Rental Marbella: Group Fun on the Costa del Sol
intro:
You want a day on the water with a group of friends or family, and you want space, stability, and a sun deck that doesn't tip. A catamaran rental Marbella gives you exactly that: a wide, stable platform that lets you move around without feeling every swell. And because every charter includes a licensed skipper, you don't need a licence or any experience.

We are BoatHire24, a marketplace that connects you with boat owners and charter companies along the Costa del Sol. We cover the main ports from Sotogrande to Benalmádena, with the biggest concentration in Puerto Banús and Marbella Marina. Below you will find what to expect, which catamaran types are available, how much it costs, and how to book without the usual hassle.
bodyHtml:
What a Catamaran Rental Marbella Includes

Every catamaran charter on BoatHire24 comes with a professional skipper. That means you do not need a boat licence or any prior sailing experience. The skipper handles navigation, mooring, and safety. You just relax, swim, eat, and drink. Most catamarans also include a hostess or steward if you book a larger vessel, but that varies by operator. Fuel is usually extra, though some all-inclusive packages cover it. Check the listing details before you confirm. Standard equipment on most catamarans: a bimini top for shade, a bow trampoline for lounging, a small galley kitchen, a fridge or coolbox, a bluetooth speaker, snorkelling masks, and life jackets. Some premium catamarans add a paddleboard, a kayak, or a water slide. You can bring your own food and drink on most boats, but some operators offer catering at an extra cost. Ask when you book.

Ports for Catamaran Rental Marbella

The most popular departure point is Puerto Banús, the glitzy marina in the heart of the Marbella coastline. From there you can head west toward the Cala del Faro or east toward Cabopino. The second most common port is Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo), which sits right next to the old town. It is slightly cheaper for mooring fees, so some charter companies pass that saving on to you. Other options: Cabopino (a small, quiet marina near the dunes of Artola), Estepona (charming and less crowded), Sotogrande (upmarket and close to Gibraltar), and Benalmádena (busy, with lots of nightlife options). Each port gives you a different coastline. From Puerto Banús you can reach the Marbella coastline in 15 minutes. From Estepona you can see the Strait of Gibraltar and often spot dolphins. From Sotogrande you can cross to Gibraltar or head north to the Guadiaro river mouth. Choose based on where you are staying and what you want to see.

Types of Catamarans Available

Catamaran rental Marbella splits into two main categories: motor catamarans and sailing catamarans. Motor catamarans are fast, usually 40 to 60 feet, with powerful engines. They have a flybridge, a large sun deck, and a spacious saloon. They are ideal for day trips where you want to cover more distance, say from Marbella to the Nerja caves or across to the African coast (though the latter is a long day). Sailing catamarans are slower but quieter. They use the wind, so you get a proper sailing experience. They tend to have more deck space because there is no flybridge. Typical sizes range from 38 to 52 feet. Both types have between 4 and 6 cabins, which means they can sleep 8 to 12 guests for overnight trips. For day charters, capacity is usually larger: up to 12 or 14 passengers. Some catamarans are licence-free day boats, but those are rare. Most require a skipper anyway. If you want a pure party boat, look for a motor catamaran with a sound system and a water slide. If you want a relaxed, eco-friendly cruise, go for a sailing catamaran.

Pricing Guidance for Catamaran Rental Marbella

Prices vary by boat size, age, amenities, season, and duration. For a half-day (4 hours) on a 40-foot sailing catamaran in low season (November to March), expect from around EUR 800. A full day (8 hours) on the same boat in high season (June to September) costs from around EUR 1,200 to EUR 1,800. A 50-foot motor catamaran with a flybridge and a hostess can run from EUR 2,000 to EUR 3,500 for a full day in peak season. Superyacht catamarans over 70 feet are price on request, but expect EUR 5,000 or more. Fuel is usually extra and can add EUR 200 to EUR 600 depending on distance and speed. If you want catering, add EUR 30 to EUR 60 per person. Some operators include a welcome drink and snacks in the base price. Always ask for a written quote that lists what is included. We recommend booking through our search page so you can compare multiple options side by side.

Best Season for Catamaran Rental Marbella

The Costa del Sol has a Mediterranean climate with mild winters and hot summers. The best months for catamaran rental Marbella are May to October. In May and June, temperatures range from 22 to 28 degrees C, the sea is calm, and the crowds are smaller. July and August are peak season: temperatures hit 30 to 35 degrees C, the sea is warm (22 to 25 degrees C), and the marinas are packed. Book at least two weeks in advance for summer weekends. September and October are still warm, with fewer tourists and lower prices. November to April is low season. The weather can still be good (15 to 20 degrees C), but the sea is rougher, and some charter companies reduce their fleet or operate only on request. If you want to see dolphins, the best time is early morning from June to September, when they feed near the Strait of Gibraltar. For swimming, the water is comfortable from June to October.

How to Book a Catamaran in Marbella

Booking through BoatHire24 is straightforward. First, go to our search page and filter by boat type (catamaran), port, date, and number of guests. You will see a list of available boats with photos, descriptions, and prices. Click on a listing to see the full details: what is included, the cancellation policy, the skipper’s experience, and any extras. Then send an enquiry through the platform. The owner or charter company will respond within a few hours with availability and a final price. You can ask questions about food, drinks, music, or specific routes. Once you agree, you pay a deposit (usually 30% to 50%) to secure the date. The balance is due on the day of the charter, either in cash or by card. We recommend reading the cancellation policy carefully. Most operators offer a full refund if you cancel more than 14 days before the charter. For last-minute cancellations, you may lose the deposit. If the weather is bad and the skipper decides it is unsafe, you get a full refund or a reschedule.

Who Should Book a Catamaran Rental Marbella

Catamarans are perfect for groups that want space and stability. If you have a group of 8 to 12 people, a catamaran gives you more room than a monohull of the same length. The wide beam means you can walk around without bumping into each other. The bow trampoline is great for sunbathing, and the stern platform makes swimming easy. Families with children appreciate the low risk of seasickness because catamarans don't roll as much. Corporate groups use catamarans for team-building events or client entertainment. Birthday parties and hen or stag groups also work, but check with the operator first because some have noise restrictions. If you want a romantic couple's charter, a catamaran might feel too big. A smaller sailing yacht or a RIB might suit you better. For fishing enthusiasts, catamarans are not ideal because they are slow and have a shallow draft. A dedicated fishing boat is better. But for pure fun on the water with friends, a catamaran is hard to beat.

What to Expect on the Day

On the day of your catamaran rental Marbella, you meet the skipper at the designated port at the agreed time. The skipper gives a safety briefing and shows you the boat. Then you cast off. The skipper decides the route based on the wind, weather, and your preferences. Typical routes: from Puerto Banús east to Cabopino and the dunes of Artola, then back, stopping for a swim. Or west to the Cala del Faro and the cliffs of Marbella, then to the port of Estepona for lunch. Some skippers take you to the Marbella coastline to see the luxury villas and the La Concha mountain backdrop. If you book a full day, you can go further: to the Strait of Gibraltar to see dolphins and whales, or to the Nerja caves (about 30 NM from Puerto Banús, a 2-hour trip each way). The skipper handles all navigation. You just enjoy the ride. Most charters include a stop for swimming and snorkelling. The skipper will anchor in a calm bay. You can jump off the boat, use the paddleboard, or just float. Lunch is usually at a beach club or a chiringuito where the skipper can moor or anchor near the shore. Some operators offer a BBQ on board. , you return to the port. The skipper cleans the boat. You pay the balance and tip if you want. Tipping is not mandatory but is appreciated. 10% of the charter fee is common for good service.

Local Points of Interest Near Marbella

Marbella is not just about the marina. The old town, Casco Antiguo, has narrow streets, orange trees, and tapas bars. You can walk there from Marbella Marina. Puerto Banús is the place for luxury shopping and people-watching. The beach of Cabopino has a nudist section and a protected dune ecosystem. Estepona old town is full of flower pots and whitewashed houses. The Strait of Gibraltar is a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve and a prime spot for dolphin watching. If you have a full-day charter, you can go as far as the Nerja caves, which are about 50 km east of Marbella. They are one of the largest cave systems in Europe, with stalactites and stalagmites. The caves are not accessible by boat directly, but you can anchor nearby and take a taxi. For a shorter trip, the Cala del Faro is a secluded cove west of Marbella with a small lighthouse. It is a popular swimming stop. For more local tips, read our blog about the best anchorages and beach clubs on the Costa del Sol.

Why Book Through BoatHire24

We are a marketplace, not a charter company. That means you get multiple options from different owners, all in one place. You compare prices, boat specs, and reviews. You book directly with the owner, so there is no middleman markup. We verify that every boat has a valid licence and insurance. We also provide a secure payment system for deposits. If something goes wrong, we mediate. You can read our terms and conditions on the website. We have been operating since 2018 and have helped thousands of groups find the right boat. Our search filters let you narrow down by port, boat type, capacity, and price. We also have a blog with guides on how to choose a boat, what to bring, and the best routes. If you have questions, you can contact us through the website. We respond within 24 hours. For a catamaran rental Marbella, start your search on our search page and see what is available today.

Best Catamarans for Large Groups in Marbella

If you have 12 to 14 people, look for a Lagoon 52 or a Bali 4.6. These are the most common models on the Costa del Sol. The Lagoon 52 has a huge flybridge with a wet bar and a sun pad. The Bali 4.6 has an open saloon that connects directly to the bow, so you can walk from the cockpit to the trampoline without going inside. Both have a swim platform with a ladder, which makes getting in and out of the water easy. For smaller groups of 6 to 8, a Lagoon 42 or a Fountaine Pajot Lucia 40 works well. These are cheaper and easier to manoeuvre. Check the listing for the exact model, because some owners upgrade the sound system or add a water slide. If you want a water slide, confirm it in the enquiry because most catamarans do not have one as standard.

Best Routes for a Catamaran Day Trip from Marbella

From Puerto Banús, the most popular route is west to the Cala del Faro and then to the Marbella coastline. You see the luxury villas of the Golden Mile and the La Concha mountain behind them. The Cala del Faro is a small cove with a lighthouse, good for swimming. From Marbella Marina, head east to Cabopino. The Cabopino beach has a chiringuito called Chiringuito Cabopino where you can moor and have lunch. The dunes of Artola are behind the beach, a protected natural area. From Estepona, go south toward the Strait of Gibraltar. You often see dolphins within 20 minutes. From Sotogrande, cross to Gibraltar or go north to the Guadiaro river mouth, which is calm and shallow. For a full-day trip from any port, you can go to the Nerja caves, but that is a 2-hour sail each way, so you need a fast motor catamaran. Ask the skipper for the best route based on the wind and weather on the day.

What to Bring on a Catamaran Charter

Bring sunscreen (factor 50, spray is easier to apply), a hat, sunglasses with a strap, and a towel. The sun is strong even in May and October. Bring a light jacket or a windbreaker because the wind on the water can be cool, especially in the morning or late afternoon. If you want to swim, bring a swimsuit and a change of clothes. Most catamarans have a freshwater shower on the swim platform. Bring your own snorkelling mask if you have one, though most boats provide them. For food and drink, bring a cooler bag with drinks and snacks. Most catamarans have a fridge, but it is small. Do not bring glass bottles because they break and are dangerous on the deck. Bring a plastic cup or a reusable bottle. If you plan to play music, bring a bluetooth speaker, but check if the boat already has one. Leave valuables at home or in a waterproof bag. The skipper is not responsible for lost items.

How to Choose Between a Motor Catamaran and a Sailing Catamaran

Motor catamarans are faster and have more space on the flybridge. They are better if you want to cover distance, like going from Marbella to the Nerja caves or to the Strait of Gibraltar. They also have a higher fuel cost, typically EUR 300 to EUR 600 for a full day. Sailing catamarans are slower but cheaper on fuel, usually EUR 100 to EUR 200 for a full day. They are quieter and give you the feeling of sailing. If you want to relax and not rush, a sailing catamaran is better. If you want to party or see a lot of coastline, a motor catamaran is better. Both types have similar deck space and capacity. The choice depends on your budget and your preference for speed versus silence. Ask the operator for the fuel estimate before you book so you know the total cost.

Practical Tips for First-Time Catamaran Renters

Book at least one week in advance for low season and two weeks for high season. Confirm the meeting point and the time with the skipper the day before. Arrive 15 minutes early. Bring cash for the balance and for tips. Most operators accept card, but cash is easier. Do not bring more guests than the boat capacity allows. The skipper can refuse to take you if you exceed the limit. If you want to change the route on the day, ask the skipper. They know the local conditions and can suggest alternatives. If you feel seasick, take a motion sickness tablet an hour before departure. Catamarans are stable, but some people still feel it. If you want to anchor for lunch, tell the skipper in advance so they can plan the route. For more tips, read our blog on how to prepare for a boat charter on the Costa del Sol.
