title: Private Yacht Hire Puerto Banus: Exclusive
metaDescription: Book private yacht hire Puerto Banus with a licensed skipper. Motor yachts, catamarans, superyachts from EUR 1,500. Instant online booking at BoatHire24.
h1: Private Yacht Hire Puerto Banus: Exclusive Charters
intro:
You want to hire a private yacht in Puerto Banus without the faff. The good news is that every charter on BoatHire24 comes with a licensed skipper who knows the coast from Marbella to Estepona. No hidden fees, no surprise charges.

Puerto Banus is the glitziest marina on the Costa del Sol, and private yacht hire here means you get the full experience: champagne on the flybridge, a swim at Cala del Faro, and a view of La Concha mountain from the water. Prices start from around EUR 1,500 for a half-day on a 12-metre motor yacht, and go up to price on request for a 40-metre superyacht.

You can browse and book directly on BoatHire24.
bodyHtml:
What Private Yacht Hire in Puerto Banus Includes

Every private yacht hire includes a professional skipper and fuel for the agreed itinerary. You get a dedicated host or stewardess on most motor yachts and catamarans, plus a cooler with ice and bottled water. Some boats offer a sound system, Wi-Fi, and a tender for beach drops. What is not included: food and drink (you can bring your own or pre-order catering), dockage fees beyond the charter period, and gratuities for the crew. The typical charter runs from 10am to 6pm, but you can arrange sunset or evening cruises for a surcharge.

Puerto Banus: The Starting Point for Your Charter

Puerto Banus is not just a marina, it is a destination. You board at the port where the superyachts park, right next to designer boutiques and beach clubs. The departure procedure is straightforward: you arrive 15 minutes before the charter, the skipper greets you at the gangway, and you are off within 10 minutes. The port has ample parking (around EUR 30 per day) and taxi ranks. If you are staying in Marbella or Estepona, the drive is 15-20 minutes. For a full list of available boats from this port, check the search page.

Boat Types for Private Yacht Hire

You have five main categories to choose from. Motor yachts from 10 to 25 metres are the most popular for private hire: Azimut, Princess, and Sunseeker models dominate the fleet. Catamarans (Lagoon, Fountaine Pajot) offer more deck space and stability, ideal for groups of 8-12. Sailing yachts are slower but quieter, perfect for a relaxed day with a gentle breeze. RIBs and speedboats (4-8 metres) are cheaper and faster, but less luxurious. Superyachts over 30 metres require advance booking and a deposit of 50%. Licence-free day boats (under 6 metres) are available from Cabopino but not from Puerto Banus itself.

Pricing Guidance for Private Yacht Hire Puerto Banus

Prices vary by season, boat age, and duration. In high season (June to September), a 12-metre motor yacht costs from around EUR 1,500 for 4 hours, EUR 2,200 for 8 hours. A 15-metre catamaran runs from EUR 2,000 for half a day. Superyacht charters start at EUR 6,000 per day and go up to EUR 25,000 for a 40-metre vessel with full crew. Low season (November to February) discounts of 20-30% are common. Always confirm the final price including VAT (21%) and any extras such as jet ski rental or water toys. The best way to get an accurate quote is to use the online booking tool on BoatHire24.

Best Seasons for Yacht Hire in Puerto Banus

The Mediterranean climate gives you reliable weather from April to October. July and August average 30 degrees C, with sea surface temperatures around 24 degrees C. May and June are cooler but less crowded, and you might spot dolphins near the Strait of Gibraltar. September and October offer warm days and calm seas, ideal for a sunset cruise. Winter charters are possible but the sea can be choppy, and some boats are laid up for maintenance. If you want to swim, aim for June to September when the water is warmest. For more seasonal tips, read our blog.

Popular Itineraries from Puerto Banus

The standard route heads west along the coast to Cala del Faro, a secluded cove with clear water, about 3 NM from the port. You anchor for an hour, swim, and have lunch. The return trip takes you past the Marbella Marina and the golden beaches of San Pedro. Another option is east to Cabopino (5 NM) and the dunes of Artola, then back via the Rio Verde estuary. For a longer day, go south to Estepona old town (12 NM) and its colourful marina. If you have 8 hours, you can push to Sotogrande (25 NM) for a lunch at the yacht club. The skipper will suggest a plan based on wind and swell.

Who Should Book Private Yacht Hire in Puerto Banus

This is for groups of 6 to 12 people who want a premium experience without the hassle of organising a skipper or fuel. Birthday parties, corporate events, and family reunions are common. If you are a couple, a smaller motor yacht or a sailing yacht works better. If you are a group of 15 or more, consider a catamaran or a superyacht. Families with children should choose a boat with a bathing platform and a tender. The minimum age for children is usually 2 years, and life jackets are provided. Pets are not allowed on most boats.

How to Book a Private Yacht in Puerto Banus

You can book directly on BoatHire24 by selecting your date, boat type, and duration. The system shows real-time availability and allows you to pay a 30% deposit online (credit card or bank transfer). The balance is due 7 days before the charter. You receive a confirmation email with the skipper’s contact details and boarding instructions. Cancellations made 14 days or more before the charter get a full refund; within 14 days, the deposit is forfeited. For last-minute bookings (less than 48 hours), call the support line or use the live chat.

Local Knowledge and Hidden Gems

Your skipper knows the secret spots: a rocky inlet near Cala del Faro where the water is 5 metres deep and the fish are plentiful, or a quiet anchorage off Estepona where the beach club delivers lunch to the boat. If you are lucky, you might see dolphins playing in the wake near the Strait of Gibraltar. For a longer charter, the Nerja caves are 40 NM east, but that requires an overnight trip. The crew can arrange a private chef, a photographer, or a DJ for an extra fee. Just ask when you book.

Safety and Licensing Requirements

All skippers hold a valid Spanish Patron de Embarcaciones de Recreo (PER) or higher qualification, plus a VHF radio licence and first aid certificate. The boat is insured for charter use and inspected annually. You do not need a boating licence to hire a private yacht with a skipper. Life jackets are available for all guests, and the skipper gives a short safety briefing before departure. In case of bad weather, the skipper can modify the itinerary or cancel the charter with a full refund. The port of Puerto Banus has a 24-hour security team and a fuel dock.

Comparing Private Hire with Other Options

Private yacht hire is more expensive than a shared boat tour (which costs around EUR 80 per person) but gives you exclusivity and flexibility. You choose the route, the music, and the pace. A shared tour is fine if you are solo or on a budget, but private hire is better for groups who want a tailored experience. Licence-free day boats (up to 6 metres) are cheaper but require a licence to operate in Spain, and you cannot leave the port area without a skipper. For the full Puerto Banus experience, private yacht hire is the way to go.

Why Puerto Banus Is the Best Base for Private Yacht Hire

Puerto Banus sits at the western edge of Marbella, sheltered by the Sierra Blanca mountains. Its location gives you quick access to both the calm waters of the Costa del Sol and the more open sea near Estepona. The marina itself has 915 berths, many of them reserved for charter boats, so you are not waiting for a slot. The port’s fuel dock is open from 8am to 8pm, and the chandlery stocks everything from suncream to spare parts. If you are flying in, Malaga airport is 55 km away, a 40-minute taxi ride. The private jet terminal at Marbella Airport (AGP) is closer, 20 minutes by car. For more details on the port, visit the BoatHire24 homepage.

How to Choose the Right Boat for Your Group Size

Group size dictates the boat type more than budget does. For 6 people, a 12-metre motor yacht like a Princess V48 gives you a saloon, a small galley, and a sunpad. For 8 to 10 people, a 15-metre catamaran like a Lagoon 450 offers two hulls with separate cabins and a flybridge that seats everyone. For 12 to 15 people, you need a 20-metre motor yacht or a larger catamaran. Superyachts over 30 metres can handle up to 30 guests, but you pay for the extra crew and fuel. If you are a group of 4 or fewer, a 9-metre RIB or a sailing yacht is more cost-effective. The skipper will confirm the maximum capacity at booking.

What to Expect from the Crew on a Private Charter

The skipper handles navigation, anchoring, and safety. Most skippers speak Spanish and English, and some speak German or French. The host or stewardess serves drinks, prepares snacks, and cleans up. On superyachts, you get a chef who can cook a three-course meal in the galley. The crew works on a gratuity basis: 10-15% of the charter fee is standard if you are happy with the service. You can tip in cash or via bank transfer after the charter. The crew will not push for extras, but they appreciate a polite request. If you want a specific playlist or dietary requirements, tell the booking team 48 hours in advance.

Seasonal Pricing Variations for Private Yacht Hire

High season runs from June 1 to September 30. In July and August, demand peaks and prices rise by 20-30% compared to May or October. Shoulder season (April, May, October) offers the best value: a 12-metre motor yacht costs from around EUR 1,200 for 4 hours instead of EUR 1,500. Low season (November to March) sees discounts of up to 40%, but the weather is less reliable. Some boats are laid up for maintenance in January and February. If you book a superyacht in low season, you might negotiate a half-day rate. Always check the cancellation policy, as winter storms can force itinerary changes. Our blog has a monthly price guide.

Extra Costs to Budget For on a Private Yacht Charter

Fuel is included in the charter price for the agreed itinerary, but if you change the route mid-day, you may pay extra. Dockage fees at Puerto Banus are included for the start and end of the charter, but if you stop at another marina for lunch, you pay the fee (around EUR 50-100 for a 12-metre boat). Water toys like jet skis, paddleboards, and inflatable slides cost extra, typically EUR 100-300 per item per day. Catering from a local deli costs EUR 20-40 per person for a basic lunch, or EUR 60-80 per person for a gourmet menu with wine. Gratuities are not included, so budget 10-15% of the charter fee.

How to Combine Private Yacht Hire with a Puerto Banus Day

You can anchor near the port and take the tender to shore for lunch at a beach club like Ocean Club or Purobeach. The skipper will drop you at the beach and pick you up at a set time. Alternatively, you can moor at the Puerto Banus fuel dock and walk to a restaurant on the promenade. For a full day, start with a 2-hour cruise to Cala del Faro, swim for an hour, then head back to Puerto Banus for a late lunch. After the charter, you can shop at the port’s designer stores or have a drink at a yacht club. The crew can recommend a local restaurant for dinner.

What to Bring and What Not to Bring on a Private Yacht

Bring swimwear, a towel, sunscreen (SPF 50+), sunglasses, a hat, and a light jacket for the afternoon breeze. Non-slip shoes are recommended for boarding, but barefoot is fine on deck. You can bring your own food and drink, but avoid glass bottles on most boats. The cooler has space for a few bottles of wine or beer. Do not bring heavy suitcases or large bags, as storage is limited in the saloon. If you want to use a drone, ask the skipper first, as some marinas restrict drone flights. The crew provides life jackets for children and non-swimmers, but you can bring your own if you prefer.

How to Handle Weather Cancellations and Refunds

The skipper monitors the forecast 24 hours before the charter. If the wind exceeds 25 knots or the swell is over 1.5 metres, the charter may be cancelled for safety. You get a full refund or a reschedule with no fee. If the weather is marginal, the skipper can offer a modified itinerary, such as staying in sheltered bays instead of heading out to open water. You can accept or cancel for a full refund. If you cancel on the day for personal reasons, the deposit is forfeited. Travel insurance with weather cover is recommended for high-value charters.

Private Yacht Hire for Corporate Events and Special Occasions

Corporate groups use private yacht hire for client entertainment, team-building, or product launches. The crew can set up a projector for presentations in the saloon, or you can hold a meeting on the flybridge with a portable speaker. For a birthday or anniversary, the crew can arrange decorations, a cake, and a photographer. The skipper can coordinate a fireworks display for a special event, but you need to book at least two weeks in advance and pay a surcharge of EUR 500-1,000. The port authority allows fireworks in designated areas only, so the skipper will handle the permits.

How to Get the Best Deal on Private Yacht Hire in Puerto Banus

Book early, especially for July and August. Many boats are reserved by March for the summer season. If you are flexible on the date, ask for a mid-week charter (Tuesday to Thursday) which is often 10-15% cheaper than weekends. Consider a half-day charter (4 hours) instead of a full day if you just want a cruise and a swim. Compare prices on the search page for different boat types and sizes. Some owners offer a discount for repeat bookings or for groups that bring their own catering. Always ask for a written quote with a breakdown of included and excluded costs.

What Happens After You Book a Private Yacht

You receive a confirmation email with the boat details, skipper name, and boarding instructions. The skipper calls you the day before to confirm the meeting time and weather forecast. On the day, you arrive at the designated berth, sign a waiver, and pay any remaining balance. The skipper gives a safety briefing and shows you the life jackets, fire extinguisher, and emergency exits. You then set off. During the charter, the skipper checks in with you every hour to see if you want to change the plan. At the end, you return to the berth, tip the crew if you wish, and leave.

faqs.q: How much does private yacht hire in Puerto Banus cost?
faqs.a: Prices start from around EUR 1,500 for a half-day on a 12-metre motor yacht in high season. Catamarans cost from EUR 2,000, and superyachts from EUR 6,000 per day. Always check the final price including VAT and extras.

faqs.q: Do I need a boating licence to hire a private yacht?
faqs.a: No. Every charter includes a licensed skipper who handles the navigation. You just relax and enjoy the day.

faqs.q: What is the maximum group size for a private yacht charter?
faqs.a: Most motor yachts and catamarans take 8 to 12 guests. Superyachts can accommodate up to 20. Check the boat's capacity on the listing before booking.

faqs.q: Can I bring my own food and drink?
faqs.a: Yes. You can bring your own or pre-order catering through BoatHire24. Some boats have a galley with a fridge and microwave.

faqs.a: The skipper can modify the itinerary or cancel the charter. If cancelled by the operator, you get a full refund. If you cancel within 14 days, the deposit is forfeited.

faqs.q: How far in advance should I book?
faqs.a: For high season, book at least 2 weeks ahead. For superyachts, 4 weeks is recommended. Last-minute bookings (48 hours) are possible but limited.

faqs.q: Are pets allowed on private yacht charters?
faqs.a: Most boats do not allow pets. Check the specific boat's policy on the listing. Some catamarans may accept small dogs with prior approval.

title: Speedboat Rental Fuengirola: Affordable Fun
metaDescription: Book a speedboat rental Fuengirola with BoatHire24. Explore the Costa del Sol coast with a licensed skipper. Prices from around EUR 300. Check availability.
h1: Speedboat Rental Fuengirola: Affordable Fun on the Water
intro:
If you want a fast, fun day out on the Costa del Sol without the hassle of navigating or owning a boat, a speedboat rental Fuengirola is your best bet. You get a licensed skipper, a powerful RIB or speedboat, and the freedom to explore the coastline from Fuengirola to Marbella, Puerto Banús, or even Estepona. No licence needed, no hidden costs, just you, the sun, and the sea.

Fuengirola sits right on the Costa del Sol, 30 km from Málaga airport and 15 km from Marbella. Its marina, Puerto Deportivo de Fuengirola, is a busy hub with bars, restaurants, and direct access to the Mediterranean. You can rent a speedboat here for a couple of hours, a half day, or a full day. The skipper handles everything, so you can focus on swimming, sunbathing, and spotting dolphins near the Strait of Gibraltar.
bodyHtml:
What Is Included in a Speedboat Rental Fuengirola

Every speedboat rental Fuengirola through BoatHire24 includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, and safety equipment. The skipper knows the local waters, the best coves, and the safest anchor points. You do not need a boating licence, and you do not need to worry about navigation or docking. The boat is a RIB or a small motor yacht, typically 6 to 9 metres long, with a powerful outboard or inboard engine. Top speeds range from 25 to 50 knots, depending on the model. You can expect a maximum of 8 to 12 passengers, depending on the boat's certification. Some boats have a sun pad at the bow, a small cabin for changing, and a cooler for drinks. The skipper will bring ice, but you bring your own food and drink. You can also request a paddleboard or snorkel gear for an extra fee, usually around EUR 50.

Departure Ports for Speedboat Rental Fuengirola

Fuengirola marina, officially called Puerto Deportivo de Fuengirola, is the main departure point. It is located near the town centre, next to the beach and the promenade. You can also depart from Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo), Puerto Banús, Cabopino, Estepona, Sotogrande, or Benalmádena. Each port has its own character. Fuengirola is the most convenient for visitors staying in Fuengirola, Mijas Costa, or Los Boliches. Marbella and Puerto Banús are busier and more glamorous. Cabopino is quieter, with a natural dune system and a small beach. Estepona offers a more laid-back vibe, with the old town and the promenade. Sotogrande is exclusive and expensive. Benalmádena is a lively resort with a large marina and a cable car. If you are staying in Fuengirola, the local marina is the obvious choice. The skipper will meet you at the boat, usually 15 minutes before departure. You can park in the marina car park, or take a taxi from Fuengirola town centre, which costs around EUR 10.

Boat Options for Speedboat Rental Fuengirola

The most common boat type for a speedboat rental Fuengirola is a RIB (rigid inflatable boat). These are fast, stable, and easy to board. Typical models include the Zodiac Pro 650, the Brig Falcon 750, or the Williams Jet Tender. Prices start from around EUR 300 for a 2-hour trip, EUR 450 for a half day (4 hours), and EUR 700 for a full day (8 hours). For a larger group, you can rent a motor yacht with a speedboat tender. These are bigger and more comfortable, but slower. Prices for a 10-metre motor yacht start from around EUR 600 for a half day. If you want pure speed, look for a RIB with a 250 HP outboard. These can reach 40 knots and are perfect for water sports like wakeboarding or tubing. Some boats have a T-top for shade, a stereo system, and a Bluetooth connection. You can also rent a fishing boat if you want to combine speed with angling. These are usually 7 to 9 metres long, with rod holders and a fish finder. Prices are similar to speedboats. For a truly luxurious experience, consider a superyacht with a speedboat tender. These are price on request, but expect EUR 2,000 or more for a half day.

Pricing Guidance for Speedboat Rental Fuengirola

Prices for a speedboat rental Fuengirola vary depending on the season, the boat, and the duration. High season (June to September) is the most expensive. Low season (November to February) is cheaper, but the weather is cooler and the sea is rougher. Mid season (March to May and October) offers a good balance. Here is a rough guide:

  • 2-hour trip: from around EUR 300 (low season) to EUR 450 (high season).
  • Half day (4 hours): from around EUR 450 to EUR 700.
  • Full day (8 hours): from around EUR 700 to EUR 1,200.
  • Sunset trip (2 hours): from around EUR 350 to EUR 500.

These prices include the skipper, fuel, and insurance. You do not pay extra for the skipper's time or fuel. The only extras are food, drink, and optional equipment like snorkel gear or a paddleboard. You can also tip the skipper, but it is not mandatory. Most skippers expect a tip of 10% to 15% if you are happy with the service. To get the best price, book in advance and avoid last-minute bookings in August. You can search for speedboat rentals in Fuengirola on BoatHire24 and compare prices from different operators.

Seasons and Weather for Speedboat Rental Fuengirola

The Costa del Sol has a Mediterranean climate with mild winters and hot summers. The best time for a speedboat rental Fuengirola is from May to October. In May and June, the sea is calm, the water temperature is around 18 to 22 degrees C, and the air temperature is 25 to 30 degrees C. July and August are the hottest months, with air temperatures up to 35 degrees C and water temperatures up to 26 degrees C. The sea is usually flat in the morning, but a light breeze picks up in the afternoon. September and October are still warm, with fewer crowds. November to February are cooler, with air temperatures around 15 to 20 degrees C and water temperatures around 15 to 18 degrees C. The sea can be choppy, especially in the Strait of Gibraltar. Some operators run all year, but trips are weather-dependent. If the wind is strong, the skipper may cancel or reroute. Always check the forecast before booking. You can also ask the operator about their cancellation policy. Most offer a full refund if the trip is cancelled due to weather.

How to Book a Speedboat Rental Fuengirola

Booking a speedboat rental Fuengirola is straightforward. Go to BoatHire24, enter Fuengirola as the port, select the date and number of passengers, and browse the available boats. You can filter by boat type, price, and duration. Once you find a boat you like, click on it to see the details, photos, and the skipper's profile. You can then book online with a deposit of 30% to 50%. The balance is paid on the day of the trip, usually in cash or by card. You will receive a confirmation email with the meeting point and the skipper's contact details. On the day, arrive at the marina 15 minutes early. The skipper will meet you at the boat, give you a safety briefing, and answer any questions. You can then set off. The trip is private, so you decide where to go. Popular destinations from Fuengirola include Cala del Faro (a small cove near Marbella), the Río Verde (a river mouth with a beach), and the coast towards Estepona. If you want to see dolphins, ask the skipper to head towards the Strait of Gibraltar, where pods of common dolphins and bottlenose dolphins are often spotted. The trip takes about 30 minutes from Fuengirola. You can also combine a speedboat rental with a visit to the Nerja caves, but that requires a full day and a drive from the port. The skipper can drop you off at a beach for lunch, or you can eat on the boat.

Who Is a Speedboat Rental Fuengirola For

A speedboat rental Fuengirola is for anyone who wants a fast, fun, and flexible day on the water. It is ideal for families with children, groups of friends, couples, and solo travellers. The boat is small enough to feel intimate, but fast enough to cover a lot of ground. You can swim in secluded coves, sunbathe on the boat, or explore the coastline. It is also a great option for water sports enthusiasts. Some boats are equipped with a tow rope for wakeboarding or tubing. You can also use the boat as a platform for snorkelling or paddleboarding. The skipper will help you with the equipment and show you the best spots. If you are celebrating a birthday, anniversary, or bachelor party, a speedboat rental is a memorable way to mark the occasion. You can bring your own music, drinks, and food. The skipper will not mind, as long as you are respectful and safe. If you are a first-time boater, the skipper will make you feel comfortable and explain everything. You do not need any experience. Just show up, relax, and enjoy the ride.

Local Attractions and Itineraries for Speedboat Rental Fuengirola

Fuengirola is a great base for exploring the Costa del Sol by speedboat. Here are some suggested itineraries:

  • 2-hour trip: Head south towards Cabopino. Stop at Cala del Faro for a swim. Then cruise back along the coast, passing the Marina of Fuengirola and the castle of Sohail. This is a short, scenic trip that gives you a taste of the coastline.
  • Half-day trip: Go west towards Marbella and Puerto Banús. Stop at the Río Verde beach for a swim. Then continue to Puerto Banús to see the superyachts and the luxury boutiques. On the way back, stop at a beach club for lunch. This trip covers about 20 NM each way.
  • Full-day trip: Go all the way to Estepona. Stop at the old town for a walk and a drink. Then head to the Strait of Gibraltar to look for dolphins. On the way back, stop at a beach for a swim. This trip covers about 40 NM each way. You will need a full day to do it comfortably.

If you want to see the Nerja caves, you can combine a speedboat rental with a car trip. The caves are 60 km east of Fuengirola, about 45 minutes by car. You can rent a speedboat for the morning, then drive to Nerja in the afternoon. Alternatively, you can take a longer boat trip to Nerja, but that is a full day and requires a larger boat with more fuel capacity. The skipper can advise on the best option.

Safety and Regulations for Speedboat Rental Fuengirola

Safety is a priority for all BoatHire24 operators. Every speedboat rental Fuengirola includes life jackets for all passengers, a first aid kit, a fire extinguisher, and a VHF radio. The skipper is licensed and experienced. They will give you a safety briefing before departure, including how to use the life jackets and what to do in an emergency. You are not allowed to operate the boat yourself, but you can ask the skipper to show you how to steer if you want to try. The boat is insured for public liability and damage. You are not liable for any damage unless it is caused by gross negligence. The skipper will also monitor the weather and sea conditions. If the forecast is bad, they will cancel the trip or suggest a safer route. Do not drink alcohol excessively on the boat. The skipper has the right to terminate the trip if you are intoxicated and posing a risk. Children are welcome, but they must wear a life jacket at all times. The minimum age for a speedboat rental is usually 2 years old. Some operators have a minimum age of 5. Check with the operator before booking.

Why Book Through BoatHire24

BoatHire24 is a marketplace that connects you with local boat operators in Fuengirola and the Costa del Sol. We vet all operators for safety, licensing, and insurance. You can read reviews from previous customers, compare prices, and book online with a secure payment. We also offer a best-price guarantee. If you find the same boat cheaper elsewhere, we will match the price. Our customer service team is available by phone and email. We speak English, Spanish, and German. We can help you choose the right boat, plan your itinerary, and answer any questions. We also have a blog with tips on boating in Spain, the best beaches, and the local marine life. For more information, check out our search page to find the perfect speedboat rental Fuengirola for your trip.

Related guides

  • Speedboat Hire Fuengirola: Fast Rentals with Skipper
  • Luxury Yacht Charter Puerto Banus: Elite Service
  • Boat Rental Puerto Banus: Wide Fleet with Skipper

faqs.q: Do I need a licence to rent a speedboat in Fuengirola?
faqs.a: No. Every speedboat rental Fuengirola through BoatHire24 includes a licensed skipper. You do not need any boating licence or experience. The skipper handles all navigation and docking.

faqs.q: How many people can fit on a speedboat in Fuengirola?
faqs.a: Most speedboats can carry 8 to 12 passengers, depending on the model and certification. Check the boat details on BoatHire24 for the exact capacity. Children count as passengers.

faqs.q: What is the cancellation policy for a speedboat rental Fuengirola?
faqs.a: Cancellation policies vary by operator. Most offer a full refund if you cancel 48 hours in advance. If the trip is cancelled due to weather, you get a full refund. Check the terms before booking.

faqs.q: Can I bring my own food and drink on the speedboat?
faqs.a: Yes. You can bring your own food and drink. The skipper will provide a cooler with ice. Glass bottles are not allowed for safety reasons. Use plastic or cans instead.

faqs.q: What happens if the weather is bad on the day of my trip?
faqs.a: The skipper will assess the conditions. If it is unsafe, they will cancel the trip and you get a full refund. You can also reschedule. Contact BoatHire24 for help.

faqs.q: How far can I travel on a speedboat rental Fuengirola?
faqs.a: A 2-hour trip covers about 10 to 15 NM along the coast. A half-day trip can reach Marbella or Cabopino. A full-day trip can go as far as Estepona or the Strait of Gibraltar. The skipper will advise on the best route.

faqs.q: Can I go water skiing or wakeboarding on a speedboat rental Fuengirola?
faqs.a: Yes. Some speedboats are equipped with a tow rope and can pull a skier or wakeboarder. You need to request this when booking. An extra fee may apply. The skipper will handle the driving.

title: Yacht Charter Benalmadena: Marina Departures
metaDescription: Book a yacht charter Benalmadena with a licensed skipper. Depart from Puerto Marina. Motor yachts, catamarans and more. Check availability now.
h1: Yacht Charter Benalmadena: Depart from Puerto Marina
intro:
You want a yacht charter Benalmadena, but you don't want to deal with the hassle of finding a crew or a boat that actually works. Fine. BoatHire24 lets you book a private yacht or catamaran from Puerto Marina Benalmadena, with a licensed skipper included. No licence needed, no hidden costs. Just pick your boat, pick your date, and turn up.

Benalmadena's marina is one of the most practical departure points on the Costa del Sol. It's closer to Malaga airport than Puerto Banus, the parking is less of a nightmare, and you can be sipping a cold drink on the water within 15 minutes of leaving the dock. Whether you want a half-day cruise to see dolphins, a full-day trip to Marbella, or a sunset party on a catamaran, this is where it starts.
bodyHtml:
What's Included in a Benalmadena Yacht Charter

When you book through BoatHire24, every charter comes with a professional skipper who knows the local waters. That means you don't need a boating licence, and you don't have to worry about navigation, anchoring, or dealing with the marina radio. The skipper handles all that. You just decide where you want to go and how long you want to stay.

Fuel is usually included for standard itineraries, but check the listing details because some longer trips or high-performance boats may charge extra. Onboard, you'll typically find a sound system, a cooler or fridge, and a shaded area. Most boats also have a Bluetooth speaker so you can play your own playlist. Food and drink are not included unless you opt for a catering package, but you can bring your own or ask the crew to arrange a stop at a chiringuito.

The skipper will also give you a safety briefing and point out the life jackets, fire extinguisher, and first aid kit. It's not a party boat in the Ibiza sense, but it's private, so you control the vibe. Want to swim? The skipper will find a calm spot. Want to just cruise and take photos of the coastline? Fine. Your call.

Departure Port: Puerto Marina Benalmadena

Puerto Marina Benalmadena is a purpose-built leisure marina with over 900 berths. It's not as flashy as Puerto Banus, but it's more relaxed and the facilities are solid. There are plenty of bars and restaurants right on the waterfront, so you can grab a coffee or a beer before you board. The marina has a fuel dock, a chandlery, and a small supermarket for last-minute supplies.

Parking is available in the underground car park (pay and display) and there are taxi ranks and bus stops nearby. If you're flying into Malaga Airport, it's about a 15-minute drive. The marina is also a short walk from the Benalmadena cable car and the Selwo Marina dolphinarium, so you can make a day of it if you arrive early.

From the marina, you can head east towards Malaga or west towards Fuengirola and Marbella. The coastline here is mostly built-up, but once you're a mile offshore, the views of the mountains and the sea are excellent. On a clear day, you can see the Atlas Mountains of Morocco.

Boat Options for Your Benalmadena Yacht Charter

You've got a few choices the type of boat. The most popular for groups is a motor yacht between 30 and 50 feet. These have a cabin, a toilet, a small galley, and plenty of deck space. A 40-foot motor yacht will comfortably take 8 to 10 guests for a day trip. If you want something more stable and with more shade, a catamaran is a good bet. Catamarans have a wide beam, so they don't roll as much, and they have a large trampoline net at the front where you can lie down.

Sailing yachts are available too, but they're slower and require more wind. If you're after a relaxing, quiet cruise, a sailing yacht is fine. But if you want to cover distance or have a set itinerary, go for a motor yacht. For smaller groups of 2 to 6 people, a RIB or speedboat is a fun option. They're fast, open, and you can get to places like Cala del Faro or the Nerja caves in less time. Just be aware that RIBs have limited shade and no toilet.

Fishing boats are also available if you want to try your luck at catching sea bass or bream. And if you have a larger budget, there are a few superyachts based in Benalmadena that can take 12 or more guests. Prices on request for those.

Pricing Guidance for a Yacht Charter in Benalmadena

Prices vary depending on the boat size, age, season, and duration. For a half-day (4 hours) on a 30-foot motor yacht, expect to pay from around EUR 450 to EUR 700. A full day (8 hours) on the same boat will be EUR 800 to EUR 1,200. Catamarans are usually more expensive because they're bigger and more stable. A 40-foot catamaran for a half-day starts at around EUR 1,000, and a full day from EUR 1,500.

RIB charters are cheaper. A 2-hour speedboat ride with a skipper can cost from EUR 250 to EUR 400. Fishing charters are similar, with half-day trips from EUR 350 to EUR 600. Superyachts are in a different league entirely: a day on a 70-foot yacht can cost EUR 3,000 to EUR 6,000 or more.

All prices are per boat, not per person. So if you have a group of 10, splitting the cost makes it very reasonable. The peak season is July and August, when prices can be 20-30% higher than in May or September. If you can, book in late spring or early autumn for better rates and fewer crowds.

Best Time of Year for a Benalmadena Yacht Charter

The Costa del Sol has a Mediterranean climate, so the sailing season runs from April to October. July and August are the hottest months, with average temperatures of 30 degrees C and sea temperatures around 24 degrees C. The water is warm enough for swimming from June to September. If you want to avoid the peak crowds, May and June are excellent, with long daylight hours and fewer boats on the water.

September and October are also good, but the sea can get a bit choppier as the weather changes. The wind in Benalmadena is usually light to moderate, which is ideal for motor yachts. Sailing yachts might struggle in July when the wind drops. The best time for dolphin spotting is early morning, when the sea is calm and the dolphins are feeding.

Winter charters are possible but less common. The water is cold (around 16 degrees C) and the days are shorter. Some boats still operate for special events or private parties, but most of the fleet is laid up for maintenance from November to March.

Popular Itineraries from Benalmadena Marina

A typical half-day charter from Benalmadena heads west along the coast towards Fuengirola and Mijas Costa. You'll pass the beaches of Carvajal and Torreblanca, and you can stop for a swim near the Cala del Faro lighthouse. The water there is clear and there's usually a bit of shelter from the wind. If you want to see dolphins, the skipper will head further out towards the Strait of Gibraltar. The resident pod of common dolphins is often spotted between Benalmadena and Marbella.

For a full-day charter, you can go all the way to Marbella and Puerto Banus, which takes about 1.5 hours each way at cruising speed. That gives you 4-5 hours to explore the port, have lunch, and swim. Alternatively, head east towards Malaga city and the Nerja caves. The caves are a bit far for a day trip (about 2.5 hours from Benalmadena), but you can combine the cruise with a stop at the beach of Maro or the cliffs of Cala del Faro.

If you're after a party vibe, a sunset charter is a solid choice. You leave around 5pm, cruise for an hour, drop anchor for a swim, and then watch the sun go down over the Mediterranean. The skipper will bring you back to the marina by 9pm or so, and you can head straight to one of the marina's restaurants for dinner.

Who Is a Benalmadena Yacht Charter For

This is for anyone who wants a private boat experience without the stress of owning or operating a boat. Families with kids love it because the skipper handles safety and the kids can swim and play. Groups of friends use it for birthdays, stag or hen parties (within reason), or just a day out. Couples sometimes book a private charter for a proposal or anniversary. It's also popular with remote workers and digital nomads who want a change of scenery for a day.

If you're a corporate group, a yacht charter is a good way to impress clients or reward your team. Some boats have Wi-Fi and can host small meetings. Just be aware that mobile reception can be patchy once you're a few miles offshore.

One thing to note: this is not a booze cruise. The skipper is professional and will not tolerate excessive drunkenness or dangerous behaviour. You can bring alcohol, but the skipper has the right to cut the trip short if anyone becomes a risk to themselves or others. So keep it civil.

How to Book a Yacht Charter in Benalmadena

Head to the search page on BoatHire24 and select Benalmadena as your departure port. You'll see a list of available boats with photos, specs, and prices. Pick the one that suits your group size and budget, then select your date and duration. You can book online and pay by card. The booking includes a deposit (usually 30-50%) and the balance is due 7 days before departure.

If you're not sure what you need, you can contact us and we'll recommend a boat based on your group size and preferences. We don't charge extra for advice. Just be clear about what you want: number of guests, preferred boat type, and any special requests like catering or a specific itinerary.

Cancellation policies vary by boat, but most offer a full refund if you cancel 14 days before the charter. Within 14 days, you may lose the deposit or get a partial refund. Check the terms before you book.

For more tips on planning your trip, check out our blog for destination guides and packing lists.

Why Choose Benalmadena Over Puerto Banus or Marbella

Benalmadena is often cheaper than Puerto Banus for a yacht charter. The marina fees are lower, so charter companies pass that saving on to you. A 40-foot motor yacht that costs EUR 1,200 for a full day in Benalmadena might be EUR 1,500 or more from Puerto Banus. You also avoid the traffic and parking chaos of Banus in August. The downside is that Benalmadena lacks the glitz of Banus, but if you care more about the sea than the Instagram backdrop, it's a better deal. The coastline from Benalmadena is less crowded, and you'll have more anchorages to yourself.

Licence-Free Day Boats: A Cheaper Alternative

If you're on a tight budget, some Benalmadena charters offer licence-free day boats. These are small motorboats under 15 feet that you can drive yourself without a licence, provided you stay within a defined coastal zone (usually within 2 NM of the shore). The skipper is not included, so you're on your own. Prices start from around EUR 150 for a half-day. But be careful: the rental company will give you a quick briefing, but you need to be confident handling a small boat in light winds and currents. The sea can get choppy in the afternoon, so go in the morning when it's calm. These boats have no toilet and minimal shade, so bring a hat and sunscreen.

Food and Drink Options on Your Charter

Most Benalmadena yacht charters let you bring your own food and drink. There's a Carrefour Express and a Lidl within 5 minutes of the marina. Stock up on snacks, water, and soft drinks. If you want a proper meal, ask the skipper to stop at a chiringuito like El Tintero in Malaga or La Luna in Fuengirola. These beach restaurants serve fresh grilled fish and paella. The skipper will anchor offshore and the restaurant will send a small boat to pick you up. It's a nice touch. Alternatively, some charters offer a catering package for around EUR 25-40 per person, which includes a cold buffet of Spanish ham, cheese, bread, and salad. Alcohol is not included in the catering, but you can bring your own wine or beer.

What to Pack for a Benalmadena Yacht Charter

You need a swimsuit, a towel, and sunscreen (factor 50, the sun is strong even in May). Bring a light jacket or a windbreaker because the sea breeze can be cool, especially in the evening. A hat and sunglasses are essential. If you plan to swim, bring a snorkel and mask if you have one. The water clarity is good along this coast, and you might see bream or sea bass near the rocks. Don't forget seasickness tablets if you're prone to motion sickness. The Mediterranean is usually calm, but the swell can pick up in the afternoon. Take one 30 minutes before departure. Leave valuables at home or in a waterproof bag. The boat will have a dry cabin, but it's not a safe.

Environmental Rules and Responsible Chartering

Benalmadena is part of the Costa del Sol, which has strict environmental regulations. You cannot throw anything overboard, including cigarette butts or food waste. The skipper will have a bin bag for rubbish. Anchoring is prohibited in seagrass meadows (Posidonia oceanica), which are protected. The skipper will know where to anchor. If you see a sea turtle or a dolphin, keep a distance of at least 60 metres. Do not feed the wildlife. Some charters offer eco-friendly options, like biodegradable sunscreen and reusable water bottles. Ask the charter company if they support any local conservation projects. It's not a big deal, but it's good to know.

Last-Minute and Discount Charters in Benalmadena

If you're flexible, you can often get a discount on a Benalmadena yacht charter by booking 2-3 days in advance. Some boats have empty slots and would rather sail with a reduced price than stay in the marina. Check the BoatHire24 search page for last-minute deals. You might find a 35-foot motor yacht for EUR 350 instead of EUR 600. The catch is that you have to be ready to go at short notice, and the itinerary might be limited to a half-day. Also, early June and late September are the cheapest months, with prices 15-20% lower than July. If you book a full week in advance, you can sometimes negotiate a better rate for a full-day charter.

faqs.q: Do I need a boating licence for a yacht charter in Benalmadena?
faqs.a: No. Every charter includes a licensed skipper who handles the boat. You just relax.

faqs.q: Can I bring my own food and drink?
faqs.a: Yes. Most boats have a cooler or fridge. You can also ask the skipper to stop at a beachside restaurant.

faqs.q: How many people can fit on a Benalmadena yacht charter?
faqs.a: It depends on the boat. A 40-foot motor yacht typically takes up to 10 guests. Catamarans can take 12 or more.

faqs.a: The skipper will decide if it's safe to sail. If the charter is cancelled due to weather, you get a full refund or a reschedule.

faqs.a: Most motor yachts and catamarans have a marine toilet. RIBs and small speedboats usually do not.

faqs.a: Yes. Many boats can be decorated or arranged for special events. Contact us for details.

faqs.q: How far in advance should I book?
faqs.a: For summer months, book at least 2 weeks ahead. For other seasons, 1 week is usually enough.

title: Motor Yacht Hire Marbella: Power Options
metaDescription: Find motor yacht hire in Marbella with skipper included. Compare power options from Puerto Banús, Estepona & more. Book your luxury day charter today.
h1: Motor Yacht Hire Marbella: Power Options & Skipper Included
intro:
If you want to cover more coastline in less time, motor yacht hire in Marbella is the straightforward answer. A decent power yacht will cruise at 20-25 knots, which means you can zip from Puerto Banús to Estepona in under 30 minutes, or head out to the Strait of Gibraltar to spot dolphins and still be back for a late lunch. Every boat on BoatHire24 comes with a licensed skipper, so you don't need a licence or any experience. You just turn up, decide on a route, and let someone else handle the navigation while you work on your tan.

Motor yachts here range from 30-foot day cruisers that sleep four to 80-foot flybridge monsters with Jacuzzis and a crew of three. The common thread is power: inboard diesels, outboards or sterndrives, all pushing you across the Mediterranean at a speed that makes a sailing yacht feel like a rowing boat. This page covers what you actually get, which ports you can leave from, what it costs, and who should bother.
bodyHtml:
What You Get With Motor Yacht Hire in Marbella

Every motor yacht charter on BoatHire24 includes a professional skipper. That is not optional. It is Spanish law for vessels over a certain size, and even for smaller boats, the insurance usually demands it. The skipper knows the local waters, the anchor spots, and where the sandbanks are off Cabopino. They also handle the mooring, which saves you the embarrassment of scraping the gelcoat on a neighbour's RIB.

Most motor yachts come with a host or stewardess on the larger models (40 feet plus). Their job is to serve drinks, prepare the food you bring, and keep the interior tidy. On smaller day cruisers, you get the skipper only, and you handle your own catering. Fuel is not included in the base price. Expect to pay around EUR 200-400 for a day's cruising, depending on distance and speed. Some operators include a fuel allowance; others charge strictly what you burn. Ask before you book.

Typical inclusions: towels, snorkelling gear, paddleboard, Bluetooth speaker, fridge, ice, and a bimini top for shade. Air conditioning works only when the engines are running or the generator is on, which is usually fine because you are outside most of the day. Check the listing for specifics.

Departure Ports for Motor Yacht Charters

Your starting point determines how much of the coast you can see in a day. The main ports are:

Puerto Banús, The obvious choice. Glitzy, full of superyachts, and 10 minutes from Marbella town. From here you can head west to the Cala del Faro for a swim, or east to Cabopino and the dunes. The port itself is a spectacle, but parking is a nightmare and the restaurants are overpriced. You have been warned.

Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo), Quieter than Banús, closer to the old town, and easier to park. Good for a relaxed departure. The marina has a decent chiringuito for pre-boat beers.

Cabopino, Small, sandy, and less crowded. The channel is shallow, so only smaller motor yachts (up to 40 feet) can berth here. Ideal if you want to head straight to the Cabopino dunes or the Cala del Moral.

Estepona, The western gateway. From Estepona marina you can reach the Strait of Gibraltar in about 40 minutes at 20 knots. The old town is worth a visit after the charter.

Sotogrande, Exclusive, residential, and a bit quiet. Good for golf groups who want to combine a day on the water with a round at Valderrama. The marina is deep enough for large motor yachts.

Benalmádena, East of Marbella, closer to Málaga airport. A practical choice if you are staying on the Costa del Sol's eastern side. The marina has a large charter fleet.

Motor Yacht Types and Sizes

Not all motor yachts are the same. Here is the breakdown:

Day cruisers (30-40 feet), Open bow rider or cuddy cabin layouts. Speeds of 25-30 knots. Suitable for 6-8 guests. Examples: Jeanneau Leader, Sea Ray Sundancer. Price from around EUR 800 for a half day.

Flybridge motor yachts (40-60 feet), The most popular charter category. Upper helm station, large saloon, cabins below. Speeds of 20-25 knots. Sleeps 4-8. Examples: Princess V58, Azimut 55. Price from around EUR 1,500 for a full day.

Sport cruisers (35-50 feet), Sleeker, faster, often with a single-level deck. Good for a younger crowd who want speed and style. Examples: Pershing 45, Riva Aquariva. Price from around EUR 2,000 for a full day.

Superyachts (80 feet plus), Crew of 3-5, Jacuzzis, jet skis, full catering. Prices on request, but expect EUR 10,000-plus per day. Available from Puerto Banús only.

Pricing Guidance for Motor Yacht Hire Marbella

Prices vary by season, boat size, and duration. Here is a rough guide:

  • Half day (4 hours): EUR 600-1,500 for a 30-40 footer; EUR 1,500-3,000 for 40-60 feet.
  • Full day (8 hours): EUR 1,000-2,500 for 30-40 feet; EUR 2,500-5,000 for 40-60 feet.
  • Full day superyacht: from EUR 10,000, price on request.

These are bare-boat charter rates with skipper. Add food, drinks, fuel (EUR 200-600), and a tip for the crew (10-15%). Some operators include soft drinks and water. Check the listing details.

High season (July-August) sees prices spike 30-50%. Low season (November-February) can be 20% cheaper. May, June, September, and October offer the best balance of weather and price.

Best Routes for a Motor Yacht Day Charter

With a motor yacht, you can cover distance. Here are three popular itineraries:

Route 1: Puerto Banús to Cala del Faro and back, 20 NM round trip. Cala del Faro is a rocky cove with crystal water, popular for anchoring. Swim, lunch, then a slow cruise back past the Marbella skyline. Total time: 4-5 hours.

Route 2: Estepona to the Strait of Gibraltar, 40 NM each way. The Strait is where the Atlantic meets the Mediterranean, and the currents bring plankton, which attracts dolphins and sometimes whales. You need a calm day for this. Total time: 6-8 hours.

Route 3: Marbella to Nerja, 50 NM one way. Nerja has the famous caves and a lovely beach. The journey takes you past Cabopino, Calahonda, and the cliffs of the Costa Tropical. Long day, but worth it.

Seasons and Weather for Motor Yacht Charters

The Costa del Sol has over 300 sunny days a year, but the sea is not always flat. The Levante wind (easterly) can kick up a chop, especially around Gibraltar. The Poniente (westerly) is lighter and more comfortable.

Summer (June-September), Water temperature 22-26 degrees C. Calm seas 80% of the time. Book ahead; charters sell out weeks in advance.

Spring and autumn (April-May, October-November), Air temperature 18-25 degrees C. Water cooler (16-20 degrees C). Fewer crowds, lower prices. Still good for cruising, but take a jacket.

Winter (December-March), Many boats are out of the water for maintenance. Some operators run charters on request. Air temperature 12-18 degrees C. Not ideal for swimming, but fine for a lunch cruise.

How to Book Your Motor Yacht Hire Marbella

Use the search page on BoatHire24 to filter by boat type, size, port, and price. Each listing shows the boat, photos, availability calendar, and the operator's contact details. You can book directly through the site or request a quote for larger yachts.

When you book, you will need to provide a deposit (usually 30-50%) to secure the date. The balance is due 7-14 days before the charter. Cancellation policies vary: some operators offer full refunds up to 14 days before, others are stricter. Read the terms.

On the day, you meet the skipper at the port 15 minutes before departure. They will brief you on safety, the route, and what is on board. Bring sunscreen, a hat, and a swimsuit. Leave your high heels on the dock (they damage the teak).

Who Should Hire a Motor Yacht in Marbella

Motor yacht hire is for anyone who wants to cover ground without effort. It suits:

  • Groups of 6-12 friends who want a party boat with music and drinks.
  • Families with children who want to swim in different coves without long sailing trips.
  • Corporate groups hosting clients or team-building days.
  • Couples celebrating an anniversary or engagement.

If you want a quiet, slow, wind-powered experience, consider a sailing yacht charter instead. If you want to drive the boat yourself without a licence, look at licence-free boat hire.

Why Book Through BoatHire24

BoatHire24 is a marketplace, not a charter company. We list boats from multiple operators, so you can compare prices, sizes, and availability in one place. Every listing is verified, and we provide direct contact with the operator. No middleman markups. You book with the actual boat owner or charter company.

We also publish a blog with local tips, weather guides, and port reviews to help you plan your trip. Check it out before you book.

For more reading, see our guide to catamaran hire in Marbella or the Puerto Banús boat hire page.

Fuel Costs and Consumption for Motor Yacht Hire Marbella

Fuel is the variable that catches people out. A 40-foot motor yacht burns roughly 80-120 litres per hour at cruising speed (20-25 knots). At current prices around EUR 1.50 per litre for diesel, that is EUR 120-180 per hour. A full day covering 40-50 nautical miles might cost EUR 400-600 in fuel alone. Smaller day cruisers (30-35 feet) with petrol engines burn less, around 50-70 litres per hour, but petrol is more expensive, about EUR 1.70 per litre. Some operators offer a fuel-inclusive package, adding EUR 200-300 to the charter fee. Others ask you to pay the actual cost after the trip. Ask the operator for an estimated fuel bill before you confirm the booking. If you plan to anchor for lunch and swim for two hours with the engine off, you save a chunk of cash.

Motor Yacht Hire for Large Groups: Capacity and Comfort

A 50-foot flybridge motor yacht typically has a maximum capacity of 12 guests, including the skipper and crew. That is a legal limit set by the boat's safety certificate, not a suggestion. If you have 14 people, you need a bigger boat or a second vessel. For groups of 10-12, look for yachts with a large aft deck and a spacious saloon. The Princess V58 or Azimut 55 have decent layouts for this. For 16-20 guests, you need a superyacht or a catamaran (which has more deck space). Check the listing for the "maximum guests" figure. If the boat says 12, do not try to squeeze 13 on board. The skipper will refuse to leave the port, and you lose your deposit.

Motor Yacht Hire from Estepona: The Western Option

Estepona marina is 15 minutes west of Puerto Banús by car, but the cruising time to the same spots is shorter because you start further west. From Estepona, you can reach the Strait of Gibraltar in 30-40 minutes at 20 knots. The Strait is a prime dolphin-watching area, with common dolphins, striped dolphins, and sometimes pilot whales. The water is deeper and greener here, and the current can be strong. Skippers know the safe channels. Estepona also has a well-preserved old town with flower-filled streets and a good selection of tapas bars for after the charter. The marina itself is modern, with plenty of parking and a few decent restaurants. Motor yachts available here range from 30-foot day cruisers to 50-foot flybridges. Prices are slightly lower than Banús because the location is less flashy.

Motor Yacht Hire in Winter: What You Need to Know

Winter charters (December to February) are possible but limited. Air temperatures average 12-18 degrees C, and sea temperatures drop to 14-16 degrees C. Swimming is cold but possible with a wetsuit. Many motor yachts are lifted out of the water for annual maintenance between November and March, so the available fleet is smaller. Some operators keep a few boats in the water for winter charters, usually flybridge models with enclosed saloons and heating. These are good for a lunch cruise with a glass of cava and a view of the snow-capped La Concha mountain. Prices are 20-30% lower than summer. Book through the BoatHire24 search page and filter by availability. Expect to pay from around EUR 800 for a half-day winter charter on a 40-footer.

Motor Yacht Hire for Fishing Trips

Some motor yachts are set up for fishing, with rod holders, a fish finder, and a bait station. The Costa del Sol has good fishing grounds for bream, sea bass, and amberjack closer to shore, and tuna, dorado, and swordfish further out. A 40-foot motor yacht can reach the deep-water spots 10-15 nautical miles offshore in under an hour. Fishing charters usually include the skipper, who knows the local marks, plus basic tackle. You bring your own bait or buy it at the port. Prices are similar to standard charters, around EUR 1,200-1,800 for a full day. Check the listing to confirm the boat is equipped for fishing. Some operators specialise in fishing and offer half-day trips focused on trolling for tuna. The best months for offshore fishing are June to October.

Motor Yacht Hire for Sunset Cruises

A sunset cruise on a motor yacht is a popular option for couples and small groups. The typical itinerary is a 2-3 hour trip leaving around 18:00 in summer (sunset at 21:30) or 16:00 in winter (sunset at 18:00). You cruise west towards the Strait of Gibraltar, watching the sun drop behind the mountains. The best views are from the flybridge, with a glass of wine and the engine ticking over at idle speed. Prices for sunset cruises are lower than full-day charters, from around EUR 500-800 for a 30-40 footer. Some operators include a bottle of cava and snacks. The boat returns to port in the twilight, which is a nice end to the day. Book in advance, especially in summer, because sunset slots are limited.

Motor Yacht Hire for Weddings and Proposals

Marbella is a popular wedding destination, and motor yachts are used for reception cruises, pre-wedding parties, or private proposal settings. A 50-foot motor yacht can host a small wedding ceremony on the aft deck, with a captain officiating (if they have the licence). The boat then cruises along the coast while guests enjoy drinks and canapés. For proposals, the skipper can anchor in a quiet cove like Cala del Faro, where you pop the question on the bow at sunset. Costs depend on the boat size and duration. A 4-hour charter for a proposal on a 40-footer costs around EUR 1,200-1,800. A wedding reception for 20 guests on a 60-footer can be EUR 4,000-6,000 for a half day. Confirm with the operator that they allow events and whether they provide catering or you need to arrange it separately.

Motor Yacht Hire with Jet Skis and Water Toys

Some motor yachts come with a jet ski or a tender stored on the swim platform. This is common on superyachts and larger flybridge models (60 feet plus). The jet ski adds fun for guests who want to ride around the bay while the yacht is anchored. The skipper will brief you on safety and the permitted areas. Jet skis are not allowed close to swimmers or within 200 metres of the shore in some zones. Rental of the jet ski is usually extra, around EUR 200-300 per hour. Smaller motor yachts (30-50 feet) often include a paddleboard and snorkel gear instead. If water toys are important to you, filter the search on BoatHire24 for listings that mention "jet ski" or "water toys".

Motor Yacht Hire and Insurance: What Is Covered

Every motor yacht listed on BoatHire24 has insurance that covers the vessel and third-party liability. Your personal belongings are not covered by the boat's insurance. If you drop your phone overboard, that is your problem. Some operators offer a damage waiver for an extra fee, which reduces your liability if you damage the boat (e.g., scratching the gelcoat on a dock). The standard deposit is EUR 500-2,000, depending on the boat's value. This is returned after the charter if there is no damage. Read the terms on the listing page before paying the deposit. If the boat is involved in an accident due to the skipper's error, you are not liable. The skipper is a professional and their actions are covered by the operator's insurance.

Related guides

  • Luxury Boat Rental Marbella: Premium Fleet with Skipper
  • Speedboat Charter Marbella: Fast Adventures on the Costa del Sol
  • Yacht Rental Benalmadena: Marina Charters
  • Boat Hire Fuengirola Port: Easy Booking with Skipper
  • Speedboat Hire Benalmadena: Fast Fun on the Costa del Sol

faqs.q: Do I need a licence to hire a motor yacht in Marbella?
faqs.a: No. Every motor yacht charter on BoatHire24 includes a licensed skipper. You do not need any licence or experience. Just turn up and enjoy the ride.

faqs.q: What is the average price for a motor yacht charter in Marbella?
faqs.a: Half-day charters start from around EUR 600 for a 30-foot boat. Full-day charters for a 40-60 foot motor yacht range from EUR 2,500 to EUR 5,000 in high season. Superyachts are price on request.

faqs.q: Can I choose my own departure port?
faqs.a: Yes. You can depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona, Sotogrande, or Benalmádena. The boat's location determines which ports are available for that vessel.

faqs.q: Is fuel included in the charter price?
faqs.a: Generally no. Fuel is extra and costs around EUR 200-600 for a day's cruising, depending on distance and boat size. Some operators include a fuel allowance; check the listing details.

faqs.q: What is the best time of year for a motor yacht charter?
faqs.a: June to September offers the warmest water and calmest seas, but prices are highest. May, June, and September-October give good weather with fewer crowds and lower rates.

faqs.q: How many people can fit on a motor yacht?
faqs.a: Day cruisers (30-40 feet) typically take 6-8 guests. Flybridge yachts (40-60 feet) take 8-12. Superyachts can take 12-20. Check the boat's maximum capacity on the listing.

faqs.q: Can I cancel my booking?
faqs.a: Cancellation policies vary by operator. Most offer a full refund if you cancel at least 14 days before the charter. Some require 30 days' notice for larger yachts. Read the terms before booking.

title: Catamaran Charter Fuengirola: Relaxed Sailing
metaDescription: Book a catamaran charter Fuengirola for a relaxed day on the Costa del Sol. Spacious deck, skipper included, sunbathing and swimming. Check prices and availability.
h1: Catamaran Charter Fuengirola: Relaxed Sailing
intro:
If you want a day on the water without the heave and roll of a monohull, a catamaran charter Fuengirola is the obvious answer. Catamarans are stable, roomy, and built for lounging. You get two hulls, a wide trampoline net at the front, and enough deck space to spread out with a book or a cold drink. No skipper license required, every charter includes a professional captain who handles the navigation while you do the relaxing.

Fuengirola sits right on the Costa del Sol, about 30 minutes east of Marbella. Its marina, Puerto Deportivo de Fuengirola, is a working harbour with a few restaurants and a handy car park. From here, you can head west toward Cabopino and Marbella, or east toward Benalmádena and the open bay of Málaga. The water is calm most of the year, and the prevailing winds are light (10-15 knots), ideal for a catamaran’s twin engines or a lazy sail.
bodyHtml:
What a Catamaran Charter Fuengirola Includes

Every catamaran charter through BoatHire24 comes with a licensed skipper as standard. That means you do not need a boating licence or any experience. The skipper handles the mooring, the safety briefing, and the route. You just choose where you want to go and whether you want to swim, eat, or sleep on deck.

The boats we list range from 38 to 50 feet, with capacities between 8 and 12 guests. Typical features include a flybridge with sun pads, a shaded cockpit with a table, a bow trampoline, and a galley with a fridge and hob. Most have a Bluetooth speaker system, a freshwater shower on the swim platform, and a below-deck cabin with a toilet and a small berth for changing or resting. Fuel, port fees, and VAT are usually included in the advertised price, but check the listing details because some operators add a fuel surcharge for longer trips.

You can also add extras such as a paddleboard, snorkel gear, a jet ski tow, or a catering package with local seafood and Spanish wine. Those are optional and priced separately, typically EUR 50-150 per item.

Departure Ports for Your Catamaran Charter Fuengirola

Fuengirola’s own marina, Puerto Deportivo de Fuengirola, is the most convenient starting point if you are staying in the town itself. It has 300 berths, a fuel dock, and a small beach right next to the breakwater. From here, a typical half-day trip heads west toward the dunes of Cabopino or east toward the Benalmádena skyline. The full-day options can reach Marbella’s Puerto Banús in about 45 minutes at cruising speed (15-20 knots).

If you are based in Marbella or Estepona, you can also book a catamaran charter that departs from any of the other ports we cover: Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona, Sotogrande, or Benalmádena. The boats are the same, but the cruising grounds change. From Puerto Banús you get the glitz of the Golden Mile and the cliffs of the Costa del Sol. From Estepona you get the quieter beaches and the views of the Sierra Bermeja mountains. From Sotogrande you can head toward Gibraltar and watch the tankers queue in the Strait.

Boat Options for a Catamaran Charter Fuengirola

The catamarans available through our marketplace are mostly production models from Lagoon, Fountaine Pajot, and Bali. These are the workhorses of the charter industry: reliable, spacious, and easy to handle. A typical Lagoon 42 has four double cabins, two heads, and a large saloon with full standing headroom. On deck, you get a forward trampoline big enough for four adults to lie down, a flybridge helm station with a bimini, and a stern platform with a ladder for swimming.

If you want something sportier, there are a few smaller catamarans like the Bali 4.0 or the Fountaine Pajot Lucia 40. These are 40-footers with a more open layout, a hardtop roof, and a wider beam. They still have at least three cabins and a decent galley, but they feel more modern and airy. For larger groups, we can source a Lagoon 52 or a Privilege 585, both of which sleep up to 12 guests plus crew. Those are price on request, but expect EUR 3,000-6,000 per day depending on the season.

You can also find RIBs and motor yachts on the site, but for this specific page we focus on catamarans. If you want a faster, more adrenaline-filled ride, check the speedboat and RIB options. If you want a classic sailing experience, look at the sailing yachts. But for pure relaxation and social space, a catamaran is the better bet.

Pricing Guidance for a Catamaran Charter Fuengirola

Prices for a catamaran charter Fuengirola vary by season, boat size, and duration. As a rough guide, a half-day (4 hours) on a 40-foot catamaran costs from around EUR 800 in low season (November to March) and from around EUR 1,200 in high season (June to September). A full day (8 hours) on the same boat runs from EUR 1,500 to EUR 2,500. These figures include the skipper, fuel, and standard equipment. They exclude food, drinks, and extras like jet skis or paddleboards.

For a larger catamaran (50 feet or more), the price jumps to EUR 2,500-4,000 for a full day in high season. Superyacht catamarans, which are rare on the Costa del Sol, are price on request and usually require a minimum of three days. You can filter by budget on the search page to see live availability.

We do not list exact prices per boat because owners update their rates each season. The search results show the current price for your selected date and group size. Book early for July and August because those months sell out by May.

Best Seasons for a Catamaran Charter Fuengirola

The Costa del Sol has a Mediterranean climate with mild winters and hot summers. The best months for a catamaran charter are May, June, September, and October. In those months, the air temperature is 22-28 degrees C, the water is 18-24 degrees C, and the wind is usually a gentle thermal breeze (8-15 knots). July and August are hotter (30-35 degrees C) and busier, but the water is warmest (24-26 degrees C) and the evenings are long. Winter charters (November to March) are cheaper but riskier: the wind can pick up to 25 knots, and the sea state can be choppy. That said, a calm winter day with 18 degrees C and clear skies is still pleasant if you wear a jacket.

If you are interested in wildlife, the best time to see dolphins and whales is from April to October. The Strait of Gibraltar, which you can reach from Sotogrande or Estepona, is a migration corridor for pilot whales, orcas, and common dolphins. From Fuengirola, you are more likely to see bottlenose dolphins and the occasional sea turtle near the Cabopino dunes.

How to Book Your Catamaran Charter Fuengirola

Booking through BoatHire24 is straightforward. You search by date, group size, and port. The results show available catamarans with photos, specs, and the total price including skipper and taxes. You select your boat, choose any extras, and pay a deposit (usually 30-50%) to secure the slot. The balance is due 7 days before departure. Cancellations are refundable up to 14 days before the charter, minus a small processing fee.

If you are unsure which boat to pick, use the contact form on the listing page. The owner or the charter manager will answer within a few hours. They can also suggest a route: for example, a Fuengirola to Cabopino loop with a swim stop at the Cala del Faro beach, followed by lunch at a chiringuito on the sand. Or a longer trip to Marbella with a view of La Concha mountain from the water.

We do not charge a booking fee. The price you see is the price you pay, minus the deposit. Payment is by credit card or bank transfer, and you receive a confirmation email with the skipper’s contact details and the meeting point at the marina.

Who Is a Catamaran Charter Fuengirola For

A catamaran charter is a good fit for families with children, groups of friends, and anyone who gets seasick on monohulls. The stability of two hulls means you can walk around without holding on, eat a full meal without spilling, and sit on the trampoline without feeling the roll. It is also a popular choice for birthday parties, bachelor/bachelorette groups, and corporate days out. The deck space allows for catering, music, and games.

If you are a solo traveller or a couple, a catamaran might feel too large unless you book a shared charter. Shared catamarans are rare on our platform, but some owners offer individual berths on longer trips. For a private charter with two people, a smaller sailing yacht or a RIB is more cost-effective.

For more tips on planning your day on the water, read our blog about the best anchorages on the Costa del Sol and what to pack for a catamaran trip.

Why Fuengirola Works as a Catamaran Base

Fuengirola sits roughly halfway between Málaga and Marbella, which makes it a practical hub for a catamaran charter. The marina is a 20-minute drive from Málaga airport, and the train station is a 10-minute walk from the port. You can fly in from the UK, take the Cercanías train from the airport to Fuengirola (EUR 2.80, 30 minutes), and be on the boat by 10am. That ease of access is why many charter operators base their catamarans here rather than in the more expensive marinas further west.

The coastline east of Fuengirola is less developed than the Marbella strip. You get the Cala del Moral and the Cala del Faro coves, which are quieter than the beaches in town. The water is clearer there because there is less sand runoff. If you head west, you pass the Torreblanca cliffs and the Benalmádena cable car before reaching the open bay of Málaga. That variety of scenery in a single day trip is hard to beat from any other port on the coast.

Specific Routes for a Catamaran Charter Fuengirola

A half-day route from Fuengirola that works well is the eastern loop: leave the marina, turn left, and cruise past the Castillo Sohail castle. Stop at the Cala del Faro for a swim in 6-8 metres of water. The cove has a small pebble beach and a rocky outcrop that blocks the swell. After 30 minutes, motor back toward the marina and anchor off the Fuengirola beach for lunch at a chiringuito that sends a dinghy to collect you. The total distance is about 8 nautical miles, which takes 2 hours of cruising and leaves 2 hours for swimming and eating.

For a full day, the western route to Cabopino is better. From Fuengirola, head west past the Benalmádena marina and the Torre del Quebrado watchtower. Cabopino is 10 nautical miles away, about 40 minutes at cruising speed. Anchor in the sandy area east of the dunes, where the water is shallow (2-4 metres) and the bottom is sand. You can swim to the shore and walk to the beach bars. After lunch, continue west to the Cala de las Golondrinas, a small cove with a concrete jetty and a seafood restaurant. The return trip takes you past the Puerto Marina Benalmádena, which is worth a slow pass for the architecture.

Catamaran Charter Fuengirola for Sunset and Evening Trips

Sunset charters are a popular option on the Costa del Sol, and Fuengirola is well positioned for them. The sun sets behind the Sierra de Mijas mountains, which turn orange and red for about 20 minutes. A typical sunset charter runs from 6pm to 9pm in summer, or 4pm to 7pm in winter. The boat anchors off the Fuengirola beach, and you watch the lights come on along the promenade. The price for a sunset catamaran charter is usually the same as a half-day rate, because the duration is similar. Some operators offer a reduced rate for evening trips, around EUR 600-900 for a 40-foot catamaran in low season.

Evening charters that include dinner are also available. The skipper moors at a marina restaurant, or the crew prepare a barbecue on the boat. You need to arrange this in advance, and it adds EUR 30-50 per person for the food and drinks. The boat returns to port by 11pm, which is the last time the marina gate is open without a fee.

What to Pack for a Catamaran Charter Fuengirola

Packing for a catamaran charter is straightforward, but a few items make the difference. Sunscreen with SPF 50 is essential because the reflection off the water intensifies the UV. A rash vest or a light long-sleeved shirt protects your shoulders and back. A towel that dries fast, not a heavy cotton one, is better because the salt water soaks it and it stays damp. A waterproof phone pouch or a dry bag keeps your phone and wallet safe when you swim.

If you are prone to seasickness, take a motion sickness tablet an hour before departure. Catamarans are stable, but the anchor swell can cause a gentle rocking that some people find unsettling. A hat with a strap, polarised sunglasses, and a light jacket for the evening wind are also useful. The boat has a fridge, so you can bring your own drinks and snacks. Most operators allow you to bring alcohol, but they charge a corkage fee if you want the crew to serve it.

Catamaran Charter Fuengirola for Special Occasions

Catamaran charters from Fuengirola are often booked for birthdays, anniversaries, and proposals. The open deck space allows for decorations, a photographer, and a small ceremony. Some operators offer a dedicated event package that includes a cake, a bottle of cava, and a playlist. The cost is around EUR 200 extra on top of the charter fee. For a proposal, the skipper can anchor at a scenic spot like the Cala del Faro at sunset, and the crew can set up a table with flowers and champagne on the bow trampoline.

Corporate events are also common. Companies book a catamaran for team-building days, client entertainment, or product launches. The boat can accommodate up to 12 guests, which is a good size for a small team. The skipper stays out of the way during meetings, and the Wi-Fi on board (most catamarans have 4G routers) allows for calls and emails. The cost for a corporate charter is the same as a private charter, but you can claim VAT back if the company is registered in Spain.

Catamaran Charter Fuengirola and the Local Marine Life

The waters off Fuengirola are part of the Alborán Sea, which is the westernmost basin of the Mediterranean. The nutrient-rich currents from the Atlantic attract fish and marine mammals. On a catamaran charter, you are likely to see common bottlenose dolphins, especially near the Cabopino dunes and the entrance to the Fuengirola marina. The dolphins feed on sardines and anchovies, which school in the shallow waters during the summer. If you are lucky, you might see a loggerhead sea turtle, which surfaces to breathe every 15-20 minutes.

For a more reliable dolphin sighting, book a charter that goes further west toward Estepona or Sotogrande. The Strait of Gibraltar is a 45-minute drive from Fuengirola, but a catamaran takes 2-3 hours to reach it. Most operators do not offer that trip as a day charter because the distance is too far. Instead, they recommend a dedicated dolphin-watching tour from Tarifa or Algeciras. If you want to combine a catamaran charter with dolphin watching, the best option is to depart from Estepona or Sotogrande, which are closer to the strait.

Catamaran Charter Fuengirola and the Weather Windows

The Costa del Sol has a reliable weather pattern, but the wind can change quickly. The predominant wind is the Levante, which comes from the east and blows 10-20 knots. It is strongest in the afternoon, from 2pm to 6pm. In summer, the Levante is usually light, 10-15 knots, and creates a gentle chop of 0.5-1 metre. In winter, it can pick up to 25 knots and create 2-metre waves. The Poniente wind comes from the west and is warmer and lighter, 5-10 knots. It is more common in spring and autumn.

If you book a catamaran charter in July or August, the wind is almost always a light Levante that dies down by evening. That makes it ideal for a sunset charter. In May and June, the wind can be stronger in the afternoon, so a morning charter is smoother. The skipper monitors the forecast and can adjust the route to stay in sheltered waters, such as the lee of the Cabopino dunes or the Fuengirola bay. If the wind is too strong, the charter is cancelled and you get a full refund.
