faqs.q: Do I need a licence for a catamaran charter Fuengirola?
faqs.a: No. Every charter includes a licensed skipper who handles the boat. You just relax and enjoy the day.

faqs.q: What is the maximum number of guests on a catamaran charter?
faqs.a: Most catamarans on our platform take 8 to 12 guests. The exact capacity is listed on each boat's page. Larger groups can book a bigger catamaran or a second boat.

faqs.q: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faqs.a: Yes. You can bring your own, or order a catering package from the owner. Most catamarans have a fridge, a hob, and a sink. Alcohol is allowed, but the skipper will not serve it to intoxicated guests.

faqs.q: What happens if the weather is bad?
faqs.a: The skipper will assess the conditions on the day. If the sea is unsafe, the charter is rescheduled or refunded. Light rain or a slight breeze does not usually cancel the trip.

faqs.q: How long does a catamaran charter last?
faqs.a: Half-day (4 hours) and full-day (8 hours) are the most common. Some owners offer sunset cruises (2-3 hours) or multi-day charters. Check the listing for duration options.

faqs.q: Can I swim from the catamaran?
faqs.a: Yes. The skipper will stop at a sheltered cove or a calm bay. Most catamarans have a swim ladder and a freshwater shower. Bring a towel and sunscreen.

faqs.q: Is the fuel included in the price?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes. The advertised price typically includes fuel, port fees, and VAT. A few owners charge a fuel surcharge for trips over 50 nautical miles. Check the listing details.

title: Luxury Boat Rental Marbella: Premium Fleet
metaDescription: Book a luxury boat rental Marbella with a licensed skipper. Browse a premium fleet of yachts and catamarans from Puerto Banús. Instant online booking.
h1: Luxury Boat Rental Marbella: Premium Fleet with Skipper
intro: You want a luxury boat rental Marbella, and you want it done properly. No hidden fees, no language barriers, no last-minute cancellations. BoatHire24 gives you instant access to a premium fleet of motor yachts, catamarans, sailing yachts, and superyachts, all with a licensed skipper included. Depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona, Sotogrande, or Benalmádena. Prices start from around EUR 400 for a half-day on a 8-metre motor yacht, up to EUR 15,000 for a full-day superyacht charter. You pick the boat, we handle the rest. BoatHire24 is the only marketplace you need.
bodyHtml:
What You Get with a Luxury Boat Rental Marbella

Every luxury boat rental Marbella from BoatHire24 includes a professional skipper who knows the coast, the weather, and the best spots for swimming, dining, and dolphin watching. You do not need a boating licence. The boat is fully insured, fuelled, and cleaned before your charter. Most bookings include a welcome pack with water, soft drinks, ice, and sometimes cava. You can add catering, a photographer, a DJ, or water toys like jet skis and paddleboards at extra cost. The skipper stays on board the whole time, so you can relax, swim, or party without worrying about navigation or safety. Fuel costs are usually included in the price for the first 3-4 hours; beyond that, expect to pay around EUR 100-150 per hour for a 12-metre motor yacht. Check the listing details before booking.

Departure Ports for Your Luxury Charter

Your luxury boat rental Marbella can start from six main ports. Puerto Banús is the most popular: superyacht central, with designer boutiques and celebrity spotting. Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo) is quieter, with easy parking and a short walk to the old town. Cabopino is a small, sandy-bottomed marina near the dunes of Artola, perfect for a relaxed start. Estepona has a charming old town and a long promenade. Sotogrande is exclusive, with luxury villas and a golf course. Benalmádena is a bit further east, near the marina with the Buddha statue and the Sea Life centre. Each port has its own character, but all offer direct access to the Costa del Sol’s best cruising grounds. You can also arrange a one-way charter, for example departing Puerto Banús and ending in Estepona, for an extra transfer fee.

The Premium Fleet: Yachts, Catamarans, and More

The fleet for a luxury boat rental Marbella ranges from 7-metre RIBs to 40-metre superyachts. Motor yachts are the most common: brands like Azimut, Sunseeker, Princess, and Ferretti. Speeds of 25-30 knots mean you can reach the Strait of Gibraltar in about 90 minutes from Puerto Banús. Catamarans offer more deck space and stability, ideal for groups of 10-16 people. Sailing yachts (Bavaria, Jeanneau, Beneteau) are slower but quieter, with a classic feel. RIBs and speedboats are for thrill-seekers: 0-30 knots in seconds, perfect for watersports. Licence-free day boats (narrow, under 5 metres) are available from Marbella Marina for a budget-friendly option. Superyachts (over 24 metres) come with a crew of 2-5, including a stewardess and a chef, and can cost upwards of EUR 5,000 per day. Browse the full fleet on our search page.

Pricing Guidance for Luxury Boat Rental Marbella

Prices for a luxury boat rental Marbella vary by boat size, age, season, and duration. As a rough guide, a half-day (4 hours) on a 10-metre motor yacht from Puerto Banús starts at around EUR 600. A full day (8 hours) on a 15-metre yacht is around EUR 1,500-2,500. Catamarans of similar length cost about 20% more due to their extra beam. Superyachts (20 metres plus) are price on request, but expect EUR 5,000-15,000 per day. High season (June-September) adds 30-50% to base rates. You can also book a sunset cruise (2-3 hours) from around EUR 400. All prices include skipper, insurance, and basic fuel for the first 3-4 hours. Additional fuel, food, drinks, and port fees are extra. Always confirm the total with the owner before paying. We recommend booking at least 2 weeks in advance for July and August.

Best Times for a Luxury Boat Rental Marbella

The Costa del Sol has over 300 sunny days a year, but the best time for a luxury boat rental Marbella is May to October. Sea temperatures range from 18 degrees C in May to 26 degrees C in August. The Levante wind (easterly) can pick up in July and August, creating choppy conditions in the afternoon, so morning charters are often calmer. September and October are warm (25 degrees C) with less wind and fewer crowds. Winter charters (November-February) are possible but colder (15 degrees C air temp) and rougher seas. The Strait of Gibraltar is always cooler and windier, even in summer. Check the marine forecast on the day of your charter. If you are prone to seasickness, choose a catamaran or a larger motor yacht (over 15 metres) for a smoother ride.

What to See and Do on a Luxury Boat Rental Marbella

A luxury boat rental Marbella opens up the entire Costa del Sol coastline. Head east to the cliffs of Cala del Faro, where you can anchor for a swim in crystal-clear water. Continue to the Nerja caves (a 2-hour cruise from Marbella) for a guided visit. Westbound, cruise past the golden beaches of Estepona, then on to the Strait of Gibraltar for dolphin and whale watching. The strait is one of the best places in Europe to see common dolphins, striped dolphins, and even pilot whales. You can also stop at the island of Alborán (a 3-hour cruise from Marbella) for a remote beach day, but that requires an overnight charter. For a shorter trip, anchor at the sandbanks of Cabopino or the coves of Benalmádena. Many charters include a lunch stop at a beachside chiringuito like El Chiringuito de Puerto Banús or La Escollera in Marbella. Talk to your skipper about your preferences.

Who Is a Luxury Boat Rental Marbella For?

This is not a budget option. A luxury boat rental Marbella is for groups of 6-16 people who want privacy, comfort, and style. It works for birthdays, bachelor and bachelorette parties, corporate events, family reunions, and romantic anniversaries. The skipper handles all the boating, so you can focus on enjoying the experience. If you are a solo traveller, you can join a shared charter (a group booking with other guests) on platforms like GetYourGuide, but BoatHire24 only lists private charters. Children are welcome on most boats, but check the listing for age restrictions. Some superyachts do not allow children under 12 for safety reasons. Pets are not allowed on most boats, but some catamarans accept small dogs with prior agreement. If you have mobility issues, look for a boat with a wide gangway and a swim platform; most motor yachts can accommodate wheelchairs with assistance.

How to Book a Luxury Boat Rental Marbella

Booking a luxury boat rental Marbella on BoatHire24 takes less than 10 minutes. Go to the search page, filter by port, boat type, capacity, and budget. Read the description, check the photos, and look for the 'instant book' badge. Enter your date, time, and number of guests. Pay a 30% deposit online (credit card or PayPal). The owner confirms your booking within 24 hours. The balance is due 7 days before the charter. If you cancel more than 14 days before, you get a full refund minus a 5% processing fee. Cancellation within 7 days is non-refundable. We recommend taking out travel insurance that covers charter cancellation. If you have special requests (catering, DJ, decorations), message the owner through the platform before booking. All communication is logged for your protection. For more tips, read our blog on what to expect on a yacht charter.

Why Choose BoatHire24 for Your Luxury Boat Rental Marbella

BoatHire24 is a marketplace, not a broker. We list boats directly from owners and skippers, so you get real availability and transparent pricing. No middleman markups. Every boat is vetted for safety, insurance, and skipper qualifications. We have been operating in Marbella since 2018 and have over 500 verified charters. Our customer support team speaks English, Spanish, French, and German. You can call or WhatsApp us during office hours. If you have a problem during the charter, we act as a mediator. We also offer a price-match guarantee: if you find the same boat cheaper elsewhere, we will match it. The only exception is superyachts, where pricing is often negotiable. Start your search now and book with confidence.

Frequently Asked Questions

Do I need a boating licence for a luxury boat rental Marbella?

No. Every BoatHire24 booking includes a licensed skipper who handles all navigation and safety. You just relax and enjoy.

What is the cancellation policy?

Free cancellation up to 14 days before the charter (minus a 5% processing fee). Within 7 days, it is non-refundable. We recommend travel insurance.

Can I bring my own food and drink?

Yes. Most boats allow you to bring your own food and drink. Some owners provide a welcome pack. Glass bottles are often prohibited for safety reasons.

What happens if the weather is bad?

The skipper decides if it is safe to sail. If the charter is cancelled due to weather, you get a full refund or can reschedule. No charge.

How many people can fit on a luxury boat?

Typically 6-12 guests on a motor yacht, up to 16 on a catamaran, and 20+ on a superyacht. Check the listing for exact capacity.

Can I charter a boat for a wedding ceremony?

Yes. Some superyachts and large catamarans are licensed for civil ceremonies. Contact the owner directly for details and pricing.

Is fuel included in the price?

Basic fuel for the first 3-4 hours is usually included. Additional fuel is charged at cost, typically EUR 100-150 per hour for a 12-metre motor yacht.

How to Choose the Right Luxury Boat for Your Group

Picking the right vessel for your luxury boat rental Marbella depends on your group size and priorities. For a couple or a small group of four, a 10-metre motor yacht like an Azimut 40 or a Sunseeker Portofino offers a private cabin, a sun pad, and a small galley. These boats cruise at 25 knots, so you can zip from Puerto Banús to the Strait of Gibraltar in under two hours. For a group of 8-12, a 15-metre Princess or Ferretti gives you a flybridge with a wet bar, a saloon with air conditioning, and a swim platform with a shower. Catamarans like the Lagoon 42 are better for larger groups of 12-16 because they have two hulls, which means more deck space and no heeling. The trade-off is speed: catamarans cruise at 10-15 knots, so you need longer charter times. If you want watersports, choose a RIB or a speedboat with a towing ring and a powerful outboard. The Zodiac Pro 850 with a 250hp engine can pull a wakeboard or a ringo at 35 knots. Check the boat’s specifications on the listing page before booking.

Cost Breakdown: What Adds to Your Luxury Boat Rental Marbella

The base price for a luxury boat rental Marbella covers the boat, skipper, insurance, and basic fuel for the first 3-4 hours. But you need to budget for extras. Catering is a common add-on: a three-course lunch with drinks costs around EUR 50-80 per person from a catering company like Marbella Yacht Catering. A photographer for a couple of hours is EUR 200-400. A DJ with a sound system is EUR 300-500. Water toys like a jet ski (EUR 150 per hour), a paddleboard (EUR 50 per hour), or a seabob (EUR 100 per hour) are rented separately. Port fees are usually not included: expect EUR 50-100 for a day mooring at a chiringuito like El Chiringuito de Puerto Banús. If you want to extend the charter beyond the agreed time, the skipper charges the hourly rate, typically EUR 150-250 per hour for a 12-metre yacht. Tips for the skipper are not mandatory but appreciated: 10-15% of the charter fee is standard. Always ask for a full cost breakdown before paying the deposit.

Local Spots Only Accessible by Luxury Boat

One advantage of a luxury boat rental Marbella is reaching places you cannot get to by car. Cala del Faro, east of Marbella, is a secluded cove with a small beach and clear water, only accessible by boat or a steep hike. The sandbanks of Cabopino, near the Artola dunes, are a shallow area where you can anchor and wade in knee-deep water, perfect for families. The coves of Benalmádena, like Cala de la Mezquita, have rocky cliffs and caves you can explore with a paddleboard. West of Marbella, the coast of Estepona has several quiet beaches like Playa del Cristo, which has a beach bar and calm water. For a longer trip, the Strait of Gibraltar is a 90-minute cruise from Puerto Banús, where you can see dolphins, pilot whales, and sometimes orcas. The skipper knows the best anchorages and can adjust the itinerary based on the weather and your preferences. Ask for a stop at a chiringuito for lunch: La Escollera in Marbella and El Chiringuito de Puerto Banús are popular spots with boat moorings.

Seasonal Variations in Pricing and Availability

The cost of a luxury boat rental Marbella changes significantly by season. High season runs from June to September, when base rates are 30-50% higher than in low season. A half-day on a 10-metre motor yacht in July costs around EUR 800-1,000, compared to EUR 600 in April. Availability is tight in August: you need to book at least 3-4 weeks in advance. Shoulder season (May and October) offers the best balance of good weather and lower prices. Sea temperatures in May are 18-20 degrees C, warm enough for swimming if the sun is out. October is still warm (22-24 degrees C air temp) with fewer crowds. Low season (November to March) sees the lowest prices, sometimes 40% less than high season, but the weather is cooler (15-18 degrees C) and the sea can be rough. Winter charters are best for whale watching in the Strait of Gibraltar, where the whales are more active. Some boats are taken out of the water for maintenance in winter, so check availability on the search page.

Practical Tips for a Smooth Luxury Boat Charter

To get the most out of your luxury boat rental Marbella, follow a few practical tips. Arrive at the port 15 minutes before the scheduled departure time. The skipper will give a safety briefing and show you the boat’s facilities. Bring sunscreen (factor 50), a hat, and sunglasses: the sun is strong even in October. If you are prone to seasickness, take a motion sickness pill an hour before departure, or choose a catamaran or a larger motor yacht for a smoother ride. Most boats have a fridge and a cooler, so bring your own drinks and snacks. Glass bottles are often banned for safety; use plastic or cans. For swimming, bring a towel and a change of clothes. The boat has a freshwater shower to rinse off after the sea. If you want to play music, most boats have a Bluetooth speaker or a stereo system. Confirm with the owner if you need a specific input. For a sunset cruise, book a 2-3 hour charter starting at 18:00 in summer or 16:00 in winter. The skipper will anchor in a spot with a clear view of the sunset behind La Concha mountain.

Why Marbella is the Best Base for a Luxury Boat Rental

Marbella is the ideal starting point for a luxury boat rental Marbella because of its location and infrastructure. Puerto Banús is the most famous marina on the Costa del Sol, with 900 berths, designer shops, and Michelin-starred restaurants. The port is a 15-minute drive from Marbella town and 45 minutes from Málaga airport. The coastline offers variety: to the east, the cliffs of Cala del Faro and the beaches of Benalmádena; to the west, the golden sands of Estepona and the wild coast of the Strait of Gibraltar. The weather is reliable: over 300 sunny days a year, with sea temperatures above 20 degrees C from June to October. The local marine industry is well-developed, with experienced skippers, catering companies, and water sports operators. BoatHire24 has been operating here since 2018, so we know the best boats and the most reliable owners. Start your search on BoatHire24 to find the perfect luxury boat for your group.

faqs.q: Do I need a boating licence for a luxury boat rental Marbella?
faqs.a: No. Every BoatHire24 booking includes a licensed skipper who handles all navigation and safety. You just relax and enjoy.

faqs.q: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.a: Free cancellation up to 14 days before the charter (minus a 5% processing fee). Within 7 days, it is non-refundable. We recommend travel insurance.

faqs.q: Can I bring my own food and drink?
faqs.a: Yes. Most boats allow you to bring your own food and drink. Some owners provide a welcome pack. Glass bottles are often prohibited for safety reasons.

faqs.q: What happens if the weather is bad?
faqs.a: The skipper decides if it is safe to sail. If the charter is cancelled due to weather, you get a full refund or can reschedule. No charge.

faqs.q: How many people can fit on a luxury boat?
faqs.a: Typically 6-12 guests on a motor yacht, up to 16 on a catamaran, and 20+ on a superyacht. Check the listing for exact capacity.

faqs.q: Can I charter a boat for a wedding ceremony?
faqs.a: Yes. Some superyachts and large catamarans are licensed for civil ceremonies. Contact the owner directly for details and pricing.

faqs.q: Is fuel included in the price?
faqs.a: Basic fuel for the first 3-4 hours is usually included. Additional fuel is charged at cost, typically EUR 100-150 per hour for a 12-metre motor yacht.

title: Speedboat Charter Marbella: Fast Adventures
metaDescription: Book a speedboat charter Marbella with BoatHire24. RIBs and speedboats from Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, and more. Licensed skipper included. Fast adventures from EUR 450.
h1: Speedboat Charter Marbella: Fast Adventures on the Costa del Sol
intro:
If you want to feel the wind in your hair and the Mediterranean spray on your face, a speedboat charter Marbella is the best way to do it. Forget the traffic on the A-7, forget the crowds on the beach. You get a powerful RIB or speedboat, a licensed skipper who knows the coast, and the freedom to explore hidden coves like Cala del Faro or zip across to see dolphins near the Strait of Gibraltar.

BoatHire24 connects you with the best speedboat rental options in Marbella and across the Costa del Sol. You don't need a licence, you don't need to worry about navigation, and you don't need to spend a fortune. Prices for a half-day speedboat charter start from around EUR 450, depending on the boat, the season, and the departure port. You can book online, pay securely, and turn up at the marina ready to go.
bodyHtml:
What Is a Speedboat Charter Marbella?

A speedboat charter Marbella means you rent a high-performance motor boat for a few hours or a full day, with a professional skipper included. The boats are usually RIBs (rigid inflatable boats) or compact speedboats with outboard or inboard engines ranging from 150 hp to over 600 hp. They can hit 30 to 50 knots, so you cover serious distance quickly. You don't need any boating experience or a licence. The skipper handles everything, from docking to safety briefings. You just sit back, hold on, and enjoy the ride.

These charters are not about lounging on a sun pad. They are about speed, agility, and reaching places bigger yachts cannot get to. Think cliff jumping at Cabopino, snorkelling at the Marbella sea caves, or a fast run to Estepona for lunch. The skipper can tailor the route to what you want, whether that's a sightseeing tour of Puerto Banús or a high-speed dash to the dolphin hotspots off Tarifa.

Departure Ports for Your Speedboat Adventure

You can start your speedboat charter from several ports along the Costa del Sol. Each has its own vibe and advantages.

Puerto Banús is the most famous. It is glitzy, full of superyachts, and a short drive from Marbella town. From here you can blast out to the open sea in minutes, heading west towards the Sierra Blanca or east towards Cabopino. The marina itself is a spectacle, so arriving early to walk the docks is part of the experience.

Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo) is more low-key and closer to the old town. It is a good choice if you are staying in Marbella centre. The channel is quick to clear, and you are soon past the La Concha mountain backdrop.

Cabopino is a smaller, quieter marina near Artola dunes. It is ideal if you want a relaxed start with less crowd. The nearby Cabopino beach and the nudist area are popular stops.

Estepona offers a longer run west, past the new marina and the old town. It is a good base for trips to the Strait of Gibraltar and the famous dolphin pods. Sotogrande is further west, a luxury resort with a deep-water marina. Benalmádena is east of Marbella, close to Fuengirola and Mijas, and gives easy access to the Costa del Sol coastline.

Whichever port you choose, the skipper will meet you at the dock, go through the safety briefing, and have you out on the water within 15 minutes.

What Boats Are Available for Speedboat Charter Marbella?

The fleet varies by operator, but you will typically find these types of speedboats for charter in Marbella.

RIBs (Rigid Inflatable Boats) are the most common. They have a fibreglass hull and inflatable tubes, making them stable and light. A 7-metre RIB with a 200 hp engine can carry 6 to 8 passengers and hit 35 knots. A 9-metre RIB with twin 300 hp engines can take 10 to 12 passengers and reach 45 knots. Prices for a half-day RIB charter start from around EUR 450 to EUR 800.

Sports speedboats are fibreglass mono-hulls with a more traditional look. They are usually a bit slower but more comfortable in choppy seas. A 6-metre bowrider with a 150 hp engine is good for 4 to 6 people and costs from EUR 350 for a half-day. Larger ones with cabins and toilets are available for longer trips.

Licence-free day boats are a different category. They are slower (under 10 knots) and do not need a licence to drive yourself. But if you want speed, stick with a RIB or speedboat with a skipper.

All boats include safety equipment, life jackets, insurance, and fuel for the agreed itinerary. You can often add extras like a cooler with drinks, snorkelling gear, or a GoPro mount.

Pricing for a Speedboat Charter Marbella

Prices depend on the boat size, engine power, season, and duration. Here is a rough guide.

Half-day (4 hours): from EUR 450 for a 6-7 metre RIB up to EUR 1,200 for a 10-metre sports speedboat. Most operators offer morning (9am-1pm) or afternoon (2pm-6pm) slots. High season (June to September) costs about 20% more.

Full day (8 hours): from EUR 800 to EUR 2,500. You can go further, maybe to Nerja or the Strait of Gibraltar, with a lunch stop at a chiringuito.

Evening sunset cruise (2-3 hours): from EUR 300 to EUR 600. A shorter, cheaper option that still gives you the thrill of speed and the golden light over the sea.

You pay a deposit (usually 30%) at booking and the balance on the day, in cash or by card. Some operators ask for a damage deposit (EUR 300-500) which is refunded if you do not damage the boat. The skipper's tip is not included, but 10-15% is standard if you had a good time.

What Is Included in a Speedboat Charter?

Every speedboat charter Marbella from BoatHire24 includes a licensed skipper. That is non-negotiable. The skipper drives the boat, handles navigation, and points out landmarks. They also know the best spots for swimming and where the dolphins have been seen recently.

Fuel is included for the agreed route. If you want to go much further than planned, you might pay extra for fuel, but most charters have a fixed itinerary. Insurance is included, covering the boat and third-party liability. The boat comes with safety equipment, a first aid kit, and a VHF radio. Some operators provide snorkelling masks, fins, and a floating mat.

What is not included: food and drinks (though you can bring your own or ask the operator to arrange a catering package), dockage fees if you stop at a different marina, and personal accident insurance. Check your travel insurance covers water sports.

Best Time of Year for a Speedboat Charter Marbella

The Costa del Sol has over 320 sunny days a year, so you can charter a speedboat in any season. But the experience changes.

May to October is the peak season. Air temperatures are 25-35 degrees C, water is 20-26 degrees C. The sea is calm, especially in the morning before the Levante wind picks up. You can swim comfortably. July and August are busy, so book at least a week ahead.

November to April is quieter and cheaper. Air temperatures are 15-20 degrees C, water is 14-18 degrees C. You can still go out, but you will need a windbreaker. The sea can be choppier, which actually makes the ride more exciting if you like adrenaline. Dolphin sightings are still common, and you have the coastline to yourself.

The best advice: book a morning slot in summer to avoid the afternoon heat and wind. In winter, book a midday slot when the sun is highest.

How to Book a Speedboat Charter Marbella with BoatHire24

Booking is straightforward. Go to BoatHire24 and use the search filters. Select Marbella as the location, choose speedboat or RIB as the boat type, pick your date and group size. You will see a list of available boats with photos, specs, and prices. Each listing shows the departure port, the skipper's experience, and any extras.

Click on a boat you like, check the cancellation policy (most offer free cancellation up to 48 hours before), and proceed to payment. You pay a deposit online. The operator confirms your booking within 24 hours. On the day, you meet the skipper at the marina, sign a waiver, and off you go.

If you are not sure which boat to choose, contact us via the website. We know the fleet and can recommend the best option for your group size and budget.

Browse all available boats on our search page.

Who Is a Speedboat Charter For?

Speedboat charters suit different people. Couples who want a private excursion without sharing with strangers. Groups of friends or families with older children (the minimum age is usually 4-6 years, depending on the boat). Corporate groups looking for a team-building day with a bit of thrill. Stag and hen parties, as long as the group is responsible and the skipper agrees.

It is not ideal for elderly people with mobility issues, because boarding from the dock can be tricky and the ride can be bumpy. It is also not great if you get seasick easily, though the fast speed means you spend less time in rough water. If you want a slower, more stable ride, consider a catamaran or sailing yacht charter instead.

For more tips on planning your trip, check our blog for posts on the best beaches, hidden coves, and what to pack.

Safety and Practical Tips

Your skipper is responsible for safety. They will give a briefing before departure. Listen to it. Life jackets are provided, but you are not required to wear them unless the skipper decides conditions warrant it. Children under 12 must wear a life jacket at all times on deck.

Sun protection is essential. Bring sunscreen (SPF 50+), a hat, and sunglasses with a strap. The reflection off the water is intense. Also bring a towel, a change of clothes, and a waterproof bag for your phone. The boat will have a small dry storage area, but it is not a locker room.

Alcohol is allowed on board, but the skipper cannot drink. They will refuse to operate if they think anyone on board is dangerously intoxicated. Keep it sensible. You are on a boat, and the sea can be unpredictable.

Motion sickness: if you are prone, take a tablet an hour before departure. The fast speed actually helps, but if the skipper stops for swimming, the boat will rock.

What to Expect on the Day

Arrive at the marina 15 minutes before the booked time. Find the dock number given in your booking confirmation. The skipper will be there, usually wearing a polo shirt or uniform. They will help you board, stow your bags, and give a quick safety talk. Then the engine starts, the lines are cast off, and you glide out of the harbour.

Once clear of the breakwater, the skipper opens the throttle. The bow lifts, the engine noise rises, and the boat planes. The sensation is like a fast car on a smooth road, but with the horizon rushing towards you. The wind noise is loud, so conversation is limited to shouts. That is part of the fun.

After 10-15 minutes, the skipper will slow down at a point of interest: a sea cave, a cliff, or a beach. You can swim, snorkel, or just float. Then back to speed for the next stop. The skipper will ask what you want to do. If you want to go faster, say so. If you want to stop for a longer swim, say that too. It is your charter.

At the end of the time, you return to the same dock. You thank the skipper, settle any extras (like additional fuel or catering), and leave. The whole experience is usually 3 to 8 hours, depending on your booking.

Why Choose BoatHire24 for Your Speedboat Charter Marbella

BoatHire24 is not a booking agent that takes a cut and disappears. We are a marketplace that vets operators, verifies their licences and insurance, and provides secure online payment. You book directly with the operator, but we handle the transaction and support. If there is a problem, you contact us, and we sort it out.

We cover all the main ports on the Costa del Sol, so you can choose the most convenient departure point. Our listings include detailed boat specs, skipper profiles, and real photos. No stock images, no fake reviews. You see exactly what you are getting.

We also offer a price-match guarantee. If you find the same boat and time slot cheaper elsewhere, we will match it. That is how confident we are in our rates.

Start planning your speedboat charter Marbella now. Go to BoatHire24 and find your ride.

Related guides

  • Fishing Charter Marbella Half Day: Morning or Afternoon
  • Speedboat Hire Marbella No Licence: Easy Rental Options
  • Catamaran Rental Marbella Family: Kid-Friendly Trips with a Skipper
  • Private Boat Hire Puerto Banus Evening: Sunset Cruises
  • Yacht Charter Marbella Hen Party: Your Ultimate Celebration

faqs.q: Do I need a licence for a speedboat charter Marbella?
faqs.a: No. Every speedboat charter from BoatHire24 includes a licensed skipper. You do not need any boating experience or licence. The skipper drives the boat, so you can relax and enjoy the ride.

faqs.q: How much does a speedboat charter in Marbella cost?
faqs.a: Prices start from around EUR 450 for a half-day (4 hours) on a 6-7 metre RIB. Full-day charters cost from EUR 800 to EUR 2,500 depending on the boat size and season. High season (June to September) is about 20% more.

faqs.q: How many people can fit on a speedboat?
faqs.a: Most RIBs and speedboats carry 6 to 12 passengers. A 7-metre RIB typically holds 6-8 people. A 9-metre RIB can take up to 12. Check the boat listing for the maximum capacity. The skipper counts in the total.

faqs.q: What should I bring on a speedboat charter?
faqs.a: Bring sunscreen, a hat, sunglasses with a strap, a towel, a change of clothes, and a waterproof bag for your phone. You can bring your own drinks and snacks. The boat has a small dry storage area.

faqs.q: Can I see dolphins on a speedboat charter?
faqs.a: Yes, especially if you head towards the Strait of Gibraltar. Common dolphins and bottlenose dolphins are frequently spotted. The skipper knows the best areas. Sightings are not guaranteed, but the chances are good, especially in spring and summer.

faqs.q: Can I cancel my speedboat charter booking?
faqs.a: Most operators offer free cancellation up to 48 hours before the departure. Check the specific cancellation policy on the boat listing before booking. You will receive a full refund if you cancel within the allowed window.

faqs.q: What happens if the weather is bad?
faqs.a: The skipper decides if conditions are safe. If the charter is cancelled due to bad weather, you get a full refund or can reschedule. BoatHire24 will help you arrange an alternative date.

title: Yacht Rental Benalmadena: Marina Charters
metaDescription: Find yacht rental Benalmadena for your Costa del Sol holiday. Compare motor yachts, catamarans and RIBs with skippers from Marina Benalmadena. Book online.
h1: Yacht Rental Benalmadena: Marina Charters
intro:
Benalmadena Marina is one of the busiest and most accessible ports on the Costa del Sol, and it’s a solid base for a day afloat. If you’re looking for yacht rental Benalmadena, you’re in luck: the marina has a good mix of motor yachts, catamarans and RIBs, all available with a licensed skipper who knows the local coastline. You don’t need a boating licence yourself, and you can head out for a few hours or a full day.

We’re BoatHire24, and we list boats from owners and charter companies in Benalmadena and the surrounding ports. You book through us, pay online, and the skipper takes care of the rest. This page covers what you can expect from a Benalmadena charter, the types of boats available, typical pricing, and how to get it sorted.
bodyHtml:
Why Choose Benalmadena for a Yacht Charter

Benalmadena sits about 15 km west of Malaga city, with the marina right next to the town centre and the cable car up Mount Calamorro. The port has around 1,000 berths, plenty of restaurants and bars, and a decent beach (Playa de la Marina) if you want to combine a boat trip with a walk on land. The main advantage over Puerto Banus is that Benalmadena is less flashy and slightly cheaper, though the boats are still well-maintained. You’re also closer to Malaga airport, which makes it convenient for a day trip or a half-day charter.

The sailing grounds from Benalmadena are straightforward: head east towards Malaga and the Nerja caves, or west towards Fuengirola, Marbella and Puerto Banus. The coast is mostly built-up, but there are a few decent anchorages like Cala del Faro near Fuengirola and the beach at Playa de la Carihuela. If you want to go further, you can cross to Africa (about 40 NM south) or head to the Strait of Gibraltar for dolphin spotting, but that’s a full-day trip.

What’s Included in a Benalmadena Yacht Rental

Every charter we list includes a licensed skipper as standard. You don’t pay extra for the skipper, and you don’t need a licence yourself. The skipper will handle navigation, mooring and safety, and they usually speak English and Spanish. Fuel is typically not included in the base price, so budget around EUR 100-300 for a half-day on a motor yacht, depending on engine size and how far you go. Some catamarans and sailing yachts use less fuel, so factor that in.

Other inclusions vary by boat, but most charters supply: life jackets, a basic first-aid kit, a stereo system (Bluetooth or USB), and a cooler or fridge for drinks. Some boats have a BBQ or a small kitchen (galley) for preparing food. You can bring your own food and drink, or ask the owner to arrange catering for an extra cost. Tenders and water toys (paddleboards, snorkel gear) are sometimes available on request, but check the listing details before you book.

Boat Types Available in Benalmadena

The fleet in Benalmadena covers most of the common charter categories. Here’s a quick breakdown of what you can expect.

Motor Yachts

These are the most popular for a day charter. You’ll find boats from 8 m (Azimut, Sunseeker, Sea Ray) up to 20 m (Princess, Ferretti). A typical 12 m motor yacht with twin engines will cruise at 20-25 knots, which gets you to Marbella in about 40 minutes or to Malaga in 30. The cabins are usually comfortable for a couple of hours, but don’t expect superyacht luxury at this size. Prices start from around EUR 600 for a half-day (4 hours) and go up to EUR 2,500 for a full day on a larger yacht.

Catamarans

Catamarans are wider and more stable, so they’re a good choice if you’re prone to seasickness or have a large group. Benalmadena has a few Lagoon and Fountaine Pajot models, typically 12-15 m, with 4-6 cabins. They sail slower than motor yachts (8-12 knots), but the deck space is generous and you can anchor in shallow coves. Half-day prices start around EUR 800, full day from EUR 1,500.

Sailing Yachts

If you want the experience of sailing rather than motoring, there are a handful of Bavaria and Dufour models in the 10-14 m range. They’re cheaper to run than motor yachts (fuel cost is minimal), but you’ll need a bit of wind to get moving. Half-day charters start from EUR 400, full day from EUR 700.

RIBs and Speedboats

RIBs (rigid inflatable boats) are fast and fun, but they’re not for everyone: the ride is bumpy in choppy seas, and there’s no cabin or toilet. They’re good for a short blast to a beach or for water sports. Prices start around EUR 250 for a 2-hour trip, or EUR 500 for a half-day.

Pricing Guide for Yacht Rental Benalmadena

Prices vary by season, boat size and duration. Here’s a rough guide based on what we see on the platform.

  • Half-day (4 hours): Motor yacht 10-12 m: EUR 600-1,200. Catamaran 12-15 m: EUR 800-1,500. Sailing yacht 10-14 m: EUR 400-800. RIB 6-8 m: EUR 250-500.
  • Full day (8 hours): Motor yacht 10-12 m: EUR 1,200-2,500. Catamaran 12-15 m: EUR 1,500-3,000. Sailing yacht 10-14 m: EUR 700-1,500. RIB 6-8 m: EUR 500-1,000.
  • Sunset cruise (2-3 hours): Usually EUR 300-600 for a motor yacht, EUR 400-800 for a catamaran.
  • Multi-day: Some owners offer overnight charters, but these are less common in Benalmadena. Expect EUR 2,000-5,000 per day for a motor yacht with accommodation.

Fuel is extra: for a half-day on a motor yacht, budget EUR 100-200. For a catamaran, EUR 50-100. Sailing yachts under sail use almost no fuel, but you’ll need the engine for manoeuvring in port. Also, note that a deposit (usually EUR 500-2,000) may be required, refundable after the trip if there’s no damage.

Best Time to Charter a Yacht in Benalmadena

The season runs roughly April to October, with July and August being the busiest and most expensive. Water temperatures hit 22-25 degrees C in summer, and air temperatures often exceed 30 degrees C. The sea is usually calm in the mornings, with a light breeze picking up in the afternoon. If you’re booking in August, expect higher prices and less availability, book at least a month ahead.

Spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October) are good alternatives: fewer crowds, lower prices, and still pleasant weather (20-28 degrees C). The sea can be a bit choppier in spring, but it’s usually manageable. Winter charters are possible but less common: the water is cold (15-18 degrees C) and the weather can be unpredictable, though you might get a sunny day in December. Some boats are laid up for maintenance over winter, so check availability.

Routes and Destinations from Benalmadena

Your skipper will suggest a route based on the weather and your preferences. Here are some common options.

  • Coastal cruise west: Head to Fuengirola (20 minutes), then on to Cala del Faro (a small cove with a beach club) or Mijas Costa. You can anchor for a swim and lunch. Return via the same route.
  • Coastal cruise east: Malaga city is about 30 minutes east. You can anchor off La Malagueta beach or dock at the Muelle Uno marina (extra cost). Further east is the Nerja cliffs and the Nerja caves (1 hour), but that’s a full-day trip.
  • Puerto Banus and Marbella: A 40-minute run west takes you to Puerto Banus, where you can dock for lunch (extra cost) or just cruise past the superyachts. Marbella’s Old Town is a short walk from the marina.
  • Open sea and dolphin watching: Head south towards the Strait of Gibraltar. You’ll see dolphins (common, striped and bottlenose) most days, and in summer you might spot a whale. This is a full-day trip (8+ hours) and costs more in fuel.
  • Overnight or multi-day: You can go to Gibraltar (about 2 hours south), Tangier (3 hours across the strait) or along the coast to Almeria. These require a larger boat with cabins and a crew that’s happy to stay out overnight.

How to Book a Yacht in Benalmadena

Booking through BoatHire24 is straightforward. You browse the listings for Benalmadena, filter by boat type, size, price and availability. Each listing shows the boat details, photos, the skipper’s profile, and the total price (excluding fuel and optional extras). You select your date and time, pay a deposit (usually 30-50%) via credit card or bank transfer, and the owner confirms the booking. The balance is due on the day of the charter, either in cash or by card.

We recommend reading the booking conditions carefully: cancellation policies vary, and some owners charge a fee for last-minute changes. You can also contact the owner directly through the platform to ask questions before booking. For more tips, check our blog on how to prepare for a day charter.

Who Is a Benalmadena Yacht Charter For

Honestly, it’s for anyone who wants a day on the water without the hassle of owning a boat. Families with kids will appreciate the stability of a catamaran or the space on a motor yacht. Couples can book a smaller sailing yacht or a RIB for a romantic sunset cruise. Groups of friends often go for a motor yacht with a sound system and a cooler for drinks. Business clients might prefer a larger motor yacht for a corporate event or a client entertainment trip. The key is to match the boat size to your group: a 10 m motor yacht fits 6-8 people comfortably, while a 15 m catamaran can take 12-15.

If you’re not sure what you want, use our search to compare options. You can filter by price, boat type and availability to find something that works for your budget and group size.

Practical Tips for Your Charter

  • Arrive at the marina 15 minutes before departure. The skipper will meet you at the boat.
  • Bring sunscreen, a hat, sunglasses and a towel. The sun is strong, even in spring and autumn.
  • Sea sickness: if you’re prone to it, take medication an hour before departure. Catamarans are more stable than motor yachts.
  • Food and drink: you can bring your own, or ask the owner to arrange catering (usually EUR 15-30 per person for a lunch spread).
  • Toilets: most boats over 10 m have a toilet (head) and a shower. RIBs and small speedboats usually don’t.
  • Children: life jackets for kids are provided, but check the listing for size availability.

Benalmadena is a solid choice for a yacht charter on the Costa del Sol. The marina is easy to reach, the fleet is decent, and the prices are generally lower than Puerto Banus. If you want a straightforward day out with a skipper who knows the area, it’s hard to go wrong. Browse the listings, pick your boat, and we’ll handle the rest.

Benalmadena Marina: Where You Board

Your charter departs from Puerto Deportivo de Benalmadena, the main marina right next to the town. It has around 1,000 berths and is well-sheltered by the breakwater. The meeting point is usually at the charter company’s office near the yacht club (Real Club Náutico de Benalmádena) or directly on the pontoon. Parking nearby costs around EUR 2-3 per hour or EUR 15-20 for a full day in the underground car park. If you’re flying into Malaga airport, it’s a 15-minute taxi ride (EUR 25-35). The marina has a few cafes and a supermarket if you need to grab supplies before you go.

Comparing Benalmadena to Other Costa del Sol Ports

Benalmadena is often compared to Puerto Banus, Marbella and Estepona. Puerto Banus is flashier and more expensive, with superyachts and designer boutiques, but the charter prices there are typically 20-30% higher for the same boat size. Marbella Marina is smaller and quieter, good for a more relaxed vibe. Estepona is further west (about 40 minutes by car) and has a similar range of boats, but the sailing grounds are closer to the Strait of Gibraltar and the dolphin spotting areas. Benalmadena sits in the middle, so you can go east to Malaga or west to Marbella without spending hours on the water. It’s also the most convenient for Malaga airport, which makes it a good choice if you’re on a short trip.

Seasonal Price Variations in Benalmadena

Prices change significantly between high season (July-August) and low season (November-February). In high season, a half-day motor yacht charter that costs EUR 600 in May might jump to EUR 900 in August. Catamarans see a similar 30-50% premium. Low season discounts can be substantial: you might find a full-day sailing yacht for EUR 500 instead of EUR 700. The shoulder months (April-June, September-October) offer the best balance of decent weather and lower prices. If you’re flexible on dates, booking a Wednesday or Thursday in May can save you 15-20% compared to a Saturday in July.

What to Expect from the Skipper in Benalmadena

The skipper is included in the price, but their role is clearly defined. They drive the boat, handle mooring, and point out landmarks like the cable car station on Mount Calamorro or the Castillo de Bil Bil castle. They won’t act as a tour guide or entertainer, though they’ll answer questions. Most skippers in Benalmadena are local and speak English well. They expect you to bring your own food and drink, though some will offer to stop at a beach club for lunch if you want. Tipping is not mandatory but appreciated: EUR 20-50 for a half-day is standard if you’re happy with the service.

Licence-Free Day Boats in Benalmadena

If you don’t want a skipper, you can rent a licence-free day boat. These are small motorboats (up to 15 hp) that don’t require a boating licence in Spain. They’re usually 4-6 m long and can carry up to 6 people. Benalmadena has a few operators offering these for EUR 150-250 for a half-day. The catch is you can’t go far: you’re restricted to within 2 NM of the coast, and you can’t go out in rough weather. It’s fine for a lazy afternoon cruising along the beach and anchoring for a swim, but don’t expect to reach Marbella or Malaga. These boats are not listed on our platform, but you can find them at the marina kiosks.

Water Sports and Add-Ons in Benalmadena

Many charter boats in Benalmadena offer optional water toys. Paddleboards (SUP) are common on catamarans, usually free or EUR 20-30 extra. Snorkel gear is often included, but the underwater visibility around Benalmadena is average (5-10 m) compared to clearer spots like Cabo de Gata. Some motor yachts have a towable inflatable ring or a wakeboard, but you’ll need to ask the owner if they allow it. Jet skis are rarely included on a charter boat, but you can rent one separately from the marina for around EUR 100 per hour. If you want a dedicated water sports trip, a RIB with a powerful engine is your best bet.

Weather and Sea Conditions in Benalmadena

The Mediterranean here is generally calm, but it can get choppy when the Levante wind blows from the east. This wind is most common in spring and autumn, with gusts up to 30 knots. The skipper will check the forecast and may suggest a different route or cancel if conditions are unsafe. Summer mornings are usually glassy calm, with a light westerly breeze (Poniente) picking up around midday. Afternoon thunderstorms are rare but possible in August. If you’re prone to seasickness, book a morning slot and a catamaran for the most stable ride. Water temperature in summer is 22-25 degrees C, comfortable for swimming without a wetsuit.

How to Find the Best Deals on Yacht Rental Benalmadena

To get the best price, book at least two weeks in advance, especially in summer. Last-minute bookings (within 48 hours) sometimes get discounts of 10-20% if the boat is still available, but you risk missing out on popular dates. Compare listings for the same boat type across different owners: a 12 m motor yacht might be EUR 800 from one owner and EUR 1,000 from another, with similar equipment. Check the cancellation policy: flexible cancellation (free up to 7 days before) is worth paying a small premium for. Use our search filters to sort by price and availability, and read the owner’s response time and reviews if available.

faqs.q: Do I need a boating licence to rent a yacht in Benalmadena?
faqs.a: No. Every charter on BoatHire24 includes a licensed skipper, so you don’t need any licence or experience. The skipper handles everything.

faqs.q: What is the cancellation policy for a Benalmadena yacht rental?
faqs.a: Cancellation policies vary by owner. Most offer a full refund if you cancel 7-14 days before the charter, and a 50% refund for cancellations 3-7 days ahead. Check the listing details before booking.

faqs.q: Can I bring my own food and drink on the yacht?
faqs.a: Yes, you can bring your own food and drink. Some boats have a fridge or cooler. You can also ask the owner to arrange catering for an extra cost.

faqs.q: How many people can fit on a yacht charter in Benalmadena?
faqs.a: It depends on the boat. A 10 m motor yacht typically fits 6-8 guests, a 12-15 m catamaran fits 12-15, and a RIB fits 4-6. Check the listing for the maximum capacity.

faqs.q: Is fuel included in the rental price?
faqs.a: No, fuel is usually not included. For a half-day on a motor yacht, budget EUR 100-200 extra. Catamarans and sailing yachts cost less. The skipper will advise on the exact amount.

faqs.q: What happens if the weather is bad on the day of my charter?
faqs.a: The skipper will decide if it’s safe to go out. If the charter is cancelled due to bad weather, you’ll usually get a full refund or the option to reschedule. Check the owner’s weather policy.

faqs.q: Can I dock at Puerto Banus or Marbella during the charter?
faqs.a: Yes, you can dock at Puerto Banus or Marbella if the skipper agrees and the marina has space. There may be an additional docking fee, usually EUR 50-150, depending on boat size.

title: Boat Hire Fuengirola Port: Easy Booking
metaDescription: Book boat hire Fuengirola port with a licensed skipper. Compare motor yachts, catamarans & RIBs. Depart from Cabopino or Marbella. Instant online booking.
h1: Boat Hire Fuengirola Port: Easy Booking with Skipper
intro:
Fuengirola port doesn't have a dedicated marina for private charters, so boat hire Fuengirola port actually means departing from nearby Cabopino or Marbella Marina. You get a licensed skipper, a clean boat, and no hidden fees. All bookings are handled through BoatHire24, so you can compare options, see real availability, and pay online without calling around.

You choose the boat type, the duration (typically 4 or 8 hours), and whether you want to stop for lunch at a chiringuito. The skipper handles navigation, mooring, and safety. You just turn up with sunscreen and a cooler bag.
bodyHtml:
What You Get with Boat Hire Fuengirola Port

Every charter includes a professional skipper who knows the local coast. That means no licence required on your part, no worries about anchoring, and no stress about weather. The boat is cleaned and fuelled before you board. You get a safety briefing, life jackets, and basic snorkelling gear on most boats. Some larger yachts also include paddleboards, a Bluetooth speaker, and a cooler with ice.

You can bring your own food and drink, or ask the skipper to stop at a beach club for lunch. The skipper will also point out landmarks like the Castillo Sohail ruins or the cliffs at Cala del Moral. The whole experience is private, so it is just your group on board.

Departure Ports for Fuengirola Area Charters

Fuengirola itself has a small fishing port but no commercial charter marina. The nearest departure points are Puerto Deportivo de Cabopino (10 minutes east) and Marbella Marina (15 minutes west). Both are fully serviced marinas with parking, cafes, and easy access to the open sea.

Cabopino is quieter and closer to the dunas de Artola nature reserve. Marbella Marina puts you right in the middle of the action, with quick access to Puerto Banús and the western coast. For longer charters, you can also depart from Estepona or Sotogrande, but that adds travel time. Most Fuengirola visitors pick Cabopino because it is closest and has free street parking nearby.

Boat Types Available for Fuengirola Hire

You can choose from motor yachts (22 to 40 feet), catamarans (30 to 50 feet), sailing yachts (30 to 45 feet), and RIBs/speedboats (18 to 28 feet). Motor yachts are the most popular for day trips because they are fast, stable, and have a sunpad on the bow. Catamarans offer more deck space and are better for groups of 8 to 12 people.

RIBs are ideal if you want a sporty ride and plan to anchor in shallow coves. Sailing yachts are quieter and more traditional, but they are slower and require more wind. Fishing boats with rod holders and fish finders are also available for half-day or full-day fishing trips. Licence-free day boats (under 15 hp) are not available in this area because the distances between coves are too long.

For a full list of options, browse our boat search and filter by port or boat type.

Pricing Guidance for Boat Hire Fuengirola Port

Prices vary by boat size, age, and season. A 4-hour charter on a 28-foot motor yacht starts from around EUR 450 in low season and EUR 650 in high season (June to September). An 8-hour charter on a 40-foot catamaran runs from EUR 1,200 to EUR 2,200 depending on the month. RIBs are cheaper, from EUR 300 for 4 hours in low season.

All prices include the skipper, fuel for the booked duration, and basic insurance. Extras like premium catering, water toys, or extra hours are charged separately. You pay a 30% deposit online to confirm the booking, and the balance is due in cash or card on the day of departure. No hidden cleaning fees or port charges.

For exact pricing on specific boats, you can BoatHire24 and check availability for your dates.

Best Season for Boat Hire in Fuengirola

The Costa del Sol has a Mediterranean climate with over 300 sunny days a year. The peak season runs from June to September, when sea temperatures reach 24-26 degrees C and the wind is light. July and August are the busiest months, so you need to book 2-3 weeks ahead. The water is calmest in the morning, so a 10am to 2pm slot is ideal.

May and October are still warm (22-24 degrees C air temperature) and quieter, with lower prices and more availability. April and November are cooler but still pleasant for a short cruise, especially if you stay inside the cabin. December to March is low season; many boats are in maintenance, but you can still find a few charters if you book early.

What to Expect on the Water

From Cabopino or Marbella, you can head east towards the cliffs of Cala del Moral and the mouth of the Río Fuengirola, or west towards Puerto Banús and the Sierra Blanca mountains. The skipper will choose a route based on wind and sea conditions. Typical stops include a swim break at a sandy cove, a pass by the Castillo Sohail, and a slow cruise along the shoreline.

If you go west, you can spot the luxury yachts in Puerto Banús and the mansions of the Golden Mile. Going east takes you past the Cabopino dunes and the open beaches of Calahonda. Dolphin sightings are common, especially near the Strait of Gibraltar, but that is a longer trip (2+ hours each way). For a shorter trip, stick to the coastal coves.

How to Book Boat Hire Fuengirola Port

Booking through BoatHire24 takes about 5 minutes. You select your date, group size, and preferred boat type. The system shows available boats with photos, specs, and real-time pricing. You choose a boat, fill in your details, and pay the deposit by card. You receive a confirmation email with the meeting point and skipper contact.

On the day, you arrive at the marina 15 minutes before departure. The skipper meets you at the boat, does a safety briefing, and you cast off. No paperwork, no queues. If you need to cancel or reschedule, check the boat's cancellation policy (most allow free cancellation up to 48 hours before).

For more tips on planning your trip, read our blog about Costa del Sol boat hire.

Who Is Boat Hire Fuengirola Port For

This is for anyone who wants a private, hassle-free day on the water without needing a licence or experience. It works for couples, families with older children, and groups of friends. It is also a good option for corporate team outings or birthday celebrations, as the skipper can arrange catering and decorations for an extra fee.

If you are a solo traveller or a couple on a tight budget, consider joining a shared charter (group tour) on a catamaran, which costs around EUR 60-90 per person. Those are not private, but they are cheaper and still include a skipper and drinks. Private hire is better if you want flexibility, privacy, and the ability to choose your own itinerary.

Frequently Asked Questions

Why Fuengirola Port Isn't a Charter Hub

Fuengirola's port is a working fishing harbour, not a recreational marina. It has no dedicated charter berths, no fuel dock for pleasure boats, and limited parking. That is why all boat hire for Fuengirola actually departs from Cabopino or Marbella. The distance is negligible: Cabopino is a 10-minute drive east along the A-7, and Marbella Marina is 15 minutes west. Both marinas have full facilities, including restaurants, toilets, and boat ramps. You lose no time on the water because the skipper motors straight out of the marina while you settle in. If you are staying in Fuengirola centre, Cabopino is the most practical option because it avoids the traffic jams on the N-340 near Marbella.

Cabopino Port in Detail

Puerto Deportivo de Cabopino is a small, modern marina with 300 berths and a sandy beach right next to it. It sits inside the Dunas de Artola nature reserve, so the surroundings are pine trees and dunes rather than high-rise hotels. The marina has a chiringuito (beach bar) called Chiringuito Cabopino, a supermarket, and free street parking on the road leading in. For boat hire Fuengirola port purposes, Cabopino is ideal because it is the closest departure point to Fuengirola and the least crowded. The channel out to sea is short and straight, so you are on the open water within 5 minutes of leaving the berth. Boats available here include motor yachts up to 36 feet, catamarans up to 44 feet, and RIBs. There are no superyachts based here, but you can charter one from Marbella if you want something larger.

Marbella Marina as an Alternative

Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo de Marbella) is larger and busier than Cabopino. It has more charter boats available, including larger yachts and sailing boats. The marina is right next to the Old Town, so you can grab tapas before or after your trip. Parking is paid and limited, so arrive early or use the car park under the Avenida del Mar. From Marbella Marina, you can head west to Puerto Banús in 15 minutes or east to Cabopino in 20 minutes. The advantage of departing from here is the variety of boats: you can find everything from a 22-foot RIB to a 60-foot motor yacht. The downside is that it is further from Fuengirola, so add 15 minutes of driving time each way. For a full-day charter, the extra travel is irrelevant. For a half-day, Cabopino is more efficient.

Specific Boat Models You Might Find

On BoatHire24, you will see real boats with real names. Common motor yachts include the Jeanneau Leader 33, the Beneteau Gran Turismo 35, and the Azimut 42. These have cabins with a toilet, a small galley, and a sunpad. Catamarans often include the Lagoon 38 or the Nautitech 40, both with a trampoline net at the bow and a spacious cockpit. RIBs are typically Zodiac or Brig models with a 150-250 hp outboard. Sailing yachts are usually Beneteau Oceanis or Jeanneau Sun Odyssey models, 32 to 40 feet. Each boat listing on the site includes the year built, number of seats, and a photo gallery. You can also see what extras are included, such as a paddleboard or a Bluetooth speaker. If you have a specific model in mind, use the search filters to narrow down options.

Half-Day vs Full-Day Charters

A half-day charter is usually 4 hours, from 10am to 2pm or 2pm to 6pm. This is enough time to cruise east to Cala del Moral, drop anchor for a swim, and return. You cover about 10-15 nautical miles. A full-day charter is 8 hours, typically 10am to 6pm. With 8 hours, you can go further: west to Puerto Banús and the Benabola beach clubs, or east to the cliffs of Maro-Cerro Gordo. You can also stop for a longer lunch at a beach club, or take a slow cruise along the coast with multiple swim stops. Fuel is included in the price for the booked duration, so you do not pay extra for distance. If you want to go to the Strait of Gibraltar to see dolphins, that requires a full day or longer because it is 30 nautical miles from Cabopino.

Weather and Sea Conditions

The Costa del Sol is generally calm, but the wind can pick up in the afternoon. The Poniente wind blows from the west and is usually light (5-10 knots). The Levante wind comes from the east and can be stronger (15-25 knots), creating choppy seas. Skippers monitor the forecast and will adjust the route or recommend a morning departure if the wind is forecast to rise. In summer, the sea is flat calm until about 1pm, then a light chop develops. If you are prone to seasickness, take a motion sickness pill 30 minutes before boarding. Most boats have a cabin where you can sit if you feel queasy. The water temperature in August is 26 degrees C, so swimming is very comfortable. In May and October, it is around 20 degrees C, so a wetsuit is recommended for longer swims.

What to Bring on Your Charter

Bring sunscreen (SPF 50+, water-resistant), a hat, sunglasses with a strap, and a towel. The sun reflects off the water and is stronger than on land. Wear non-slip shoes or go barefoot on deck. Most boats have a freshwater shower on the swim platform to rinse off salt. Bring a light jacket or windbreaker even in summer, because the wind on a moving boat can feel cool. For food and drink, bring a cooler with sandwiches, fruit, and drinks. The skipper will provide ice if you ask. Many boats have a fridge, but it is small. If you want a beach club lunch, book ahead because popular spots like Ocean Club Marbella or Playa Padre fill up. The skipper can call ahead to reserve a table for your group.

Dolphin Watching from Fuengirola

Dolphin sightings are common on charters from Cabopino and Marbella, but they are not guaranteed. The best area is between Cabopino and the Strait of Gibraltar, where the water is deeper and the currents bring plankton. Common dolphins, striped dolphins, and bottlenose dolphins are the species you are likely to see. They often approach the boat and ride the bow wave. The skipper will slow down or stop if dolphins are spotted, but they cannot chase them. For a dedicated dolphin-watching trip, book a full-day charter heading west towards Estepona. The probability of sightings is higher in the morning. Do not expect a dolphin show every time; it is wildlife, not a theme park. If seeing dolphins is your priority, mention it when booking so the skipper can choose a route that passes through known feeding areas.

Fishing Charters from Fuengirola

If you want to fish, there are dedicated fishing boats available for hire. These are usually RIBs or small motorboats with rod holders, a fish finder, and a live bait tank. Half-day fishing charters start from around EUR 350 in low season. You can target species like sea bass, bream, mackerel, and dentex. The skipper knows the best spots around the Cabopino reef and the Fuengirola river mouth. Catch-and-release is standard, but you can keep a few fish for dinner if they are within legal size limits. No fishing licence is needed for recreational fishing from a chartered boat. The skipper handles all the gear and bait. Fishing is best in the early morning (6am to 10am) or late afternoon (4pm to 8pm). If you book a fishing charter, confirm that the boat has a toilet on board, because some small RIBs do not.

Corporate and Special Events

Boat hire Fuengirola port is also used for corporate events, team building, and product launches. You can charter a catamaran for a group of 12 to 20 people and add catering, drinks, and a photographer. The skipper acts as host and ensures the itinerary runs smoothly. For a birthday or anniversary, you can arrange decorations, a cake, and champagne. Some boats allow a DJ or a Bluetooth speaker for music. The cost for a corporate event on a 40-foot catamaran for 8 hours starts from around EUR 2,500 in high season. This includes the skipper, fuel, insurance, and basic cleaning. Catering is extra and usually costs EUR 30-50 per person for a buffet lunch with drinks. Book at least 2 weeks ahead for corporate events, especially in July and August.

Accessibility and Special Needs

Most boats are not wheelchair accessible. The gangway from the dock to the boat is narrow and steep, and the deck has steps. If you have mobility issues, contact BoatHire24 before booking to check if a suitable boat is available. Some catamarans have a wider deck and a lower boarding platform, but they still require stepping over a gunwale. The skipper can assist with boarding and seating, but cannot carry a passenger. For hearing or visual impairments, the safety briefing is given verbally and with hand signals. The skipper can provide a written safety card if needed. Service dogs are allowed on board, but they must stay in the cockpit and not on the sunpad. The boat has a shaded area in the cockpit or cabin for passengers who need to avoid direct sun.

How to Get to Cabopino from Fuengirola

By car, take the A-7 east towards Málaga. Exit at 208 for Cabopino/Artola. Follow the signs to Puerto Deportivo Cabopino. The drive takes 10 minutes from Fuengirola centre. Parking is free on the street outside the marina, but it fills up by 10am in summer. Alternatively, use the paid car park inside the marina for EUR 5 per day. By taxi, expect to pay around EUR 15-20 each way. By bus, take the M-220 line from Fuengirola bus station to the Cabopino stop, then walk 5 minutes to the marina. The bus runs every 30 minutes. For Marbella Marina, take the A-7 west to Marbella, exit at 185, and follow signs to Puerto Deportivo. Parking is paid and costs EUR 2 per hour. A taxi from Fuengirola to Marbella Marina costs about EUR 25-30.

Last-Minute Bookings and Availability

If you want to book on short notice, call BoatHire24 or use the website. Same-day bookings are possible if a boat is available, but you need to book by 8am for a morning departure. In high season, last-minute availability is rare, especially for popular boats like catamarans. In low season, you can often book the day before. The deposit is 30% and is refundable if you cancel 48 hours before. For last-minute bookings, the full payment may be required upfront. If you are flexible on boat type and size, you have a better chance of finding a last-minute slot. The search page shows real-time availability, so you can see what is free today or tomorrow. Do not show up at the marina without a booking, because there are no walk-up charter desks at Cabopino or Marbella Marina.

faqs.q: Where exactly do I meet the boat for a Fuengirola charter?
faqs.a: You meet at the departure marina, usually Puerto Deportivo de Cabopino or Marbella Marina. The exact berth number is in your confirmation email. Fuengirola port itself is not used for charters.

faqs.a: It depends on the boat. A 28-foot motor yacht holds 8-10 passengers. A 40-foot catamaran holds up to 12. RIBs usually hold 6-8. Check the boat listing for the maximum capacity.
