title: Speedboat Hire Benalmadena: Fast Fun
metaDescription: Book speedboat hire Benalmadena with a licensed skipper. Fast RIBs & speedboats from Puerto Marina. Prices from around EUR 300. Instant online booking.
h1: Speedboat Hire Benalmadena: Fast Fun on the Costa del Sol
intro:
You want speedboat hire Benalmadena because you want the wind in your hair and the sun on your back, not a slow cruise with grandma’s yacht club playlist. Benalmadena’s Puerto Marina is one of the best launch points on the Costa del Sol for a fast RIB or speedboat, and every charter on BoatHire24 comes with a licensed skipper who knows the local waters. No licence needed from you, just turn up and go.

You can blast out to the Torremolinos coastline, chase the dolphins off Cabopino, or anchor up at Cala del Faro for a swim. The boats are modern, the skippers are professional, and the booking is dead simple. Pick your date, choose your boat, and pay online. No hidden fees, no messing about.
bodyHtml:
What You Get with Speedboat Hire Benalmadena

Every speedboat hire through BoatHire24 includes a fully licensed skipper. You do not need a boating licence, a day skipper certificate, or any experience at all. The skipper handles the driving, the navigation, and the safety briefings. You just enjoy the ride.

Most speedboats are RIBs (rigid inflatable boats) between 6 and 10 metres, powered by outboard or inboard engines that push 30 to 60 knots. Some are open console boats with a T-top or bimini for shade. Fuel is usually included in the hire price, but always check the listing details. Typical hire durations are 2 hours, half day (4 hours), or full day (8 hours).

You also get life jackets, basic safety equipment, and often a cooler box for drinks. Some operators will let you bring your own food and drink. The skipper will point out landmarks, wildlife, and the best spots for a swim. You are not just renting a boat; you are buying a local’s knowledge of the coast.

Benalmadena Puerto Marina: Your Departure Point

Benalmadena’s Puerto Marina is a purpose-built leisure harbour with over 900 berths, restaurants, shops, and a casino. It is also one of the most sheltered marinas on the Costa del Sol, which means the water is usually calm enough for speedboat hire even when the wind picks up elsewhere.

The marina is about 15 minutes from Malaga Airport by taxi or 30 minutes from Marbella. There is a large car park right next to the port entrance, and the boat operators are based on the main pontoon. You will meet your skipper at the designated berth, get a quick safety briefing, and be underway within 10 minutes.

From Puerto Marina, you can head east towards Torremolinos and Malaga city, or west towards Fuengirola, Cabopino, and Marbella. The coast is a mix of sandy beaches, rocky coves, and cliffside villas. The visibility is usually excellent, and the water temperature hits 24 degrees C in summer.

Boat Options for Speedboat Hire Benalmadena

You have a few choices speedboat hire Benalmadena. The most common are RIBs, which are lightweight, fast, and stable. A 7-metre RIB with a 200hp outboard will do around 40 knots and seat up to 8 people. That is enough for a family or a group of friends.

If you want something a bit more stylish, look for a centre-console speedboat like a Beneteau or a Boston Whaler. These are fibreglass hulls with a walkaround deck, plenty of seating, and a smoother ride in chop. They are slightly slower than a RIB but more comfortable for longer trips.

For pure adrenaline, some operators offer high-performance RIBs with twin engines that push 60 knots. These are not for the faint-hearted. You will get a full safety briefing and the skipper will keep the speed sensible if the sea state is rough. Prices for speedboat hire Benalmadena start from around EUR 300 for a 2-hour trip and go up to EUR 1,200 for a full day on a larger boat.

Where You Can Go on a Speedboat from Benalmadena

With a speedboat, you can cover a lot of coast in a short time. From Benalmadena, a 30-minute run west brings you to Cabopino, a small natural harbour with a sandy beach and a couple of chiringuitos (beach bars). The water there is clear and shallow, good for a swim stop.

Further west, you can reach Marbella and Puerto Banus in about 45 minutes. The coastline is lined with luxury villas, hotels, and the occasional celebrity sighting. Your skipper will know the best anchorages, like the small cove at Cala del Faro just past Puerto Banus.

Heading east, you can zip past Torremolinos and into Malaga bay. The city skyline is impressive from the water, and you can stop at the beach of La Malagueta for a swim. If you have a half-day hire, you can easily make it to the mouth of the Rio Guadalhorce nature reserve and spot flamingos from the boat.

For a longer trip, some speedboat charters will take you all the way to the Strait of Gibraltar, where you can see dolphins, pilot whales, and even orcas in season. That is a full-day excursion and costs more, but it is a genuine wildlife experience.

Pricing Guidance for Speedboat Hire Benalmadena

Prices vary by boat size, engine power, duration, and season. Here is a rough guide based on typical listings on BoatHire24:

  • 2-hour hire: from around EUR 300 to EUR 500
  • Half day (4 hours): from around EUR 500 to EUR 800
  • Full day (8 hours): from around EUR 900 to EUR 1,500

Fuel is usually included in the price, but check the listing. Some operators charge extra for fuel if you go beyond a certain distance. Skipper gratuity is not included and is at your discretion, but 10% is standard if you had a good time.

Peak season (June to September) is more expensive and books up weeks in advance. Shoulder season (April, May, October) is cheaper and the weather is still good, with air temperatures around 22-28 degrees C. Winter is not ideal for speedboat hire because the sea can be rough and the wind is strong.

How to Book Speedboat Hire Benalmadena on BoatHire24

Booking is straightforward. Go to the search page, set your location to Benalmadena, choose your date, and filter by boat type “speedboat” or “RIB”. You will see a list of available boats with photos, specs, and prices.

Select the boat you like, pick your duration, and proceed to checkout. You pay a deposit online (usually 30-50%) and the balance on the day, directly to the operator. The system sends you a confirmation email with the meeting point and skipper contact details.

You can cancel up to 48 hours before the trip for a full refund, subject to the operator’s policy. If the weather is bad, the operator will cancel and you get a full refund. Always check the forecast before you book, but the skipper will make the final call on the day.

Who Is Speedboat Hire Benalmadena For?

Speedboat hire Benalmadena is for anyone who wants a fast, fun day on the water without the hassle of owning a boat. It works for couples looking for a romantic trip, families with older kids (most boats have a minimum age of 4-6 years), and groups of friends celebrating a birthday or stag/hen do.

It is also a good option for corporate events or team-building days. You can hire two or three speedboats and have a convoy along the coast, stopping for lunch at a beach club. The skippers will coordinate the itinerary.

If you are a fishing enthusiast, some speedboats can be used for light tackle fishing. Ask the operator when you book. They will provide basic rods and bait for an extra fee.

Other Ports for Speedboat Hire on the Costa del Sol

If Benalmadena is not your base, you can also book speedboat hire from Puerto Banus, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona, and Sotogrande. Each port has its own character and range of boats. BoatHire24 covers the whole coast, so you can compare options and pick the one that suits your itinerary.

For example, speedboat hire from Puerto Banus tends to focus on luxury and style, with higher-end RIBs and centre consoles. Estepona is quieter and closer to the dolphin-watching grounds near Gibraltar. Sotogrande is more exclusive and often used for day trips to Gibraltar or Tangier.

Read our blog for detailed guides on each port and the best times to go. We update it regularly with local tips and operator reviews.

Safety and What to Wear

Your skipper holds a valid Spanish licence and the boat is insured and inspected. Life jackets are provided and must be worn during the high-speed sections. The skipper will give a safety briefing before you leave the dock.

Wear swimwear or light clothing, a hat, sunglasses, and plenty of sunscreen. The sun reflects off the water and you will burn faster than you think. Bring a towel, a change of clothes, and a waterproof bag for your phone and camera. Non-slip shoes are a good idea, but barefoot is fine on most RIBs.

If you are prone to seasickness, take a tablet an hour before departure. The boats are fast and the ride can be bumpy in a chop. The skipper will adjust the speed to keep you comfortable, but it is still a speedboat, not a cruise liner.

Frequently Asked Questions

Do I need a licence for speedboat hire Benalmadena?

No. All BoatHire24 charters include a licensed skipper who operates the boat. You just sit back and enjoy.

How many people can go on a speedboat?

Most RIBs and speedboats seat between 6 and 8 passengers. Check the specific boat listing for the maximum capacity.

Can I bring my own food and drink?

Yes, most operators allow you to bring your own. Some provide a cooler box. Glass bottles are usually not allowed for safety.

What happens if the weather is bad?

The skipper will cancel the trip if conditions are unsafe. You get a full refund or the option to reschedule.

How far in advance should I book?

In peak season, book at least 2 weeks ahead. Shoulder season, a few days is usually fine. Last-minute bookings are possible but limited.

Can I go fishing on a speedboat hire?

Some operators allow light tackle fishing. Ask when booking. Rods and bait may be available for an extra fee.

Is fuel included in the price?

Usually yes, but check the listing. Some operators charge extra for fuel if you exceed a certain distance or duration.

Why Benalmadena Is Ideal for Speedboat Hire

Benalmadena sits right in the middle of the Costa del Sol, giving you equal access to both the glamorous west coast and the more rugged eastern shoreline. From Puerto Marina, you can reach Malaga city in 20 minutes at full throttle or Marbella in 45. That central position means you get more coastline per hour of hire than you would from a port at either end of the strip.

The marina itself is a destination. You can grab breakfast at one of the waterfront cafes before your trip, and the skipper will meet you right on the pontoon. No shuttles, no transfers, no waiting around. The harbour master keeps the basin clean and the water calm, so even a short 2-hour hire feels like a proper day out.

Another advantage is the variety of nearby anchorages. Within 15 minutes of leaving the marina, you can be swimming at a quiet cove near Torrebermeja or pulling up to a beach club in Fuengirola. The skipper knows which spots are quiet on which days, and they will adjust the route based on wind direction and sea state.

Best Times of Year for Speedboat Hire Benalmadena

The speedboat season runs from April to October. June, July, and August are the busiest months, with air temperatures around 30 degrees C and water temperatures hitting 24-26 degrees C. The sea is usually flat in the morning, with a light thermal breeze picking up after lunch. That afternoon chop can make the ride bumpy, so morning departures are generally smoother.

May and September are the sweet spots. The weather is still warm (22-28 degrees C), the water is pleasant for swimming, and the crowds are thinner. You will find more availability and slightly lower prices. October can be good too, but the sea gets cooler and the chance of wind increases. The skipper will still run trips as long as conditions are safe.

Winter is not recommended for speedboat hire. The wind picks up, the swell increases, and many operators store their boats ashore. You can still charter a larger motor yacht or catamaran in winter, but the fast RIBs stay in the garage until spring.

What to Expect on a 2-Hour Speedboat Trip

A 2-hour hire is the most popular option for speedboat hire Benalmadena. It is short enough to fit into a morning or afternoon, but long enough to get a proper feel for the coast. You will meet the skipper at the marina, get the safety briefing, and head out of the harbour at a slow speed until you clear the breakwater.

Once outside, the skipper will open the throttle and you will feel the acceleration. Most RIBs plane quickly and cruise at 25-30 knots. The skipper will point out the landmarks: the Benalmadena cable car station on the hilltop, the marina skyline, and the distant silhouette of La Concha mountain near Marbella. After 10-15 minutes of high-speed cruising, you will stop for a swim in a sheltered cove.

The skipper will cut the engine and you can jump in. The water is clear, and you will see sandy bottom and maybe some fish. You get about 20-30 minutes for swimming, then you head back. The return trip is usually a bit slower so you can dry off. Total time on the water is around 1 hour 45 minutes, with the rest taken up by docking and briefing.

Half-Day Speedboat Itinerary: Benalmadena to Cabopino and Back

A half-day hire (4 hours) gives you time to explore further. A good route is to head west from Benalmadena towards Cabopino, a small natural harbour about 8 nautical miles away. At 30 knots, that is a 15-minute run. Cabopino has a sandy beach backed by dunes and a couple of chiringuitos where you can stop for a drink and a plate of fried fish.

From Cabopino, you can continue to the Cala del Faro, a small cove just past Puerto Banus. The water there is deep and clear, and the cliffs provide shelter from the wind. You can anchor for a swim and a snack. The skipper will have a cooler with water and soft drinks, and you can bring your own lunch.

The return trip takes you past the Puerto Banus skyline, the luxury hotels of Marbella, and the pine-clad hills of the Sierra Blanca. You will be back at Benalmadena with time to spare. Total distance covered is around 20-25 nautical miles, which is plenty for a half-day trip without feeling rushed.

Full-Day Speedboat Hire: Benalmadena to Estepona or Malaga

If you book a full day (8 hours), you can push further. One option is to head west to Estepona, about 25 nautical miles from Benalmadena. That takes around 45 minutes at cruising speed. Estepona has a pretty old town, a long promenade, and a marina with good restaurants. You can tie up for lunch, explore the town, and then head back, stopping at a beach club on the way.

Another option is to go east to Malaga city. The port of Malaga is about 12 nautical miles from Benalmadena, a 25-minute run. You can dock at the Muelle Uno area, walk to the Alcazaba fortress or the Picasso Museum, and have lunch in the city. The skipper will stay with the boat or meet you at a pre-arranged time for the return trip.

A full-day trip also allows time for multiple swim stops, dolphin watching (more common near the Strait of Gibraltar), and a longer lunch break. The skipper will plan the route based on your preferences and the weather. Fuel consumption is higher on a full-day trip, but most operators include it in the price.

Speedboat Hire for Groups and Events

Speedboat hire Benalmadena works well for groups. Most RIBs seat 6 to 8 passengers, so you can split a larger party across two or three boats. The skippers will coordinate by radio, keeping the group together and stopping at the same spots. This is a popular option for stag and hen parties, birthday celebrations, and corporate team days.

For a corporate event, you can hire a fleet of speedboats for a morning, have lunch at a beach club in Cabopino or Marbella, and then return in the afternoon. The total cost for 20 people (three boats plus lunch) comes to around EUR 2,500 to EUR 4,000 depending on the season and the restaurant. That is competitive with a golf day or a private yacht charter.

If you are planning a proposal or a small wedding party, some operators will help arrange champagne, flowers, and a photographer. The speedboat can stop at a quiet cove for the moment. Ask when you book, and the skipper will coordinate the details.

What to Bring and What to Leave Behind

Bring swimwear, a towel, sunscreen (factor 50, waterproof), sunglasses with a strap, and a hat. A waterproof phone case or a dry bag is useful for keeping valuables dry. Most speedboats have a small storage compartment under the seats where you can leave bags.

Leave behind glass bottles, high heels, and anything that could blow away at speed. The skipper will provide life jackets for all passengers, and children under 12 must wear them at all times. If you have young children, check the minimum age with the operator. Most set the limit at 4 or 5 years old for safety reasons.

If you are prone to seasickness, take a motion sickness tablet 30 minutes before departure. The ride is generally smooth in calm conditions, but a speedboat can bounce in a chop. The skipper will slow down if anyone feels unwell, but prevention is better than cure.

How to Choose the Right Speedboat for Your Trip

When browsing speedboat hire Benalmadena on BoatHire24, look at the boat length, engine power, and seating layout. A 7-metre RIB with a single 200hp outboard is fine for a family of four or a group of six. It will cruise at 25-30 knots and top out around 40 knots. That is fast enough for most people.

If you want more speed, look for a boat with twin engines or a larger outboard. A 9-metre RIB with twin 300hp engines can hit 60 knots. That is a different experience altogether. The ride is firmer, the noise is louder, and the wind is intense. Not everyone enjoys it, so be honest with yourself about your tolerance for speed.

For a smoother ride in choppy conditions, a centre-console speedboat with a deep-V hull is better than a flat-bottomed RIB. The trade-off is that it is heavier and uses more fuel, so the price may be higher. Check the listing for details on hull type and passenger comfort ratings.

title: Catamaran Rental Fuengirola: Group Deals
metaDescription: Book a catamaran rental Fuengirola with BoatHire24. Group deals from EUR 1,200, private skipper, Costa del Sol. Check availability today.
h1: Catamaran Rental Fuengirola: Group Deals with Skipper
intro:
If you are after a catamaran rental in Fuengirola, you have probably seen the packed beach clubs and the overpriced day beds. A private catamaran is a better bet. You get your own deck, a licensed skipper who does the work, and the whole group can move around without stepping on strangers.

BoatHire24 lists catamarans from 36 to 52 feet, based in Fuengirola’s marina and nearby ports such as Puerto Banús and Cabopino. Prices run from around EUR 1,200 for a half-day for up to 12 people. That works out less per head than a VIP table at a beach bar, and you get the sea, the skyline, and the La Concha mountain views instead of a DJ you did not ask for.
bodyHtml:
What a Catamaran Rental in Fuengirola Includes

Every catamaran charter through BoatHire24 comes with a professional skipper. You do not need a licence. The skipper handles navigation, mooring, and safety. Most boats also include a hostess or steward who serves drinks and food (you bring or pre-order). Standard inclusions: fuel for a typical 4-6 hour itinerary, insurance, onboard toilet, fresh water, a sound system, and basic snorkelling gear. Some catamarans have a trampoline net at the bow, a sundeck, and a bimini shade. Check the listing for specifics.

You can add extras like a barbecue lunch, a paddleboard, a jet ski tow, or a photographer. The skipper will suggest a route depending on wind and sea state. Typical runs go west to Cala del Faro or east past Cabopino towards the dunes of Artola. You anchor, swim, eat, and head back. No stress.

Fuengirola’s Marina and Departure Points

Fuengirola has a central marina (Puerto Deportivo de Fuengirola) with direct access to the bay. Most catamarans depart from here. The marina is a 10-minute walk from the train station and has paid parking nearby. If you stay in Fuengirola, you can walk to the boat. For groups based in Marbella or Benalmádena, the skipper can arrange a pick-up from Puerto Banús or Cabopino at no extra cost, but check the listing. Departure ports available: Fuengirola, Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona, Sotogrande, Benalmádena. The boat comes to you.

Boat Types on Offer

Catamarans are the main draw, but the same platforms also list motor yachts, sailing yachts, RIBs, fishing boats, licence-free day boats, and superyachts. For a group day out, a catamaran gives you the most deck space per person. The beam (width) is wider than a monohull, so you do not feel crowded. Popular models include the Lagoon 42, Bali 4.1, and Fountaine Pajot Lucia 40. Sailing catamarans use the wind, but they have engines for calm days. Power catamarans (like the Aquila 44) are faster and more stable at anchor. You can filter by boat type on the search page.

Pricing Guidance for Fuengirola Catamaran Rentals

Half-day charters (4 hours) start at around EUR 1,200 for a 36-foot catamaran for up to 10 guests. Full-day (8 hours) from EUR 1,800. Larger vessels (44-52 feet) for 12-18 guests cost EUR 2,500-4,000 per day. Prices vary by season, boat age, and included extras. High season (July-August) is 20-30% higher. You can often negotiate a discount for weekday bookings or multiple days. Payment is typically 30-50% deposit on booking, balance on the day. BoatHire24 handles secure payments. Always confirm what is included in the price before you book.

Best Seasons for Catamaran Rental Fuengirola

The Costa del Sol has a Mediterranean climate. Summer (June-September) is hot, 28-32 degrees C, light winds, flat sea. Perfect for catamarans. Spring (April-May) and autumn (October) are milder, 20-25 degrees C, with a bit more breeze. Winter (November-March) is still pleasant, 15-18 degrees C, but fewer boats operate. If you want to swim, July and August water temperatures hit 24-26 degrees C. For a quieter experience, May and September offer good weather and smaller crowds. The Strait of Gibraltar dolphins are visible year-round, but more common in spring and autumn.

How to Book a Catamaran in Fuengirola

Go to the catamaran search on BoatHire24. Filter by date, group size, and budget. Each listing shows photos, specs, skipper bio, and reviews. You can send a direct enquiry or book online. The platform confirms availability within 2 hours. You will receive a contract and payment link. On the day, meet the skipper at the marina 15 minutes before departure. The skipper will give a safety briefing. That is it. No hidden fees. If you need help choosing, use the chat or call the number on the site.

Who a Catamaran Rental Suits Best

Groups of friends, families with older children, corporate teams, hen and stag parties (within reason), and anyone who wants a floating base for swimming and sunbathing. Catamarans are stable, so they are fine for people who get seasick on monohulls. The wide deck makes them good for socialising. If you want a party boat, some catamarans have a sound system and a grill. For a romantic couple, a smaller sailing catamaran or a motor yacht might be more intimate. The BoatHire24 blog has guides on choosing the right boat for your group.

Local Highlights Around Fuengirola

Fuengirola sits between Benalmádena and Marbella. From the sea, you see the Sierra de Mijas mountains and the La Concha peak. The coastline has small coves like Cala del Faro and the beach at Cabopino. You can anchor off the dunes of Artola, a protected natural area. Further east, the cliffs of Nerja and the famous caves are a longer trip (2+ hours by catamaran). Westwards, Estepona old town has a pretty marina. The Strait of Gibraltar is about an hour by boat from Fuengirola, where you often spot dolphins and sometimes whales. Your skipper will know the best spots.

Important Practical Details

Bring sunscreen, a towel, a hat, and a light jacket for the evening. Most catamarans have a fridge and a cooler. You can bring your own food and drink, or order a catering package from the charter company. Alcohol is allowed, but the skipper will not tolerate excessive drunkenness. Maximum group size is usually 12-18 passengers depending on the boat’s licence. Children are welcome, but life jackets are mandatory for under-12s. Check the cancellation policy: most operators offer free cancellation up to 7 days before. Bad weather cancellations are at the skipper’s discretion and usually result in a full refund or reschedule.

Why Choose a Catamaran Over a Motor Yacht in Fuengirola

You get more living space on a catamaran. The beam is wider, so the deck feels like a floating terrace. Motor yachts have a narrower beam and a sharper roll at anchor. Catamarans are more stable, which matters if anyone in your group gets queasy. The trampoline net at the bow is a catamaran-only feature: you lie there and watch the water pass beneath you. Motor yachts are faster (20-30 knots vs 6-10 knots for a sailing catamaran), but speed is rarely the point on a day charter. You want to swim, eat, and relax, not race to a destination. For a group of 10-12, a 40-foot catamaran costs about the same as a 50-foot motor yacht. The catamaran gives you more usable square metres per euro. Power catamarans (like the Aquila 44 or the Fountaine Pajot MY6) split the difference: they have the beam and stability of a sailing catamaran but the speed of a motor yacht. They cost more, starting at around EUR 3,000 per day for 12 guests. Check the catamaran listings to compare.

Specific Catamaran Models Available in Fuengirola

The Lagoon 42 is a common sight. It has four cabins, four bathrooms, a large saloon, and a forward deck with sun pads. It sleeps eight but can take 12 for day charters. The Bali 4.1 has an open transom and a folding platform that makes boarding from the water easy. The Fountaine Pajot Lucia 40 is slightly smaller (40 feet) and lighter, so it sails better in light wind. The Lagoon 450 is a 45-footer with more beam and a flybridge. For larger groups, the Lagoon 52 or the Bali 5.4 can take up to 18 guests. These are luxury vessels with air conditioning, a generator, and a fully equipped galley. Some owners keep their catamarans in Puerto Banús but will deliver to Fuengirola for an extra fee. Always confirm the model and year in the listing. Older boats (pre-2015) may have less efficient engines and worn upholstery. Newer boats (2020 onwards) have better sound systems, LED lighting, and updated safety gear.

Departure Ports Near Fuengirola and Logistics

Fuengirola marina is the obvious choice. It has 285 berths and is a 5-minute walk from the town centre. Parking is limited; use the public car park at Avenida de la Marina, EUR 2 per hour. If you are based in Marbella, the skipper can meet you at Puerto Banús (20 minutes east by car) or Cabopino (15 minutes west). Cabopino is smaller but quieter, with a sandy beach and a dune reserve. Estepona marina is 30 minutes west and has good restaurants. Sotogrande is further (45 minutes) but offers a more exclusive setting. Benalmádena marina is 10 minutes east and has a large charter fleet. The drive from Fuengirola to Benalmádena is 8 minutes by car. If your group is staying in Málaga city, the train from Málaga María Zambrano to Fuengirola takes 40 minutes and costs EUR 5.60 one way. The marina is a 10-minute walk from Fuengirola station. For groups of 8 or more, the skipper can arrange a minibus transfer from your hotel to the marina for around EUR 50 total. Ask when you book.

Sample Itineraries from Fuengirola

A 4-hour half-day trip goes west to Cala del Faro. You motor out of the marina, pass the Fuengirola castle, and head towards the cliffs. Cala del Faro is a small cove with clear water and a rocky shore. You anchor, swim, and have lunch. The skipper will put out the paddleboard. You head back to port by 2pm. A full-day trip goes east to Cabopino. You leave at 10am, sail past the Benalmádena marina and the Torrequebrada hotel, and reach Cabopino by 11:30. You anchor off the Artola dunes, swim, and eat. After lunch, you motor to the Cabopino marina for a walk or continue east towards Marbella. You return to Fuengirola by 6pm. A longer trip (8-10 hours) goes to the Strait of Gibraltar. You leave at 8am, motor south-west for 1.5 hours, and reach the strait where the Atlantic meets the Mediterranean. Dolphins are almost guaranteed. You anchor at a quiet cove near Estepona for lunch. You return by 6pm. This itinerary requires calm seas and a fast catamaran (power catamaran recommended). The skipper will decide on the day. For a sunset trip, you leave at 5pm, sail west, anchor for a swim, and watch the sun set behind La Concha mountain. You return by 9pm. Sunset trips are popular in June and July when the sun sets at 9:30pm.

Group Size and Capacity Rules

Spanish maritime law limits passenger numbers based on the boat’s licence. For a catamaran under 12 metres (39 feet), the maximum is 10 guests plus the skipper. For 12-15 metres (39-49 feet), it is 12 guests. For 15-18 metres (49-59 feet), it is 18 guests. Some boats have a licence for 12 but can sleep 8. Always check the listing for the maximum day charter capacity. If your group is larger than 18, you need two catamarans or a superyacht. For a group of 20-30, charter two catamarans and raft them together at anchor. The cost per person drops because you share the skipper and fuel. For example, two 40-foot catamarans for 20 guests cost around EUR 3,000 total, or EUR 150 per person. That is cheaper than a single 60-foot motor yacht for 20 guests, which costs EUR 4,500-6,000. The search page lets you filter by max guests.

Seasonal Pricing and Booking Windows

High season (July-August) is the most expensive and books out 2-4 weeks in advance. A 40-foot catamaran for a full day costs EUR 2,500-3,000. Mid-season (May-June, September) is 15-20% cheaper, EUR 1,800-2,200. Low season (October-April) is 30-40% cheaper, EUR 1,200-1,600. Weekdays (Monday-Thursday) are 10-15% cheaper than weekends. Public holidays (Semana Santa, August 15, December 6-8) are peak rates. If you book less than 7 days in advance in high season, you may find last-minute cancellations at a discount. Some operators offer a 10% discount for repeat customers or for booking two consecutive days. Payment is usually 30% deposit by bank transfer or card, balance in cash or card on the day. Some operators accept PayPal. Always get a written confirmation with the boat name, skipper contact, and cancellation policy. The BoatHire24 blog has a seasonal pricing guide updated every year.

Food and Drink Options Onboard

Most catamarans have a galley with a fridge, a sink, and a gas stove. You can bring your own food and drink. Common choices: a Spanish spread of jamón, chorizo, manchego cheese, olives, bread, and tomato salad. For the grill, bring pre-marinated chicken, beef, or fish. The skipper can provide a barbecue on request for an extra EUR 50-100. Drinks: beer, wine, cava, soft drinks, and water. Some operators offer a catering package: a platter of cold meats and cheeses for EUR 20 per person, or a full hot meal for EUR 35 per person. Paella is popular: the hostess cooks it onboard. For a group of 12, a paella lunch costs around EUR 300-400 total. You can also order from a local restaurant and have it delivered to the marina. The skipper will help you arrange it. No glass bottles on the boat: use plastic cups. The skipper will provide a cooler with ice. You can also bring a cake for a birthday celebration. Just tell the skipper in advance.

What to Wear and Bring

Swimwear, a t-shirt, and a hat. The sun is strong even in spring. Bring a long-sleeved rash guard if you burn easily. A light jacket or fleece for the evening: the wind can pick up after 5pm. Non-slip shoes or bare feet on deck. No black-soled shoes that mark the deck. A dry bag for your phone and wallet. Sunscreen (factor 50, water-resistant). Sunglasses with a strap. A towel. A reusable water bottle. The boat has fresh water but bring your own if you are particular. If you plan to snorkel, the boat provides masks and fins, but bring your own if you have a good fit. A waterproof camera or a GoPro. Seasickness tablets if you are prone: take them 30 minutes before departure. The skipper will have ginger biscuits and wristbands as backup. For children: a life jacket (the boat has them in sizes, but check for toddlers). A favourite toy or book for quiet time. The skipper will brief you on safety at the start. Listen to the briefing.

Local Attractions Visible from the Catamaran

From Fuengirola bay, you see the Sohail Castle on a hill above the marina. It dates from the 10th century and is lit up at night. To the east, the Benalmádena marina has a Buddhist stupa and a cable car that goes to the top of Mount Calamorro. To the west, the Cabopino dunes are a protected area with rare plant species. Further west, the Marbella coastline has the Golden Mile with luxury hotels and villas. The La Concha mountain (1,215 metres) is visible on clear days. If you go south-west towards Estepona, you pass the Punta de la Doncella lighthouse and the old town with its whitewashed houses. The Strait of Gibraltar is the highlight: you can see the coast of Africa on a clear day. The skipper will point out the migratory birds and the dolphins. In spring, you might see orcas following the tuna. The Nerja caves are a 2-hour drive from Fuengirola, not visible from the catamaran, but you can combine a charter with a land trip if you have two days. The BoatHire24 team can arrange a multi-day itinerary.

Related guides

  • Catamaran Charter Marbella Wedding: Vows at Sea
  • Fishing Charter Puerto Banus Half Day: Morning Trip

title: Affordable Yacht Charter Marbella: Luxury Boats at Low Prices
metaDescription: Find affordable yacht charter Marbella with BoatHire24. Luxury boats, licensed skippers, low prices. Book your budget-friendly trip from Puerto Banús today.
h1: Affordable Yacht Charter Marbella: Luxury on a Budget
intro:
You want to cruise the Costa del Sol on a yacht without breaking the bank. It sounds like a contradiction, but affordable yacht charter Marbella is a real thing. At BoatHire24, we connect you with boat owners who offer luxury boats at prices that won't make you wince. Think motor yachts, catamarans, and sailing yachts, all with a licensed skipper included, starting from around EUR 300 for a half-day trip. No hidden fees, no nonsense.

Marbella's coastline, from Puerto Banús to Estepona, is made for exploring. You can anchor at Cala del Faro, spot dolphins near the Strait of Gibraltar, or just laze on deck with a cold drink. The secret is booking smart: choose the right boat, the right season, and the right port. We'll show you how to get that luxury experience for less.
bodyHtml:
What Does Affordable Mean on the Costa del Sol?

Affordable yacht charter Marbella usually means a half-day trip (4 hours) on a motor yacht or catamaran for a group of 6 to 10 people. Prices range from around EUR 300 to EUR 800, depending on the boat size, season, and extras. You get a licensed skipper, fuel, and basic amenities. That works out to about EUR 30 to EUR 80 per person, which is less than a fancy dinner. For a full day (8 hours), expect from EUR 600 to EUR 1,500. Compare that to a hotel room or a round of golf, and it starts to make sense.

The key is to avoid peak summer weekends (July and August) if you can. Book on a weekday or in May, June, or September. You'll find better availability and lower rates. Also, consider smaller boats like RIBs or licence-free day boats, which start at around EUR 200 for 4 hours. They're faster and more nimble, perfect for zipping between beach clubs.

Which Ports Should You Depart From?

Your affordable yacht charter Marbella experience starts at one of several ports. Puerto Banús is the most glamorous, with superyachts and designer shops, but it's also the busiest. Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo) is quieter and closer to the old town. Cabopino, near Cala del Faro, is small and charming. For a longer trip, Estepona offers a beautiful old town and access to the Strait of Gibraltar. Sotogrande is upscale but less crowded. Benalmádena is a bit further east but has good facilities.

Each port has its own vibe. If you want to see dolphins, head from Estepona or Sotogrande. For a sunset cruise, Puerto Banús or Marbella Marina work best. The distance between ports is about 10 to 30 nautical miles, so a half-day trip can cover two or three. Your skipper will handle the navigation, so you just relax.

Boat Options for Every Budget

Motor yachts are the most common for affordable yacht charter Marbella. A 30- to 40-foot motor yacht can carry 8 to 12 guests and costs from around EUR 400 for half a day. Catamarans offer more deck space and stability, ideal for families or groups prone to seasickness. They start from EUR 500 for half a day. Sailing yachts are cheaper, from EUR 300, and give you a more traditional experience. RIBs and speedboats are the budget champions, from EUR 200 for 4 hours, and they're fast enough to reach Marbella's beaches in minutes.

Licence-free day boats are a unique option. You don't need a boating licence, and they're simple to handle. They cost from EUR 150 for 2 hours. Perfect for a quick spin around Puerto Banús. Fishing boats are also available, from EUR 350 for half a day, with gear included. If you want to go big, superyachts are price on request, but you can sometimes find last-minute deals.

What's Included in the Price?

Every affordable yacht charter Marbella booking includes a licensed skipper. That's non-negotiable. The skipper knows the waters, the weather, and the best spots. Fuel is usually included for the agreed itinerary. Some boats include soft drinks, water, and ice. Others have a cooler you can fill yourself. Check the listing on BoatHire24 for specifics. Extras like food, snorkelling gear, or paddleboards might cost extra, or you can bring your own. Most boats have a Bluetooth speaker and a basic galley (kitchenette).

What's not included is a tip for the skipper, usually 10-15% of the charter fee. Also, port fees and cleaning fees might apply, but we list them upfront. You won't get surprised at the dock. Insurance is covered by the owner, but you should check the terms for damage deposits.

When Is the Best Time for an Affordable Charter?

The Costa del Sol has a Mediterranean climate, with over 300 sunny days a year. The best time for affordable yacht charter Marbella is May, June, and September. The weather is warm (24-30 degrees C), the sea is calm, and the crowds are thinner. Prices are lower than in July and August. April and October can work, but expect cooler temperatures (18-22 degrees C) and occasional wind. Winter charters are possible, but many boats are in maintenance, and the sea can be rough.

Book at least two weeks in advance for the best rates. Last-minute deals sometimes appear, but you risk limited availability. For a group of 10, a weekday charter in June can cost as little as EUR 50 per person. That's a bargain for a day on the water.

How to Book Your Affordable Yacht Charter Marbella

Start by searching on BoatHire24. Filter by port, boat type, and price. Look for listings that say "affordable" or "budget friendly." Read the description carefully. Check the cancellation policy. Some owners offer free cancellation up to 48 hours. Others are stricter. Contact the owner directly through our platform to ask questions. They're usually responsive and can customise the itinerary.

Once you book, you'll get a confirmation with the skipper's contact details. On the day, arrive 15 minutes early at the port. The skipper will do a safety briefing. Then you're off. Bring sunscreen, a hat, swimwear, and your own snacks if you want. Most boats have a fridge. Don't forget a camera for the views of La Concha mountain and the Marbella coastline.

Who Is Affordable Yacht Charter Marbella For?

This is for anyone who wants a taste of luxury without the price tag. Couples celebrating an anniversary, families with kids, groups of friends, or solo travellers looking to meet people. It's also for business clients who want to impress without overspending. The boats are clean, well-maintained, and the skippers are professional. You don't need any experience. Just show up and enjoy.

If you're visiting Marbella for a week, a half-day charter is a perfect break from the beach. You'll see the coastline from a new perspective. You might even spot the dolphins that play near the Strait of Gibraltar. For a longer trip, consider a full-day charter to Nerja caves, but that's a longer sail (around 3 hours each way).

For more tips on planning your trip, check our blog on the best beaches and anchorages in the area. We cover everything from Cala del Faro to the quiet coves near Estepona old town.

Common Questions About Affordable Yacht Charter Marbella

Can you negotiate the price? Sometimes, especially in low season or for repeat bookings. Ask the owner politely. They might offer a discount for cash or a longer charter. Is fuel really included? Yes, for the agreed itinerary. If you want to go far, like to Gibraltar, extra fuel costs might apply. Are children allowed? Yes, but life jackets for kids are provided. The skipper will ensure safety. What about seasickness? The coast is usually calm, but if you're prone, take medication beforehand. Catamarans are more stable.

Can you bring your own alcohol? Most owners allow it, but some have a corkage fee. Check the listing. Do you need a deposit? Some boats require a security deposit (refundable) of around EUR 200 to EUR 500. It's held via card or cash. You'll get it back if there's no damage.

Why Choose BoatHire24 for Your Charter?

We list only verified boats with licensed skippers. You book directly with the owner, so no middleman markups. Our search filters make it easy to find affordable yacht charter Marbella options. We have real-time availability and transparent pricing. No hidden fees. Plus, our customer support is based in Marbella, so we know the local scene. You can reach us by phone or email if you need help choosing.

Start planning your affordable yacht charter Marbella today. It's simpler than you think. You'll get luxury on the water without the luxury price tag. And you'll leave with memories that last longer than a sunburn.

How to Split Costs for a Group Charter

Affordable yacht charter Marbella works best when you split the bill. A half-day motor yacht for 8 people at EUR 600 means EUR 75 each. That is cheaper than a VIP table at a Puerto Banús nightclub. For 12 people on a catamaran at EUR 900, you are looking at EUR 75 per head again. Use a payment app like Bizum or Revolut to collect money upfront. One person books the boat on BoatHire24, and everyone else transfers their share. Avoid the awkwardness of collecting cash on the dock. If you are a solo traveller, some owners offer shared charters where you join a group. Prices start at around EUR 100 per person for a half-day trip. Check the listing for "shared" or "join-in" options.

Hidden Costs That Can Ruin Your Budget

Affordable yacht charter Marbella can turn expensive if you ignore the fine print. Some owners charge extra for port fees, which can be EUR 50 to EUR 150 per trip. Others add a cleaning fee of EUR 30 to EUR 80 if you leave the boat messy. Fuel is usually included for a standard itinerary, but if you ask the skipper to go to Marbella from Estepona and back, that is 30 nautical miles. Extra fuel might cost EUR 50 to EUR 100. Food and drinks are almost never included unless specified. A catering package from a local deli costs around EUR 20 to EUR 40 per person. Or you can bring your own picnic from Mercadona for EUR 10 per person. Always ask the owner for a full breakdown before booking.

Best Anchorages for a Budget Charter

Your affordable yacht charter Marbella skipper knows the free mooring spots. Cala del Faro near Cabopino is a sandy cove with clear water and no port fees. You drop anchor and swim. The beach club there charges EUR 5 for a drink, but you can stay on the boat. Another spot is the area off Río Verde, east of Puerto Banús. It is shallow and calm, perfect for paddleboarding. The beach of San Pedro de Alcántara has a long stretch of sand and a chiringuito (beach bar) where you can dock a small tender. For a more adventurous trip, head south from Estepona toward the Strait of Gibraltar. You might see dolphins, and the water is deeper and bluer. Just confirm with the skipper that the fuel is enough for the distance.

Why Weekday Charters Are Cheaper

Affordable yacht charter Marbella prices drop significantly on weekdays. A motor yacht that costs EUR 800 on a Saturday in July might be EUR 500 on a Tuesday. Owners know that demand is lower Monday to Thursday, so they discount to fill the calendar. You also get more attention from the skipper because the ports are quieter. In May or June, a weekday half-day charter can cost as little as EUR 300 for a 30-foot motor yacht. That is EUR 50 per person for a group of six. Compare that to EUR 150 per person for a weekend in August. If you are flexible, book a Tuesday or Wednesday for the best deal. Use the search filters on BoatHire24 to show only weekday availability.

What to Bring on a Budget Charter

Packing smart saves money on your affordable yacht charter Marbella. Bring a reusable water bottle; most boats have a tap or a water dispenser. Sunscreen is essential, but buy it at a supermarket in Marbella rather than at the port shop where it costs double. A hat and sunglasses are obvious. A dry bag for your phone and wallet costs EUR 10 on Amazon and stops saltwater damage. Snorkel gear is often available on board, but if not, a basic set costs EUR 15 at Decathlon. Towels are usually provided on larger boats, but bring your own for RIBs or speedboats. A cooler with ice packs is handy for keeping drinks cold. Most boats have a fridge, but it might be small. And bring a Bluetooth speaker for music; the boat's system might be old or broken.

How to Negotiate a Better Price

Affordable yacht charter Marbella prices are not always fixed. Owners are often willing to negotiate, especially in low season or for last-minute bookings. Start by checking the listing price on BoatHire24. Then send a polite message asking if there is any flexibility for a weekday in May. Offer to pay in cash, which saves the owner credit card fees. If you book for two consecutive days, ask for a 10% discount. Some owners will throw in extras like a free hour or a bottle of cava instead of a price reduction. Be respectful and realistic. A EUR 300 boat is not going to drop to EUR 100. But a EUR 600 boat might come down to EUR 500 if the owner has no other bookings that week.

Best Boat Types for a Tight Budget

If affordable yacht charter Marbella is your goal, skip the superyachts and focus on smaller vessels. RIBs and speedboats are the cheapest option, from EUR 200 for 4 hours. They are fast, easy to board, and perfect for a quick tour of the coast. The downside is limited shade and no toilet. Licence-free day boats are also budget-friendly, from EUR 150 for 2 hours. They are simple to drive, but you still have a skipper on board. For a group of 6 to 8, a 30-foot motor yacht offers a good balance of space and cost. Sailing yachts are another bargain, from EUR 300 for half a day. They are slower but quieter and more romantic. Catamarans are more expensive but offer more deck space, which can be worth it for a large group. Check the maximum capacity on the listing; some boats claim 12 guests but only have seating for 8.

How Weather Affects Your Charter

The Costa del Sol has a Mediterranean climate, but the wind can change your affordable yacht charter Marbella plans. The Levante wind blows from the east and can create choppy seas, especially near the Strait of Gibraltar. The Poniente wind from the west is milder. Your skipper will check the forecast and may suggest a different route or time. In summer, the sea is usually calm in the morning, with wind picking up after 2 PM. That is why half-day morning charters are often smoother and cheaper. If the weather is bad, most owners offer a free reschedule or a full refund. Read the cancellation policy before booking. Some owners charge a fee for cancellations within 48 hours, even for weather. Ask about this specifically if you are booking in a windy month like March or November.

Why You Should Book Directly with the Owner

Affordable yacht charter Marbella prices are lower when you book directly through a marketplace like BoatHire24 rather than a middleman. We connect you with the owner, so there are no agency commissions. You pay the same price as if you walked up to the boat at Puerto Banús. Plus, you can read the owner's profile, see photos of the actual boat, and read reviews from previous guests. Some owners offer discounts for repeat bookings or referrals. If you book through a third-party tour operator, they might add a 20% markup. Always compare prices on our site before committing. And if you find the same boat cheaper elsewhere, let us know and we will try to match it.

What to Do If the Boat Is Not as Described

Your affordable yacht charter Marbella experience should match the listing. If the boat is dirty, broken, or smaller than advertised, you have rights. First, talk to the skipper. They might offer a partial refund or a different boat. If that fails, contact BoatHire24 support within 24 hours. We will mediate with the owner. Take photos and videos as evidence. Most owners are honest, but a few cut corners. Booking through a marketplace gives you protection that a private deal does not. Always read the terms and conditions. Some boats have a "no refund" policy for minor issues like a broken speaker. For major problems like a non-functioning engine, you should get a full refund. Do not accept a verbal promise; get it in writing via our platform's messaging system.

faqs.a: Yes, for a half-day charter on a smaller motor yacht or sailing yacht, especially on a weekday in low season. Prices from around EUR 400. You get a licensed skipper, fuel, and basic amenities. It's luxury on a budget.

title: Catamaran Rental Puerto Banus Hourly: Book by the Hour
metaDescription: Book a catamaran rental Puerto Banus hourly with a licensed skipper. Compare rates for half-day, full-day or by the hour. Instant quotes on BoatHire24.
h1: Catamaran Rental Puerto Banus Hourly: Book by the Hour
intro:
You want to get on the water in Puerto Banus but you don't need a full day charter. A catamaran rental Puerto Banus hourly lets you take a short cruise, watch the sunset, or just escape the marina crowds for a couple of hours. Every boat comes with a licensed skipper, so you don't need a licence or experience. You pay for the time you use, nothing more.

BoatHire24 lists catamarans from 30 to 60 feet, available by the hour from Puerto Banus and nearby ports. Prices start from around EUR 150 per hour for a 30-foot catamaran, including fuel and crew. You book online, choose your slot, and turn up. The skipper handles navigation, mooring, and safety. You just bring drinks and sunscreen.
bodyHtml:
What You Get with an Hourly Catamaran Rental

An hourly catamaran rental in Puerto Banus is exactly what it sounds like: you hire the boat for a set number of hours, typically 2 to 6, and the skipper takes you out for a cruise. The boat is yours for that time. You can anchor for a swim, drift along the coast, or just motor around the bay. The skipper will suggest a route based on conditions and your preferences.

Included in the hourly rate: the boat, the licensed skipper, fuel, basic safety equipment, and usually a Bluetooth speaker. Some boats also have a small fridge, a sun pad on the netting, and a shaded cockpit. You don't pay extra for the crew or the fuel. What you do pay extra for: food and drinks (you bring your own or order from a catering service), dockage fees if you stop at a restaurant, and any damage you cause.

Most hourly rentals operate on a minimum of 2 hours. That's enough for a quick spin past the Puerto Banus skyline and a dip near the Rio Verde estuary. For sunset cruises, 3 hours is the sweet spot. For a longer trip up to the Cala del Faro or down to Estepona, you'll want 4 to 6 hours.

Puerto Banus: The Departure Port

Puerto Banus is the main hub for luxury yachts and catamarans on the Costa del Sol. The marina is packed with restaurants, shops, and superyachts. Your hourly catamaran rental departs from one of the charter company's berths inside the marina. You meet the skipper at the boat, usually 15 minutes before the booked time.

The advantage of departing from Puerto Banus: you're right in the centre of the action. Within 10 minutes of leaving the marina, you're past the breakwater and into open water. To the west, you have the coast towards Marbella and Cabopino. To the east, the Golden Mile and the La Concha mountain backdrop. The marina itself is worth a walk around before or after your charter.

Other ports close to Puerto Banus that also offer hourly catamaran rentals: Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo), Cabopino, and Estepona. If you're staying in Sotogrande or Benalmádena, you can find catamarans there too, but the options for hourly bookings are fewer. BoatHire24 covers all of them, but Puerto Banus has the highest number of hourly catamarans available.

Types of Catamarans Available by the Hour

Catamarans for hourly rental in Puerto Banus generally fall into three size categories: 30-35 feet, 38-45 feet, and 50-60 feet. The smaller ones are often older models, basic but functional. The mid-size ones are the most common: Lagoon 42s, Fountaine Pajot 40s, and Leopard 45s. The big ones are luxury cruising catamarans with flybridges, multiple cabins, and more deck space.

All of them have two hulls, which means stability. You won't get seasick easily, even if the sea is choppy. The netting between the hulls is perfect for sunbathing. The cockpit is usually shaded by a bimini or a hardtop. Most catamarans have a bow area with sun loungers. The skipper stays in the helm seat or the flybridge, so you have privacy.

If you want a party vibe, look for a catamaran with a powerful sound system and a grill. If you want a quiet family cruise, pick one with a small cabin and a toilet (heads). Some hourly rentals include use of a paddleboard or snorkelling gear. Check the boat's listing on BoatHire24 for exact features.

Pricing Guidance for Hourly Catamaran Rentals

Prices for a catamaran rental Puerto Banus hourly vary by boat size, age, and season. In low season (November to March), rates start from around EUR 150 per hour for a 30-foot catamaran. In high season (June to September), the same boat might be EUR 200-250 per hour. Mid-size catamarans (38-45 feet) run from EUR 250 to EUR 400 per hour. Large luxury catamarans (50-60 feet) start at EUR 500 per hour and can go up to EUR 1,000 or more.

These rates include the skipper and fuel. They do not include VAT (21% in Spain) unless stated. Some companies add a cleaning fee of EUR 50-100. Always confirm the total price before booking. You can see the exact price on each boat's page on BoatHire24, or request a quote for custom hours.

Minimum booking is usually 2 hours, but some companies require 3 or 4 hours for certain boats. The hourly rate often decreases if you book more hours. For example, a boat that costs EUR 200 per hour for 2 hours might drop to EUR 175 per hour for 4 hours. Always check the price breakdown on the listing.

Best Times of Year for an Hourly Catamaran Rental

The Costa del Sol has a Mediterranean climate with mild winters and hot summers. The best months for an hourly catamaran rental are May through October. In May and June, the sea temperature is around 18-22 degrees C, and the air is warm but not scorching. July and August are peak season: air temperatures often exceed 30 degrees C, sea temperature hits 24-26 degrees C. September and October are still warm, with fewer crowds.

Winter months (November to March) are cooler, with air temperatures around 15-20 degrees C and sea temperatures dropping to 15-17 degrees C. You can still charter a catamaran by the hour, but you'll want to bring a jacket. The advantage of winter: lower prices and empty beaches. The sunsets are earlier, so a 3-hour afternoon cruise works well.

Wind conditions: the prevailing wind in summer is the Poniente, a light westerly breeze. In winter, the Levante from the east can blow stronger, especially near the Strait of Gibraltar. Skippers will avoid going out if the wind exceeds 25 knots, but you can reschedule.

Popular Routes for an Hourly Catamaran Cruise

From Puerto Banus, the most common hourly route is a coastal cruise east towards the Rio Verde estuary and the Marbella coastline. You pass the luxury villas of the Golden Mile, the Marbella Club hotel, and the beach clubs. The skipper will anchor near the Rio Verde beach for a swim. The water is clear and shallow, good for snorkelling. This route takes about 1 hour each way, leaving 1 hour for anchoring.

Another route goes west towards the Cala del Faro, a small cove with a lighthouse. This is a quieter spot, less built up, with rocky cliffs and pine trees. The sea here is deeper, so anchoring is possible but you'll need a longer rope. This route is better for a 3-hour charter because it takes about 1.5 hours to reach the cove.

If you want to see dolphins, the skipper can head south towards the Strait of Gibraltar, but that requires at least 4 hours. The dolphin pods are common in the summer months, especially near the Alboran Sea. For a sunset cruise, the skipper will take you west to watch the sun drop behind the Sierra Blanca mountains. The light on La Concha is worth the trip alone.

How to Book an Hourly Catamaran Rental on BoatHire24

Booking is straightforward. Go to BoatHire24 and search for catamarans in Puerto Banus. Use the filter to select 'hourly' or 'per hour' under the pricing options. You'll see a list of available boats with photos, specs, and hourly rates. Click on a boat to see the full details, including what's included, the skipper's bio, and the cancellation policy.

Choose your date and time. Most boats allow you to book a specific slot, like 10:00-12:00 or 16:00-19:00. You'll pay a deposit (usually 30-50%) to confirm the booking. The balance is due on the day, either in cash or card. Some companies require full prepayment for hourly rentals. Read the terms carefully.

If you're unsure which boat suits you, use the search page to compare side by side. You can also contact the charter company directly through the BoatHire24 messaging system. They'll answer within a few hours. For more tips on choosing a catamaran, check the blog for guides on boat types and what to expect.

Who Should Book an Hourly Catamaran Rental

Hourly catamaran rentals are ideal for small groups, couples, or families who want a short, affordable taste of the sea. If you're staying in Marbella for a weekend and don't have time for a full day charter, a 2-hour sunset cruise is perfect. If you're celebrating a birthday or an anniversary, a 3-hour trip with champagne and snacks works well. If you're a solo traveller, some boats allow you to join a shared hourly charter, though that's less common.

Business groups also use hourly rentals for client entertainment or team building. You can combine a short cruise with a lunch at a beach club. The skipper can drop you off at a restaurant dock and pick you up later. Just confirm the dockage fees in advance.

If you want a party boat, look for catamarans that allow loud music and large groups. Some boats have a limit of 12 passengers due to insurance. Check the passenger capacity on the listing. For a quiet family trip, choose a smaller boat with fewer passengers.

What to Bring on an Hourly Catamaran Charter

Bring sunscreen (SPF 50 minimum, the sun is strong), a hat, sunglasses, and a towel. Wear non-marking soles on your shoes or go barefoot. Bring a light jacket or windbreaker even in summer because the wind on the water can be cool. For drinks, bring your own or order from a catering service. Most catamarans have a cooler with ice. Avoid glass bottles, they're banned on many boats.

If you plan to swim, bring a swimsuit and a dry bag for your phone. The skipper will provide life jackets if needed. Snorkelling gear is sometimes included, but check the listing. For a sunset cruise, bring a camera. The light on the water and the mountains is spectacular.

Don't bring: heavy luggage, large coolers (the boat has limited space), or pets unless the listing specifically allows them. Some boats allow small dogs, but ask first.

Safety and Skipper Information

Every catamaran rental on BoatHire24 includes a licensed skipper. The skipper holds a Spanish Professional Skipper Licence (PER or Patrón de Yate) and has insurance. They are responsible for the safety of the boat and passengers. They will give a short safety briefing before departure, covering life jacket locations, emergency procedures, and the toilet.

The skipper decides the route based on weather and sea conditions. If the sea is rough, they may stay in sheltered waters. If there's a storm warning, they may cancel the charter. In that case, you get a full refund or reschedule. The skipper is not a tour guide, but they know the coast well and can point out landmarks and wildlife.

You are expected to follow the skipper's instructions. No jumping off the boat without permission, no reckless behaviour, and no excessive drinking that puts safety at risk. If you have any medical conditions, inform the skipper before departure.

Frequently Asked Questions

Why Choose an Hourly Rental Over a Full-Day Charter

The main advantage of booking a catamaran rental Puerto Banus hourly is flexibility. A full-day charter typically runs 8 hours and costs EUR 1,200-3,000, which is overkill if you just want a quick afternoon on the water. Hourly rentals let you test the experience without the commitment. You can book a 2-hour slot between lunch and dinner, or a 3-hour sunset cruise after work. The total cost is lower, and you don't waste time sitting around waiting for the day to end. For first-time visitors to Marbella, an hourly rental is a low-risk way to see if you enjoy being on a catamaran. If you love it, you can book a full day next time. If you don't, you've only lost a couple of hours and a few hundred euros.

How Hourly Catamaran Rentals Compare to Other Boat Types

Catamarans are the most stable option for hourly rentals, but they are not the only one. Motor yachts in Puerto Banus also offer hourly charters, typically starting at EUR 100-150 per hour for a 25-foot boat. The difference: motor yachts are faster (20-30 knots vs 8-12 knots for a catamaran), so you can cover more distance in the same time. But they have less deck space and a rougher ride in choppy seas. Sailing yachts are cheaper per hour (EUR 80-120) but slower and require more skill from the skipper to tack and jibe. RIBs and speedboats are the cheapest hourly option at EUR 60-100 per hour, but they are open boats with no shade and limited comfort. For a group of 6-12 people who want to relax, socialise, and swim, a catamaran is the best choice. For a couple who wants a fast run to a beach club, a motor yacht might be better. You can compare all options on the search page by filtering by boat type and hourly pricing.

What Happens If You Want to Extend an Hourly Rental

Sometimes you book a 2-hour catamaran rental Puerto Banus hourly and decide you want more time. Most charter companies allow extensions if the boat is available. You simply tell the skipper, and they contact the office to extend the booking. The additional hours are charged at the same hourly rate or sometimes a premium if it's last minute. For example, if you booked 14:00-16:00 and want to stay until 18:00, you pay for two more hours. The skipper will adjust the route to make the most of the extra time. Extensions are easier in low season when boats are less booked. In August, the next group might be waiting, so extensions are rarely possible. Always ask the skipper early if you think you might want to extend, so they can check availability. If you know in advance you'll want more time, book 3 or 4 hours from the start to avoid disappointment.

What to Do Around Puerto Banus Before or After Your Charter

Puerto Banus is a destination in itself, so plan to spend some time there before or after your hourly catamaran rental. The marina has dozens of restaurants serving Spanish, Italian, and international cuisine. For a pre-charter breakfast or coffee, try one of the cafes on the waterfront. For lunch after your cruise, the beach clubs like Ocean Club or Nikki Beach are a short walk from the marina. The shopping area has designer boutiques (Gucci, Louis Vuitton, Cartier) if you want to browse. The port is also a good spot for people-watching, especially in the evening when the superyachts are lit up. If you have time, walk to the nearby dunes of Artola or the Roman ruins at Villa Romano. For more ideas on what to do in the area, check the blog for local guides on Marbella and Puerto Banus.

faqs.q: Can I book a catamaran rental in Puerto Banus for just 1 hour?
faqs.a: Most charters require a minimum of 2 hours for a catamaran rental Puerto Banus hourly. Some companies offer 1-hour sunset cruises, but they are rare. The minimum is usually 2 hours to cover the skipper's time and fuel costs.

faqs.q: What is the average cost for a 3-hour catamaran rental in Puerto Banus?
faqs.a: For a 30-35 foot catamaran, expect around EUR 450-600 in high season for 3 hours. For a 40-45 foot catamaran, EUR 750-1,200. Prices include skipper and fuel. Always check the exact rate on the listing.

faqs.q: Do I need a boating licence to rent a catamaran by the hour?
faqs.a: No. Every hourly catamaran rental on BoatHire24 includes a licensed skipper. You do not need any licence or experience. The skipper handles everything.

faqs.q: Can I bring my own food and drinks on an hourly catamaran charter?
faqs.a: Yes, you can bring your own food and drinks. Most catamarans have a cooler with ice. Avoid glass bottles. Some companies offer catering packages at an extra cost.

faqs.q: What happens if the weather is bad on the day of my hourly rental?
faqs.a: The skipper will assess conditions and may cancel if winds exceed 25 knots or there is a storm warning. You will receive a full refund or can reschedule. Check the cancellation policy on the listing.

faqs.q: Are hourly catamaran rentals available in winter?
faqs.a: Yes, but availability is lower. The best months are May to October. In winter (November to March), you can still book, but the sea is cooler and days are shorter. Prices are lower.

faqs.q: How many passengers can fit on an hourly catamaran rental?
faqs.a: Capacity varies by boat. Small catamarans (30-35 feet) carry up to 10-12 passengers. Larger ones (50-60 feet) can carry up to 18-20 passengers. Check the passenger limit on the boat's page.

title: Luxury Motor Yacht Hire Marbella: Premium Charter Deals
metaDescription: Book luxury motor yacht hire Marbella with a licensed skipper. Explore Puerto Banús, Costa del Sol. Premium charters from EUR 1,500. Compare deals now.
h1: Luxury Motor Yacht Hire Marbella: Premium Charter Deals
intro:
You want to know the best way to charter a luxury motor yacht in Marbella without the nonsense. Here it is. Luxury motor yacht hire Marbella means getting on a boat that is at least 15 metres long, with a crew that knows the coast, and a price that reflects the fact you are not on a budget. At BoatHire24 we list the real inventory from local operators, not some broker who has never been to Puerto Banús. Every booking includes a licensed skipper, so you can relax and let someone else handle the paperwork.

The Costa del Sol has more than 300 days of sun a year, and the water temperature hits 24 degrees C in August. You can depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, or any of the other ports we cover. The typical luxury motor yacht charter runs from 4 hours to a full day, and you can book a vessel that sleeps up to 12 guests. Prices start from around EUR 1,500 for a half-day on a 40-foot yacht, going up to EUR 10,000 or more for a 30-metre superyacht with a full crew.

If you want to skip the sales pitch and just see what is available, go to our search page and filter by length, price, and availability. Or keep reading for the details that actually matter.
bodyHtml:
What You Get with Luxury Motor Yacht Hire Marbella

When you book a luxury motor yacht through BoatHire24, you get a boat that is maintained to commercial standards, a licensed skipper who knows the local waters, and a contract that covers insurance and liability. The skipper is not just a driver. They handle docking, navigation, and safety briefings. You do not need a boating licence. You do not need to worry about fuel costs or cleaning up afterwards. The price includes the crew, the fuel for a standard itinerary, and basic insurance. Extras like food, drinks, jet skis, or water toys are usually charged separately and can be arranged in advance.

The yachts we list range from 12-metre motor yachts with a single cabin up to 40-metre superyachts with five cabins, a jacuzzi, and a flybridge. All of them have a licensed skipper, and most have a steward or deckhand for larger groups. You can expect air conditioning, a sound system, a sunbathing area, and a bathroom with a shower. Some have a galley where the crew can prepare food if you bring provisions. The standard is high, but you are paying for it.

Departure Ports for Your Charter

You have six main ports to choose from. Puerto Banús is the most famous, with the biggest concentration of luxury yachts and the most expensive restaurants. It is also the busiest. Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo) is quieter and closer to the town centre. Cabopino is small and relaxed, near the dunes of Artola. Estepona has a modern marina with good facilities and easy access to the western coast. Sotogrande is further west, near the Gibraltar border, and attracts a more exclusive crowd. Benalmádena is east of Marbella and has a large marina with plenty of charter options.

Each port has its own character. If you want to be seen, Puerto Banús is where the Ferraris park next to the 40-metre yachts. If you want a peaceful cruise along the coast with fewer crowds, try Estepona or Cabopino. The distance between ports is about 15 to 30 nautical miles, so a half-day charter from one port can easily include a stop at another for lunch. For example, you can leave Puerto Banús, cruise past the cliffs of Cala del Faro, anchor for a swim, and then head to Estepona old town for paella. The skipper will advise on the best route based on weather and your preferences.

Boat Options for Luxury Motor Yacht Hire Marbella

Not all luxury motor yachts are the same. You have flybridge yachts with an upper deck for sunbathing and dining, sport yachts with a more streamlined design and higher speeds, and superyachts that are essentially floating hotels. Flybridge yachts are the most common for charter because they offer space for groups. Sport yachts are faster and more fun if you want to feel the speed, but they have less deck space. Superyachts are for those who want a crew of four or five, a jacuzzi, and a tender for trips to shore.

Sizes range from 12 metres (about 40 feet) to 30 metres (100 feet) and beyond. A 12-metre yacht typically has one or two cabins and can take up to six guests. A 20-metre yacht has three cabins and can take up to ten guests. A 30-metre yacht has five cabins and can take up to twelve guests. The number of guests is limited by the boat's safety certificate, not by how many people can squeeze on. You can find specific models like the Azimut 60, Princess 72, or Sunseeker 88 on our platform. Each listing includes photos, specifications, and the exact port location.

For a full list of available boats, check our search page and filter by length or price.

Pricing Guidance for Luxury Motor Yacht Hire Marbella

Prices vary depending on the size of the yacht, the season, and the duration. As a rough guide, a half-day (4 hours) on a 12-metre motor yacht in low season (November to February) starts from around EUR 1,500. A full day (8 hours) on the same boat is about EUR 2,500. In high season (June to September), prices can double. A 20-metre yacht in high season for a full day is typically EUR 4,000 to EUR 6,000. A 30-metre superyacht is price on request, but expect EUR 8,000 to EUR 15,000 per day.

These prices include the skipper, fuel for the standard itinerary (usually up to 60 nautical miles), and insurance. Extras like food, drinks, jet skis (EUR 200-400 per hour), or water toys are not included. Some operators offer all-inclusive packages with catering and drinks for an additional fee. You can also book a sunset charter for a shorter duration, typically 2 to 3 hours, which costs less but still gives you the luxury experience. Always confirm what is included before you pay.

For the best value, book in advance and avoid the peak weeks of August. May and September offer good weather with lower prices. You can see real-time availability and prices on our platform, but note that some superyachts require a direct inquiry because the owners are picky about who they charter to.

Seasons and Weather on the Costa del Sol

The Costa del Sol has a Mediterranean climate with mild winters and hot summers. The charter season runs from March to November, with the peak being June to September. In July and August, temperatures hit 30 to 35 degrees C, and the sea is calm most days. The wind picks up in the afternoon, but it rarely stops a charter. In spring and autumn, you get 20 to 25 degrees C, which is pleasant for cruising but cooler for swimming. Winter charters are possible but less common because the water is around 15 degrees C and the days are shorter.

The prevailing wind is from the west, so the sea state is usually calm in the morning and choppier in the afternoon. Skippers plan itineraries to avoid rough conditions. The Strait of Gibraltar, about 50 nautical miles from Marbella, can have strong currents and winds, but most charters stick to the coast between Benalmádena and Sotogrande. If you want to see dolphins, the area near Gibraltar is reliable, but it is a longer trip. A full-day charter from Marbella to Gibraltar and back is doable but tight on time.

How to Book a Luxury Motor Yacht in Marbella

Booking through BoatHire24 is straightforward. You go to our site, search by port and date, compare listings, and send a booking request. The operator confirms availability and sends a contract. You pay a deposit (usually 50%) to secure the date, and the balance is due a week before the charter. Cancellation policies vary, but most operators offer a full refund if you cancel 30 days before. For last-minute bookings, you can sometimes pay the full amount and get a discount.

We recommend booking at least two weeks in advance for high season. For superyachts, book a month or more ahead. If you have a specific boat in mind, contact us directly and we can negotiate with the owner. Do not book through random Instagram ads. Use a platform that lists real boats with real prices. BoatHire24 is the one.

Who Is Luxury Motor Yacht Hire Marbella For

This is for people who want to celebrate something, impress someone, or just spend a day on the water without the hassle of owning a boat. It is for corporate events, birthdays, anniversaries, bachelor parties, and family reunions. It is also for couples who want a private cruise with a champagne brunch. If you are a group of 6 to 12 adults, a luxury motor yacht is the right size. If you are a solo traveller or a couple, you can still charter a smaller yacht, but the cost per person is higher.

It is not for budget travellers or backpackers. If you want a cheap day out, rent a licence-free boat for EUR 300 and drive it yourself. But if you want a crewed yacht with a professional skipper, a clean boat, and no surprises, luxury motor yacht hire is the way to go. The average client is 35 to 60 years old, with disposable income and a taste for the finer things. They know what they want and they do not want to haggle.

For more tips on what to look for when hiring a yacht, read our blog.

Local Highlights You Can Visit on Your Charter

The coast between Benalmádena and Sotogrande offers plenty of spots to anchor and explore. Cala del Faro is a small cove near Marbella with clear water and rocks for jumping. The Río Verde estuary is a nature reserve where you can see birds and maybe a few fish jumping. Estepona old town has a charming square with orange trees and a church that dates back to the 18th century. If you go further west, you can see the coast of Africa on a clear day and watch dolphins playing in the wake of the boat.

For a longer trip, you can go east to Nerja and see the caves, but that is a full-day journey from Marbella. Most clients prefer to stay within 20 nautical miles of their departure port and spend time swimming, eating, and sunbathing. The skipper will suggest a route based on the wind and your interests. You are not stuck on a fixed itinerary. You can change plans on the day if the weather is good.

What to Bring and What to Expect

Bring sunscreen, a hat, sunglasses, a towel, and a change of clothes. The boat will have a bathroom, but it is small. Do not bring high heels or black-soled shoes because they mark the deck. Most yachts have a shoe policy: barefoot or soft soles. You can bring your own food and drink, or arrange catering through the operator. Some yachts have a minimum spend on catering, so check before you buy. Alcohol is allowed, but the skipper will cut you off if you get too drunk. Safety first.

The crew will give a safety briefing at the start. Listen to it. They will show you where the life jackets are, how to use the toilet, and where not to go. If you have children, tell the crew in advance so they can prepare. Children under 12 usually need a life jacket when on deck. The boat will have a first aid kit and a radio for emergencies. In the unlikely event of a problem, the skipper knows the local coastguard and hospitals.

Why Book with BoatHire24

We are a marketplace, not a broker. We list boats from multiple operators, so you can compare prices and features without calling ten different companies. Every boat on our site is verified, with photos and specifications. We do not charge you a booking fee. The price you see is the price the operator charges. We make money from a commission paid by the operator. This means you get a fair deal, and we have an incentive to keep the operators honest.

We cover the entire Costa del Sol, from Benalmádena to Sotogrande, and we know the local market. If you have a special request, like a specific model or a custom itinerary, we can handle it. Our support team is based in Spain and speaks English and Spanish. You can call or email us, and we will answer within a few hours. We have been doing this since 2018, and we have helped hundreds of clients charter luxury motor yachts in Marbella. Book with confidence.

Related guides
