  • Private Catamaran Hire Marbella Sunset
  • Motor Yacht Rental Estepona Day: Luxury on Water
  • Catamaran Charter Marbella Birthday: Party Package

faqs.q: Do I need a boating licence to hire a luxury motor yacht in Marbella?
faqs.a: No. Every charter includes a licensed skipper who handles the boat. You just relax and enjoy the ride.

faqs.q: What is the average cost of luxury motor yacht hire in Marbella?
faqs.a: Half-day charters start from around EUR 1,500 for a 12-metre yacht in low season. Full-day rates for larger yachts can reach EUR 10,000 or more.

faqs.q: Can I choose my departure port?
faqs.a: Yes. You can depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona, Sotogrande, or Benalmádena. The skipper will meet you at the marina.

faqs.q: What is included in the price?
faqs.a: The skipper, fuel for the standard itinerary, and insurance are included. Food, drinks, and water toys are usually extra.

faqs.q: How many guests can a luxury motor yacht accommodate?
faqs.a: It depends on the yacht. A 12-metre yacht can take up to 6 guests. A 30-metre superyacht can take up to 12 guests. Safety limits apply.

faqs.q: What is the best time of year for a luxury motor yacht charter in Marbella?
faqs.a: May to September offers the best weather, with July and August being the hottest and busiest. September is quieter with still-warm water.

faqs.q: Can I cancel my booking?
faqs.a: Cancellation policies vary by operator, but most offer a full refund if you cancel at least 30 days before the charter. Check the terms before booking.

title: Speedboat Rental Marbella Port: Quick Bookings Available
metaDescription: Book a speedboat rental Marbella port with a licensed skipper. Quick bookings at Puerto Banús. Prices from EUR 350. No experience needed.
h1: Speedboat Rental Marbella Port: Book Your Rib Today
intro: You want to get on the water fast, without the usual booking headache. A speedboat rental Marbella port is the quickest way to blast across the bay, feel the Costa del Sol sun on your face, and maybe spot a dolphin or two. At BoatHire24, we handle the paperwork, the skipper, and the fuel, so you just show up at the port and go. We work with the main departure ports from Puerto Banús to Estepona, and every boat comes with a licensed skipper who knows the local waters. No licence needed on your part. Just pick a date, choose a boat, and we confirm within hours.
bodyHtml:
What You Get with a Speedboat Rental in Marbella Port

When you book through BoatHire24, the speedboat is ready at the marina with a full tank of fuel, a professional skipper, and safety equipment. You are not renting a bare boat. You are chartering the whole experience. The skipper handles navigation, mooring, and any questions you have about the coastline. You just sit back, or take the wheel if you want. Most speedboats are RIBs (rigid inflatable boats) with outboard engines, ranging from 5 to 12 metres in length. They hit 30 to 50 knots, depending on the model and sea conditions. Typical trips last 2, 4, or 8 hours. You can customise the route: head east toward Cabopino and Cala del Faro, or west toward Estepona and the Strait of Gibraltar. The skipper knows the best spots for swimming, snorkelling, and photo stops. Lunch at a beach club can be arranged, but you pay separately for food and drinks.

Why Choose Marbella Port for Your Speedboat Rental

Marbella port, specifically Puerto Deportivo de Marbella, sits right in the centre of the action. It is a short walk from the old town and the beachfront promenade. The port has parking, restaurants, and a fuel dock. Departing from here means you are minutes from the open sea. The coastline is a mix of sandy coves and rocky cliffs. You can cruise past the luxury villas of the Golden Mile, see the La Concha mountain rising behind the city, and anchor at a quiet beach for a swim. The water is calm most of the year, especially from May to October. Average sea temperatures range from 18 degrees C in May to 26 degrees C in August. The wind is usually light, making for a smooth ride. If you want a longer trip, you can head to the Nerja caves or the dolphin colonies near Gibraltar. The skipper will adjust the itinerary based on your group's preferences and the weather.

Speedboat Rental Marbella Port: Boat Options and Prices

The speedboats available for rental in Marbella port vary by size, engine power, and amenities. A typical 6-metre RIB with a 150 hp outboard costs from around EUR 350 for a 2-hour trip. A larger 9-metre RIB with twin engines, a sunpad, and a bimini top runs from EUR 600 for 2 hours. For bigger groups, up to 12 passengers, there are 12-metre RIBs with cabins and toilets, priced from EUR 900 for 2 hours. All prices include the skipper, fuel, and insurance. You do not pay extra for the driver. Some boats have Bluetooth speakers, a cooler box, and a shower. If you want to go fishing, there are specialised speedboats with rod holders and fish finders. The best way to see current availability is to search our listings for your preferred date and group size. Prices are higher in July and August, and lower in the shoulder months. You can book online with a deposit, and pay the balance on the day.

Departure Ports for Your Speedboat Rental

Marbella port is one of several departure points we serve. You can also start from Puerto Banús, which is 10 minutes west by car. Puerto Banús is the glitzy marina with superyachts, designer shops, and a lively atmosphere. If you want a quieter start, Cabopino port is 15 minutes east of Marbella, with a small fishing harbour and a long sandy beach. Estepona port is 30 minutes west, with a charming old town and a large marina. Sotogrande is further west, a luxury resort with a golf course and a calm marina. Benalmádena is east of Marbella, near the cable car and the marina. Each port has its own character, but the boats are the same quality. You choose the port that is most convenient for your accommodation. If you are staying in Marbella, the port is the obvious choice. If you are in Puerto Banús, you can walk to the boat. We can also arrange transfers from your hotel to the port for an extra fee.

Best Time for a Speedboat Rental Marbella Port

The season runs from April to October, with peak months in July and August. In April and May, the water is cooler, around 17 to 20 degrees C, but the sun is strong and the beaches are less crowded. June and September offer warm water and pleasant temperatures, 25 to 30 degrees C air temperature. July and August are hot, often above 35 degrees C, and the sea is at its warmest. The wind is usually light in the morning, picking up in the afternoon. Most trips are scheduled in the morning, 10am to 2pm, or afternoon, 2pm to 6pm. You can also book a sunset cruise, from 6pm to 9pm, which is popular for couples and small groups. The sunset over the Mediterranean is spectacular from the water. If you want to see dolphins, the Strait of Gibraltar is a good option, but it is a longer trip, around 3 to 4 hours from Marbella port. The skipper will advise on the best time based on tide and weather forecasts.

How to Book Your Speedboat Rental Marbella Port

Booking is straightforward. Go to our BoatHire24 homepage, enter your preferred date, port (Marbella), and group size. You will see a list of available speedboats with photos, specs, and prices. Select the boat you like, choose a duration (2, 4, or 8 hours), and proceed to checkout. You pay a deposit, usually 30% of the total, by credit card or PayPal. The balance is paid in cash to the skipper on the day of the trip. We send you a confirmation email with the meeting point, skipper contact, and any instructions. If you need to cancel, you can do so up to 48 hours before the trip for a full refund of the deposit. We recommend booking at least a week in advance during peak season, as popular boats sell out quickly. If you have a last-minute request, call us or use the live chat on the website. We can often find a boat within 24 hours.

Who Is a Speedboat Rental Marbella Port For

A speedboat rental is ideal for small groups, couples, and families. The boats carry up to 12 passengers, but most are for 6 to 8. If you have a larger group, consider a catamaran or a motor yacht. Speedboats are perfect for a half-day adventure, a birthday celebration, or a corporate outing. You do not need any boating experience. The skipper handles everything. If you want to drive, you can, as long as you are over 18 and have a valid driving licence. The skipper will give you a quick briefing. Speedboats are not for overnight stays. They are day boats with basic amenities. If you want to sleep on board, look for a sailing yacht or a motor yacht with cabins. For a quick thrill, a speedboat is the best choice. You get the wind in your hair and the speed without the commitment of a longer charter.

Safety and Skipper Information

All our skippers are licensed by the Spanish Maritime Authority. They have years of experience in the Costa del Sol waters. They speak English and Spanish. They carry first aid kits, life jackets for all passengers, and a VHF radio. The boats are inspected regularly and insured. Before departure, the skipper gives a safety briefing. You must wear a life jacket if you are not a strong swimmer, and children under 12 must wear one at all times. The skipper monitors weather conditions and can change the itinerary if there is a risk of storms or high winds. Your safety is the priority. If the weather is bad, we reschedule or refund your deposit. Do not worry about seasickness. The water is usually calm, and the boat is stable. If you are prone to motion sickness, take a pill an hour before the trip.

What to Bring on Your Speedboat Rental

Pack light. Bring swimwear, a towel, sunscreen (SPF 50+), sunglasses, a hat, and a light jacket for the wind. You can bring your own food and drinks, but glass bottles are not allowed. Use plastic or cans. Some boats have a cooler, but bring your own if you have specific items. The skipper often has a Bluetooth speaker, but you can bring your own. Do not bring large bags or valuables. There is limited storage on a speedboat. If you plan to swim, bring a waterproof phone case. The skipper can take photos of your group. You can also bring a GoPro. If you want to fish, some boats have fishing gear, but check in advance. You can keep any fish you catch, subject to local regulations.

Local Attractions Near Marbella Port

Marbella port is surrounded by things to do. The old town, with its orange trees and narrow streets, is a 10-minute walk. The beachfront promenade, Paseo Marítimo, stretches for kilometres. You can rent a bike or just stroll. The port itself has several good restaurants and bars. If you have time before or after your speedboat trip, visit the Museo del Grabado Español Contemporáneo or the Parque de la Constitución. For a more active day, hike the La Concha mountain trail, which offers panoramic views of the coast. The cable car in Benalmádena is also a popular attraction. For a different perspective, take a day trip to the Nerja caves, about 45 minutes east by car. The caves are spectacular, with stalactites and stalagmites. If you are interested in wildlife, the dolphin watching trips from the Strait of Gibraltar are unforgettable. You can combine a speedboat rental with a visit to these attractions, but you need to plan your time carefully.

Frequently Asked Questions About Speedboat Rental Marbella Port

We hear the same questions from our customers. Here are the answers to the most common ones. For more details, read our blog on boat rentals in Marbella.

Do I need a boat licence to rent a speedboat?

No. All our speedboat rentals include a licensed skipper. You do not need any licence or experience. The skipper is in charge. If you want to drive, you can, but the skipper supervises.

Can I bring my own alcohol?

Yes, you can bring beer, wine, or spirits. But glass bottles are not allowed on the boat. Use plastic cups or cans. The skipper will not serve alcohol to minors or anyone who is intoxicated.

What happens if the weather is bad?

If the weather is unsafe, we cancel the trip and refund your deposit or reschedule for another day. The skipper makes the call. You do not lose your money.

How many people can fit on a speedboat?

Most speedboats carry 6 to 8 passengers. Some larger RIBs carry up to 12. The boat's capacity is listed on the booking page. Children count as passengers. Life jackets are provided for all sizes.

Is there parking at Marbella port?

Yes, there is a large car park at Puerto Deportivo de Marbella. It costs around EUR 2 per hour. There is also a free parking area a short walk away, but it fills up quickly in summer. We recommend arriving 30 minutes before your departure time.

Can I book a speedboat for a stag or hen party?

Yes, speedboats are popular for celebrations. The skipper can arrange a stop at a beach club for lunch or drinks. Just let us know in advance. We do not allow loud music that disturbs other boats or the environment.

How far in advance should I book?

For peak season (July and August), book at least 2 weeks ahead. For other months, a week is usually enough. We also accept last-minute bookings if boats are available. Use the search tool to check availability.

Related guides

  • Marbella vs Puerto Banús: Which Departure Port to Choose

title: Private Yacht Charter Marbella Birthday: Celebrate at Sea
metaDescription: Celebrate your birthday with a private yacht charter in Marbella. Book a boat with skipper from Puerto Banús. Prices from around EUR 1,200. Reserve now.
h1: Private Yacht Charter Marbella Birthday: Celebrate at Sea
intro:
You want a birthday that doesn't involve queuing for a bar or fighting for table space. A private yacht charter in Marbella for your birthday gives you the whole Mediterranean as your venue, with a licensed skipper who does the navigating while you do the celebrating. No crowds, no set menus, no last-minute cancellations.

BoatHire24 connects you with motor yachts, catamarans, and sailing yachts based out of Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona, Sotogrande, and Benalmádena. You pick the boat, we handle the booking, and the skipper takes care of the rest. Prices start from around EUR 1,200 for a half-day charter, depending on the size and type of vessel.
bodyHtml:
What a Private Yacht Charter for Your Birthday Includes

When you book a private yacht charter in Marbella for your birthday through BoatHire24, the package is straightforward. Every charter includes a licensed skipper who knows the local waters, fuel for the agreed itinerary, and the boat itself for the duration of your hire. You do not need a boating licence or any prior experience. The skipper handles mooring, navigation, and safety, so you can focus on your guests and the cake.

Most charters also include basic safety equipment, life jackets, and a first-aid kit. Some boats come with a sound system for music, a sun deck for lounging, and a shaded saloon for when the Andalusian sun gets too enthusiastic. You can often add extras like a private chef, a bartender, or water toys such as paddleboards and snorkelling gear. Check the specific boat listing for what is included, and ask us about upgrades when you book.

One thing to note: the skipper is there for navigation and safety, not for serving drinks or cleaning up. If you want a full catering service, you need to arrange that separately, or book a boat that offers a crew package. We can help you find options that include a host or chef.

Departure Ports for Your Birthday Charter

Your private yacht charter Marbella birthday can start from any of the main ports along the Costa del Sol. Each has its own character and advantages.

Puerto Banús is the obvious choice if you want glitz and glamour. It is the most famous marina on the coast, lined with luxury boutiques and restaurants. Departing from here puts you right in the middle of the action, and you can moor up for a drink at one of the waterfront bars before or after your charter. The marina is crowded in summer, so allow extra time for boarding.

Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo) is a more laid-back alternative. It is closer to Marbella's old town and the Golden Mile, and it tends to be less hectic than Puerto Banús. You get easy parking and a shorter walk to the boat.

Cabopino is a small, charming port near the dunes of Artola. It is quieter and more relaxed, ideal if you want a low-key start to your birthday celebrations. The beach at Cabopino is one of the least crowded on the coast.

Estepona offers a traditional Andalusian feel. The marina is modern but the town behind it has whitewashed streets and flower-filled squares. Departing from Estepona gives you quick access to the waters around the Strait of Gibraltar, where you can spot dolphins.

Sotogrande is an exclusive resort area with a large marina and a polo club. It is further west, so it works well if you are staying in that part of the coast. The surrounding coastline is less developed, with more open water.

Benalmádena is a lively port with a big marina and plenty of restaurants. It is close to the airport and Torremolinos, making it convenient for groups flying in for the weekend.

Boat Options for a Birthday Celebration

The boat you choose for your private yacht charter Marbella birthday depends on your group size, budget, and preferred style. Here is a breakdown of the main types available.

Motor yachts are the most popular choice for birthdays. They are fast, spacious, and come with plenty of deck space for sunbathing and socialising. A 40-foot motor yacht can comfortably take 8 to 10 guests. Prices for a half-day charter on a mid-range motor yacht start from around EUR 1,500. Larger vessels, like a 60-foot yacht, can handle up to 12 guests and cost from EUR 2,500 for a half day.

Catamarans offer more stability and space. They are wider than monohull motor yachts, so you get more deck area and a shallower draft, meaning you can anchor closer to beaches. A 40-foot catamaran can take 10 to 12 guests and costs from around EUR 1,800 for a half day. Catamarans are particularly good if your group includes people who get seasick, because they roll less.

Sailing yachts are quieter and more traditional. They are slower than motor yachts, but the experience is more relaxed. A 40-foot sailing yacht can take 6 to 8 guests and costs from around EUR 1,200 for a half day. Sailing yachts are best for groups who want a peaceful day on the water rather than a party.

RIBs and speedboats are smaller, faster, and more affordable. A 30-foot RIB can take 6 to 8 passengers and costs from around EUR 600 for a half day. These are good for a short, adrenaline-filled trip to a nearby cove for a swim, but they are less comfortable for a full-day celebration.

Licence-free day boats are small motorboats that you can drive yourself without a licence. They are limited to 10 to 15 horsepower and a maximum of 5 passengers. They are cheap, from around EUR 200 for a half day, but they are not suitable for a large birthday party.

Superyachts are the top end. Anything over 24 metres qualifies. These come with full crew, including a captain, chef, and steward. Prices are on request, but expect to pay upwards of EUR 10,000 for a half day. They are for serious budgets.

Pricing Guidance for a Marbella Birthday Charter

Prices for a private yacht charter Marbella birthday vary widely based on boat size, duration, season, and extras. Here is a rough guide to what you can expect to pay.

Half-day charters (4 hours) are the most common for birthdays. For a motor yacht or catamaran suitable for 8 to 10 guests, budget between EUR 1,200 and EUR 2,500. Full-day charters (8 hours) typically cost 1.5 to 2 times the half-day rate. Overnight charters are possible but rare for birthday parties; they cost from around EUR 3,000 for a 40-foot yacht, plus marina fees and fuel.

Fuel is usually included in the charter price for a standard itinerary, but if you want to travel further, say to Marbella from Puerto Banús, the skipper may charge extra. Always confirm what is included before booking.

Extras add up. A private chef costs around EUR 300 to EUR 500 for a half day, plus the cost of food. A bartender or host is around EUR 200. Water toys like a jet ski or inflatable slide can cost EUR 150 to EUR 300 each. Drinks and catering are not included unless you book a full crew package.

Deposits are usually 30 to 50 percent of the total, with the balance due on the day. Cancellation policies vary by boat owner, but many offer a full refund if you cancel more than 14 days before the charter.

Best Seasons for a Birthday Yacht Charter

The Costa del Sol has a Mediterranean climate with over 300 sunny days a year. You can book a private yacht charter Marbella birthday in any season, but the experience changes.

Summer (June to September) is peak season. Temperatures hit 30 to 35 degrees C. The water is warm, around 22 to 26 degrees C. This is the best time for swimming, sunbathing, and water sports. Crowds are heavy, and prices are at their highest. Book at least two weeks in advance for a popular boat.

Spring (March to May) and autumn (October to November) are shoulder seasons. Temperatures are 20 to 28 degrees C. The water is cooler but still swimmable. Fewer tourists mean more availability and lower prices. You might get a discount of 20 to 30 percent compared to summer.

Winter (December to February) is the low season. Temperatures are 15 to 20 degrees C. The water is cold, around 16 degrees C. Swimming is less appealing, but you can still enjoy the scenery, and prices are at their lowest. Some boats are not available during winter, so check availability.

For a birthday, spring and autumn offer the best balance of good weather, fewer crowds, and reasonable prices. Summer works if you want a lively atmosphere and don't mind the heat and cost.

How to Book Your Birthday Yacht Charter

Booking a private yacht charter Marbella birthday through BoatHire24 is a simple process. Here is how it works.

First, go to our search page and enter your preferred date, group size, and port. You can filter by boat type, price range, and amenities. Browse the listings and read the details for each boat. Pay attention to the maximum passenger capacity, included equipment, and any restrictions.

Once you find a boat you like, click the booking button. You will be asked to provide your contact details, the date and time of your charter, and any special requests, such as catering or water toys. You will receive a confirmation email with the total price and payment instructions.

We recommend booking at least one week in advance for summer, and two weeks for popular boats. For last-minute bookings, contact us directly and we will check availability.

Payment is usually by bank transfer or credit card. We will send you a secure payment link. The deposit is due at booking, and the balance is due on the day of the charter, usually in cash to the skipper, unless otherwise agreed.

On the day, arrive at the port 15 minutes before your scheduled departure. The skipper will meet you at the boat, give a safety briefing, and go over the itinerary. You are free to suggest changes, but the skipper has the final say based on weather and safety.

For more tips on planning your trip, check out our blog for guides on Marbella's best anchorages and what to bring on a yacht charter.

Who Is a Private Yacht Charter Birthday For

A private yacht charter Marbella birthday suits a range of groups and occasions. It works well for milestone birthdays like 30th, 40th, or 50th, where you want something memorable. It is also good for a small group of friends who want to celebrate without the noise and expense of a nightclub.

It is less ideal for very large groups. Most boats have a maximum capacity of 10 to 12 passengers for comfort and safety. If you have more than 12 guests, you may need to charter two boats or choose a larger vessel, which costs more.

Children are welcome on most boats, but check the specific boat's policy. Some skippers prefer not to take very young children due to safety concerns. Life jackets are provided, but you should bring any special equipment your child needs.

If you want a party with loud music and heavy drinking, be aware that the skipper is responsible for safety and may limit alcohol consumption if it becomes excessive. Most charters allow you to bring your own drinks, but glass bottles are often banned for safety reasons. Use plastic cups and cans.

For a more relaxed celebration, a sailing yacht or catamaran with a chef is a good option. You can anchor in a quiet cove, swim, and enjoy a meal prepared on board.

Local Colour and Highlights on the Water

The coastline around Marbella offers several memorable spots for a birthday charter. Your skipper can suggest an itinerary based on your preferences.

Puerto Banús itself is a sight, with its superyachts and designer shops. Cruising past the marina gives you a view of the wealth on display.

La Concha mountain is the iconic peak behind Marbella. From the sea, it forms a dramatic backdrop, especially in the evening when the sun sets behind it.

Río Verde is a small river that flows into the sea near Puerto Banús. The beach there is popular for anchoring and swimming. The water is clear and the seabed is sandy.

Cala del Faro is a cove near the lighthouse at the western end of Marbella's coast. It is quieter than the main beaches and good for a stop.

Estepona old town is visible from the water. You can anchor off the coast and take a tender to the shore for a walk through the whitewashed streets.

The Strait of Gibraltar is about 30 nautical miles west of Marbella. If you have a full-day charter and the weather is good, your skipper might take you there to see dolphins and pilot whales. The crossing takes about 1.5 hours from Estepona.

Nerja caves are about 40 nautical miles east of Marbella. They are not directly accessible by boat, but you can anchor near Nerja and take a taxi to the caves. This is a full-day excursion.

For a birthday, a typical itinerary is a two-hour cruise to a cove, a swim stop, lunch on board, and a slow cruise back. You can also arrange to moor at a beach club for a drink.

For more ideas, read our blog post on the best anchorages on the Costa del Sol.

What to Bring on Your Birthday Yacht Charter

Preparation makes the difference between a good day and a great one. Here is a checklist for your private yacht charter Marbella birthday.

Bring sunscreen with a high SPF, a hat, and sunglasses. The sun reflects off the water and you will burn faster than on land. Bring a towel and swimwear. A light jacket or windbreaker is useful for the evening, even in summer, because the wind picks up.

Bring your own drinks and food unless you have arranged catering. Most boats have a cooler or fridge. Avoid glass bottles; use plastic or cans. Bring a speaker if the boat does not have a sound system, but keep the volume reasonable.

Bring a camera or phone for photos. The light on the water is excellent for pictures. A waterproof case is a good idea.

If you are prone to seasickness, take medication an hour before departure. The water is usually calm in summer, but it can get choppy in the afternoon.

Finally, bring cash for the balance payment and any tips for the skipper. Tips are not mandatory but are appreciated, typically 10 to 15 percent of the charter fee.

Why Book with BoatHire24

BoatHire24 is a marketplace that connects you directly with boat owners and skippers. We do not own the boats, so we can offer a wide selection without the overhead. We vet each listing for safety and reliability.

We offer transparent pricing with no hidden fees. The price you see on the listing includes the boat, skipper, and fuel for the standard itinerary. Extras are listed separately. We provide secure payment and support in English and Spanish.

We have been operating since 2018 and have helped hundreds of groups celebrate birthdays, anniversaries, and corporate events. Our team knows the local ports and can advise on the best boat for your needs.

Start your search on our BoatHire24 homepage, or go directly to the search page to find available boats for your birthday date.

Related guides

  • The Best Anchorages and Swimming Spots near Marbella

  • Stag & Hen Boat Party Ideas in Marbella

faqs.q: How much does a private yacht charter for a birthday in Marbella cost?
faqs.a: Prices start from around EUR 1,200 for a half-day charter on a motor yacht suitable for 8 to 10 guests. Full-day charters cost more, and larger boats or luxury vessels can go up to EUR 10,000 or more. The price depends on boat size, duration, season, and extras.

faqs.q: Do I need a boating licence to charter a yacht for a birthday?
faqs.a: No. Every charter through BoatHire24 includes a licensed skipper. You do not need any licence or experience. The skipper handles all navigation and safety. You just bring your guests and enjoy the day.

faqs.q: Can I bring my own food and drinks on a private yacht charter?
faqs.a: Yes, you can bring your own food and drinks on most charters. Glass bottles are usually not allowed for safety reasons, so use plastic or cans. Some boats have a fridge or cooler. If you want catering, you can arrange a private chef or order from a local caterer.

faqs.q: What is the maximum number of guests for a birthday yacht charter?
faqs.a: Most boats have a maximum capacity of 10 to 12 passengers for comfort and safety. Some larger yachts can take up to 20 guests. Check the specific boat listing for its capacity. If you have a larger group, you may need to charter two boats or choose a superyacht.

faqs.q: What happens if the weather is bad on my birthday charter date?
faqs.a: The skipper has the final say on whether it is safe to go out. If the weather is bad, you can reschedule for another day or get a full refund, depending on the boat owner's policy. We recommend checking the forecast a few days before and staying flexible.

faqs.q: Can I request a specific itinerary for my birthday charter?
faqs.a: Yes, you can discuss the itinerary with the skipper before departure. Common stops include coves like Cala del Faro or beaches near Río Verde. The skipper will advise on what is feasible based on weather and time. You are not locked into a fixed route.

faqs.q: How far in advance should I book a private yacht charter for a birthday?
faqs.a: For summer (June to September), book at least two weeks in advance. For spring and autumn, one week is usually enough. For winter, you can often book a few days ahead. Popular boats and weekends go fast, so early booking is recommended.

title: Boat Hire Marbella Wedding Reception: Romantic Venue
metaDescription: Boat hire Marbella wedding reception: book a private yacht for your ceremony, dinner or party on the Costa del Sol. Includes skipper, catering & port options.
h1: Boat Hire Marbella Wedding Reception
intro:
If you are planning a wedding reception in Marbella and want something more memorable than a hotel ballroom, a private boat hire might be exactly what you need. You can exchange vows on the bow with La Concha mountain behind you, then cruise along the coast with champagne and canapés while your guests watch the sunset over Puerto Banús. Every charter includes a licensed skipper, so you can focus on the celebration rather than navigation.

BoatHire24 lists motor yachts, catamarans, sailing yachts and superyachts available for wedding receptions from ports such as Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona, Sotogrande and Benalmádena. Prices start from around EUR 2,500 for a half-day catamaran for 20 guests, going up to EUR 15,000+ for a superyacht with a full catering crew. You can browse available yachts on our search page or read on for the details.
bodyHtml:
What a Boat Wedding Reception Includes

When you book a boat for a wedding reception through BoatHire24, you get more than just a vessel. Every charter comes with a licensed skipper who handles all navigation and safety. Most boats also include a host or steward to serve drinks and food. You can add catering, a DJ, flowers, decorations and even a photographer. The standard package is a four-hour charter, but you can extend to six or eight hours for a full evening reception. Some boats allow ceremonies on board, but you need to check with the port authority and the skipper for legal requirements. Marbella Marina and Puerto Banús are the most common departure points for wedding charters because they offer easy access and parking for guests.

Best Ports for a Wedding Reception Charter

Puerto Banús is the obvious choice if you want glamour. The marina has luxury shops, restaurants and a lively atmosphere. You can board your yacht right next to the famous port, and the backdrop of the marina lights at night is stunning. Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo) is quieter and more relaxed, with a long promenade and views of the old town. Cabopino is smaller and more private, ideal for an intimate reception with fewer guests. Estepona offers a traditional Andalusian feel, and its port is close to the old town where you can take photos. Sotogrande is exclusive and upmarket, but further from Marbella. Benalmádena has a large marina with plenty of parking and hotels nearby. Choose based on your guest list size and the vibe you want. For a full list of available yachts, visit our search page.

Types of Boats Suitable for a Wedding Reception

Catamarans are popular for wedding receptions because they have a lot of deck space, a shallow draft (so you can anchor close to shore) and a stable platform. You can fit 20 to 60 guests on a catamaran, depending on the size. Motor yachts offer more luxury, with air-conditioned salons, a flybridge for panoramic views and a swim platform for photos. Sailing yachts are romantic but have less space and more motion, so they are better for small groups of 10 to 15. RIBs and speedboats are not suitable for wedding receptions. Superyachts can host 50 to 100 guests with full catering and a professional crew. Licence-free day boats are too small and basic for a wedding. For a wedding reception, we recommend a catamaran or a motor yacht from 15 metres upwards. You can see examples on our BoatHire24 homepage.

Pricing Guidance for Boat Wedding Receptions

Prices vary widely based on boat size, duration, season and catering. A half-day (four-hour) charter on a 15-metre catamaran for 20 guests costs from around EUR 2,500. A full-day (eight-hour) charter on a 20-metre motor yacht with a DJ and open bar can cost EUR 8,000 to EUR 12,000. Superyachts with a Michelin-star chef and a dedicated wedding coordinator start at EUR 15,000 and go up to EUR 40,000 for a full-day event. All prices include the skipper, fuel, insurance and VAT. Catering is extra, typically EUR 50 to EUR 150 per person for a sit-down dinner. Flowers and decorations cost extra. You should budget at least EUR 3,000 for a basic reception for 20 people, or EUR 10,000 for a more lavish affair. Always request a detailed quote from the charter company before booking. Our search page lets you filter by price and guest capacity.

Best Season for a Boat Wedding Reception in Marbella

The peak season for boat weddings in Marbella runs from May to October. June, July and August offer the warmest weather, with average temperatures around 28 to 32 degrees Celsius. The sea is calm, and the sunset is around 9.30 pm, giving you long evenings. September and October are also good, with slightly cooler temperatures and fewer crowds. April and November can work if you choose a boat with a covered deck or a salon, but the sea can be choppier and the evenings cooler. December to March is not ideal for a boat wedding reception because of colder weather and higher chance of wind. If you want to sail past the Strait of Gibraltar and see dolphins, book a charter from Estepona or Sotogrande in late spring or early autumn. For more seasonal tips, read our blog.

How to Book a Boat for Your Wedding Reception

Booking through BoatHire24 is straightforward. You can use the search form on our homepage to filter by port, date, guest number and budget. Once you find a boat you like, click the enquiry button and fill in your details. The charter company will respond within 24 hours with availability and a custom quote. We recommend booking at least three months in advance for summer weddings, and six months for superyachts or large groups. You will need to pay a deposit (usually 30 to 50 percent) to secure the date. The balance is due two weeks before the charter. Make sure to discuss the itinerary, menu, drinks, music and any decorations with the skipper or charter manager. Some boats allow you to anchor in a quiet cove like Cala del Faro for a private ceremony. Others stay in the marina for a dockside reception. Clarify this before you book. For a step-by-step guide, check our blog.

Who Is a Boat Wedding Reception For?

A boat wedding reception works best for couples who want a small to medium-sized event (10 to 60 guests). It is ideal if you love the sea, want a unique setting and do not mind a bit of movement. It is also great for destination weddings, because you combine the ceremony, reception and sightseeing in one trip. If you have more than 60 guests, you may need to hire two boats or choose a superyacht. If you or your guests get seasick easily, choose a catamaran (more stable) or a motor yacht with stabilisers. Avoid sailing yachts if motion is a concern. Also consider that some elderly guests or those with mobility issues may find boarding a boat difficult. Most marinas have gangways, but superyachts often have hydraulic platforms. Discuss accessibility with the charter company. For more advice on choosing the right boat, visit our BoatHire24 homepage.

What to Expect on the Day

On the day of your wedding reception, you and your guests arrive at the designated port about 15 minutes before departure. The skipper welcomes you on board and gives a safety briefing. You can then decorate the boat if you have brought flowers or banners (check with the charter company first). The crew serves welcome drinks while you sail out of the marina. Depending on the itinerary, you may anchor in a bay for the ceremony or hold it on the deck while cruising. After the ceremony, the catering begins: canapés, a buffet or a sit-down dinner. The DJ or playlist provides music. You can swim from the boat if the weather permits. The charter ends back at the port. Most charters include a return time, but you can extend if you want to watch the sunset from the water. Remember that the skipper has the final say on safety, so if the sea is rough, they may alter the route or stay in sheltered waters.

Additional Services and Extras

You can customise your wedding reception with a range of extras. Catering options include Spanish tapas, fresh seafood, paella, barbecue or a formal three-course dinner. Drinks can be a welcome cocktail, champagne, wine, beer and soft drinks. Some charter companies offer a dedicated wedding coordinator who helps with flowers, cake, photographer and even a live musician. You can also arrange a fireworks display or a drone photo shoot (subject to port authority approval). If you want to visit the Nerja caves or the Strait of Gibraltar as part of your reception, you need a full-day charter (8+ hours) and a larger boat. Discuss all extras with the charter company when you enquire. For a list of recommended suppliers, check our blog.

Frequently Asked Questions

Can we get married on the boat legally?

Yes, but only if the boat is registered for civil ceremonies and the skipper is authorised. In Spain, you need a civil registry officer on board, which is possible in some ports. Most couples do a symbolic ceremony on the boat and handle the legal paperwork at the town hall before or after the charter. Check with your charter company for options.

What is the maximum number of guests for a boat wedding reception?

It depends on the boat. Catamarans typically hold 20 to 60 guests. Motor yachts hold 10 to 40 guests. Superyachts can hold 50 to 100 guests. The number is limited by safety regulations and the boat's capacity certificate. Always confirm the maximum guest count with the charter company before booking.

Can we bring our own decorations and cake?

Most charter companies allow you to bring decorations, but you must avoid anything that could damage the boat (like confetti or glitter). You can bring a wedding cake, but the crew may need to store it in the galley. Check with the charter manager about any restrictions and whether they provide a table for the cake.

What happens if it rains on the day?

Spain's Costa del Sol has over 300 sunny days a year, but rain is possible, especially in autumn. Most boats have a covered deck or a salon with air conditioning. If the weather is too bad to sail, the charter may be rescheduled or cancelled. Check the cancellation policy before booking. Some companies offer a weather guarantee with a free reschedule.

Is the skipper included in the price?

Yes, every charter on BoatHire24 includes a licensed skipper. The skipper handles navigation, safety and anchoring. You do not need a boating licence. The skipper can also act as a host if needed, but for larger weddings, a separate crew member is usually provided for service.

How far in advance should we book?

For a wedding reception, book at least three months ahead, especially for summer dates. Superyachts and popular catamarans can be booked six to twelve months in advance. Last-minute bookings are possible if you are flexible with the boat type and date, but availability is limited.

Can we have a DJ or live music on the boat?

Yes, most charter companies allow a DJ or a small live band, as long as the volume is reasonable and the equipment is battery-powered (no generators on board). Some boats have built-in sound systems. Check with the charter company about power supply and noise restrictions in the marina.

Why Puerto Banús Works Best for a Wedding Reception

Puerto Banús is the most photographed marina on the Costa del Sol, and for a wedding reception, that matters. Your guests arrive past the rows of superyachts and the Roberto Polo statue. The port authority allows boarding directly from the dock, so no tender transfers are needed. Evening charters depart around 7 pm, just as the sun drops behind La Concha mountain. You get a golden hour sail past the Marbella coastline, then anchor near the Río Verde estuary for dinner. The return to the illuminated port makes for memorable photos. If you want a dockside ceremony, some charter companies can arrange for the boat to stay tied up for the first hour, then sail for the meal. This option works well if you have guests who prefer not to sail. For a list of Puerto Banús charter boats, visit our search page.

How to Handle Seasick Guests at a Boat Wedding

Seasickness can ruin a wedding reception, but you can minimise the risk. Book a catamaran rather than a monohull sailing yacht. Catamarans have a much lower roll motion because the hulls are separate. Choose a motor yacht with stabilisers if you prefer a more enclosed space. Avoid departing in strong Levante winds, which blow from the east and create choppy seas in the Alboran Sea. Check the wind forecast three days before your date. If the forecast shows winds over 15 knots, ask the skipper to stay inside the bay of Marbella or anchor in a sheltered cove like Cala del Faro. Provide ginger sweets, seasickness bands and medication (consult a doctor). Keep the boat heading into the swell during the ceremony. Most guests feel fine on a calm day, but it is worth planning for the 10 percent who might not. For more tips on sea conditions, read our blog.

What a Wedding Reception Menu Looks Like on a Boat

Catering on a boat is different from a land venue because galley space is limited. Most charters offer a buffet or canapés rather than a plated sit-down dinner for large groups. A typical menu for 20 guests on a 15-metre catamaran includes Spanish jamón ibérico, manchego cheese, prawn skewers, chicken brochettes, vegetable paella and a selection of salads. Dessert is churros with chocolate or a mini wedding cake. Drinks are served from a portable bar: cava, white wine, beer and soft drinks. For a premium option, you can add a live paella station on the deck or a sushi platter. The cost for catering is around EUR 60 to EUR 100 per person for a four-hour event, including service. You can also bring your own cake, but the crew may charge a corkage fee. Discuss dietary requirements with the caterer at least two weeks before the charter. For a list of caterers we recommend, check our BoatHire24 homepage.

Legal Requirements for a Boat Wedding in Spain

If you want a legally binding wedding ceremony on the boat, you need a civil registry officer from the local town hall to come on board. This is possible in Marbella, Estepona and Sotogrande, but you must book the officer months in advance and pay a fee (around EUR 300 to EUR 500). The boat must be stationary (anchored or docked) during the ceremony. Most couples choose to do the legal marriage at the Marbella town hall a few days before the charter, then hold a symbolic ceremony on the boat with a friend or a non-denominational officiant. This is simpler and avoids the administrative hassle. If you want a religious blessing, you need a priest from a local church, which is harder to arrange. Check with your charter company for the specific requirements in your chosen port. For more legal guidance, visit our blog.

How to Decorate a Boat for a Wedding Reception

Decorating a boat requires some thought because space is tight and wind can blow things away. Use heavy vases or weighted floral arrangements. Avoid balloons, confetti and glitter, which can damage the boat's deck and pollute the sea. Most charter companies allow flowers, ribbons and bunting as long as they are secured with tape or ties, not nails or staples. Popular themes include white and cream linens with eucalyptus and roses, or a Mediterranean look with oranges, lemons and olive branches. You can also use the boat's existing cushions and awnings, which are often white or navy. If you want a custom backdrop for photos, bring a freestanding frame that the crew can set up on the deck. The cost for a basic decoration package from a supplier is around EUR 400 to EUR 800. For a full floral arch and table centrepieces, budget EUR 1,500 to EUR 3,000. Always send a photo of your planned decorations to the charter company for approval before the day.

Related guides

  • Dolphin Watching in Puerto Banus: Best Times & Tips
  • Sunset Catamaran Marbella: Top Spots for Golden Hour
  • Snorkeling Spots on Costa del Sol: Hidden Gems by Boat
  • Fishing Charter Puerto Banus: Deep Sea Adventures
  • Hen Party Boat Marbella: Unforgettable Celebration Ideas
