  • Photoshoot Yacht Marbella: Professional Tips

faqs.q: Do I need a fishing licence for a half day charter in Marbella?
faqs.a: No. The skipper holds a commercial fishing licence that covers all passengers on board. You do not need to bring or buy a separate licence.

faqs.q: Can I keep the fish I catch?
faqs.a: Yes, you can keep legal-sized fish within the daily bag limits. The skipper will tell you which species are in season and what the size limits are. You can take your catch back to your accommodation or to a local restaurant that cooks it for you.

faqs.q: What happens if the weather is bad on my booked day?
faqs.a: The skipper monitors the forecast closely. If conditions are unsafe, he will cancel the trip and offer you a full refund or a reschedule. You are not charged if the skipper calls it off.

faqs.q: How many people can go on a half day fishing charter?
faqs.a: Most boats carry between 4 and 8 passengers. Smaller RIBs take 4 to 6, while motor yachts can take up to 8. Check the boat listing for the maximum capacity.

faqs.q: What should I bring with me?
faqs.a: Bring sunscreen, a hat, sunglasses, a light jacket, and a camera. You can bring your own snacks and drinks, but most charters include water and soft drinks. Do not bring glass bottles.

faqs.q: Is the half day trip suitable for children?
faqs.a: Yes, children aged 8 and up can join. The skipper will give them a simple rod and show them how to fish. Younger children may get bored or struggle with the reel.

faqs.q: Can I book a half day charter for a group of friends?
faqs.a: Yes, private charters are ideal for groups. You have the boat to yourselves. You can choose the morning or afternoon slot. The skipper will tailor the trip to your group's skill level.

title: Speedboat Hire Marbella No Licence: Easy Rental Options
metaDescription: Speedboat hire Marbella no licence needed. Rent a boat without a permit from Puerto Banús or Marbella Marina. Book online with a licensed skipper included.
h1: Speedboat Hire Marbella No Licence: Easy Rental Options
intro:
You want to drive a speedboat along the Costa del Sol without spending weeks studying for a Spanish boating licence. Good news: you can. At BoatHire24, every rental includes a licensed skipper who handles the paperwork while you take the wheel. That means you can hire a RIB or a small motorboat from Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, or Cabopino without holding a single qualification.

This page covers what you need to know about speedboat hire in Marbella with no licence required. We will look at the ports, the boat types, the costs, and the practicalities so you can get on the water as quickly as possible.
bodyHtml:
What Does No Licence Mean for Speedboat Hire in Marbella

Spanish law requires anyone operating a recreational vessel with an engine over 15 hp (11 kW) to hold a licence. But the law does not stop you from hiring a boat. The charter company provides a licensed skipper who stays on board for the entire trip. You steer, you throttle, you decide where to go. The skipper sits next to you, ready to take over in a tight spot or if the weather turns. This arrangement is legal, safe, and common across all Spanish coastal resorts.

BoatHire24 lists only vetted operators who include a skipper in every booking. You do not pay extra for the licence cover. The skipper’s fee is built into the price you see on the listing. So when you book a speedboat from Puerto Banús for EUR 350 for half a day, that includes the person who makes it all legal.

Ports for No-Licence Speedboat Hire in Marbella

You can pick up a no-licence speedboat from several ports along the Costa del Sol. Each has its own character and cruising grounds.

Puerto Banús is the obvious starting point. It is the glitzy marina with the big yachts and the designer shops. The channel out to sea is straight and well marked. Once outside, you can head east towards Marbella beach or west towards the Cala del Faro and the cliffs of El Saladillo. The water is usually calm in the morning before the Levante wind picks up.

Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo) is a smaller, less flashy base. It sits right on the Marbella promenade. From here you can zip along the coast to the Cabopino dunes or go south to see the La Concha mountain from the sea. The marina has plenty of free parking within walking distance.

Cabopino is a quiet harbour near the Artola sand dunes. Ideal if you want to avoid the crowds. The water here is clear, and you can anchor off the nudist beach for a swim.

Estepona port is about 20 minutes west of Marbella. The old town is worth a visit before or after your trip. From Estepona you can cruise to the Strait of Gibraltar to spot dolphins, or head east to the Selwo Marina area.

Benalmádena and Sotogrande are further options. Benalmádena has a large marina and is close to the Fuengirola coastline. Sotogrande is a luxury estate with a long river estuary, good for a gentle cruise.

You can find all these ports on the search page of BoatHire24. Filter by location and boat type to see available speedboats.

Types of Speedboats Available Without a Licence

The term speedboat covers a few different hulls. The most common is the RIB (rigid inflatable boat). These have a fibreglass hull and inflatable tubes. They are light, fast, and stable. A 6-metre RIB with a 150 hp outboard can hit 35 knots in flat water. Perfect for a quick blast to the Cala del Faro and back.

Small motor yachts under 10 metres are also available without a licence. These are more comfortable, with a cabin, a small toilet, and seating for six to eight people. They top out at around 20 knots but offer shade and a smoother ride.

Licence-free day boats are another option. These are usually open-hulled bowriders or deck boats with a single outboard. They are easy to drive and have low freeboard so you can step straight into the sea for a swim.

Fishing boats are sometimes listed as no-licence rentals too. If you want to fish for bream or mackerel off the Marbella coast, you can hire a small fishing boat with a skipper who knows the local marks.

All these boats are listed on BoatHire24. The listing will state whether a licence is required. For no-licence boats, the skipper is included by default.

How Much Does Speedboat Hire Cost Without a Licence

Prices vary by boat size, engine power, season, and port. As a rough guide, expect to pay from around EUR 300 for a half day (4 hours) on a 5-metre RIB in low season. A full day (8 hours) on a 7-metre motor yacht can cost EUR 600 to EUR 900 in summer. Superyachts and large catamarans are price on request, but they are not speedboats anyway.

Fuel is usually extra and charged at the end of the trip. The skipper will fill the tank before you leave and you pay for what you use. A half-day trip in a RIB might burn EUR 80 to EUR 150 of fuel depending on how hard you drive.

Deposits are common. The operator holds a refundable amount on your card, typically EUR 200 to EUR 500, against damage or late return. You get it back if you bring the boat back in one piece and on time.

Book through BoatHire24 and you see the total price including VAT and any service fees before you confirm. No hidden extras.

Best Time of Year for Speedboat Hire in Marbella

The season runs from April to October. July and August are the hottest and busiest months. Sea temperatures reach 24 degrees C. The air temperature hits 30 degrees C plus. The wind is often light in the morning, building to a moderate Levante in the afternoon. If you want calm water, go out at 9 am and be back by 1 pm.

May and June are pleasant with fewer crowds. September and October still have warm water and sunshine, but the evenings draw in earlier. Winter rentals are possible but the sea can be choppy and the air temperature drops to 15 degrees C. Most operators close from November to March.

For dolphin watching, the best months are April to June and September to October when the Strait of Gibraltar is rich with baitfish. The dolphins come closer to shore then.

What to Bring and What to Expect on the Day

Bring swimwear, a towel, sunscreen (factor 50 minimum), sunglasses with a strap, and a light jacket or windbreaker. The boat will have life jackets for everyone. The skipper will give a safety briefing before you leave. You will go over the controls, the radio, and the emergency procedures.

You can bring your own food and drink. Most boats have a cooler. Alcohol is allowed on board but the skipper will not let you drink if you are the one steering. That is a legal requirement.

You will meet the skipper at the marina at the agreed time. They will walk you to the boat, do the handover, and then you are off. The skipper will suggest a route based on the conditions and your preferences. You can ask to stop for a swim, to anchor near a beach, or to open the throttle on a straight stretch.

At the end of the trip, you return to the same port. The skipper will refuel and you pay for the fuel used. Then you get your deposit back.

Who Is Speedboat Hire Without a Licence For

It is for anyone who wants to drive a boat without the hassle of a course. Tourists on a week-long holiday. Groups of friends who want a day on the water. Families with kids who want to explore the coast at their own pace. Couples looking for a private excursion. You do not need any previous boating experience. The skipper will show you the basics in ten minutes.

It is also for people who have a licence from another country but do not want to go through the process of getting it recognised in Spain. You can just hire a boat with a skipper and avoid the paperwork.

If you are a qualified skipper and you want to rent a boat without a crew, you can do that too. Just filter the search for licence-required boats. But for most visitors, the no-licence option is simpler.

For more tips on renting boats in Spain, check the BoatHire24 blog.

How to Book a No-Licence Speedboat on BoatHire24

Go to the BoatHire24 homepage. Use the search bar to enter Marbella and your preferred date. Select the boat type speedboat or RIB. Look for the filter that says licence required. Set it to no. The results will show only boats that include a skipper.

Click on a listing to see photos, the full description, the cancellation policy, and the price. You can send the operator a message if you have questions. When you are ready, book online and pay a deposit. The balance is usually due on the day, either in cash or by card to the skipper.

You will receive a confirmation email with the meeting point and the skipper’s contact details. Show up on time and enjoy the ride.

If you are unsure which boat suits you, the search page has a compare feature. You can shortlist two or three boats and see the details side by side.

Frequently Asked Questions About Speedboat Hire Marbella No Licence

Why Spanish Law Allows No Licence Rentals With a Skipper

The key distinction is between operating a vessel and chartering it. Spanish Royal Decree 2/2011 on inland and maritime navigation states that any recreational craft under 24 metres can be chartered with a professional skipper on board. The skipper holds a valid Spanish licence (Patrón de Yate or similar) and is legally responsible for the vessel. You as the hirer are a passenger, even if you are the one at the wheel. This is not a loophole. It is the standard commercial charter model used across Europe. The same applies in Greece, Croatia, and Italy. So when you search for speedboat hire Marbella no licence, you are looking for a crewed charter, not a bareboat rental. The distinction matters because the insurance cover changes. The operator’s policy covers the vessel when the skipper is present. If you took the boat out alone without a licence, the insurance would be void. That is why every BoatHire24 partner insists on the skipper staying onboard.

How the Skipper Makes the Trip Better, Not Worse

People sometimes worry that having a skipper on board will spoil the fun. The opposite is true. The skipper knows the local waters, the hidden coves, and the best spots to anchor. They can tell you where the sea bass are biting or which beach has the clearest water. They handle the docking, the refuelling, and the paperwork. You focus on driving and enjoying the day. A good skipper will sit in the background and only intervene when needed. They are not there to micro-manage. They are there to keep you legal and safe. On a typical half-day trip from Puerto Banús, the skipper might suggest heading west to the Cala del Faro, a rocky cove with a small lighthouse that is popular for swimming. They know the depth and the currents. They will anchor the boat while you jump in. Without them, you would be guessing and potentially running aground. The cost of the skipper is already in the hire price, so you are not losing anything.

What Happens if the Weather Turns Bad

The Costa del Sol is generally calm, but the Levante wind can pick up in the afternoon. The skipper monitors the weather forecast and will decide whether it is safe to go out. If the sea state is too rough, they have the authority to cancel or postpone the trip. This is not a sales tactic. It is a legal obligation under the SOLAS convention. You get a full refund or a reschedule if the skipper calls it off due to weather. On the day, if the wind builds while you are out, the skipper will take over the controls and bring you back to port. They are trained to handle rough seas. You are not expected to manage a sudden squall. This is the reassurance you pay for. When you book speedboat hire Marbella no licence through BoatHire24, the cancellation policy is clearly stated. Most operators offer a full refund if the trip is cancelled due to adverse weather before departure.

Which Speedboat Models Are Most Common for No Licence Hire

The most popular model is the Zodiac Pro 650 or similar RIB. It is 6.5 metres long, has a 150 hp outboard, and seats six to eight people. The inflatable tubes make it stable at rest and forgiving in a chop. Another common boat is the Quicksilver 675, which has a deeper V-hull and handles better at speed. Some operators use the Brig Falcon 650, which is a slightly lighter RIB and feels faster. For a more comfortable ride, you might find a Jeanneau Merry Fisher 795. This is a 7.95-metre motor yacht with a cabin, a chemical toilet, and a small galley. It has a 200 hp outboard and cruises at 18 knots. It is not a speedboat in the traditional sense, but it is fast enough for a day trip and offers more comfort. All these boats are listed on BoatHire24 with photos and specifications. You can see the engine power, the length, and the number of seats before you book.

Speedboat Hire From Estepona: A Quieter Alternative

Estepona port is less crowded than Puerto Banús and has cheaper parking. The marina is modern with a good restaurant and a fuel dock. From Estepona, you can head west towards the Strait of Gibraltar. The water is deeper and the dolphin sightings are frequent. A 30-minute run from the port brings you to the Punta de la Doncella, a rocky headland where the seabed drops off. This is a good spot for fishing or snorkelling. You can also cruise east towards the Selwo Marina area, where the coast is lined with pine trees and small beaches. Estepona old town is a short walk from the port, so you can combine a morning boat trip with a tapas lunch. The price for a half-day RIB hire from Estepona is similar to Marbella, from around EUR 320 in low season. The advantage is that the sea is often calmer here because the coast faces south-west and is sheltered from the Levante by the Sierra Bermeja mountains.

How to Combine Speedboat Hire With a Visit to Marbella’s Beaches

Many people hire a speedboat to visit beaches that are hard to reach by land. The Cala del Faro is one example. It sits at the foot of a cliff and has no road access. The only way to get there is by boat. You can anchor in the cove and swim ashore. The water is clear and the rocks are good for jumping. Another beach is Playa de la Cañada, near Cabopino. It is a long stretch of sand with a dune system behind it. You can anchor the boat and walk along the beach for a kilometre without seeing a crowd. For a longer trip, head to the Nerja coast, about 40 nautical miles east of Marbella. The trip takes about two hours in a fast RIB. You can see the Nerja caves from the sea and anchor at the Burriana beach for lunch. The skipper will plan the route and manage the fuel stops. This is not a trip you would do without a skipper because the coastline has several shallow areas and the currents near the cliffs can be strong.

What the Insurance Covers When You Hire Without a Licence

All commercial charter boats in Spain are required to have third-party liability insurance and passenger accident insurance. The minimum cover is EUR 600,000 for third-party damage, but most operators carry EUR 1.5 million or more. The insurance covers damage to the boat, injury to passengers, and damage to other vessels or property. It does not cover reckless behaviour. If you deliberately run the boat aground or ignore the skipper’s instructions, you could be held personally liable. The deposit you pay covers the excess on the insurance policy. If you damage the boat through negligence, the deposit is used to pay the excess, and you may be charged more if the damage exceeds the deposit. For normal wear and tear, such as a scratched tube from docking, the operator usually absorbs the cost. Before you go, the skipper will do a walkaround with you and note any existing damage on a checklist. You sign it, and you are covered for anything not on the list.

How to Get the Best Price for Speedboat Hire Marbella No Licence

Book early. Prices rise as the season approaches and availability drops. If you book a month in advance, you can often get a 10 to 15 percent discount compared to a last-minute booking. Midweek is cheaper than weekends. Tuesday to Thursday are the quietest days. Avoid the peak weeks of August when prices are at their highest and the sea is busiest. Consider a half-day trip instead of a full day. Four hours is enough to visit two or three beaches and have a swim. You save on fuel and the hire cost. Compare multiple listings on BoatHire24. The same boat type can vary in price by EUR 100 between operators depending on the condition of the boat and the reputation of the company. Read the listing carefully. Some operators include fuel in the price, others charge it separately. The total cost including fuel can be the same or different. Use the compare feature on the search page to see the differences side by side.

Related guides

faqs.q: Do I need any boating experience to hire a speedboat without a licence in Marbella?
faqs.a: No. The skipper will give you a short briefing and show you how to steer, accelerate, and stop. You can drive as soon as you are comfortable. The skipper stays on board for safety.

faqs.q: Can I take the speedboat to the Strait of Gibraltar?
faqs.a: Yes, from Estepona or Sotogrande you can head south towards the strait. The distance is about 10 to 15 nautical miles. Check the weather forecast first because the strait can get choppy. The skipper will advise.

faqs.q: What happens if the weather is bad on the day of my booking?
faqs.a: The skipper decides if it is safe to go out. If the trip is cancelled due to weather, you get a full refund or you can reschedule. BoatHire24’s cancellation policy covers this.

faqs.q: How many people can fit on a no-licence speedboat?
faqs.a: A typical RIB seats 6 to 8 people including the skipper. A small motor yacht seats up to 10. Check the listing for the maximum capacity. Overloading is not allowed.

faqs.q: Is fuel included in the rental price?
faqs.a: No. Fuel is charged separately after the trip based on consumption. The skipper will fill the tank before you leave and you pay for what you use. Expect EUR 80 to EUR 150 for a half day.

faqs.q: Can I bring my own music on board?
faqs.a: Yes. Most boats have a Bluetooth speaker or an auxiliary input. Ask the skipper to connect your phone. Keep the volume reasonable so you can hear the skipper’s instructions.

faqs.q: What is the youngest age allowed to drive the speedboat?
faqs.a: There is no strict age limit, but the skipper must be satisfied that the person can handle the controls safely. Typically, anyone over 18 can drive. Children under 12 are not usually allowed to steer.

title: Catamaran Rental Marbella Family: Kid-Friendly Trips
metaDescription: Book a catamaran rental Marbella family trip with a licensed skipper. Kid-friendly charters from Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina & more. Safe, spacious & fun.
h1: Catamaran Rental Marbella Family: Kid-Friendly Trips with a Skipper
intro:
If you are looking for a catamaran rental Marbella family trip that keeps everyone happy, you have found the right place. Parents get the sun, the sea and the sangria. Kids get the trampoline nets, the shallow coves and the chance to spot dolphins. And nobody has to worry about navigation, safety or cleaning up afterwards because every charter includes a licensed skipper who handles the boat, the anchor and the local knowledge.

At BoatHire24, we list catamarans from 30 to 60 feet that depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona, Sotogrande and Benalmádena. All are equipped with life jackets for children, shaded saloons, and plenty of deck space for running around. You book online, pay securely, and the skipper takes care of the rest.
bodyHtml:
Why a Catamaran Rental Marbella Family Trip Works Better Than a Monohull

Catamarans have two hulls instead of one, which means they do not heel over like a sailing yacht. For kids, that translates to a stable platform where they can walk, play and even nap without feeling seasick. The trampoline net at the bow is a built-in playground. You can lie on it, jump on it, or just watch the wake through the mesh. The saloon is usually at deck level, so you are not climbing ladders to get inside. And the shallow draft lets the skipper anchor close to beaches like Cala del Faro or the sandbanks near Cabopino.

For parents, the extra beam width means more space for sunbathing, dining and storing coolers, towels and toys. Most catamarans in our fleet have a forward cockpit or a flybridge with shade, so you can keep an eye on the kids while you relax. The skipper handles the mooring, the weather checks and the itinerary. You just decide whether to stop for lunch at a chiringuito or anchor in a quiet cove.

What Is Included in a Kid-Friendly Catamaran Charter

Every catamaran rental Marbella family booking through BoatHire24 comes with a licensed skipper who knows the coast and the local regulations. The skipper will brief you on safety, show the kids how to wear a life jacket, and point out points of interest like the La Concha mountain ridge or the Río Verde estuary. You do not need a boating licence or any prior experience.

Standard inclusions vary by boat owner, but most charters provide:

  • Life jackets for all ages (infant, child and adult sizes)
  • Snorkelling masks and fins
  • Stand-up paddleboards (SUPs) or a small inflatable dinghy
  • Bluetooth speaker and USB charging points
  • Fully equipped galley with fridge, gas hob and sink
  • Deck shower and fresh water rinse
  • Sun cushions and bimini shade

You can usually add a catering package with kid-friendly meals, soft drinks and snacks. Some owners offer a paddleboard lesson or a dolphin-watching guarantee. Check the boat listing for specifics before you book.

Best Departure Ports for a Family Catamaran Trip

The Costa del Sol has several marinas that work well for families. Here is how they compare.

Puerto Banús

This is the most famous port on the coast, with shops, restaurants and a lively atmosphere. The marina is well sheltered, and the channel out to sea is short. From Puerto Banús you can head west to the sandbanks of Cabopino or east towards Cala del Faro. The boat traffic is busy, but your skipper knows the protocols. Parking nearby is expensive, so consider a taxi or a shuttle.

Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo)

Located east of the old town, this marina is quieter than Banús and has cheaper parking. The departure takes you past the Marbella promenade and the Sierra Blanca mountains. It is a good choice for a half-day trip to the beaches of Nagüeles or the dune system at Artola.

Cabopino

Cabopino is a small, family-friendly marina with a sandy beach right next to it. The channel is narrow, but the skipper will handle it. From here you can reach the Cabopino sandbanks in 10 minutes. The water is shallow and clear, ideal for young swimmers.

Estepona

The marina in Estepona is close to the old town with its flower-potted streets and playgrounds. The coast west of Estepona has fewer boats and more secluded coves. You can anchor near the Playa del Cristo or head towards the Strait of Gibraltar for dolphin sightings.

Sotogrande

Sotogrande is an upmarket residential marina with wide pontoons and a relaxed atmosphere. It is further west, so the trip to the Strait of Gibraltar is shorter. Families staying in the area appreciate the lack of crowds.

Benalmádena

Benalmádena marina has a large car park and a cable car nearby. From here you can cruise east towards the cliffs of Torremolinos or west to the beaches of Fuengirola. The marina has plenty of restaurants and ice cream shops for after the trip.

Boat Types and Sizes Suitable for Families

Most family groups of 4 to 8 people choose a catamaran between 38 and 50 feet. A 40-foot catamaran typically has two double cabins, a saloon that converts to a berth, and a galley. The deck space includes a trampoline, a forward seating area and a rear cockpit with a table. For larger groups or two families travelling together, a 50- to 60-foot catamaran offers three or four cabins, multiple bathrooms and a flybridge.

At BoatHire24 you can filter by length, number of cabins and amenities. Some catamarans are designed for day charters only, with no overnight cabins, while others are fully equipped for week-long cruising. If you want to spend the night on board, look for listings that say "overnight permitted" or "weekly charter".

Pricing Guidance for a Family Catamaran Rental in Marbella

Prices depend on the season, the boat size and the duration. As a rough guide:

  • Half-day (4 hours): from around EUR 700 to EUR 1,200 for a 40-foot catamaran
  • Full day (8 hours): from around EUR 1,200 to EUR 2,500
  • Overnight or weekly: price on request, typically EUR 2,500 to EUR 5,000 per night for a 50-foot catamaran

These prices include the skipper, fuel for a standard itinerary, and basic cleaning. Extras like catering, drinks, water toys or a longer route cost extra. You can see the exact price on each listing or request a quote for custom trips.

Best Seasons for a Family Catamaran Trip on the Costa del Sol

The Mediterranean climate means you can charter a catamaran from April to October. July and August are the hottest months, with air temperatures reaching 30 to 35 degrees C and sea temperatures around 24 to 26 degrees C. The water is warm enough for swimming without a wetsuit. The downside is that the coast is busier, and some coves get crowded.

June and September are the sweet spots. The weather is still warm (25 to 30 degrees C), the sea is calm, and the marinas are less frantic. Schools in Spain are still in session during June, so you will find fewer families on the water. October can be pleasant too, but the sea temperature drops to around 20 degrees C and the chance of wind increases.

Winter charters are possible but less common. The air temperature hovers around 15 to 18 degrees C, and the sea is rougher. If you want a winter trip, look for a catamaran with a fully enclosed saloon and heating.

Sample Itineraries for a Family Day Out

Here are three example itineraries that work well for families.

Puerto Banús to Cabopino Sandbanks

Depart at 10:00 from Puerto Banús. Cruise east for 20 minutes to the Cabopino sandbanks. Anchor in the shallow water. Kids can swim, snorkel and paddleboard. Lunch on board or at a beach chiringuito. Head back at 15:00, stopping for a quick dip at Cala del Faro on the way. Total distance: about 12 NM.

Marbella Marina to Cala del Faro

Depart at 11:00 from Marbella Marina. Cruise west for 15 minutes to Cala del Faro, a small cove with a lighthouse. Anchor and swim. After lunch, motor further west to the Río Verde estuary, where you might see flamingos and herons. Return by 17:00. Total distance: about 10 NM.

Estepona to the Strait of Gibraltar

Depart at 09:00 from Estepona. Cruise south-west for 45 minutes towards the Strait of Gibraltar. This area is known for dolphin and whale sightings. The skipper will keep a safe distance. Anchor near Playa del Cristo for a swim and lunch. Return by 16:00. Total distance: about 20 NM.

How to Book a Catamaran Rental Marbella Family Trip

Booking through BoatHire24 is straightforward. Go to the search page, set the port, the date and the number of guests. Filter by boat type: catamaran. Browse the listings, check the photos and read the description. Each listing shows the price, the included amenities and the cancellation policy. Click "Book Now" or "Request a Quote" to proceed.

You will need to pay a deposit at the time of booking, usually 30 to 50 percent of the total. The remainder is due on the day of the charter. You can pay by credit card or bank transfer. The skipper will contact you a few days before the trip to confirm the meeting point and answer any questions.

If you are not sure which boat to choose, contact us through the website. We can recommend a catamaran based on your group size, budget and preferred itinerary.

Who Is This Type of Charter For

A catamaran rental Marbella family trip is for anyone who wants a safe, spacious and stress-free day on the water with children. It suits families with toddlers who need a stable boat, families with teenagers who want to snorkel and paddleboard, and multi-generational groups where grandparents want to sit in the shade and watch the scenery.

It is also a good choice for birthday parties, school holiday outings or just a break from the hotel pool. The skipper handles all the logistics, so you can focus on spending time together.

For more tips on planning a family boating holiday, read our blog about kid-friendly itineraries, safety advice and packing lists.

Safety Equipment and Procedures for Children Onboard

Every catamaran in our fleet carries life jackets in multiple sizes, but you should check the listing to confirm infant jackets are available. The skipper will do a safety drill before departure, showing everyone where the fire extinguisher, first aid kit and emergency flares are stored. Kids are usually fascinated by the VHF radio and the GPS plotter. Let them ask questions. The skipper will also explain the rules about staying seated while the boat is moving, keeping arms and legs inside, and not running on the wet deck. The trampoline net is safe for supervised play, but the skipper may ask children to wear a life jacket while on it. If your child is prone to seasickness, ask the skipper to stay inside the marina for the first 15 minutes to see how they cope. You can always turn back if needed.

What to Pack for a Family Catamaran Day in Marbella

Bring more sun protection than you think you need. The reflection off the water amplifies UV rays, and kids burn quickly even on cloudy days. Pack SPF 50+ sunscreen, rash vests, wide-brimmed hats and polarised sunglasses. A light jumper or windbreaker is useful for the afternoon breeze, especially in June or September. Towels, swim nappies and a change of clothes are essential. For entertainment, bring waterproof toys, a floating ring or a snorkel set. The boat has a Bluetooth speaker, so you can play the kids' favourite playlist. Snacks that do not melt are best: crackers, fruit, dried mango. Avoid chocolate unless you have a cool box. Most catamarans have a fridge, but it is small. Bring your own water bottles, at least 1.5 litres per person. The skipper will have a first aid kit, but bring your own plasters and antihistamines if your child has allergies.

Best Times of Day for a Family Charter to Avoid Heat and Crowds

July and August midday sun is brutal, especially for young children. Book a half-day morning charter from 09:00 to 13:00. The sea is calmer, the light is softer for photos, and the beaches are less crowded. You can anchor, swim and have an early lunch before the heat peaks. Alternatively, an afternoon charter from 15:00 to 19:00 catches the golden hour light and avoids the hottest part of the day. The water temperature is still warm from the afternoon sun. Evening charters from 18:00 to 22:00 are popular with families who want a sunset cruise and dinner on board. The skipper will anchor in a sheltered cove while you watch the sun drop behind La Concha mountain. Just bring extra layers because the temperature drops quickly after sunset, even in summer.

Catamaran Rental Marbella Family: Budget-Friendly Tips

A full-day catamaran charter can feel expensive, but there are ways to reduce the cost. Book a half-day instead of a full day. The kids will be tired after four hours anyway, and you save 30 to 40 percent. Share the charter with another family. A 40-foot catamaran fits two families of four comfortably, and you split the hourly rate. Choose a weekday rather than a weekend. Monday to Thursday rates are often lower because demand is softer. Book early in the season, April to mid-June, or late season, late September to October, when prices drop by 15 to 25 percent. Avoid the August peak if you can. Bring your own food and drinks instead of buying the catering package. Most catamarans allow you to bring your own cooler, but check the listing first. Some owners charge a corkage fee for wine or champagne, so ask the skipper before you open a bottle.

What to Do If the Weather Turns Bad During Your Charter

The skipper monitors the weather forecast constantly and will not take you out if there is a storm warning. But the Mediterranean can change quickly. If the wind picks up or a thunderstorm approaches, the skipper will head back to port or find a sheltered anchorage. The catamaran's shallow draft and twin engines make it manoeuvrable in tight spaces. You will not be stuck in open water. If the trip is cut short by more than two hours, most operators offer a partial refund or a reschedule. Check the cancellation policy on the listing before you book. Some operators have a "weather guarantee" that lets you cancel for free up to 24 hours before departure if the forecast is bad. For families with very young children, it is worth booking with an operator that offers this flexibility.

Local Restaurants and Chiringuitos Accessible by Catamaran

One of the best parts of a family catamaran trip is eating at a beachfront restaurant that you can only reach by boat. Near Cabopino, the chiringuito at Cabopino Beach serves fresh grilled fish and paella. They have a kids' menu with chicken nuggets and chips. At Cala del Faro, there is a small restaurant on the beach that does simple tapas and drinks. In Estepona, anchor near Playa del Cristo and walk up to the chiringuito for squid rings and ice cream. If you want to stay on the boat, many operators offer a catering option with a Spanish picnic: jamón, cheese, bread, olives, melon and cold drinks. Some even do a barbecue on the back deck. Ask the skipper if they can stop at a fish market on the way out so you can buy fresh catch for lunch. The galley has a gas hob and a grill, so you can cook it yourself.

Environmental Considerations for Family Charters

The Costa del Sol's marine ecosystem is fragile. The skipper will anchor only on sand or mud, not on seagrass beds. You should not feed the fish, touch the corals or take shells home. If you see dolphins, the skipper will keep a distance of at least 100 metres and cut the engines to avoid disturbing them. The Strait of Gibraltar is a protected area for cetaceans, and there are strict rules about approaching them. Use reef-safe sunscreen to avoid damaging the water quality. Most catamarans have a holding tank for toilet waste, so nothing goes overboard. Bring reusable water bottles and avoid single-use plastics. The skipper will provide a bin for rubbish and recycling. Teach your kids to leave the beach cleaner than they found it. It is a small effort that keeps the coast beautiful for everyone.

faqs.a: No. Every catamaran rental Marbella family charter through BoatHire24 includes a licensed skipper who operates the boat. You do not need any licence or experience.

faqs.q: What is the minimum age for children on a catamaran charter?
faqs.a: There is no minimum age, but the skipper will require life jackets for infants and children. Most owners provide life jackets for all ages. Check the listing or ask the skipper before booking.

faqs.q: Can we bring our own food and drinks on board?
faqs.a: Yes, you can bring your own food and drinks. Most catamarans have a fridge, a galley and a cooler. Some owners offer catering packages with kid-friendly meals. Check the listing for details.

faqs.q: What happens if the weather is bad on the day of our charter?
faqs.a: The skipper will assess the weather and sea conditions. If it is unsafe to sail, the charter can be rescheduled or cancelled with a full refund. Check the cancellation policy in the listing.

faqs.q: Are there toilets and changing facilities on the catamaran?
faqs.a: Yes, most catamarans have one or two toilets with a shower. Some larger catamarans have separate changing areas. The listing will specify the number of bathrooms.

faqs.q: Can we swim and snorkel during the trip?
faqs.a: Yes, swimming and snorkelling are standard activities. The skipper will anchor in a calm cove or near a sandbank. Snorkelling equipment is usually included.

faqs.q: How far in advance should we book a family catamaran in Marbella?
faqs.a: In peak season (July and August), book at least two weeks in advance. In June and September, one week is usually enough. For last-minute bookings, check the availability on the search page.

title: Private Boat Hire Puerto Banus Evening: Sunset Cruises
metaDescription: Book private boat hire Puerto Banus evening for sunset cruises. Motor yachts, catamarans, sailboats with skipper. From EUR 450. Reserve now.
h1: Private Boat Hire Puerto Banus Evening: Sunset Cruises
keyword: private boat hire Puerto Banus evening
intro:
You want to watch the sun drop into the Mediterranean from the deck of a private boat, not from a crowded beach bar. Private boat hire Puerto Banus evening is the only way to do it properly. You get a whole vessel, a licensed skipper who knows the coast, and a few hours of golden light that makes the sea look like a swimming pool. No queuing, no strangers, no loud music you didn't choose.

BoatHire24 connects you with owners and charter companies in Puerto Banus, Marbella, and the rest of the Costa del Sol. You pick the boat, pick the time (usually 18:00 to 21:00 in summer), and the skipper handles the rest. Prices start from around EUR 450 for a small motorboat and go up to several thousand for a superyacht with crew. Every booking includes fuel, insurance, and a skipper. You just bring drinks and a camera.
bodyHtml:
What You Get With Evening Boat Hire in Puerto Banus

An evening charter is not the same as a daytime trip. The light changes, the wind drops, and the sea calms down. You get a different perspective on the coast. Most evening hires run for three to four hours, starting around 17:00 or 18:00 and returning after dark. The skipper will take you along the coast towards Marbella or out towards Cabopino, depending on the wind and your preference.

Every booking includes a licensed skipper who speaks English and Spanish. They know the local waters, the best spots for photos, and where to anchor for a swim if the water is warm enough. Fuel is included in the price, as is basic insurance. You can bring your own food and drink, or ask the charter company to arrange catering. Some boats have a barbecue on the back deck, which works well for evening charters.

The boat is yours for the duration. The skipper stays on board but stays out of your way. You can sunbathe, swim, eat, drink, or just sit and watch the sunset. There is no fixed itinerary. You tell the skipper what you want, and they make it happen.

Which Ports Can You Depart From

Private boat hire Puerto Banus evening charters usually start from the marina in Puerto Banus itself. That is the obvious choice because the marina is the centre of the action. You step off the gangway and you are in the middle of restaurants, shops, and nightlife. But you can also depart from Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo), Cabopino, Estepona, Sotogrande, or Benalmadena. Each port gives you a different stretch of coast.

From Puerto Banus you can head east towards Marbella and see the big hotels and the old town from the water. Or head west towards Estepona and the quieter beaches. From Marbella Marina you get a shorter run to the centre of Marbella bay. From Cabopino you get the dunes and the nature reserve. From Estepona you get the old town skyline and the mountains behind. From Sotogrande you get the exclusive villas and the Guadiaro river mouth. From Benalmadena you get the marina and the cable car.

If you are staying in Marbella or Estepona, the closest departure port is usually the best. But if you want a specific boat type or size, you might need to travel a bit. The search page on BoatHire24 lets you filter by port, boat type, and price.

Types of Boats Available for Evening Hire

You can hire almost any type of boat for an evening cruise. The most popular are motor yachts, catamarans, and sailing yachts. Each has its own feel.

Motor yachts are fast and stable. You can cover more distance in less time, so you can get further along the coast and still be back by 21:00. They have plenty of deck space, a cabin below for changing, and a flybridge for the best views. Prices start from around EUR 600 for a 10-metre boat and go up to EUR 3,000 for a 20-metre yacht.

Catamarans are wide and stable, with a large deck area at the front and back. They are good for groups because you can spread out. The shallow draft means you can anchor close to the beach. Prices start from around EUR 800 for a 12-metre catamaran. They are slower than motor yachts, so you will not cover as much distance, but the ride is smoother.

Sailing yachts are quieter and more traditional. You feel the wind and the movement of the boat. Evening sails are particularly pleasant because the wind usually drops and the sailing is gentler. Prices start from around EUR 500 for a 10-metre sailboat. You can also charter larger sailing yachts with crew if you want a more luxurious experience.

RIBs and speedboats are smaller and faster. They are good for a short, adrenaline-filled evening trip. You can zip along the coast and stop for a swim. Prices start from around EUR 350 for a 6-metre RIB. They are not as comfortable as a yacht but they are cheaper.

Fishing boats are available for evening fishing trips. You can combine sunset with a bit of bottom fishing or trolling. Prices start from around EUR 400 for a half-day trip.

Licence-free day boats are small motorboats that you can drive yourself without a licence. They are limited to 4-6 people and usually have a maximum speed of 10-15 knots. You can take one out for a couple of hours in the evening. Prices start from around EUR 200 for two hours. You do not need a licence, but you do need to listen to a safety briefing.

Superyachts are available for those with a bigger budget. You get a full crew, champagne, and a level of service that is hard to beat. Prices are on request, but expect to pay upwards of EUR 5,000 for a three-hour evening charter.

Pricing Guidance for Evening Charters

Prices vary by boat size, type, season, and duration. Here is a rough guide for a three-hour evening charter from Puerto Banus in summer 2025.

Small motorboat (6-8 metres): from around EUR 450. Medium motor yacht (10-12 metres): from around EUR 800. Large motor yacht (15-20 metres): from around EUR 2,000. Catamaran (12-15 metres): from around EUR 1,000. Sailing yacht (10-12 metres): from around EUR 600. RIB (6-7 metres): from around EUR 350. Fishing boat (8-10 metres): from around EUR 500. Licence-free day boat (5-6 metres): from around EUR 250 for two hours. Superyacht (20+ metres): price on request.

All prices include skipper, fuel, insurance, and VAT. Gratuities are not included but are customary (10-15 percent of the charter fee). Some boats include a small snack or drinks, but most require you to bring your own. You can order catering through the charter company for an extra fee.

Winter prices (November to March) are usually 20-30 percent lower. Summer prices peak in July and August. Book early if you want a specific boat for a specific date.

Best Time of Year for Evening Cruises

The evening cruise season runs from April to October. The best months are May, June, September, and October. The weather is warm but not too hot, the sea is calm, and the sun sets at a reasonable hour (around 20:30 to 21:30). In July and August the sun sets after 21:30, which means you can start at 19:00 and still have daylight for the whole trip. The downside is that July and August are the busiest months, so the marinas and anchorages are crowded.

Winter evenings are shorter and cooler. The sun sets around 18:00 in December and January. You can still do an evening cruise, but you will need to dress warmly and the experience is different. The sea is often rougher, and some boats are laid up for maintenance. Prices are lower, though, and you will have the coast to yourself.

Spring and autumn evenings are the sweet spot. The temperatures are 20-25 degrees C, the sea is calm, and the light is beautiful. You can anchor for a swim in the evening without getting cold.

How to Book a Private Evening Boat Hire

Booking through BoatHire24 is straightforward. You search by port, date, and boat type. You see the available boats with photos, descriptions, and prices. You send an enquiry or book directly online. The platform handles the payment and the communication with the owner.

You will need to provide the number of guests, the date and time, and any special requests (catering, specific drinks, a route you want to follow). The owner or charter company will confirm availability and send a booking confirmation. You pay a deposit (usually 30-50 percent) and the balance on the day.

Cancellation policies vary. Most require 48-72 hours notice for a full refund. Some are more flexible. Check the terms before you book.

If you are unsure which boat to choose, contact the BoatHire24 team. They know the fleet and can recommend a boat that fits your group size and budget.

Who Is Evening Boat Hire For

Evening boat hire is for couples, families, groups of friends, and corporate events. It works for birthdays, anniversaries, proposals, and just because you want to do something different.

Couples often choose a small motor yacht or a sailing yacht for a romantic evening. The skipper will anchor in a quiet spot away from other boats. You can have dinner on deck, watch the sunset, and maybe see the stars come out. Proposals are common on evening charters. The skipper can help with the timing and the setting.

Families with children prefer catamarans or large motor yachts because there is more space and the ride is stable. Children can swim, play on deck, and eat. The skipper will keep an eye on safety.

Groups of friends often hire a motor yacht or a catamaran for a party. You can bring your own music (most boats have a Bluetooth speaker or a sound system), your own drinks, and your own food. Some boats have a barbecue. The skipper will anchor in a bay where you can swim and socialise.

Corporate events are also possible. You can hire a large motor yacht or a superyacht for a client dinner, a team building event, or a product launch. The crew can arrange catering, drinks, and even a presentation if needed.

The common thread is that you want a private, exclusive experience on the water. You do not want to share the boat with strangers. You want to control the music, the food, the route, and the pace. Evening boat hire gives you that control.

Local Highlights You Will See From the Water

From Puerto Banus, the evening cruise takes you past some of the most recognisable landmarks on the Costa del Sol. To the east you see the Marbella skyline, with La Concha mountain behind it. The mountain turns pink and then purple as the sun goes down. To the west you see the coast towards Estepona, with the Sierra Bermeja mountains in the distance.

If you head towards Cabopino, you pass the dunes and the nature reserve. You might see flamingos in the lagoon. If you go further west towards Estepona, you pass the old town with its whitewashed houses and the church tower. The port of Estepona is small but pretty, with a few restaurants and bars.

If you go further west still, towards Sotogrande and the Strait of Gibraltar, you might see dolphins. The Strait is a major migration route for dolphins and whales. Evening is a good time to see them because the sea is calmer and they come closer to the surface. You cannot guarantee a sighting, but the skipper will know where they have been seen recently.

To the east, beyond Marbella, you can go towards Cala del Faro, a quiet cove with a lighthouse. It is a good spot for a swim and a photo. Further east is Benalmadena, with its marina and the cable car. And beyond that, Malaga city and the Nerja caves, though those are too far for a three-hour evening cruise.

Your skipper will suggest a route based on the conditions and your preferences. You can also just drift and watch the sunset from the bay. The choice is yours.

For more ideas on routes and destinations, check the BoatHire24 blog for guides to the best sunset spots on the Costa del Sol.

What to Bring and What to Expect

Bring sunscreen, sunglasses, a hat, and a light jacket or jumper. Even in summer, the wind on the water can feel cool after the sun goes down. Bring swimwear and a towel if you want to swim. Bring your own drinks and food, unless you have arranged catering. Most boats have a cooler and ice. Bring a camera or phone for photos. The light at sunset is excellent.

The skipper will give you a safety briefing at the start. They will show you where the life jackets are, how to use the toilet, and what to do in an emergency. You do not need to know anything about boats. The skipper does everything.

The boat will have a toilet (heads) on board, usually a small cabin with a toilet and a sink. Larger boats have a shower and a proper cabin. Catamarans have more space below deck. Motor yachts have a galley (kitchen) and a saloon (living area). You can use these facilities during the trip.

The skipper will anchor for a swim if the sea is calm and the water is warm enough. The water temperature in summer is 22-26 degrees C. In spring and autumn it is 18-22 degrees C. In winter it is 14-16 degrees C, which is cold for swimming unless you are used to it.

The return to port is usually after dark. The marina is well lit and the skipper will dock the boat. You can then go to a restaurant or bar in Puerto Banus or head back to your accommodation.

Why Book Through BoatHire24

BoatHire24 is a marketplace that lists boats from multiple owners and charter companies. You get a choice of boats, transparent pricing, and secure booking. The platform handles the payment and the communication. You do not need to contact each owner separately.

The boats are inspected and the owners are verified. You get a licensed skipper with every charter. The prices include everything except gratuities and optional extras. You can compare boats side by side and read the descriptions carefully.

If you have any questions, the BoatHire24 team can help. They know the fleet and the local area. They can recommend a boat that fits your group size, budget, and preferences. They can also help with special requests like catering, decorations, or a specific route.

Evening boat hire in Puerto Banus is a straightforward way to make a memorable evening. You get the boat, the skipper, the sunset, and the freedom to do what you want. Book online, turn up, and enjoy.

faqs.q: How much does private boat hire Puerto Banus evening cost?
faqs.a: Prices start from around EUR 450 for a small motorboat for three hours. A medium motor yacht costs from EUR 800, a catamaran from EUR 1,000, and a sailing yacht from EUR 600. Superyachts are price on request. All prices include skipper, fuel, and insurance.

faqs.q: What time does an evening cruise start in Puerto Banus?
faqs.a: Most evening cruises start between 17:00 and 19:00, depending on the season and sunset time. In summer the sun sets after 21:00, so you can start later. The skipper will confirm the best timing for your chosen date.

faqs.q: Can I bring my own food and drink on board?
faqs.a: Yes, you can bring your own food and drink. Most boats have a cooler and ice. Some boats have a barbecue. You can also order catering through the charter company for an extra fee.

faqs.q: Do I need a boating licence to hire a boat for an evening cruise?
faqs.a: No, every charter includes a licensed skipper who handles the boat. You do not need any experience or licence. The only exception is licence-free day boats, which you can drive yourself without a licence.

faqs.q: How many people can go on an evening boat hire?
faqs.a: It depends on the boat. Small motorboats take 6-8 people. Motor yachts and catamarans take 8-12 people. Larger yachts and superyachts can take 20 or more. Check the boat description for the maximum capacity.

faqs.q: What happens if the weather is bad on the day of my evening charter?
faqs.a: The skipper will assess the conditions and may cancel or reschedule if it is unsafe. Most charter companies offer a full refund or a credit for a future date if the cancellation is due to weather. Check the cancellation policy before booking.

faqs.q: Can I swim during an evening cruise?
faqs.a: Yes, if the sea is calm and the water is warm enough. The skipper will anchor in a suitable spot. Bring swimwear and a towel. In summer the water temperature is 22-26 degrees C.

title: Yacht Charter Marbella Hen Party: Ultimate Celebration
metaDescription: Plan an unforgettable yacht charter Marbella hen party with BoatHire24. Private skipper, luxury boats, and party stops from Puerto Banús. Book your celebration.
h1: Yacht Charter Marbella Hen Party: Your Ultimate Celebration
intro:
You want a hen party that doesn't involve a sticky-floored club and a hangover before the bride even gets her veil on. A yacht charter Marbella hen party is the answer: a private boat, a licensed skipper who knows the coast, and the freedom to anchor at a beach club, swim in a cove, or just float past the mansions of Puerto Banús while the prosecco flows. No crowds, no queues, no plastic wristbands.

BoatHire24 is a boat-rental and yacht-charter marketplace for Marbella and the Costa del Sol, Spain. Every booking includes a skipper who handles the navigation and safety, so your group can focus on the bride and the bubbles. Departure ports include Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona, Sotogrande, and Benalmádena. Boats range from spacious catamarans (ideal for groups of 10 to 30) to sleek motor yachts, RIBs for speed, and even superyachts for the full VIP treatment.
bodyHtml:
What a Yacht Charter Marbella Hen Party Includes

When you book through BoatHire24, you get a private boat for your group, a professional skipper, and the flexibility to design your own itinerary. Most charters are half-day (4 hours) or full-day (8 hours), with options to add catering, drinks, water toys, and even a DJ or photographer. The skipper will take you to spots like the famous Puerto Banús, the calm waters off Cabopino, or the dramatic cliffs near Cala del Faro. You can stop for a swim, anchor at a beach club like Nikki Beach or Ocean Club, or simply cruise the coastline with views of La Concha mountain. The boat is yours for the duration, so the only schedule is the one you set.

For a hen party, the typical package includes a welcome glass of cava, a cooler with ice, and sometimes a Bluetooth speaker. You can bring your own food and drink, or arrange a catering package with tapas, paella, or a barbecue. Some boats have a flybridge with a sunbathing area, others have a shaded saloon and a bow net for lounging. The skipper will give a safety briefing and stay on board, but they won't hover. They know the best spots for photos, dolphin watching (common in the Strait of Gibraltar area), and sunset views.

Choosing the Right Boat for Your Hen Group

The size of your group determines the boat type. For 10 to 16 people, a catamaran is the best option. Catamarans have a wide beam, lots of deck space, and a shallow draft, meaning you can get closer to beaches and coves. They also tend to be more stable, so less seasickness. For a group of 6 to 12, a motor yacht like a Sunseeker or Azimut offers a more glamorous feel, with leather seating, a sunpad, and a cabin for changing. For 20 or more, you need a superyacht or a large catamaran like a Lagoon 620. RIBs and speedboats are best for smaller groups (up to 8) who want a fast, exhilarating ride to a beach club or a secluded cove.

All boats on BoatHire24 include a skipper. Some boats also allow a hostess or steward for an extra fee, which can be useful for serving drinks and food. The skipper will handle the mooring, anchoring, and navigation, so you don't need a licence or experience. Just turn up with your group and your playlist.

Departure Ports and Itineraries

Most hen charters depart from Puerto Banús, the glamorous marina in Marbella filled with superyachts, designer shops, and restaurants. It is the most convenient port for a party, as you can step off the boat straight into the nightlife. Other options include Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo), which is closer to the old town and has a more relaxed vibe, or Cabopino, a quieter marina near the Artola sand dunes. Estepona is a charming option with its old town and colourful flower pots, while Sotogrande is more exclusive and quieter. Benalmádena, near Fuengirola, is a good choice if you are staying on the western Costa del Sol.

A typical half-day itinerary: depart at 10:00, cruise along the coast towards Cabopino, anchor for a swim and lunch, then head back by 14:00. A full-day itinerary might include a stop at the beach club in Puerto Banús, a visit to the Cala del Faro cove for photos, and a slow cruise past the luxury villas of the Golden Mile. For a sunset charter, depart at 17:00, watch the sun dip behind La Concha, and return under the stars.

Pricing Guidance for a Hen Party Charter

Prices vary by boat size, duration, and season. A half-day charter for a group of 10 on a catamaran starts from around EUR 1,200 in the low season (May, June, September). In high season (July, August), expect from around EUR 1,800 for the same boat. A full-day charter for 16 people on a motor yacht can range from EUR 2,500 to EUR 4,500, depending on the level of luxury. Superyachts and large catamarans for 20-plus guests are price on request, typically EUR 5,000 to EUR 10,000 per day. Catering packages add EUR 30 to EUR 80 per person, depending on the menu. Drinks packages (beer, wine, cava, mixers) are around EUR 15 to EUR 25 per person.

BoatHire24 lists prices for each boat on the search page, so you can compare options. The price always includes the skipper, fuel for the itinerary, and basic insurance. You will need to pay a deposit (usually 50%) to secure the booking, with the balance due 7 days before departure.

Best Time of Year for a Marbella Hen Party

The Mediterranean season runs from April to October. May, June, and September offer the best balance of warm weather (25 to 30 degrees C) and lower prices. July and August are the hottest (30 to 35 degrees C) and busiest, with higher charter rates and more crowds at beach clubs. If you want a lively atmosphere with other boats and beach parties, summer high season is fine. If you prefer quieter coves and easier booking, shoulder months are better. April and October can be pleasant but cooler (20 to 25 degrees C), and the sea temperature is lower, so swimming might be brief. The wind can pick up in the afternoon, so morning charters are often calmer.

Sunset charters are popular year-round. The sun sets around 21:30 in July and 19:30 in October. The golden hour light over the Mediterranean is spectacular for photos.

How to Book Your Hen Party Charter

Go to BoatHire24 and use the search filters to select your group size, date, and preferred port. Browse the boats, read the descriptions, and check the included amenities. Each boat has a calendar showing availability. Once you find one you like, click 'Book Now' to send a request. The BoatHire24 team will confirm availability and send you a quote. You can also contact them for custom itineraries, such as adding a DJ, a photographer, or a specific catering menu. The booking process is straightforward, and you can pay by bank transfer or card.

For a hen party, it is wise to book at least 2 to 3 weeks in advance, especially in high season. The most popular boats sell out quickly. If you have a large group (20+), give the team a call to discuss options, as some boats can be configured with extra seating or a dance floor.

Who Is This Charter For?

This is for any hen group that wants to celebrate without the stress of organising a night out. It works for groups of friends who want to relax, swim, and sunbathe, as well as for those who want to party with music and drinks. The skipper ensures safety, so even if someone has never been on a boat before, it is fine. The charter is also suitable for groups with different interests: some can sunbathe on the bow, others can sit in the shade, and everyone can take a dip. It is not suitable for groups who want to go very fast or do watersports (though some boats have a small tender for towing a ringo or wakeboard, but that is not standard).

If you are looking for a more active hen party, consider a RIB charter that can take you to a beach club for jet skiing or paddleboarding. But for a classic celebration with prosecco, photos, and a relaxed vibe, a catamaran or motor yacht is the way to go.

Local Tips for Your Hen Party

Bring sunscreen, sunglasses, and a hat. The sun reflects off the water and can burn quickly. Pack a change of clothes if you plan to go to a beach club after. Most boats have a small fridge and a cooler, but bring your own ice if you need a lot. The skipper will advise on the best anchor spots based on wind and sea conditions. If you want to see dolphins, ask for a route towards the Strait of Gibraltar, though sightings are not guaranteed. The crew at BoatHire24 can also arrange a photographer for a few hours to capture the group shots.

For the bride, a white dress or a veil works well for photos against the blue sea. Some groups bring decorations like balloons or banners, but check with the skipper first as some boats have restrictions. The skipper will also point out the best spots for a group photo, such as the Puerto Banús lighthouse or the cliffs of Cala del Faro.

Why Choose BoatHire24

BoatHire24 is a marketplace that lists boats from multiple private owners and charter companies in Marbella and the Costa del Sol. This gives you a wide selection, from budget-friendly RIBs to luxury superyachts. Every boat is vetted for safety and includes a licensed skipper. The search page lets you filter by group size, price, and amenities, making it easy to find the right boat. The team speaks English and Spanish and can help with any special requests. You can also read the blog for more ideas on itineraries and destinations. For a hen party, the key is to book early, be clear about your group size, and communicate any special requirements. The result is a day on the water that the bride and her friends will remember for years.

Start your search on the search page to see available boats for your dates. The process is simple, and the team is ready to help you plan the ultimate celebration.

What to Expect from the Booking Process

After you submit a request on BoatHire24, the team typically responds within a few hours. They will confirm the boat, the skipper, and the exact departure time. For a hen party, you can add extras like a bottle of cava per person (around EUR 10 to EUR 15), a fruit platter (EUR 20 to EUR 30), or a professional photographer (EUR 150 to EUR 300 for a few hours). Some boats have a sound system that can connect to your phone, but check in advance if you want to play music through the boat's speakers. The skipper will send you a meeting point at the marina, usually at the boat's berth. Arrive 15 minutes early to sort out the cooler and any decorations. The deposit is refundable if you cancel at least 14 days before, but check the specific terms on the boat listing.

How to Handle Seasickness and Safety

If anyone in your group is prone to seasickness, choose a catamaran over a motor yacht. Catamarans are more stable because they have two hulls, so they roll less in choppy water. The Costa del Sol is generally calm, especially in the morning, but the wind can pick up in the afternoon. The skipper will monitor the forecast and may suggest a different route if conditions are rough. Avoid heavy meals before boarding, and bring ginger sweets or seasickness tablets. The boat will have life jackets for everyone, and the skipper will give a safety briefing before departure. For a hen party, the biggest risk is sunburn or dehydration, so bring plenty of water and reapply sunscreen every two hours. The skipper will also have a first aid kit on board.

Best Ports for Different Vibe Preferences

If you want to start the party as soon as you step on the boat, depart from Puerto Banús. The marina itself is a spectacle, with Ferraris parked outside the restaurants and massive yachts in the slips. The atmosphere is loud and glamorous, and you can walk to the bars and clubs like Pangea or Aqwa Mist after the charter. For a more relaxed start, choose Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo). It is smaller, with a few cafes and a quieter vibe, and you can walk into the old town for a pre-charter breakfast. Cabopino is ideal if you want a sandy beach nearby; the Artola dunes are a protected area with a naturist section. Estepona offers a charming old town with flower-covered streets and a long promenade. Sotogrande is exclusive and quiet, perfect for a sophisticated hen party with a focus on luxury. Benalmádena has a large marina with plenty of restaurants and a casino, good for a group that wants to stay in the area after the charter. Each port has a different character, so choose based on your group's style.

What to Do If the Weather Turns Bad

In the Mediterranean, bad weather is rare in summer, but it can happen. If the wind is over 20 knots or there is a storm forecast, the skipper will advise you to postpone or cancel. BoatHire24's cancellation policy typically allows rescheduling at no extra cost if the skipper deems it unsafe. If you decide to cancel on the day due to personal reasons, you may lose the deposit. For a hen party, it is wise to have a backup plan, such as a lunch reservation at a beach club or a visit to the Marbella old town. The boat will not go out in heavy rain or lightning, but light drizzle or a cloudy sky is usually fine. The sea temperature in summer is around 22 to 26 degrees C, so even if the air is cool, the water is warm enough for a swim.

How to Make the Most of Your Time on the Water

Plan your itinerary around the sun. If you want to sunbathe, aim for a midday charter when the sun is highest. If you want to see the sunset, book a charter from 17:00 to 21:00. The golden hour before sunset is the best time for photos, with the light casting a warm glow over the sea and the mountains. Ask the skipper to stop near the Puerto Banús lighthouse or the cliffs of Cala del Faro for the best shots. Bring a portable speaker if the boat's system is not great. Some groups bring a polaroid camera for instant photos. For the bride, a small bouquet of flowers or a veil makes for great props. If you want to swim, bring a towel and a dry bag for wet clothes. The skipper will anchor in a calm cove, usually with clear water. You can also bring snorkel masks to see the fish near the rocks. The sea around Cabopino and Cala del Faro has good visibility, often 10 to 15 metres.
