  • Marbella Boat Photoshoot Tips: Best Poses
  • Boat Party with DJ in Marbella: Best Music on the Water
  • Best Time of Year for a Boat Trip in Marbella, Spain
  • Starlite Festival Boat Party Marbella: Pre-Concert Fun

faqs.q: Do I need a boat licence to rent a speedboat in Benalmadena marina?
faqs.a: No. Every speedboat rental through BoatHire24 includes a licensed skipper who handles navigation and safety. You can steer if you want, but you do not need any licence or prior experience.

faqs.q: Can I book a speedboat for the same day in Benalmadena?
faqs.a: Yes. If the boat shows as available on the instant booking system, you can book up to 30 minutes before departure. The system updates in real time, so same-day bookings are common.

faqs.q: What is the cancellation policy for speedboat rentals in Benalmadena?
faqs.a: Most listings offer free cancellation up to 48 hours before the trip. After that, you lose the deposit. Check the specific terms on the boat listing page before you book.

faqs.q: How many people can fit on a speedboat in Benalmadena?
faqs.a: RIB speedboats typically seat 6 to 8 passengers. Larger open motor yachts can take up to 11 passengers. The maximum is set by the boat's safety certificate and the skipper's license.

faqs.q: Are there any hidden costs with speedboat rental in Benalmadena marina?
faqs.a: No. The price you see on the listing includes the boat, skipper, fuel, insurance, and VAT. Extras like water toys or catering are optional and clearly listed as add-ons.

faqs.q: What happens if the weather is bad on the day of my speedboat rental?
faqs.a: The skipper will assess conditions on the day. If it is unsafe to go out, you get a full refund or the option to reschedule. This is covered by our weather guarantee.

faqs.q: Can I bring my own food and drink on the speedboat?
faqs.a: Yes. You can bring whatever you like, but glass bottles are not allowed for safety reasons. Most boats have a cooler and a Bluetooth speaker for music.

title: Private Yacht Hire Marbella Stag: Party Packages
metaDescription: Book private yacht hire Marbella stag party packages with skipper, drinks, and DJ. From EUR 1,500 for up to 12 guests. Instant online booking.
h1: Private Yacht Hire Marbella Stag: Party Packages
intro:
If you are planning a stag do in Marbella and want something that doesn’t involve a sticky-floored club or a crowded beach bar, private yacht hire is the obvious move. You get a whole boat to yourselves, a licensed skipper who handles the navigation, and a floating party base with views of Puerto Banús and the Mediterranean. No hangover from queuing for drinks. No fighting for space. Just your group, a sound system, and the open sea.

BoatHire24 connects you with motor yachts, catamarans, and sailing yachts based in Marbella, Puerto Banús, Estepona, and Sotogrande. Every charter includes a skipper, so you can focus on the champagne and the banter. Packages start from around EUR 1,500 for a half-day on a 12-metre motor yacht for up to 12 guests, going up to EUR 8,000 for a full-day superyacht with a DJ and catering. Prices vary by season, boat size, and extras, but you can filter by budget and guest count on our search page.
bodyHtml:
What a Stag Yacht Party Includes

A typical private yacht hire Marbella stag package covers the boat, the skipper, fuel for a set itinerary, and basic safety equipment. Most operators let you bring your own drinks and food, or you can add a catering package with tapas, paella, or a barbecue. Sound systems are standard on most motor yachts and catamarans, and many allow you to connect your own playlist. Some packages include a DJ for an extra fee, usually around EUR 300-500 for a half-day.

You also get a dedicated skipper who knows the local waters, including the best spots for swimming off Cala del Faro or anchoring near the Marbella coastline. The skipper will not join the party, but they will ensure everyone stays safe and the boat runs on time. Life jackets, first aid kits, and a VHF radio are mandatory on all vessels. If you want watersports like jet skis, paddleboards, or a towable banana, those can be added for an extra cost, typically EUR 100-250 per activity.

For the full list of what is included with each boat, check the individual listings on our search page.

Best Departure Ports for a Stag Do

Puerto Banús is the most popular departure point for stag parties. It is a 15-minute taxi from Marbella town centre and has plenty of bars and restaurants for pre- or post-charter drinks. The marina itself is a spectacle, with superyachts, designer shops, and a lively atmosphere. From Puerto Banús, you can cruise to the coast of San Pedro de Alcántara, anchor at the sandy beach of Puerto Banús, or head west towards Estepona.

Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo) is another option, closer to the old town and the Golden Mile. It is slightly quieter than Puerto Banús but still has good facilities. Cabopino, east of Marbella, is a smaller marina with a relaxed vibe, ideal if your group wants to avoid the crowds. Estepona and Sotogrande are further west and offer longer cruises towards the Strait of Gibraltar, where you can spot dolphins. Benalmádena, east of Marbella, is also an option but adds travel time from Marbella centre.

Each port has its own character, but for a stag party, Puerto Banús is the default choice. You can filter by port on BoatHire24 when you search.

Boat Options for Stag Groups

Motor yachts are the most common choice for stag parties. They offer speed, space, and a sundeck for lounging. A 12-metre motor yacht typically accommodates 10-12 guests and costs from around EUR 1,500 for a half-day. For larger groups, a 15-18 metre motor yacht can take up to 20 guests and costs from EUR 2,500-4,000 for a half-day. Catamarans are also popular because they have a wider beam, more deck space, and a shallower draft for anchoring close to shore. A 14-metre catamaran can hold up to 18 guests and starts at around EUR 2,000 for a half-day.

Sailing yachts are less common for stag parties because they have less deck space and are slower, but some groups prefer the classic feel. RIBs and speedboats are an option for smaller groups of 6-8 who want a fast, adrenaline-fuelled trip to Cala del Faro or the Marbella coastline. Licence-free day boats are available for groups who want to self-drive, but they are limited to 6-8 guests and have restricted power, so they are better for a relaxed day rather than a party.

Superyachts (over 24 metres) are available for larger budgets, starting from around EUR 8,000 for a full day. These come with a crew, a chef, and often a Jacuzzi or a flybridge. They can accommodate 20-30 guests. For a full breakdown of boat types and capacities, browse our search page.

Pricing Guidance for Stag Yacht Hire

Prices for private yacht hire Marbella stag packages vary by season, boat size, and extras. In low season (November to March), a half-day charter on a 12-metre motor yacht starts from around EUR 1,200. In high season (June to September), the same boat costs from EUR 1,800-2,500 for a half-day. Full-day charters (8 hours) are usually 1.5-2 times the half-day price.

Extras add to the cost. A DJ costs EUR 300-500. Catering ranges from EUR 25-50 per person for tapas and drinks to EUR 80-120 per person for a full sit-down meal. Watersports equipment adds EUR 100-250 per item. Fuel is usually included in the charter price for a set itinerary, but if you want to cruise longer distances or at high speed, there may be a fuel surcharge of EUR 200-500.

A typical stag group of 12 guests spending half a day on a motor yacht with basic catering and a DJ can expect to pay around EUR 2,500-3,500 total. For a full-day superyacht with 20 guests, the cost can reach EUR 10,000-15,000. Always ask the operator for a detailed quote before booking. For more tips on budgeting, read our blog post on yacht charter costs.

Best Time of Year for a Stag Yacht Party

The best months for a stag yacht party in Marbella are May to October. June, July, and August offer the warmest weather, with average sea temperatures of 22-26 degrees C and air temperatures of 28-35 degrees C. These months are also the busiest, so book at least 4-6 weeks in advance. September and October are still warm, with fewer crowds and lower prices. May can be pleasant but the water is cooler, around 18-20 degrees C.

April and November are borderline. You might get a good day with 22 degrees C and sunshine, but there is a higher chance of wind or rain. December to February is too cold for swimming and most operators run reduced schedules. If you are set on a winter stag, consider a shorter charter with a focus on the marina views and onboard party rather than swimming.

How to Book a Stag Yacht Charter

Booking through BoatHire24 is straightforward. Go to the search page, enter your group size, preferred date, and port. You can filter by boat type, price range, and extras like DJ or catering. Each listing shows the boat details, photos, skipper info, and the total price including VAT. Once you find a boat, you can send a booking request or pay a deposit (usually 30-50%) to secure the date. The remaining balance is due 2-3 weeks before the charter.

You will receive a confirmation email with the meeting point, skipper contact, and a checklist of what to bring. Most operators require a security deposit of EUR 500-1,000, which is refunded after the charter if there is no damage. Cancellation policies vary, but most offer a full refund if you cancel more than 30 days before the charter, and a 50% refund if you cancel 14-30 days before. For last-minute bookings (within 14 days), you may need to pay the full amount upfront.

If you have any questions, contact the operator directly through the listing or email us at info@boathire24.com. We recommend booking at least 3 weeks in advance for peak season.

Who This Is For

Private yacht hire Marbella stag packages are designed for groups of 6-20 people who want a private, controlled environment for their celebration. It works well for stags that include a mix of ages or fitness levels, because there is no pressure to dance or drink. You can swim, sunbathe, eat, or just chat on the deck. It is also a good option if you want to avoid the hassle of club bookings, dress codes, and overcrowded venues.

If your group is smaller (4-6 people), a RIB or speedboat might be more cost-effective. For larger groups (over 20), you may need to charter two boats or book a superyacht. Some operators allow you to combine multiple boats in the same marina, so you can have separate groups that meet up at anchor. Discuss this with the operator when booking.

If you are planning a stag that involves watersports, fishing, or a day trip to the Strait of Gibraltar, make sure the boat you choose has the right equipment and range. Most motor yachts and catamarans can handle a 2-3 hour cruise to the dolphin hotspots near Gibraltar, but you will need a full-day charter to make it worthwhile.

Itinerary Ideas for a Stag Yacht Party

For a half-day charter (4 hours), a typical route from Puerto Banús heads east along the Marbella coastline towards Cabopino. You anchor off the sand dunes of Cabopino beach for a swim and lunch, then cruise back past the golden mile hotels. That gives you about 2 hours of cruising and 2 hours at anchor. If you want more party time, choose a shorter cruise and anchor near Cala del Faro, a quiet cove west of Marbella where the water is calmer.

A full-day charter (8 hours) lets you go further. From Marbella Marina, you can head west to Estepona, stop for lunch at the port, then continue to the Strait of Gibraltar for dolphin watching. The dolphins are almost guaranteed between April and October, and the crew will usually slow down so you can take photos. The round trip from Marbella to Gibraltar is about 50 NM, so you need a boat with a cruising speed of at least 20 knots to make it in 4 hours of cruising each way.

If your group is more about relaxing than sightseeing, anchor off the coast of San Pedro de Alcántara. The water there is shallow and clear, and there are no large hotels nearby, so it feels more private. You can also request a stop at the Río Verde river mouth, though the water is less clear there. For the best swimming, aim for a spot where the seabed is sandy rather than rocky.

What to Bring and What Not to Bring

Bring sunscreen (factor 50), sunglasses, a hat, swimwear, a towel, and a change of clothes. If you are prone to seasickness, take a tablet an hour before departure. Most boats have a small fridge, so you can bring your own drinks, but avoid glass bottles on board as they break and create a hazard. Cans and plastic bottles are fine. Bring a cooler bag if you want to keep things cold.

Do not bring illegal drugs. The skipper has the right to terminate the charter and call the port police if they suspect drug use, and you will lose your deposit. Also avoid heavy suitcases or large bags, as storage space on boats is limited. Leave valuables like passports and expensive jewellery onshore. If you want music, bring a Bluetooth speaker or a phone with a playlist, but check that the boat has an auxiliary input or Bluetooth connection first.

Most operators provide life jackets for all guests, but if you have young children in the group, you might want to bring their own properly fitted life jacket. Towels are sometimes provided on larger yachts, but check the listing to be sure. For food, you can bring your own or order a catering package. If you bring your own, keep it simple: sandwiches, fruit, crisps, and snacks that do not require plates or cutlery.

How to Split Costs Fairly

Stag groups often split the cost unevenly, with the stag paying less or nothing. A common approach is to divide the total cost by the number of guests, then subtract the stag's share from the total and split the remainder among the rest. For example, if the total is EUR 3,000 for 12 guests, that is EUR 250 per person. If the stag pays nothing, the remaining 11 guests pay EUR 273 each. If the stag pays half, the remaining guests pay EUR 250 each and the stag pays EUR 125.

Another method is to split the boat cost evenly and cover extras like catering and DJ separately. That way, guests who do not want the DJ or premium catering can opt out. Use a payment app like Revolut or PayPal to collect money in advance, and set a deadline of 2 weeks before the charter to avoid last-minute chasing. Some operators allow you to pay the deposit as a group and the balance individually, but most prefer a single payment from the organiser.

If you are using a superyacht with a chef, the crew gratuity is usually 10-15% of the charter fee, so factor that into your budget. The organiser should collect this in cash on the day or add it to the final payment. For more tips on budgeting, check our blog posts on group charter costs.

Legal and Safety Requirements

All charter boats in Spain must carry a valid licence, insurance, and safety equipment. The skipper holds a professional qualification (PER or higher) and is responsible for the safety of everyone on board. The boat must have a maximum capacity, usually printed on the licence, and the skipper will not allow more guests than that. If you try to exceed the limit, the charter will be cancelled and you will lose your deposit.

Alcohol is allowed on board, but the skipper must not drink. If the skipper suspects that a guest is too drunk to swim safely, they may refuse to anchor in open water. The skipper also has the authority to return to port early if the weather turns bad or if anyone's behaviour endangers the group. Most operators have a zero-tolerance policy for fighting, harassment, or damage to the boat. If you break something, the cost will be deducted from your security deposit.

For watersports like jet skis, the operator will provide a brief safety instruction and a life jacket. Jet skis are usually towed behind the yacht and used in designated areas away from swimmers. The minimum age for driving a jet ski in Spain is 16, and you need a valid ID. For paddleboards and kayaks, no licence is needed, but you should stay within sight of the boat.

Weather Contingency Plans

If the weather is bad on your charter date, the operator will usually offer a reschedule or a full refund. The skipper makes the final call based on wind speed and wave height. In Marbella, the most common weather issue is the Poniente wind, which blows from the west and can create choppy seas, especially in the afternoon. If the wind is over 20 knots, the skipper may cancel or change the itinerary to a more sheltered area like the coast of Estepona.

If you are booking in spring or autumn, consider a flexible cancellation policy. Some operators allow you to cancel up to 48 hours before with a full refund if the forecast is bad. For peak summer, cancellations due to weather are rare, but afternoon thunderstorms can occur in August. Most operators will delay the departure by an hour or two rather than cancel entirely. If you are travelling from abroad, buy travel insurance that covers charter cancellations due to weather.

For a stag party, a backup plan could be a harbour cruise instead of open water. Some catamarans can navigate close to the coast even in moderate wind, so you still get the experience without the risk of seasickness. Ask the operator about their weather policy when you book, and get it in writing.

What Happens After the Charter

After the charter, you return to the departure port. Most operators allow you to keep your belongings on the boat until everyone is off, but you should take all valuables with you. The skipper will inspect the boat for damage and return your security deposit within 24-48 hours, usually by bank transfer. If there is damage, the operator will provide a quote and deduct the cost from the deposit. You have the right to dispute the charge with the operator or through BoatHire24.

If you want to continue the party, Puerto Banús has several nightclubs and bars within walking distance of the marina. The most famous is Pangea, which has a terrace overlooking the port, but it can be expensive and has a dress code. For a more casual option, try the beach clubs like Nikki Beach or Ocean Club, which are a short taxi ride away. Many stag groups book a table at a restaurant in the old town of Marbella for dinner after the charter. The old town has plenty of tapas bars and seafood restaurants that accept large groups with a reservation.

If your group is staying in Marbella, the taxi fare from Puerto Banús to the centre is about EUR 15-20. For Estepona or Sotogrande, book a minibus in advance to avoid waiting. Some operators offer a transfer service from your accommodation to the marina for an extra fee, usually EUR 50-100 per group. Check the listing or ask the operator when booking.

Related guides

  • Early Morning Dolphin Watching Marbella: Best Sightings
  • Snorkel Coves Marbella Boat Trip: Hidden Gems to Explore
  • Feria Boat Party Costumes Marbella: Dress to Impress
  • Winter Boat Weather in Marbella: Is It Worth Chartering?
  • Stag Do Boat Activities Marbella: Top Ideas for Groups

faqs.q: How many guests can a stag yacht party accommodate?
faqs.a: Most motor yachts and catamarans in Marbella accommodate 10-12 guests on a half-day charter. Larger boats can take up to 20 guests. Superyachts can handle 20-30 guests. Always check the maximum capacity on the listing, as it includes the skipper.

faqs.q: Can we bring our own drinks and food?
faqs.a: Yes, most operators allow you to bring your own drinks and food. Some charge a corkage fee for glass bottles or require you to use their catering. Check the boat's policy before booking. If you want a catered package, many offer tapas, paella, or barbecue options.

faqs.q: Is a skipper included in the price?
faqs.a: Yes, every charter on BoatHire24 includes a licensed skipper. They handle navigation, safety, and anchoring. They do not join the party but will ensure everyone stays safe. You do not need a boating license.

faqs.q: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.a: Cancellation policies vary by operator. Typically, you get a full refund if you cancel more than 30 days before the charter, and a 50% refund if you cancel 14-30 days before. Last-minute cancellations (within 14 days) are usually non-refundable. Check the listing for exact terms.

faqs.q: Can we swim and do watersports?
faqs.a: Yes, most charters include stops for swimming at beaches like Cala del Faro or near the Marbella coastline. Watersports like jet skis, paddleboards, and banana boats can be added for an extra fee. Ask the operator when booking.

faqs.q: What happens if the weather is bad?
faqs.a: The skipper will assess conditions on the day. If it is unsafe, the charter may be rescheduled or cancelled with a full refund. Light rain or wind does not usually cause cancellation. Most boats have a covered deck or cabin.

faqs.q: How far in advance should we book?
faqs.a: For peak season (June to September), book at least 4-6 weeks in advance. For other months, 2-3 weeks is usually enough. Last-minute bookings within 14 days are possible but availability may be limited, and you may need to pay the full amount upfront.

title: Catamaran Hire Estepona Sunset: Evening Charter Deals
metaDescription: Book catamaran hire Estepona sunset for an evening charter. Sail from Estepona port with a skipper, enjoy drinks and views. Check deals now on BoatHire24.
h1: Catamaran Hire Estepona Sunset: Evening Charter Deals
intro:
You want a sunset cruise from Estepona on a catamaran, with a skipper, drinks, and no hidden costs. That is exactly what BoatHire24 offers. We list catamarans for hire in Estepona, all with licensed skippers included, so you can watch the sun drop behind the Strait of Gibraltar without worrying about navigation or mooring fees. Evening charters typically run from around 18:00 to 21:00 in summer, and you can expect prices from around EUR 800 for a private catamaran for up to 10 guests. No surprises, no extra charges for the skipper or fuel on most deals.

Estepona is one of the quieter ports on the Costa del Sol, which means less boat traffic and more space to anchor near the coast. The sunset views from a catamaran here are clear, often with the silhouette of La Concha mountain in the distance and dolphins breaking the surface near the Strait. You book through BoatHire24, pick your date, and the catamaran is ready at Estepona Marina (Puerto Deportivo) with a local skipper who knows the best spots. No membership, no hidden fees.
bodyHtml:
What Is Included in a Catamaran Sunset Charter in Estepona

Every catamaran hire Estepona sunset charter on BoatHire24 comes with a licensed skipper as standard. That means you do not need a boating licence, and you do not have to steer the boat. The skipper handles everything from departure to anchoring to return. Most charters also include fuel, VAT, and basic insurance. Some packages add a welcome drink, soft drinks, and ice. You can often bring your own food and extra drinks, but check the specific listing because some caterers offer optional catering at extra cost. Typical evening charters last 3 to 4 hours, departing around 17:30 or 18:00 and returning after sunset, around 21:00 to 21:30 depending on the season.

Ports for Catamaran Sunset Charters in Estepona

The main departure point is Estepona Marina, also called Puerto Deportivo de Estepona. It is a modern marina with around 400 berths, located right next to the Estepona old town. You can walk from the marina to the port for a pre-charter coffee or a post-cruise dinner. The marina has parking nearby, though spaces fill up in summer. Some catamarans also depart from Puerto Banús, about 20 minutes east by road, but for a sunset charter specifically in Estepona, the local marina is the most convenient. The route usually heads west towards the Strait of Gibraltar, passing the Cala del Faro and the Punta de la Doncella lighthouse.

Boat Options for Catamaran Hire Estepona Sunset

Catamarans available for sunset charters in Estepona range from 35 to 50 feet (around 11 to 15 metres). The most common are Lagoon and Fountaine Pajot models, which have wide decks, trampolines at the front, and a flybridge for panoramic views. You can find catamarans that sleep 6 to 10 guests for a sunset cruise, though overnight stays are not typical on evening charters. The larger catamarans, 45 feet and above, often have a shaded cockpit, a galley, and a sound system. If you want a more intimate experience, smaller catamarans around 38 feet work well for 4 to 6 people. All listings on BoatHire24 search show the exact guest capacity, amenities, and photos.

Pricing Guidance for Evening Catamaran Charters in Estepona

Prices for catamaran hire Estepona sunset start from around EUR 800 for a 3-hour charter on a 38-foot catamaran for up to 8 guests. Mid-range options, like a 45-foot Lagoon for up to 12 guests, run from EUR 1,200 to EUR 1,800 for a 4-hour evening trip. High-end catamarans with extra amenities, such as a jacuzzi or premium sound system, can go up to EUR 3,000. These prices include the skipper, fuel, and basic insurance. Catering, if you want a full dinner, adds EUR 30 to EUR 60 per person. You can also book a bareboat (no skipper) if you have a licence, but for sunset cruises, most people prefer the skipper to handle everything.

Best Season for Catamaran Sunset Charters in Estepona

The sunset season in Estepona runs from April to October, with the best conditions from June to September. In July and August, sunset is around 21:30, giving you light until 22:00. Sea temperatures range from 18 degrees C in April to 26 degrees C in August. The wind is usually light in the evenings, often 5 to 10 knots, which is ideal for catamarans because they are stable and do not heel. You might get a light swell of 0.5 to 1 metre. The Strait of Gibraltar can have stronger winds, but most sunset charters stay close to the coast near Estepona, where the water is calmer. If you book in October, sunset is around 19:30, so evening charters start earlier, around 16:00.

How to Book a Catamaran Sunset Charter in Estepona

You book directly on BoatHire24. Go to the search page, select Estepona as the port, choose catamaran as the boat type, and filter by evening or sunset charters. Each listing shows the price, guest capacity, and availability calendar. You can contact the owner through the platform to ask about specific catering or timing. Payment is secure, and you get a confirmation with the skipper’s contact details. Most owners require a 50% deposit to secure the booking, with the balance paid on the day. Cancellation policies vary, but many offer free cancellation up to 7 days before. If you are unsure, read the BoatHire24 blog for tips on choosing the right charter.

Who Is a Catamaran Sunset Charter For

This type of charter works for couples, small groups of friends, and families with older children. Catamarans have a flat deck and no heeling, so they are comfortable for people who get seasick on monohulls. The sunset timing means you do not need to take a full day off work; you can leave the office at 17:00, walk to the marina, and be back by 21:30. It is also a popular choice for birthday celebrations, engagement proposals, or just a relaxed evening with drinks. If you are a solo traveller, you can join a shared sunset cruise, which costs around EUR 100 per person, but availability is limited. Private charters are more common.

What to Expect on the Day of Your Sunset Charter

On the day, you arrive at Estepona Marina 15 minutes before the scheduled departure. The skipper meets you at the boat, gives a safety briefing, and explains the plan. You cast off and head west along the coast, past the beaches of La Rada and El Cristo. The skipper usually anchors near the Cala del Faro or off the coast of the Sierra Bermeja mountains for a clear view of the sunset. You can swim if the water is warm enough, or just sit on the trampoline with a drink. After sunset, you motor back to the marina under the stars. The skipper handles the mooring, and you leave the boat by 21:30.

Local Attractions Near Estepona Marina

If you arrive early, Estepona old town is a 10-minute walk from the marina. It has whitewashed buildings, flower pots on the walls, and a few tapas bars. The Orchidarium, a glass greenhouse with over 1,000 orchid species, is worth a quick visit. For dinner after the charter, the port area has seafood restaurants like El Estrecho and La Escollera. If you want to extend your stay, Estepona has hotels like the Kempinski Resort or the Ikos Andalusia, both with direct beach access. The nearby Nerja caves are about an hour drive east, but that is a day trip, not a sunset charter.

Why Estepona for a Catamaran Sunset Instead of Puerto Banús

Puerto Banús gets all the attention, but Estepona offers a quieter, more authentic sunset experience. The marina is less crowded, the coastline is less built up, and the views of the Sierra Bermeja mountains turning pink at dusk are hard to beat. In Puerto Banús, you are surrounded by superyachts and flashy bars; in Estepona, you get a relaxed evening with the old town lights twinkling in the background. The sunset itself is also better because the coast faces west, and there is no obstruction from the Rock of Gibraltar or the African coast. If you want a party scene, go to Banús. If you want a calm, romantic sunset, Estepona wins every time.

Typical Sunset Route from Estepona Marina

Most catamaran sunset charters from Estepona Marina follow a standard route. You leave the marina and head west towards the Punta de la Doncella lighthouse, a white tower on a rocky point. From there, you continue past the Cala del Faro, a small cove with a sandy beach that is popular for anchoring. The skipper usually stops somewhere between the lighthouse and the Cala del Faro, about 2 to 3 nautical miles from the marina. The depth here is around 5 to 10 metres, good for anchoring. You get a clear view of the sun dipping behind the Strait of Gibraltar, and if the conditions are right, you can see the mountains of Morocco on the horizon. The return trip is a straight line back, about 20 minutes at cruising speed.

What to Bring on a Catamaran Sunset Charter in Estepona

Bring a light jacket or a fleece, even in summer. The temperature drops 5 to 10 degrees C after sunset, and the breeze on the water makes it feel cooler. Sunscreen is essential until the sun goes down, and sunglasses are useful against the glare. If you plan to swim, bring a towel and a change of clothes. Most catamarans have a freshwater shower on the back deck to rinse off. For drinks, bring your own wine, beer, or cocktails if the charter allows it. Some catamarans have a cooler with ice, but check the listing. Do not bring glass bottles; the skipper will ask you to transfer drinks to plastic cups. A camera or smartphone for photos is obvious, but a small pair of binoculars is useful for spotting dolphins.

Dolphin Watching Possibilities on Estepona Sunset Charters

The waters off Estepona are part of the Strait of Gibraltar migration route, and dolphin sightings are common. You are most likely to see common dolphins or striped dolphins, and occasionally bottlenose dolphins. The best time for dolphin watching is late afternoon, which overlaps with sunset charter timing. The dolphins often feed near the surface around 18:00 to 19:00, so your skipper may slow down or alter the course slightly if they spot a pod. Do not expect guaranteed sightings; it is nature, not a theme park. But the probability is high enough that many local skippers keep a lookout. If you are specifically interested in dolphins, ask the owner when booking whether the area has recent sightings.

Catamaran Hire Estepona Sunset for Special Occasions

Sunset charters are a popular choice for proposals, anniversaries, and birthdays. If you are planning a proposal, tell the skipper in advance so they can anchor at a scenic spot and give you privacy. Some catamarans allow you to arrange a photographer on the shore or on a nearby boat, but that requires coordination. For birthdays, you can bring a cake, decorations, and a playlist for the sound system. The skipper can also arrange a bottle of cava or champagne as a welcome drink, usually at an extra cost of EUR 20 to EUR 50. For a group celebration, a 45-foot catamaran with a flybridge gives everyone space to mingle. Just keep in mind that the charter is 3 to 4 hours, so plan the timing of your toast to coincide with the sunset.

How Weather Affects Catamaran Sunset Charters in Estepona

The weather in Estepona is generally reliable for sunset charters, but a few conditions can cause cancellations. Strong easterly winds, called Levante, can create choppy seas of 1.5 to 2 metres, which is uncomfortable for a catamaran despite its stability. The skipper will cancel or reschedule if the wind exceeds 20 knots or the swell is over 1.5 metres. Thunderstorms are rare in summer but can happen in September or October. If the sky is overcast, the sunset is less dramatic, but the charter still goes ahead unless there is a safety risk. Most owners offer a full refund or rescheduling if the weather forces a cancellation. Check the forecast on the day; if it looks marginal, contact the owner directly.

Combining a Catamaran Sunset with Dinner in Estepona

Many sunset charters end around 21:00 to 21:30, which is perfect for a late dinner. Estepona Marina has several restaurants with terrace seating overlooking the water. Try La Escollera for grilled fish, or El Estrecho for paella. If you want something more casual, the old town has tapas bars like Bar El Paseo or Casa del Rey. Some catamarans offer optional catering with a dinner served on board, typically a cold buffet of salads, meats, and seafood. That costs EUR 30 to EUR 60 per person and saves you the walk to a restaurant. If you book a private charter, you can arrange for a caterer to deliver food to the boat before departure. Discuss this with the owner when you book.

Catamaran Hire Estepona Sunset for Corporate Events

A catamaran sunset charter works well for small corporate events, such as client entertainment or team building. The relaxed atmosphere and limited guest capacity (usually up to 12) make it suitable for networking without being too formal. You can book a 45-foot catamaran for a 4-hour evening charter, which gives time for drinks, a short presentation if needed, and the sunset views. Some owners allow you to bring branded merchandise or set up a small banner, but check the listing rules. The cost for a corporate charter ranges from EUR 1,500 to EUR 3,000, depending on the catamaran size and catering. It is cheaper than renting a venue in Puerto Banús, and the setting is more memorable.

Accessibility and Comfort on Catamaran Sunset Charters

Catamarans are generally more accessible than monohulls because the deck is flat and there are no steep stairs to the cabin. The main deck is at the same level as the dock, so boarding is easy. However, the trampoline at the front is a net, not a solid surface, which can be tricky for people with mobility issues. The flybridge, if the catamaran has one, requires climbing a ladder, usually 5 to 6 steps. Most catamarans have a bathroom (head) below deck, accessed by a short staircase. If you have specific accessibility needs, contact the owner before booking to confirm the layout. For elderly guests or those with limited mobility, a 38-foot catamaran with a single-level deck is a good choice.

How to Get to Estepona Marina for Your Sunset Charter

Estepona Marina is easy to reach by car or taxi. From Marbella, it is a 20-minute drive west on the A-7 motorway. From Málaga airport, it takes about 50 minutes via the AP-7 toll road (toll cost around EUR 5). The marina has a paid car park with about 200 spaces, costing EUR 2 per hour in summer. If you are staying in Estepona town, it is a 10-minute walk from the old town or a 5-minute taxi ride (EUR 5 to EUR 8). There is also a bus stop at the marina entrance for the local line from Marbella to Estepona. For a sunset charter, arriving by taxi is easiest because you do not have to worry about parking after dark.

What Happens if You Miss the Departure Time

Catamaran sunset charters have a fixed departure time to catch the sunset at the right moment. If you are late, the skipper may wait 10 to 15 minutes, but they cannot delay significantly because the light changes. If you miss the departure, you lose the booking and the deposit. To avoid this, plan to arrive at the marina 30 minutes early. Traffic on the A-7 can be heavy in summer, especially on Friday and Saturday evenings. If you are coming from Marbella or Málaga, add 15 to 30 minutes to your travel time. Some owners offer a grace period of 15 minutes, but do not rely on it. The best approach is to be at the marina by 16:30 for a 17:00 departure.

faqs.q: Do I need a boating licence for catamaran hire Estepona sunset?
faqs.a: No. Every catamaran charter on BoatHire24 includes a licensed skipper, so you do not need any licence. The skipper handles navigation, mooring, and safety.

faqs.q: How long does a sunset catamaran charter last?
faqs.a: Most evening charters last 3 to 4 hours, typically from 18:00 to 21:00 or 21:30, depending on the sunset time. You can adjust the timing with the owner before booking.

faqs.q: Can I bring my own food and drinks on a catamaran sunset charter?
faqs.a: Yes, most charters allow you to bring your own food and drinks. Some owners provide a cooler and ice. Check the listing for any restrictions or optional catering.

faqs.q: What is the maximum number of guests on a sunset catamaran charter in Estepona?
faqs.a: Typical catamarans hold 8 to 12 guests for a sunset charter. Larger catamarans up to 15 metres can take up to 18 guests, but check the specific listing for capacity.

faqs.q: What happens if the weather is bad on the day of my sunset charter?
faqs.a: The skipper decides if conditions are safe. If the charter is cancelled due to weather, you get a full refund or can reschedule. Most owners offer free cancellation up to 7 days before.

faqs.q: Can I book a shared sunset catamaran cruise in Estepona?
faqs.a: Shared cruises are rare but sometimes available. They cost around EUR 100 per person. Private charters are more common and offer more flexibility. Search on BoatHire24 for shared options.

faqs.q: Is there parking near Estepona Marina for the charter?
faqs.a: Yes, there is paid parking at Puerto Deportivo de Estepona. Spaces fill up in summer, so arrive 30 minutes early. Some owners offer advice on free parking nearby.

title: Luxury Motor Yacht Rental Puerto Banus: Top Fleet
metaDescription: Book a luxury motor yacht rental Puerto Banus with a licensed skipper. Compare top motor yachts from 12m to 30m. Prices from around EUR 1,200. Instant online booking.
h1: Luxury Motor Yacht Rental Puerto Banus
keyword: luxury motor yacht rental Puerto Banus
intro:
You want to cruise the Costa del Sol in style, and Puerto Banus is the place to start. A luxury motor yacht rental in Puerto Banus gives you access to a fleet of modern motor yachts, from fast sport cruisers to spacious flybridges, all with a licensed skipper included. No licence needed, just show up and enjoy.

BoatHire24 lists the top motor yachts available for charter in Puerto Banus, with prices starting from around EUR 1,200 for a half-day trip. You can browse by size, amenities, and availability, and book directly online. The harbour is the main departure point for exploring Marbella's coastline, the Marbella Marina, and beyond to Estepona or even the Strait of Gibraltar.
bodyHtml:
Why Choose a Luxury Motor Yacht Rental in Puerto Banus

Puerto Banus is the superyacht capital of the Costa del Sol, and renting a luxury motor yacht here means you get the full experience. The marina itself is a spectacle, lined with designer boutiques and Michelin-starred restaurants. But the real draw is the water. A motor yacht gives you speed, comfort, and the ability to cover more coastline in a day than a sailing yacht. You can cruise from Puerto Banus to the Marbella Marina in 15 minutes, reach Estepona in 30, or head south to the Strait of Gibraltar for dolphin spotting.

Every charter includes a professional skipper, so you don't need a boating licence. The skipper handles navigation, safety, and can recommend the best anchor spots. You just relax, swim, and soak up the sun. The weather in Marbella averages 22-30 degrees C from May to October, making it ideal for motor yacht charters.

What's Included in Your Motor Yacht Charter

When you book a luxury motor yacht rental in Puerto Banus through BoatHire24, you get more than just a boat. The standard package includes the yacht, a licensed skipper, fuel for a standard itinerary (usually up to 40 NM), and basic safety equipment. Many yachts also come with snorkelling gear, a paddleboard, a Bluetooth sound system, and a wet bar. Some larger yachts have a flybridge with sun pads and a bimini top for shade.

You can add extras like a private chef, a hostess, jet skis, or a photographer. Catering options range from a simple tapas platter to a full gourmet menu. Just note that food and drinks are usually not included in the base price, but you can pre-order or bring your own. The skipper will handle the fridge and ice.

Departure Ports for Luxury Motor Yacht Rentals

Puerto Banus is the primary departure port for luxury motor yachts, but you can also start from other ports on the Costa del Sol. Here are the main options:

  • Puerto Banus: The most glamorous port, with direct access to Marbella's best beaches and nightlife. Most of our fleet is based here.
  • Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo): A smaller, quieter marina in the centre of Marbella. Good if you're staying in the old town or the Golden Mile.
  • Cabopino: A tiny port near the dunes of Artola. Ideal for a relaxed start with fewer crowds.
  • Estepona Marina: A modern marina with easy access to the Strait of Gibraltar. Great for dolphin watching and trips to Gibraltar.
  • Sotogrande: An exclusive residential port near the border with Gibraltar. Perfect for high-end charters and trips to the Barbate coast.
  • Benalmadena: A busy port east of Marbella, close to Torremolinos and Fuengirola.

Each port has its own charm, but Puerto Banus remains the top choice for luxury motor yacht rentals due to the concentration of high-end yachts and the vibrant atmosphere.

Boat Types Available: From Sport Cruisers to Superyachts

The luxury motor yacht fleet in Puerto Banus is diverse. You can choose from:

  • Motor Yachts (12-20m): Sleek, modern boats with a master cabin, guest cabins, and a spacious saloon. Examples include the Azimut 55, Sunseeker Manhattan 50, or Princess 58. Prices from around EUR 1,200 for half a day.
  • Flybridge Motor Yachts (20-30m): Larger yachts with an upper deck for dining and sunbathing. These have 3-4 cabins and can accommodate up to 12 guests. Examples: Ferretti 780, Pershing 82, or Sanlorenzo SL86. Prices from EUR 2,500.
  • Superyachts (30m+): Full crew, including captain, stewardess, and chef. These have 4-5 cabins, jacuzzis, and tenders. Examples: Benetti 40m, Heesen 45m. Price on request, typically EUR 8,000+ per day.
  • RIB Speedboats: Smaller, faster boats for a day trip to the Marbella coast or the Strait. Not strictly luxury, but can be fun for a quick spin. Prices from EUR 500.

All boats are maintained to high standards and inspected regularly. You can filter by length, price, and amenities on our search page.

Pricing Guidance for Luxury Motor Yacht Rentals

Prices for a luxury motor yacht rental in Puerto Banus vary widely. As a general guide:

  • Half-day (4 hours): From around EUR 1,200 for a 12-14m yacht. Typical for couples or small groups of 4-6.
  • Full day (8 hours): From EUR 2,000 for the same size. For a larger 20m yacht, expect EUR 3,000-5,000.
  • Multi-day charters: Prices drop per day for longer rentals. A week on a 25m yacht can be EUR 15,000-25,000.
  • Superyachts: Price on request, but budget EUR 8,000-15,000 per day for a 30m+ yacht.

These prices include the skipper, fuel for 40-60 NM, and basic insurance. Extras like food, drinks, jet skis, or a hostess are additional. Always check the exact inclusions before booking. You can compare prices and availability on BoatHire24.

Best Seasons for Motor Yacht Charters

The Costa del Sol has a long boating season. The best months for a luxury motor yacht rental in Puerto Banus are May through October. Here's a breakdown:

  • May-June: Warm (25-28 degrees C), less crowded. Good deals available. Sea temperature around 18-20 degrees C, so swimming is possible with a wetsuit.
  • July-August: Peak season. Hot (30-35 degrees C), busy marinas. Book at least 2-3 weeks in advance. Prices are highest.
  • September-October: Still warm (25-30 degrees C), calmer winds. Ideal for longer trips. Sea temperature peaks at 22-24 degrees C.
  • November-April: Cooler (15-20 degrees C), more chance of rain. Some yachts operate year-round, but you'll need a jacket. Prices can be 30-50% lower.

If you want guaranteed sunshine and swimming, aim for June to September. For a quieter experience, May or October work well.

Popular Itineraries from Puerto Banus

Your skipper will suggest an itinerary based on your preferences, but here are three classic routes:

  • Marbella Coast Cruise: Head east from Puerto Banus past the Marbella Marina and the Golden Mile to the Cabopino dunes. Anchor at Cala del Faro for swimming. Stop for lunch at a chiringuito or on board. Duration: half day.
  • Estepona and the Strait: Cruise west to Estepona, passing the Rio Verde and the La Concha mountain backdrop. Anchor off the Estepona old town for a swim and a walk. If time permits, head further south to the Strait of Gibraltar to see dolphins and whales. Duration: full day.
  • Gibraltar and Barbate: A longer trip (40 NM) to the Rock of Gibraltar. You can stop at the Barbate coast for lunch, then return via Sotogrande. Requires a full day or overnight.

Your skipper knows the best anchor spots and can adjust for wind and sea conditions. Just tell them what you want to see.

How to Book a Luxury Motor Yacht Rental

Booking through BoatHire24 is straightforward. Go to our search page, filter by date, length, and price, and browse the available yachts. Each listing includes photos, specs, and a price. Click 'Book Now' to send a request. The owner or charter manager will confirm availability and send a contract. Payment is typically 50% deposit upfront, balance 2 weeks before departure.

You can also call or email us for personalised recommendations. We'll ask about your group size, budget, and preferred itinerary. We work with over 50 charter companies in Puerto Banus, so we can find the best yacht for you.

For more tips on choosing a yacht and planning your trip, check out our blog.

Who Is This For

Luxury motor yacht rentals in Puerto Banus suit a range of people:

  • Couples: A romantic day out with a private skipper and champagne on the flybridge. Many yachts have a master cabin for a nap or overnight stay.
  • Families: Safe and stable yachts with space for kids. Skippers are experienced with children and can provide life jackets.
  • Groups of friends: Celebrate a birthday, bachelor party, or reunion. Most yachts can host up to 12 guests for a day charter.
  • Corporate clients: Impress clients or reward staff with a luxury yacht charter. Many yachts have Wi-Fi and AV systems for presentations.

If you want a party boat, look for yachts with a large flydeck and a powerful sound system. If you prefer quiet luxury, choose a yacht with a spacious saloon and a sun pad area.

What to Bring and What to Expect

Pack light: swimwear, sunscreen, a hat, sunglasses, and a light jacket for the evening. The skipper will provide towels and snorkelling gear. You can bring your own food and drinks, but many yachts have a minimum catering order. Check with the charter company.

On the day, arrive at the marina 15 minutes before departure. The skipper will give a safety briefing and show you around. You'll leave the harbour and head out to sea. The skipper will anchor at a chosen spot, and you can swim, snorkel, or just relax. Lunch is usually served around 1pm. You'll return to port by the agreed time.

If you have any special requests, like a specific destination or a surprise for a partner, let the booking team know in advance. Most skippers are happy to accommodate.

Safety and Insurance

All yachts on BoatHire24 are fully insured and maintained to Spanish maritime safety standards. The skipper holds a professional licence and first aid certification. The yacht carries life jackets, a first aid kit, a fire extinguisher, and a VHF radio. For larger yachts, there is also a liferaft and an EPIRB.

You will be asked to sign a charter agreement before departure. This covers liability and cancellation terms. We recommend taking out travel insurance that covers boating activities, especially if you book a multi-day charter.

Local Flavours and Onboard Dining Options

One of the perks of a luxury motor yacht rental in Puerto Banus is the food. You can pre-order a gourmet menu from a private chef who prepares everything in the galley. Think grilled sea bass, paella, Iberian ham, and chilled gazpacho. Some yachts have a barbecue on the flybridge. Costs range from around EUR 40 per person for a tapas lunch to EUR 100 for a full seafood feast with wine. Alternatively, your skipper can moor near a chiringuito like Trocadero Marbella or Chiringuito Banus, and you can wade ashore for a casual meal. If you prefer to bring your own, most yachts have a fridge and a cooler. Just avoid glass bottles on the deck.

How the Skipper Adds Value to Your Day

The licensed skipper on your luxury motor yacht rental in Puerto Banus does more than steer. They know the local waters, the wind patterns, and the quiet anchorages that tourist boats miss. For example, they can take you to a secluded cove near Cala del Faro where the water is calm and the crowds are thin. They also handle docking, anchoring, and safety. Many skippers speak English and Spanish, and some offer a commentary on the coastline. You do not need to tip, but EUR 50-100 for a full day is appreciated if the service is good. The skipper is your host, not a chauffeur.

Comparing Motor Yachts to Catamarans for Luxury Charters

You might wonder whether a motor yacht or a catamaran is better for a luxury rental in Puerto Banus. Motor yachts are faster, so you can cover 40 NM in a morning and still have time for lunch. They also have a lower profile, which means less motion in choppy seas. Catamarans offer more deck space and stability at anchor, but they cruise at 8-12 knots versus 20-25 knots for a motor yacht. For a day trip to the Strait of Gibraltar or a fast run to Estepona, a motor yacht wins. For a slow, spacious day anchored off Cabopino, a catamaran works. Your choice depends on whether you value speed or square footage.

Hidden Costs to Consider Before Booking

When budgeting for a luxury motor yacht rental in Puerto Banus, look past the base price. Fuel is usually included for a standard itinerary of 40-60 NM, but if you ask the skipper to go further, say to Gibraltar or Barbate, you may pay extra. A surcharge of EUR 200-400 applies for longer distances. Dockage fees at private marinas for lunch stops can add EUR 50-100. If you book a hostess or a chef, add EUR 150-300 per day. Cancellation insurance is optional but worth it for peak season bookings. Always read the contract for the full list of exclusions. Our BoatHire24 listings show what is included and what costs extra.

Best Anchorages for Swimming and Snorkelling

The Costa del Sol has several excellent spots for a swim stop during your luxury motor yacht rental in Puerto Banus. Cala del Faro, just west of Puerto Banus, is a rocky cove with clear water and a small beach. It is popular but not crowded on weekdays. Cabopino beach, near the Artola dunes, has a sandy bottom and gentle waves. For snorkelling, head to the rocks at Punta de la Mona, east of Marbella, where you can see bream, wrasse, and the occasional octopus. The water visibility ranges from 5-15 metres depending on the wind. Your skipper will know the best spot for the day's conditions. Bring your own mask and fins for the best fit.

Winter Charters on the Costa del Sol

Most people think of summer for a luxury motor yacht rental in Puerto Banus, but winter has its charm. From November to March, the air temperature hovers around 15-20 degrees C, and the sun is still strong. The sea is rougher, with more swell from the Atlantic, but larger motor yachts handle it well. You can cruise to Estepona for a walk in the old town, or head to the Strait of Gibraltar to see migrating whales. Prices drop by 30-50 percent, so a half-day on a 15m yacht might cost EUR 700-900. You will need a jacket and a windbreaker, but the marina is quiet and the service is personal. Some yachts have heating in the saloon.

Booking a Multi-Day Charter to Explore Further

If you have three to seven days, consider a multi-day luxury motor yacht rental in Puerto Banus. You can cruise east to Nerja for a visit to the caves, or west to Tarifa for kite surfing and the Strait. Multi-day charters include overnight stays in marinas or at anchor. The yacht becomes your floating hotel. You wake up in a new port each day. Costs for a 20m yacht for a week start around EUR 15,000, which includes the skipper and fuel for 40 NM per day. You pay for dockage and food separately. This option works well for families or groups who want to see more of the coast without rushing. Book early, as multi-day charters require more planning.

faqs.q: Do I need a licence for a luxury motor yacht rental in Puerto Banus?
faqs.a: No. Every charter includes a licensed skipper who handles all navigation and safety. You just relax and enjoy the trip.

faqs.q: What is the minimum age to rent a motor yacht in Puerto Banus?
faqs.a: There is no minimum age for passengers, but children must be accompanied by an adult. The skipper will provide life jackets for children.

faqs.q: Can I bring my own food and drinks on the yacht?
faqs.a: Yes, most charters allow you to bring your own food and drinks. Some yachts have a minimum catering order, so check with the charter company before booking.

faqs.q: How many people can fit on a luxury motor yacht?
faqs.a: It depends on the yacht size. A 12-14m yacht typically holds 6-8 guests for a day charter. A 20m+ yacht can hold up to 12 guests. For larger groups, consider a catamaran or superyacht.

faqs.q: What happens if the weather is bad on my charter day?
faqs.a: The skipper will assess the conditions. If it's unsafe to sail, the charter can be rescheduled or cancelled with a full refund. Check the cancellation policy before booking.

faqs.q: Can I charter a luxury motor yacht for a multi-day trip?
faqs.a: Yes. Many yachts in Puerto Banus are available for multi-day charters. You can cruise to Gibraltar, the Balearic Islands, or the Algarve coast. Prices are negotiable for longer bookings.

faqs.q: How far in advance should I book a luxury motor yacht in Puerto Banus?
faqs.a: For peak season (July-August), book at least 2-3 weeks in advance. For other months, a week is usually enough. Last-minute bookings are possible but may have limited availability.

title: Fishing Charter Benalmadena Full Day: Deep Sea Trip
metaDescription: Book a full-day fishing charter Benalmadena deep sea trip. Catch bluefin tuna, dentex & more with a licensed skipper. Prices from EUR 800. Reserve now.
h1: Fishing Charter Benalmadena Full Day: Deep Sea Trip
intro: You want a full day on the water, rods bent, reels screaming, and a cooler full of fish at the end of it. A fishing charter Benalmadena full day is exactly that: a deep sea trip from Benalmadena Marina, running 8 to 10 hours, targeting the big stuff off the Costa del Sol. You get a licensed skipper who knows the local marks, all the tackle, bait, and usually lunch and drinks. Prices start from around EUR 800 for a small group on a standard motor boat, going up to EUR 2,500 or more for a larger yacht with extra crew. You don't need a fishing licence yourself, the boat holds one. You just show up, fish, and take your catch home or to a local restaurant for cooking.
bodyHtml:
What You Get on a Full Day Fishing Charter Benalmadena

A full day trip means you leave Benalmadena Marina at around 8am and return by 5pm or 6pm. The boat heads out to the deeper grounds, often 10 to 20 nautical miles offshore, where the water drops to 100-300 metres. You target species like bluefin tuna (up to 300 kg), amberjack, dentex, sea bass, and sometimes swordfish. The skipper handles all the navigation, finds the fish with sonar, and sets up the rods. You get heavy tackle, lures, bait (squid, mackerel, live bait if available), and a fighting chair for the big ones. Most charters include a light lunch, soft drinks, and water. You keep whatever you catch, subject to local size and bag limits. The boat is a typical 8-12 metre motor fishing vessel, with a cabin, toilet, and deck space for 4-6 anglers. Some operators offer larger catamarans or sportfishers for bigger groups.

Benalmadena Marina: Your Departure Point

Benalmadena Marina (Puerto Deportivo de Benalmadena) is one of the busiest fishing ports on the Costa del Sol. It sits just east of Torremolinos and west of Fuengirola, with direct access to the Mediterranean. The marina has a dedicated fishing fleet, plus chandlers and bait shops. You meet your skipper at the designated pontoon, usually near the fuel dock or the fishing boat berths. Parking is available in the marina car park (paid, around EUR 15 per day). If you're staying in Benalmadena, Torremolinos, or Mijas, you can walk or take a short taxi. The marina also has cafes and a supermarket for last-minute supplies. For more on the area, check our Benalmadena Marina guide.

Deep Sea Fishing: What You Target and How It Works

The deep sea trip focuses on pelagic species that live in open water, not near the bottom. The main targets are bluefin tuna (seasonal, June to October), yellowfin tuna, skipjack, and bonito. You also get dentex, amberjack, and sea bass around wrecks and rocky reefs. The skipper uses trolling lures or live bait to attract the fish. When something hits, you fight it from the rod, using the boat's drift or slow speed to tire the fish. For big tuna, you might use a and fighting chair. The boat has a fishfinder and GPS to locate structure and temperature breaks. Most trips also include a stop for bottom fishing if the pelagics are quiet. You can expect to catch 2-5 fish per person on a good day, but some days are slower. The skipper will clean and fillet your catch at the end. You can take it back to your accommodation or to a local restaurant that cooks your catch for a fee. For a list of recommended operators, see our fishing charters.

Pricing and What Affects the Cost

A full day fishing charter Benalmadena full day costs between EUR 800 and EUR 2,500, depending on the boat size, number of anglers, and season. Here is a rough breakdown: a 6-8 metre boat for 4 people costs around EUR 800-1,200. An 8-10 metre boat for 6 people runs EUR 1,200-1,800. A luxury sportfisher with cabin and full crew for 8 people is EUR 1,800-2,500. Prices include the skipper, fuel, bait, tackle, and basic lunch. Some charters add a cleaning fee (EUR 20-50) if you want the fish filleted. Peak season (June to September) costs 20-30% more than shoulder months (April, May, October). You can book directly with the skipper or through a platform like BoatHire24, which compares multiple operators and handles the booking. Always check what is included: some exclude drinks or lunch. Deposit is usually 30-50%, with the balance paid on the day. Cancellation policies vary, but most offer full refund if cancelled 48 hours ahead.

Best Season for Deep Sea Fishing Off Benalmadena

The Mediterranean off Benalmadena has year-round fishing, but the best time for big game is summer and early autumn. Bluefin tuna arrive in June and stay through October, with peak in August and September. Yellowfin and skipjack are common from July to September. Amberjack and dentex are around from May to November. Winter (November to March) is quieter for pelagics, but you can still catch sea bass, red mullet, and bream on the bottom. Water temperature ranges from 14 degrees C in January to 25 degrees C in August. The sea is calmest from May to October, with light winds and low swell. Winter can have stronger winds (levante) and rougher seas, but many boats still run. If you want the best chance of a big tuna, book between mid-July and mid-September. For a more relaxed trip with mixed species, May, June, or October are good. Check our blog on fishing seasons for more detail.

How to Book a Fishing Charter Benalmadena Full Day

Booking is straightforward. You decide on your date, group size, and budget. Then you search for available charters on a platform like BoatHire24. You can filter by boat type, capacity, and price. Each listing shows the boat details, skipper experience, included items, and customer ratings. You select your preferred option, pay a deposit online, and receive a confirmation email with the meeting point and skipper contact. On the day, you arrive at the marina 15 minutes early, meet the skipper, and head out. Some operators require a minimum of 4 anglers, so if you are a small group, you might join a shared charter. Shared trips cost less per person (around EUR 150-250) but you fish with strangers. Private charters give you the boat to yourselves. You can also book directly with the skipper if you know them, but using a platform gives you protection and easy cancellation. For a quick search, go to our charter search page.

Who This Trip Is For

A full day deep sea fishing charter is for serious anglers who want to target big fish. It is not for beginners who get seasick easily or want a casual half-day trip. You need to be comfortable spending 8-10 hours on a boat, often in direct sun and with some motion. The skipper will help you with the fishing, but you need basic fitness to fight a fish. Families with older children (12+) can do it, but younger kids might get bored or seasick. The trip is also suitable for corporate groups or stag parties, as long as everyone is into fishing. If you just want a relaxing boat ride with a bit of fishing, consider a half-day trip instead. For a full day, you commit to the chase. But if you land a 100 kg tuna, it is worth every minute.

What to Bring and Wear

You need sun protection: high-SPF sunscreen, a hat, sunglasses with a strap, and a long-sleeve shirt. The sun is intense on the water, even in spring. Bring a light jacket or windbreaker, as it can get cool offshore. Non-slip shoes or deck shoes are essential. Do not wear black-soled shoes that mark the deck. Bring seasickness tablets if you are prone to motion sickness; take them an hour before departure. The boat provides life jackets, but you can bring your own if you prefer. Bring a camera or phone in a waterproof case. For food, the charter includes lunch, but bring extra snacks and plenty of water. Alcohol is usually allowed but in moderation; the skipper reserves the right to refuse if you are drunk. A cooler for your catch is a good idea if you plan to take it home. The skipper provides bags for the fish, but a cooler keeps it fresh.

Local Regulations and Catch Limits

Fishing in Spanish waters is regulated by the Junta de Andalucia and the EU. The boat holds a commercial fishing licence, so you do not need a personal licence. However, there are size and bag limits per species. For bluefin tuna, the minimum size is 30 kg (around 1.2 metres). You can keep one per angler per day, but the total boat limit is usually 2-3 fish. For dentex, the minimum is 30 cm, and you can keep 5 per person. Amberjack minimum is 60 cm, bag limit 3 per person. The skipper will enforce these rules and release undersized fish. You cannot sell your catch; it is for personal consumption only. Some species are protected, like the dusky grouper, and must be released immediately. The skipper will brief you on the rules before you start. If you want to keep the catch, you can store it in the boat's fish hold or a cooler with ice. Some restaurants in Benalmadena will cook your catch for a fee; ask the skipper for recommendations. For more on regulations, see the fishing regulations blog.

Other Activities and Nearby Attractions

If you are staying in Benalmadena, the area offers plenty besides fishing. The marina has restaurants, bars, and the Sea Life aquarium. The nearby Benalmadena Pueblo (old town) has whitewashed streets and a 17th-century church. The Tivoli World amusement park is a short drive away. For a change of pace, you can take a cable car up Mount Calamorro for views of the coast. The beaches of Benalmadena Costa are sandy and busy in summer. If you want to combine fishing with a day on the water, some charters offer a lunch stop at a beach cove. But on a full day deep sea trip, you are mostly offshore. If you want a more leisurely day, consider a half-day fishing trip or a sunset cruise. For a list of all boat rentals, check our BoatHire24 homepage.

Comparing Boat Types for Deep Sea Fishing

The boat you choose affects your comfort and catch rate. Most full day trips use a 8-10 metre motor fishing vessel with a single or twin diesel engine, a wheelhouse, and a small cabin. These boats handle the offshore swell well and have enough deck space for 4-6 anglers. Some operators offer a 12-14 metre sportfisher with a flybridge, fighting chair, and a larger galley. These cost more but give you a smoother ride and better amenities. Catamarans are less common for deep sea fishing but exist; they are more stable at anchor but slower on the drift. If you are prone to seasickness, a catamaran might help. RIBs and speedboats are not suitable for full day offshore trips because they lack range and comfort. Stick to a proper fishing boat with a cabin and toilet. Ask the operator about the boat's age, engine hours, and recent refits. A well-maintained boat is safer and more reliable.

Ports Beyond Benalmadena for Deep Sea Fishing

While Benalmadena is your departure point, the fishing grounds extend along the coast. The waters off Fuengirola, 10 minutes east by boat, have similar depth and structure. To the west, the area off Cabopino and Marbella offers wrecks and rocky reefs that hold dentex and amberjack. Some skippers run to the waters off Cala del Faro, about 15 NM west, where the seabed drops sharply. If you want to target bluefin tuna, the best grounds are often 20-30 NM south, near the Alboran Sea. These are accessible from Benalmadena but require a longer run. For a change of scenery, you can book a charter from Puerto Banus or Estepona, but the fishing is similar. The Strait of Gibraltar, about 60 NM west, is famous for bluefin and swordfish, but that is a separate trip requiring an overnight stay. For a full day from Benalmadena, you stay within 20 NM of the coast.

Typical Day on the Water: Hour by Hour

You meet at 7:45am at the marina. The skipper does a safety briefing and checks the weather. You leave the harbour at 8am, heading southwest at 15-20 knots. The run to the fishing grounds takes 30-60 minutes depending on the spot. By 9am, the rods are out, and you start trolling or drifting. The first hour is often quiet as the fish settle. Around 10:30am, you might get the first strike. The skipper works the sonar and adjusts position. Lunch is around 1pm: usually a sandwich, salad, fruit, and a drink. After lunch, you move to a new spot or continue drifting. The afternoon bite is often better for amberjack and dentex. By 4pm, you start the run back to port, arriving by 5pm. The skipper cleans the catch while you relax. You leave the marina by 5:30pm with your fillets. Total time on the water is about 8 hours, with 6-7 hours of actual fishing.

Cost Comparison: Private vs Shared Charters

A private full day charter for 4 people costs around EUR 1,000-1,500, or EUR 250-375 per person. For 6 people, it is EUR 1,500-2,000, or EUR 250-333 per person. A shared charter costs EUR 150-250 per person, but you fish with up to 8 strangers. The shared trip is cheaper but less flexible: you cannot choose the spot or the timing. Private charters give you the boat to yourselves, and the skipper tailors the trip to your skill level. If you are a group of 4-6, a private charter is better value. If you are solo or a couple, a shared trip is the only option unless you pay for the whole boat. Some operators offer a "mixed group" booking where they fill the boat with individual anglers. This is common in summer. For the best price, book a private charter for 6 and split the cost. Check our fishing charter listings for current prices.

Seasickness and How to Avoid It

The Mediterranean off Benalmadena is generally calm from May to October, with swell under 1 metre. But offshore, the motion is more noticeable, especially on a 8-metre boat. If you are prone to seasickness, take medication an hour before departure. Options include cinnarizine (Stugeron), meclizine (Bonine), or a scopolamine patch. Ginger tablets and acupressure bands help some people. Avoid a heavy breakfast; eat light, like toast and banana. Stay on deck, keep your eyes on the horizon, and avoid reading or looking at a phone. If you feel sick, tell the skipper. They can slow down or move to calmer water. Most boats have a cabin where you can lie down. Do not let seasickness ruin the trip. If you are unsure, do a half-day trip first to test your tolerance. For a full day, you need to be confident you can handle 8 hours on the water.

What Happens to Your Catch After the Trip

The skipper cleans and fillets the fish at the dock. You get the fillets in a plastic bag, ready to cook. Some skippers vacuum-seal the fillets for an extra fee (EUR 10-20). You can take the fish to your accommodation and cook it yourself, or find a local restaurant that cooks your catch. In Benalmadena, a few restaurants near the marina offer this service for around EUR 15-20 per person. They grill or fry the fish and serve it with sides. Ask the skipper for recommendations. If you are flying home, you can freeze the fillets and pack them in a checked bag with a cooler. Most airlines allow frozen fish as long as it is properly sealed. You cannot bring fresh fish on a plane without a cooler and ice packs. Check with your airline. If you do not want the fish, the skipper will often take it or donate it to a local charity. But most anglers want to keep their catch.
