faqs.q: Do I need a boating licence for a weekly charter?
faqs.a: No. Every charter through BoatHire24 includes a licensed skipper. You do not need any licence to enjoy the boat.

faqs.q: Can I choose my own itinerary for the week?
faqs.a: Yes, within reason. You plan the route with the skipper, who will advise on weather and safety. The final decision rests with the skipper.

faqs.q: What happens if the weather is bad during my week?
faqs.a: The skipper will adjust the itinerary to avoid dangerous conditions. You may stay in port or visit sheltered anchorages. Safety always comes first.

faqs.q: Are children allowed on weekly charters?
faqs.a: Yes, but check the boat's safety equipment. Some operators require children to wear life jackets on deck. Confirm with the operator before booking.

faqs.q: How much does fuel cost for a week?
faqs.a: Fuel is usually included for a set number of cruising hours per day (typically 4-6). Extra hours are charged at market rates, around EUR 1.50-2.00 per litre for diesel.

faqs.q: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faqs.a: Yes. You can stock the boat with your own provisions. The skipper may have rules about glass or open flames. Some operators offer catering packages for an extra fee.

faqs.q: What is the cancellation policy for weekly charters?
faqs.a: Cancellation policies vary by operator. Most require a 30-50% deposit, which is non-refundable if you cancel within 30 days of departure. Read the terms before booking.

title: Boat Hire Marbella Port Hourly: Flexible Rentals
metaDescription: Book boat hire Marbella port hourly for a flexible, no-commitment day on the Costa del Sol. Compare motor yachts, catamarans & RIBs with a skipper. Start now.
h1: Boat Hire Marbella Port Hourly: Flexible Rentals with Skipper
intro: You want to get out on the water from Marbella port without committing to a full day. Boat hire Marbella port hourly is the answer: you pay for the hours you use, not a fixed charter block. Every rental includes a licensed skipper, so you just turn up and enjoy. Departures from Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona, Sotogrande or Benalmádena. Boats range from 6-metre RIBs to 40-foot motor yachts and catamarans. Prices start from around EUR 250 for a two-hour spin up the coast, but you can book as few as one hour for a quick dash to Cala del Faro or a sunset cruise past La Concha mountain.
bodyHtml:
What Hourly Boat Hire in Marbella Port Actually Means

Hourly hire is not a timeshare or a membership. You pick a boat from the BoatHire24 marketplace, choose a departure port, and book a slot of one to eight hours. The skipper is included in the price, so there is no licence check or briefing. You can extend on the day if the boat is free, but you pay the hourly rate for the extra time. Typical minimums: one hour for a RIB or speedboat, two hours for a motor yacht or catamaran. The skipper will suggest routes based on sea conditions and your preferences, but you decide where to go within reason.

Which Marbella Ports Offer Hourly Rentals

Every port along the Costa del Sol has boats available for hourly hire. Puerto Banús is the busiest, with the widest choice of luxury yachts and catamarans. Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo) is quieter and better for RIBs and fishing boats. Cabopino, Estepona and Sotogrande offer smaller fleets but shorter waiting times. Benalmádena is a good option if you are staying further east. Hourly rentals from any of these ports can include a run past the Strait of Gibraltar to spot dolphins, or a stop at a beach club like the ones near Río Verde. The skipper knows the local anchorages and will adapt the itinerary to the time you have.

Boat Types Available for Hourly Hire from Marbella Port

Motor yachts from 30 to 50 feet are the most common hourly hire boats. They have a cabin, a bathroom and a small galley, so you can stop for lunch or a swim. Catamarans are wider and more stable, ideal for groups of six to twelve. Sailing yachts are available but less common for short hires because they need more time to get underway. RIBs and speedboats are the cheapest option for one or two hours, and they can reach 30-40 knots for a quick trip to a secluded cove. Licence-free day boats (up to 15 hp) are also available for hourly hire if you want to drive yourself, but they are slower and limited to calm days. Superyachts are rarely offered by the hour because of crew costs, but some brokers will quote a minimum of four hours on request.

Pricing Guidance for Hourly Boat Hire Marbella Port

Prices vary by boat size, age and season. A two-hour RIB rental for up to six people starts from around EUR 250. A four-hour motor yacht for eight people is typically EUR 600-900. Catamarans for ten people for four hours run EUR 800-1,200. Superyacht hourly rates are price on request and usually start from EUR 2,000 per hour for a minimum of four hours. Fuel is sometimes included for short trips within 10 nautical miles of the port, but check the listing. Skipper gratuity is not included, but 10-15% of the hire cost is normal. You can search and compare prices on the BoatHire24 search page.

Best Seasons for Hourly Boat Hire in Marbella

May to October is the high season, with water temperatures of 18-26 degrees C and calm seas. July and August are the busiest months, so book at least a week ahead for popular boats. April and November are quieter and cheaper, but you might get some wind or rain. December to March is low season, with fewer boats available for hourly hire, but the weather is still mild (15-18 degrees C) and you can often get last-minute discounts. The skipper will check the forecast before departure and can cancel if conditions are unsafe, with a full refund.

What You Can Do in One Hour from Marbella Port

A one-hour slot is tight but doable. From Puerto Banús, you can cruise east past the Marbella coastline, see La Concha mountain from the sea, and anchor briefly at Cala del Faro for a swim. From Marbella Marina, a one-hour run west takes you to the mouth of Río Verde, where you might spot seabirds and turtles. From Estepona, one hour gets you to the old town beach or a dolphin-watching spot near the Strait of Gibraltar. The skipper will keep the engine running to maximise your time on the water, so you are not wasting minutes.

Two to Four Hours: The Sweet Spot for Hourly Hire

Most people book two to four hours. That gives you time to reach a beach club, anchor for a swim, and have a drink. From Puerto Banús, a three-hour trip can take you to Cabopino beach or the dunes of Artola. From Sotogrande, four hours lets you cross to Gibraltar for a view of the Rock (you cannot land without a passport check, but you can cruise close). From Benalmáldena, four hours is enough to reach the Nerja caves by sea, though you will not have time to go inside. The skipper can recommend a lunch stop at a chiringuito along the coast, like the ones at Cala del Moral or Playa de la Rada.

Who Should Book Hourly Boat Hire in Marbella Port

Hourly hire is for people who want flexibility. If you are on a short holiday and cannot spare a full day, this works. If you are unsure about seasickness and want a trial run, book two hours first. If you have a group of friends who want a quick party on the water, a four-hour catamaran hire with a sound system is popular. Families with young children often choose a RIB for a two-hour swim stop because it is cheaper and easier to board. Business groups use hourly hire for a corporate sunset cruise or a client meeting away from the office. The BoatHire24 blog has more ideas on itineraries and tips for first-timers.

How to Book Hourly Boat Hire from Marbella Port

Go to BoatHire24, enter Marbella as the location, select the date and the number of hours you want, and filter by boat type. The search results show availability and the total price including VAT and skipper. You pay a deposit online to secure the booking, and the balance is due on the day. Cancellation policies vary, but most allow free cancellation up to 48 hours before departure. On the day, meet the skipper at the port 15 minutes before your slot. The boat will be fuelled and ready. You can bring your own food and drink unless the listing says otherwise. Some boats have a minimum spend for catering, but that is rare for hourly hires.

Local Tips for Your Hourly Rental

Bring sunscreen, a hat and a light jacket because the wind can be cold even in summer. The skipper will provide life jackets and safety equipment. If you want to swim, the boat will have a ladder or a swim platform. Ask the skipper about the best anchorages for your time slot. For a sunset cruise, book the two hours before sunset so you see the sun go down behind La Concha. For dolphin watching, the morning hours are best because the sea is calmer. If you are prone to seasickness, take medication an hour before departure. The skipper can adjust the speed to minimise motion. Remember that hourly hire is not a private charter in the sense of exclusivity; you share the boat with your group, but the skipper is the only crew member.

Frequently Asked Questions About Hourly Boat Hire Marbella Port

Can I book for just one hour? Yes, but only on RIBs and speedboats. Most motor yachts and catamarans have a two-hour minimum. Is fuel included? Usually for trips within 10 NM of the port, but check the listing. Can I bring my own alcohol? Yes, but the skipper reserves the right to refuse service if anyone becomes unsafe. What happens if the weather is bad? The skipper cancels and you get a full refund. Do I need a licence? No, the skipper handles everything. Can I extend on the day? If the boat is available, yes, at the hourly rate. How many people fit on a RIB? Typically six to eight, but check the boat's capacity. Is there a toilet on board? On motor yachts and catamarans, yes. On RIBs, no. Can I request a specific skipper? No, but all skippers are licensed and experienced. What payment methods are accepted? Credit card, debit card, or bank transfer for the deposit. Cash is accepted for the balance on the day.

How Hourly Hire Compares to Half-Day or Full-Day Charters

Hourly hire is cheaper per hour than half-day or full-day charters because you are not paying for idle time. A half-day charter (4 hours) might cost EUR 800 for a motor yacht, while the same boat booked hourly for 4 hours could be EUR 700. The difference is small, but if you only need 2 hours, you save EUR 400 compared to a half-day minimum. Full-day charters (8 hours) often include a longer range, like a trip to Gibraltar or Nerja, but you pay for the skipper's waiting time. With hourly hire, you pay only for the time you use, and you can leave when you want. The trade-off is that hourly hire boats are sometimes older or smaller than those offered for full-day charters, because owners prefer longer bookings for their best vessels.

Specific Port-by-Port Hourly Hire Options

Hourly hire from Puerto Banús gives you the widest choice of luxury motor yachts and catamarans, but expect higher prices. A 40-foot motor yacht for 2 hours from Puerto Banús starts around EUR 500. Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo) is better for RIBs and fishing boats, with 2-hour RIB hires from EUR 250. Cabopino offers smaller boats like licence-free day boats for EUR 150 per hour, ideal for a quick solo trip. Estepona has a few catamarans available for hourly hire, with 4-hour trips to the Strait of Gibraltar for dolphin watching from EUR 900. Sotogrande is the most expensive port, with hourly rates for superyachts starting at EUR 2,000, but you can also find smaller motor yachts for EUR 600 for 2 hours. Benalmádena is the cheapest option, with 2-hour RIB hires from EUR 200, but the boats are often older.

What to Expect from the Skipper During an Hourly Hire

The skipper is your host and guide, not just a driver. They will greet you at the port, give a safety briefing, and help you board. During the trip, they will point out landmarks like La Concha mountain, the Marbella skyline, or the Río Verde estuary. If you want to swim, they will find a calm spot and drop the anchor. They can also recommend restaurants or beach clubs for a stop, but you need to factor that into your time. The skipper does not serve drinks or food unless you arrange catering in advance. They will handle mooring, refuelling, and any paperwork. You can chat with them about local history or ask for photo spots. The skipper's gratuity is not included, so bring cash if you are happy with the service.

How Weather Affects Hourly Boat Hire in Marbella

The Costa del Sol has over 300 sunny days a year, but wind and waves can still disrupt hourly hires. The Levante wind (from the east) creates choppy seas, especially in the afternoon. The Poniente wind (from the west) is calmer and better for swimming. In summer, the sea is usually flat in the morning until 11 am, then gets choppy by 2 pm. If you book a 1-hour slot at 3 pm in July, you might get a bumpy ride. The skipper will advise you on the best time of day for your trip. If the forecast shows winds over 15 knots, the skipper may cancel for safety, especially for RIBs and small boats. You get a full refund in that case. Winter hires are more weather-dependent, but clear days in January can offer glassy seas and fewer crowds.

Hidden Costs and Extras for Hourly Boat Hire

The listed price usually includes the boat, skipper, VAT, and basic insurance. But watch for extras. Fuel is sometimes charged separately if you go beyond 10 nautical miles. For example, a 4-hour trip from Puerto Banús to Cabopino might add EUR 50-100 for fuel. Some boats charge a cleaning fee of EUR 30-50 if you leave the boat messy. Catering is extra: a simple platter of snacks and drinks costs around EUR 20-30 per person. If you want a full meal, expect EUR 50-80 per person. Mooring fees at beach clubs or restaurants are not included and can be EUR 20-50 per stop. Skipper gratuity is optional but common: EUR 50-100 for a 2-hour hire. Always ask the owner or broker about all potential extras before booking to avoid surprises.

Who Should Avoid Hourly Boat Hire in Marbella

Hourly hire is not for everyone. If you want to spend a full day exploring the coast, a half-day or full-day charter is better value. If you are prone to seasickness, a 1-hour RIB ride at high speed might ruin your day. If you have a large group of 12 or more, hourly hire boats rarely have the capacity, so you need a full-day catamaran charter. If you want to visit specific landmarks like the Nerja caves or Gibraltar, 1-2 hours is not enough time. If you are on a tight budget, hourly hire can still be expensive compared to a shared boat tour. And if you want complete privacy, hourly hire boats are often shared with the skipper, who is always present. For these cases, consider a private full-day charter or a group tour instead.

faqs.q: Can I book a boat for just one hour from Marbella port?
faqs.a: Yes, but only on RIBs and speedboats. Most motor yachts and catamarans require a two-hour minimum. One-hour slots are ideal for a quick sunset cruise or a dash to a nearby cove.

faqs.q: Is the skipper included in the hourly boat hire price?
faqs.a: Yes, every hourly rental on BoatHire24 includes a licensed skipper. You do not need a licence or any experience. The skipper handles navigation, docking and safety.

faqs.q: What is the cheapest hourly boat hire option in Marbella port?
faqs.a: A two-hour RIB rental for up to six people starts from around EUR 250. For a one-hour slot, you might find a speedboat for EUR 150-200, but availability is limited.

faqs.q: Can I bring my own food and drink on an hourly boat hire?
faqs.a: Yes, you can bring your own. Some boats have a small galley or cooler. The skipper will provide ice if you ask. Check the listing for any restrictions on glass bottles.

faqs.q: What happens if the weather is bad on the day of my hourly hire?
faqs.a: The skipper will check the forecast and cancel if conditions are unsafe. You get a full refund. You can also reschedule at no extra cost.

faqs.q: Do I need to pay a deposit for hourly boat hire?
faqs.a: Yes, a deposit of 30-50% is required to secure the booking. The balance is paid on the day, usually in cash or by card. Cancellation policies vary, but most allow free cancellation 48 hours before.

title: Catamaran Rental Puerto Banus Birthday: Party Package
metaDescription: Book a catamaran rental Puerto Banus birthday party with skipper, drinks and sound system. Prices from EUR 2,500. Check availability for your date.
h1: Catamaran Rental Puerto Banus Birthday: Party Package
intro: You want to celebrate your birthday on the water without the hassle of organising a venue, catering and entertainment. A catamaran rental in Puerto Banus gives you a floating party deck, a licensed skipper who handles navigation, and a crew that can arrange drinks, food and music. You just turn up with your guests. The boat leaves from Puerto Banus, cruises past the Marbella coastline, and drops anchor near Cala del Faro or Cabopino for swimming. Prices start from around EUR 2,500 for a half-day charter, depending on the size of the catamaran and the extras you add.
bodyHtml:
What Is Included in a Birthday Catamaran Charter

Every catamaran charter through BoatHire24 includes a licensed skipper and basic safety equipment. For a birthday party, you can add a host or hostess, a sound system, a BBQ or catering, and a drinks package (beer, wine, cava, soft drinks or premium spirits). Most catamarans have a flybridge with sun loungers, a shaded saloon, a bow net for lounging, and a swim platform at the stern. The crew will anchor in a calm bay so you can swim, paddleboard or use the inflatable water toys. The standard charter is 4 hours, but you can extend to 6 or 8 hours for a sunset or evening party. The boat returns to Puerto Banus at the end of the trip.

Puerto Banus: The Best Departure Port for a Birthday

Puerto Banus is the most famous marina on the Costa del Sol. It is loud, flashy and full of superyachts, designer shops and restaurants. That atmosphere works well for a birthday crowd. You meet at the port, board your catamaran, and within 10 minutes you are out on the Mediterranean. The marina has plenty of parking (paid), taxis and a bus stop. If you want a quieter departure, you can also start from Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo) or Cabopino, but Puerto Banus gives you that glamour factor. The skipper will take you east toward the Cabopino dunes or west toward the cliffs of Cala del Faro, depending on wind and sea conditions.

Catamaran Options for a Birthday Party

We list catamarans from 35 to 55 feet on BoatHire24. The most common party catamarans are the Lagoon 42, Bali 4.6 and Fountaine Pajot Lucia 40. These boats have wide decks, plenty of seating and a large cockpit. For bigger groups (up to 30 guests), you need a 50-foot-plus catamaran like the Lagoon 52 or a custom-built charter catamaran. Smaller catamarans (35-40 feet) work for groups of 10-15 people. All catamarans have a fridge, ice maker and a sound system. Some have a built-in grill on the back deck. You can also request a DJ or live musician, but that costs extra and requires a permit from the port authority.

Pricing Guidance for a Birthday Catamaran Rental

Prices vary by season, boat size and duration. In low season (October to April), a half-day charter (4 hours) for a 40-foot catamaran starts from around EUR 1,800. In high season (June to September), the same boat costs from EUR 2,500 to EUR 3,500. A full-day charter (8 hours) ranges from EUR 3,000 to EUR 6,000. Extras like catering (EUR 40-80 per person), an open bar (EUR 30-60 per person), or a water slide (EUR 200-400) add to the total. You pay a deposit (usually 30-50%) to confirm the booking, and the balance is due 7 days before departure. All prices include VAT and insurance. You can get a detailed quote on the search page.

Best Time of Year for a Birthday Party at Sea

The Mediterranean season on the Costa del Sol runs from April to October. June, July and August give you air temperatures of 28-35 degrees C and water temperatures of 22-26 degrees C. September is also warm but less crowded. May and October are cooler (air 20-25 degrees C, water 18-20 degrees C) but still pleasant if you bring a light jacket. Winter charters (November to March) are possible but less comfortable for swimming; you would focus on cruising and sightseeing. For a birthday, aim for late afternoon (16:00 to 20:00) to catch the sunset. Evening charters with city lights on the return are also popular.

How to Book a Birthday Catamaran Rental

You can check availability and prices on BoatHire24 by entering your preferred date, group size and port. We show you the available catamarans with photos, specs and crew details. You can request a quote or book directly online. If you need help choosing, email us or use the live chat. We recommend booking at least 2 weeks in advance for high season and 4 weeks for a weekend. For last-minute bookings (within 72 hours), call us and we will check availability with the skippers. Payment is by bank transfer or credit card. We send you a confirmation with the skipper's contact and meeting point details.

What to Bring and What Not to Bring

Bring swimwear, sunscreen, a towel, sunglasses, a hat and a light jacket for the evening. You can bring your own food and drink, but most charters offer catering packages. Do not bring glass bottles (they break and cut feet) or red wine (it stains the deck). The crew can provide coolers and ice. If you plan to swim, bring a dry bag for your phone and wallet. The catamaran has a toilet (heads) and a freshwater shower on the swim platform. You do not need to bring a skipper or a licence; the boat comes with a licensed professional who knows the coast and the port regulations.

Who Is This For

This is for adults celebrating a milestone birthday (30th, 40th, 50th) or a group of friends who want a private party without the club scene. It also works for a 16th or 18th birthday with a responsible adult present. The crew can adjust the music and activities to suit the age group. If you want a family-friendly birthday with kids, choose a catamaran with a shallow draft and a calm itinerary. For a stag or hen party, you can add a photographer, a champagne tower or a themed decoration. The skipper will enforce safety rules, including a maximum guest count and no jumping from the flybridge.

Local Highlights You Will See

The standard route from Puerto Banus goes east past the Marbella skyline, the Sierra Blanca mountains and La Concha peak. You will see the luxury villas of the Golden Mile and the pine forests of Cabopino. If you head west, you pass the cliffs of Cala del Faro and the dunes of Artola. Further west, you can reach Estepona's old town and the marina. In summer, you might spot dolphins near the Strait of Gibraltar, but that is a longer trip (2.5 hours each way). For a shorter cruise, stay within 5 nautical miles of the coast. The skipper will choose the best anchorage based on the wind and swell.

Frequently Asked Questions

How Many Guests Can You Fit on a Birthday Catamaran

Guest capacity depends on the boat's licence and size, not just how many people can squeeze on deck. A 40-foot catamaran is licenced for 12 to 14 passengers. A 50-foot catamaran can take 20 to 30 passengers. The crew count (skipper plus host) does not count toward the guest limit. For a birthday with 10 to 15 people, a Lagoon 42 or Bali 4.6 works well. For 16 to 25 people, you need a Lagoon 52 or a Privilege 585. For 26 to 30 guests, you need a custom charter catamaran like the Sunreef 60. Over 30 guests is rare on a catamaran; you would need a motor yacht or a superyacht. The port authority enforces the limit strictly, so do not try to exceed it.

Departure Ports Other Than Puerto Banus

Puerto Banus is the obvious choice, but you have alternatives. Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo) is 10 minutes east and has cheaper parking and fewer crowds. Cabopino is 15 minutes west of Puerto Banus and sits next to the Artola dunes, a protected natural area with a quieter beach. Estepona is 30 minutes west and offers a more relaxed vibe with the old town and the marina. Sotogrande is 30 minutes further west, near the Gibraltar border, and has a private, exclusive feel. Benalmádena is 45 minutes east of Puerto Banus and has a large marina with plenty of restaurants and bars. The skipper will adjust the route based on the departure port. For a birthday, Puerto Banus gives you the best post-party options for dinner or clubbing.

What Happens If the Weather Turns Bad

Weather cancellations are rare on the Costa del Sol, but they happen. The skipper monitors the forecast and makes the call 24 to 48 hours before departure. If the wind exceeds 25 knots or the swell is over 2 metres, the charter gets rescheduled or refunded. You do not lose your deposit. For a birthday, have a backup date in mind. The best weather window is May to October, when the Levante wind is weaker and the sea is calm. If you book in winter, the risk of cancellation is higher. BoatHire24 includes a weather clause in the terms; read it before you pay the deposit.

Can You Decorate the Catamaran for a Birthday

Yes, you can decorate the catamaran with balloons, banners and table settings. The crew can set up a birthday banner on the flybridge or the cockpit. You need to bring the decorations yourself or order them through the charter company. Avoid confetti, glitter or anything that blows into the sea; the crew will charge a cleaning fee if you leave a mess. Some skippers allow a small cake with candles, but check in advance. The port authority does not allow open flames on board, so no sparklers or fireworks. For a themed party, you can rent custom tablecloths, napkins and cups from a local party shop in Marbella or Puerto Banus.

Food and Drink Options for the Party

Most catamarans have a fridge, a freezer and a grill on the back deck. The standard catering package includes a BBQ with chicken, beef, sausages, fish, salads, bread and fruit. Premium packages add seafood platters, paella, sushi or a tapas spread. Drinks range from a basic package (beer, wine, soft drinks) to a premium open bar (cava, champagne, gin, vodka, rum, mixers). You can also bring your own food and drink, but the crew charges a corkage fee of EUR 10-20 per person. For a birthday, a champagne toast at sunset is a popular add-on. The crew provides glasses and ice. Vegetarian, vegan and gluten-free options are available if you request them 48 hours ahead.

Entertainment and Activities Onboard

Beyond the sound system and the swim platform, you can add a paddleboard, a kayak, a water slide or a floating mat. The water slide is a hit for birthday parties; it costs EUR 200-400 and attaches to the swim platform. A paddleboard or kayak costs EUR 50-100 each. If you want to see dolphins, the skipper can head toward the Strait of Gibraltar, but that adds 2 hours of cruising time each way. For a shorter trip, you can anchor off the Cabopino dunes or near the Cala del Faro cliffs. The crew can also set up a floating table for drinks in the water. Music is played through the boat's Bluetooth system or a portable speaker; you can bring your own playlist.

How to Get to Puerto Banus for Your Birthday Charter

Puerto Banus is 10 minutes by taxi from Marbella town centre and 45 minutes from Malaga airport. The nearest train station is in Fuengirola, 20 minutes away by taxi. There is a bus from Malaga bus station to Puerto Banus (line M-220) that runs every 30 minutes. Parking at Puerto Banus is limited and expensive (EUR 25-40 per day). Use the underground car park at the marina or the public car park near the bullring. If you are staying in Marbella, a taxi or Uber costs around EUR 15-20. For a group, book a minibus or a private transfer. The meeting point is usually at the fuel dock or the main entrance of the marina; the skipper sends you the exact location 24 hours before departure.

What to Do After the Birthday Catamaran Charter

After the charter, you can continue the party in Puerto Banus. The marina has dozens of restaurants, bars and clubs. For dinner, try the seafood restaurants on the waterfront or the steak houses in the port. For drinks, go to the bars near the casino or the yacht club. For clubbing, the most famous spot is Olivia Valere, but there are smaller clubs like Pangea and La Suite. If you want a quieter evening, walk to the nearby beach clubs or take a taxi to Marbella town centre. The catamaran returns to the same port, so you are already in the middle of the nightlife. Book a table in advance for a large group, especially in high season.

Is a Birthday Catamaran Charter Worth the Cost

Compare the cost to a private room in a club or a restaurant. A private room for 15 people in a decent Puerto Banus restaurant costs EUR 1,000 to EUR 2,000 for food and drinks, plus you have no view, no swimming and no privacy. A catamaran charter for 15 people costs EUR 2,500 to EUR 4,000 for 4 hours, including the boat, the skipper, the host and basic drinks. For a milestone birthday, the experience is more memorable and the cost per person (EUR 150-300) is comparable to a night out. You get a private space, a swimming stop, sunset views and a dedicated crew. If you split the cost among guests, it becomes affordable. The value is in the exclusivity and the setting, not in the price per hour.

What to Wear on a Birthday Catamaran

Dress for comfort and practicality. Swimwear, shorts, a t-shirt and bare feet are standard on the boat. The deck gets hot in summer, so wear flip-flops or boat shoes. For the evening, bring a light sweater or a jacket; the wind picks up after sunset. No high heels on the deck; they damage the teak and are dangerous on a moving boat. If you want photos, wear bright colours or white; they look good against the blue sea. The crew can store your bags in a dry cabin. For a themed birthday, coordinate outfits or wear matching shirts. The skipper will tell you if the boat has a shoe policy (some catamarans require bare feet or soft soles).

Can You Combine a Birthday with a Sunset or Dinner Cruise

Yes, a sunset catamaran charter is a popular option for birthdays. The late afternoon slot (16:00 to 20:00) covers the golden hour and the sunset. You can add a dinner package with a three-course meal served on the flybridge. The crew sets up a table with candles and tablecloths. The charter ends as the sun goes down, and you return to the port with the city lights reflecting on the water. For a dinner cruise, choose a 6-hour charter (18:00 to 00:00) that includes a full meal and drinks. The cost is higher (EUR 3,500 to EUR 6,000), but the atmosphere is romantic and exclusive. The skipper will anchor in a sheltered bay for the meal and then cruise back slowly.

How to Choose the Right Catamaran for Your Birthday Group

Start with the guest count. For 10 to 12 people, a 40-foot catamaran is enough. For 13 to 18 people, go for a 45- to 50-foot catamaran. For 19 to 30 people, you need a 50-foot-plus catamaran. Next, consider the layout. A flybridge gives you a separate sun deck and a better view. A bow net is good for lounging. A swim platform with a ladder makes swimming easier. Check the sound system; some catamarans have a built-in Bluetooth speaker, others have a full marine stereo. If you want a BBQ, confirm that the boat has a grill. If you want a water slide, ask if the boat has a reinforced swim platform. BoatHire24 lists the specs for each catamaran; compare them before you book.

Related guides

  • Boat Rental Marbella, Skipper Included from Puerto Banús
  • Catamaran Charter Marbella, Half & Full Day Cruises
  • Sunset Boat Trip Marbella, Cava & Skipper Included
  • Luxury Yacht Rental Marbella, Superyachts & Crew

faqs.q: How many guests can a catamaran hold for a birthday party?
faqs.a: Most catamarans in Puerto Banus hold 10 to 30 guests. The exact number depends on the boat's capacity and the port authority limit. Check the listing for the maximum guest count.

faqs.q: Can I bring my own decorations for the birthday?
faqs.a: Yes, you can bring balloons, banners and table decorations. Avoid glitter and confetti as they are hard to clean. The crew can help you set up before guests arrive.

faqs.q: Is there a minimum age for a birthday catamaran rental?
faqs.a: There is no minimum age, but children must be accompanied by an adult. For a party with alcohol, all guests must be 18 or over. The skipper can refuse service to underage drinkers.

faqs.q: What happens if the weather is bad on my birthday?
faqs.a: The skipper decides whether it is safe to sail. If the forecast is poor, we can reschedule or cancel with a full refund. We recommend booking in the warmer months for better odds.

faqs.q: Can I book a catamaran for a surprise birthday party?
faqs.a: Yes. Let us know it is a surprise and we will coordinate with the skipper. You can arrive early to set up, or have the crew prepare the boat before the birthday person arrives.

faqs.q: Do I need a skipper licence to rent a catamaran in Puerto Banus?
faqs.a: No. Every charter through BoatHire24 includes a licensed skipper. You do not need any boating experience or licence. The skipper handles all navigation and docking.

faqs.q: Can I extend the charter on the day?
faqs.a: If the catamaran is available and the skipper agrees, you can extend for an extra hour or two. The cost will be pro-rated based on the hourly rate. Ask the crew early in the trip.

title: Motor Yacht Hire Marbella Stag: Party on the Med
metaDescription: Motor yacht hire Marbella stag: party on the Med with a skipper, open bar, and Puerto Banús departure. Book your bachelor blowout with BoatHire24 today.
h1: Motor Yacht Hire Marbella Stag: Party on the Med
intro:
If you're planning a stag do in Marbella, you've probably already realised that the usual bar crawl or paintball session won't cut it. You want something that screams 'last night of freedom' without screaming at a bouncer. Motor yacht hire Marbella stag is the answer: a private boat, a licensed skipper, and the Mediterranean as your personal party pad. You don't need a licence, you don't need to navigate, and you definitely don't need to clean up afterwards.

BoatHire24 connects you with the best motor yachts for hire in Marbella, from sleek 40-footers to superyachts that could host a small wedding. Every charter includes a professional skipper, so you and your mates can focus on the important stuff: champagne, music, and jumping off the bow into crystal-clear water. Departure ports include Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona, Sotogrande, and Benalmádena. You pick the boat, we sort the rest.

This page gives you the lowdown on what to expect, how much it costs, which boats work best for a stag group, and how to book without the hassle. No fluff, just the facts you need to make your mate's final single weekend one for the history books.
bodyHtml:
What's Included in a Stag Motor Yacht Charter

When you book motor yacht hire Marbella stag through BoatHire24, the basics are straightforward. Every charter comes with a licensed skipper who knows the local waters, the best coves, and the spots where you can anchor without annoying the neighbours. The skipper handles navigation, safety, and docking, so you don't need a boating licence or any experience. Fuel is usually included in the price, but check the specific listing because some operators charge extra for long distances.

Most motor yachts come with a sound system (Bluetooth or AUX), a fridge or cooler for your drinks, and a shaded deck area. Some have a small galley with a hob and sink. You can bring your own food and drink, or upgrade to a catering package with paella, tapas, or a barbecue. Open bars are common for stag groups: think beer, wine, cava, and soft drinks, often at a flat rate per person. Towels and snorkel gear are usually provided, and some boats have a paddleboard or two for extra fun.

What's not included: the skipper's tip (standard is 10-15% of the charter fee), mooring fees if you stop at a beach club, and any damage you cause. Keep that in mind when the prosecco starts flowing.

Best Departure Ports for a Stag Party

Puerto Banús is the obvious choice. It's the epicentre of Marbella's nightlife, with superyachts, designer shops, and bars like Ocean Club and Pangea. You can step off your motor yacht and straight into a VIP table. The port itself is a spectacle, and the walk to the main strip takes about five minutes. If you want to maximise party time, this is your port.

Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo) is a quieter alternative, closer to the old town and the beach clubs along the Golden Mile. It's less flashy than Banús but still has good restaurants and a more relaxed vibe. Cabopino, near the dunes of Artola, is smaller and more laid-back, ideal if your group prefers a beach day over a club night. Estepona port is about 20 minutes west of Marbella and offers easy access to the Strait of Gibraltar, where you can spot dolphins. Sotogrande is upmarket and further inland, better for a luxury weekend than a wild party. Benalmádena, east of Marbella, has a lively marina with bars and a casino.

For a stag do, we recommend Puerto Banús or Marbella Marina. Both have plenty of after-boat options, and the skippers know the best routes along the coast.

Which Motor Yacht Works Best for a Stag Group

Size matters. For a group of 8-12, a 40-50 foot motor yacht like the Azimut 46 or Sunseeker Portofino 48 gives you enough deck space for dancing, sunbathing, and eating. These boats have a flybridge, which is basically a second outdoor lounge with a helm station, perfect for the skipper to steer while you party below. For 12-20 people, look at 60-70 foot models like the Princess 64 or Ferretti 68. They have larger saloons, more cabins for changing, and bigger sundecks.

If your stag group is over 20, you're into superyacht territory. Boats like the Mangusta 108 or Sanlorenzo 90 can handle up to 30 guests. They come with crew, including a stewardess who keeps the glasses full. The price jumps significantly, but so does the experience. For a real blowout, you can also book a catamaran, which is more stable and has a huge trampoline net at the front, but it's not a motor yacht. Stick to the plan.

RIB speedboats are an option for smaller groups (up to 6) who want adrenaline rather than luxury. They're fast, loud, and you can jump off them easily, but there's no shade and limited space for a sound system. Not ideal for a full-day party, but good for a two-hour blast to a beach club.

Pricing Guidance for a Stag Motor Yacht Charter

Prices depend on the boat size, duration, season, and extras. For a half-day (4 hours) on a 40-50 foot motor yacht, expect from around EUR 1,500 to EUR 2,500. A full day (8 hours) on the same boat runs from EUR 2,500 to EUR 4,000. For larger yachts (60-70 feet), half-day starts at EUR 3,000 and full day at EUR 5,000. Superyachts (80+ feet) are price on request, but you're looking at EUR 10,000 to EUR 25,000 per day.

Extras add up. An open bar for 10 people costs around EUR 500-800 for beer, wine, and cava. Catering (paella, grilled meat, salads) is about EUR 40-60 per person. Snorkel gear and paddleboards are usually included, but a jet ski tow or inflatable water park costs extra. Mooring at a beach club like Nikki Beach or Ocean Club adds EUR 100-300 per stop. The skipper's tip is separate, so budget 10-15% on top.

Peak season (June to September) is more expensive, and weekends cost more than weekdays. If you're flexible, a Friday or Monday can save you 20-30%. Book early: popular boats get snapped up months in advance for stag groups.

Seasons and Weather for Stag Yacht Hire

The Costa del Sol has over 300 days of sunshine a year, but the best time for a stag motor yacht charter is May to October. In May and June, temperatures are 22-28 degrees C, the water is warm enough to swim (around 20 degrees C), and the sea is calm. July and August are hot (30-35 degrees C) and busy, with more traffic in the marinas and at beach clubs. September and October are quieter, with temperatures still in the mid-20s and the water at its warmest (24-26 degrees C).

Winter (November to April) is possible but less popular. Temperatures drop to 15-20 degrees C, and the sea can be choppy, especially near the Strait of Gibraltar. Some boats operate year-round, but you'll need to check availability. For a stag party, stick to the summer months for the full experience.

The wind is usually light in the morning and picks up in the afternoon, so a half-day charter from 10am to 2pm is smoother than an afternoon one. If anyone in your group gets seasick, take motion sickness tablets an hour before departure. The skipper will also advise on the best route based on the day's conditions.

How to Book Your Stag Motor Yacht

Booking through BoatHire24 is simple. Go to the search page, filter by motor yacht, date, and group size. You'll see photos, specs, and prices. Click on a boat to see the full details, including what's included, the skipper's bio, and the cancellation policy. You can request a quote or book directly if the calendar shows availability. For stag groups, we recommend contacting us first to discuss extras like an open bar or catering, because some boats have limited fridge space.

Payment is usually 50% deposit to secure the booking, with the balance due a week before departure. We accept credit cards, bank transfers, and some operators take PayPal. Cancellation policies vary: most offer a full refund if you cancel 30 days before, 50% if 14 days before, and nothing after that. Read the terms carefully, especially if your stag date is during peak season.

If you need help choosing, check our blog for guides on the best boats for groups, local spots like Cala del Faro and the cliffs of Maro, and tips on what to bring. We've been doing this for years, so we know which boats handle a party and which ones don't.

Who Is a Stag Motor Yacht Charter For

This is for groups who want to celebrate without the hassle. If your stag is the type who loves the water, good music, and a cold drink in his hand, a motor yacht is perfect. It's also for groups who don't want to deal with crowded bars, overpriced drinks, and the chaos of a club before midnight. On a boat, you control the playlist, the drinks, and the vibe.

It's not for everyone. If your group is prone to seasickness, gets bored without constant stimulation, or prefers a quiet weekend, consider a different activity. Also, if you're on a tight budget, a half-day charter on a smaller boat is more affordable than a full-day on a superyacht. But for a memorable stag do that your mates will talk about for years, motor yacht hire Marbella stag is hard to beat.

One last thing: respect the skipper. They're there to keep you safe and ensure you have a good time. Don't be the group that breaks the sound system or jumps off the boat in a restricted area. Behave, and you'll get a tip-worthy experience.

What to Do on a Stag Motor Yacht: Itinerary Ideas

A typical stag charter starts around 10am at Puerto Banús. You board, the skipper runs a quick safety briefing, and you're off. Head west towards the Cala del Faro, a secluded cove with clear water and rocky cliffs. Anchor there for a swim, some snorkelling, and the first round of beers. The water is usually calm in the morning, and the cove is sheltered from the wind. After an hour or so, cruise east towards the Marbella coastline, passing the Golden Mile and the old town. The skipper can stop near the Cabopino dunes for a second swim stop, or you can motor straight to a beach club like Nikki Beach or La Sala for lunch and a table on the sand.

For a full-day charter, add a stop at the Río Verde estuary, where you can see the Marbella Club and Puente Romano from the water. Alternatively, head further west to Estepona and anchor off the old town beach. The water is shallower there, and you can see the colourful flower pots on the houses from the boat. If your group is adventurous, ask the skipper to take you towards the Strait of Gibraltar for a dolphin-spotting detour. The common dolphins and pilot whales are frequent visitors, and the sight of them bow-riding is worth the extra fuel cost. End the day back at Puerto Banús around 6pm, with enough time to shower and head to Ocean Club or Pangea for the evening.

What to Bring and What to Leave Behind

Bring swimwear, a towel, sunscreen (factor 50, the sun is strong), sunglasses, a hat, and a light jacket or hoodie for the evening breeze. A dry bag for your phone and wallet is smart, because splashes happen. Bring your own drinks if you're not paying for an open bar, but check with the skipper first: some boats have a limit on glass bottles for safety. Leave behind glass bottles if possible, and definitely leave behind any illegal substances. The skipper will call the port police if they see anything, and you'll lose your deposit and your day.

Do not bring high heels on board. They damage the teak deck and the skipper will charge you for repairs. Flat shoes or bare feet are fine. Also, leave behind any expectations of privacy: on a 40-foot motor yacht, there's one toilet (called the head) and a small cabin for changing. You're on a boat, not a hotel. Embrace the close quarters and the communal vibe. It's part of the experience.

How to Keep Costs Down Without Losing the Fun

If the budget is tight, book a half-day charter instead of a full day. A half-day from 10am to 2pm gives you enough time for a swim stop, some drinks, and a cruise along the coast. You'll save EUR 1,000-1,500 compared to a full day. Choose a weekday: Monday to Thursday are quieter and cheaper. Avoid the peak of July and August; May, June, or September offer similar weather with lower prices and less crowded marinas.

Bring your own food and drink instead of paying for catering. A trip to the local supermarket (Mercadona or Carrefour) costs about EUR 100-150 for a group of 10, covering beer, wine, soft drinks, sandwiches, and snacks. Skip the open bar and buy a few bottles of cava and beer instead. If you want a sit-down meal, stop at a beach club and pay for food there, rather than paying for a catering package on the boat. Finally, share the cost of the skipper's tip as a group, and don't forget to budget for it upfront.

What Happens When the Weather Turns Bad

The Costa del Sol is generally stable, but the wind can pick up, especially in the afternoon. The skipper monitors the forecast and will decide if it's safe to sail. If the sea state is too rough (waves over 1.5 metres), the charter may be cancelled or rescheduled. Most operators have a weather clause: if they cancel, you get a full refund or a new date. If you cancel because of rain but the sea is calm, you lose the deposit. Check the policy before booking.

If the weather is marginal, the skipper might suggest a shorter route, staying close to the coast where the water is flatter. The area between Marbella and Estepona is usually calmer than the open sea near the Strait. If the wind is strong but from the east (Levante), the skipper can anchor in the lee of the cliffs at Cala del Faro or near the Cabopino dunes. You'll still have a good time, just with less sunbathing and more shelter.

After the Boat: Where to Go for the Stag Night

Puerto Banús is the obvious spot. Ocean Club is a beach club that turns into a nightclub after dark, with DJs, bottle service, and a crowd that's there to party. Pangea is a rooftop bar with views of the port and the yachts. La Suite is another option, with a restaurant and a club. If you want something less flashy, head to Marbella old town, where the bars on Calle José Antonio and the Orange Square are more relaxed and cheaper. For a casino night, the Casino Marbella in Puerto Banús is open until 6am.

If you docked at Marbella Marina, you're a short walk from the Avenida del Mar and the beach clubs along the Golden Mile. The Buddha Beach club is a good option for dinner and drinks, and the nightlife in the marina itself is lively but not as intense as Banús. For a truly memorable stag night, book a table at one of the port-side restaurants for a late dinner, then move to a club. The skipper can recommend spots based on your group's vibe.

title: Speedboat Rental Estepona Marina: Quick Getaway
metaDescription: Book a speedboat rental Estepona marina for a quick escape along the Costa del Sol. Compare RIBs and day boats with a licensed skipper. Depart from Estepona port today.
h1: Speedboat Rental Estepona Marina: Quick Getaway
intro:
If you want to get out on the water fast without the fuss of a big yacht, a speedboat rental Estepona marina is your best bet. You can be zipping past the old town walls and heading toward the Strait of Gibraltar within minutes of handing over the keys. No long briefings, no deck shoes required, just you, a licensed skipper, and a hull that can hit 30 knots without breaking a sweat.

Estepona Marina is one of the less crowded departure points on the Costa del Sol, which means less queueing at the fuel dock and more time enjoying the spray. Whether you are a first-timer or a seasoned boater, the local skippers know the hot spots for dolphin sightings and the quiet coves where you can drop anchor for a swim. The whole point is to keep it simple and get you out there quickly.
bodyHtml:
What You Get with a Speedboat Rental in Estepona Marina

A speedboat rental from Estepona Marina includes a fully licensed skipper who handles the navigation, safety briefings, and mooring. You do not need a boating licence or any previous experience. The boat is a RIB (rigid inflatable hull) or a compact day boat, typically between 4 and 8 metres, powered by a single or twin outboard engine. Top speeds range from 25 to 40 knots depending on the model and sea conditions. Fuel is included in the price at most operators, but double-check when you book. The skipper will also bring along basic safety equipment, life jackets for all passengers, and often a small cooler for drinks. You can expect a half-day rental (4 hours) or a full day (8 hours), with the option to extend if you are having fun.

Why Choose Estepona Marina for a Speedboat Trip

Estepona Marina is a working port with a relaxed vibe, not a glitzy superyacht hub like Puerto Banús. That means lower mooring fees and less traffic on the water. The marina is right next to the Estepona old town, so you can grab a coffee or a beer before you set off. The departure channel is short, and within 10 minutes you are in open water. The coastline here is a mix of sandy beaches and rocky cliffs, with the Sierra Bermeja mountains as a backdrop. Because the port is south-facing, you get good shelter from the prevailing westerly winds, making it a reliable launch point even when the sea breeze picks up in the afternoon.

Where You Can Go from Estepona Marina in a Speedboat

With a speedboat rental Estepona marina, you have a few classic routes. Head west toward the Strait of Gibraltar, about 15 nautical miles, and you can spot common dolphins, pilot whales, and sometimes orcas in summer. The town of La Línea is about 20 NM west, and you can see the Rock of Gibraltar from a few miles out. Eastward, you can reach the Cala del Faro cove in about 20 minutes, a popular spot for cliff jumping and clear water. Further east, Marbella Marina is about 12 NM, and Puerto Banús is another 3 NM beyond that. If you want a shorter trip, hug the coast and stop at the beaches of El Cristo or La Rada for a swim. The skipper will tailor the route to your preferences and the weather conditions.

Speedboat Types Available for Rent in Estepona

The speedboat rental fleet in Estepona Marina typically includes RIBs from brands like Zodiac, Brig, and Highfield, plus smaller open day boats from Quicksilver or Bayliner. RIBs are the most popular choice because they are stable at high speeds and handle chop well. You will find models with 60 hp to 250 hp outboards, seating for 4 to 8 passengers. Some operators also offer jet boats, which are even more agile but use more fuel. For a pure speed experience, look for a RIB with a single 200 hp engine; it will do around 35 knots and still be comfortable. If you want something more relaxed, a 6-metre day boat with a 90 hp engine will cruise at 15-20 knots and use less petrol. Prices for a half-day start from around EUR 250 for a basic 4-metre RIB and go up to EUR 600 for a larger 8-metre model with more power.

Pricing Guide for Speedboat Rental Estepona Marina

Prices for a speedboat rental Estepona marina vary by season, boat size, and duration. In low season (October to April), a half-day rental for a 4-5 metre RIB starts from around EUR 250. In high season (June to September), the same boat costs from EUR 350. Full-day rentals typically double the half-day price. A 6-7 metre RIB with a 150 hp engine costs from EUR 400 for a half-day in summer. Fuel is usually included, but some operators charge extra for fuel if you cover more than 50 NM. Always confirm what is included before booking. You can also add extras like a water sports tow (ringo, wakeboard) for an additional EUR 50-100 per session. For the most accurate pricing, check the listings on BoatHire24 search page for real-time availability and rates.

Best Time of Year for a Speedboat Trip from Estepona

The Costa del Sol enjoys over 300 days of sunshine a year, so you can rent a speedboat in Estepona Marina almost any month. The best conditions for speedboating are from May to October, when the sea temperature is above 18 degrees C and the wind is light. July and August can be hot, with air temperatures reaching 35 degrees C, so morning or late afternoon trips are more comfortable. The sea breeze picks up around 2 PM, creating a slight chop that RIBs handle well. In winter, the water is cooler (around 14 degrees C) and the days are shorter, but you can still get calm days with clear skies. The skipper will advise on the best timing based on the forecast. Avoid days with strong Levante winds (east wind) as they can create rough seas in the Strait area.

How to Book a Speedboat Rental Estepona Marina

Booking a speedboat rental Estepona marina through BoatHire24 is straightforward. Go to the search page, enter Estepona as the departure port, select speedboat as the boat type, and choose your date. You will see a list of available boats with photos, specifications, and prices. Each listing includes the skipper's profile, the boat's maximum passenger capacity, and the rental duration options. You can book online with a deposit, and the balance is paid on the day. The platform handles the booking confirmation and sends you a reminder 48 hours before. If you have special requests, like a specific departure time or a water sports tow, mention them in the notes when you book. For last-minute bookings, check the same-day availability filter on the search page.

Who Should Consider a Speedboat Rental in Estepona

A speedboat rental Estepona marina is ideal for small groups or couples who want a quick, adrenaline-filled outing without the commitment of a full-day yacht charter. It suits families with older children who enjoy the speed and the chance to spot dolphins. It also works for solo travellers or pairs who want to explore the coastline at their own pace. If you are on a short holiday and have only a few hours to spare, a speedboat gives you maximum flexibility. It is less suitable for large parties (over 8 people) or for those who want a luxury lounging experience with a sun deck and cabin. For those scenarios, consider a catamaran or motor yacht instead. Check the BoatHire24 blog for tips on choosing the right boat for your group size.

What to Bring on a Speedboat Trip

Pack light for your speedboat rental Estepona marina. Bring swimwear, a towel, sunscreen (factor 50, waterproof), a hat, and sunglasses with a strap. A light windbreaker or long-sleeve top is useful even in summer because the wind chill at 30 knots can feel cool. Put your phone and valuables in a waterproof pouch. The skipper will provide life jackets, but if you have your own, you can use it. Bring a refillable water bottle; the boat will have a cooler but not always bottled water. No glass bottles on board, as they break and create hazards. If you plan to swim, bring a snorkel mask. The skipper may have a ladder for easy reboarding. Leave large bags and hard suitcases onshore; there is limited storage on speedboats.

Safety and Skipper Qualifications

Every speedboat rental Estepona marina on BoatHire24 includes a licensed skipper with a valid Spanish Patrón de Navegación Básica or higher qualification. The skipper is responsible for the safety of all passengers and the vessel. Before departure, the skipper will give a safety briefing covering life jacket locations, emergency procedures, and the use of the kill cord. Life jackets are provided for all passengers and must be worn at all times when the boat is underway. The boat is equipped with a VHF radio, flares, a first aid kit, and a fire extinguisher. The skipper monitors the weather forecast and will cancel or reschedule the trip if conditions are unsafe. You are required to follow the skipper's instructions at all times. If you have any medical conditions that could affect your safety on the water, inform the skipper before departure.

Local Attractions and Dining near Estepona Marina

After your speedboat rental Estepona marina, you can explore the port area and the old town. The marina has several seafood restaurants, including those serving fresh grilled sardines and fried fish. Try the chiringuitos on the beach for a casual meal. The Estepona old town is a 10-minute walk from the marina, with narrow streets lined with flower pots, whitewashed houses, and tapas bars. The Orchidarium is a popular indoor garden with a large collection of orchids, worth a visit if you have time. For a more active afternoon, hike up the Sierra Bermeja for panoramic views of the coast and Gibraltar. The marina also has a small supermarket and a fuel station if you need supplies before or after your trip.

Comparing Estepona Marina to Other Departure Ports

Estepona Marina is a quieter alternative to Puerto Banús or Marbella Marina. It offers easier parking and less crowded waters, especially on weekends. The distance to the Strait of Gibraltar is shorter from Estepona than from Marbella, making it a better base for dolphin-watching trips. However, Estepona has fewer boat options than Puerto Banús, which has a larger fleet of luxury yachts and catamarans. For speedboats specifically, Estepona has a good selection of RIBs and day boats, while Puerto Banús leans more toward motor yachts and superyachts. If you want a faster departure with less formality, Estepona is the better choice. If you want a wider range of boat types or a more glamorous setting, consider Puerto Banús or Marbella. Check the BoatHire24 search page to compare availability across all ports.

What to Expect from the Skipper on a Speedboat Rental Estepona Marina

The skipper on your speedboat rental Estepona marina is not just a driver. They know the local waters, the hidden coves, and the best spots for swimming away from the crowds. They will point out points of interest like the Torre de la Estepona watchtower or the old fishing huts on the beach. Most skippers speak English and Spanish, and some speak German or French. They will adjust the speed to your comfort level, so if you want a more relaxed cruise rather than a full-throttle blast, just say so. The skipper also handles the mooring at any stop, so you can jump straight into the water without worrying about anchoring. They carry a mobile phone and can call ahead to reserve a table at a beach club if you want to stop for lunch.

How the Sea Conditions Affect Your Speedboat Trip from Estepona

The Mediterranean around Estepona is usually calm in the morning, with a glassy surface and light winds. By early afternoon, the thermal breeze picks up, creating a short chop of 0.5 to 1 metre. A RIB handles this easily, but smaller day boats with a shallow V-hull may feel bumpy. The prevailing wind is from the west, so heading east toward Marbella is often smoother than going west toward Gibraltar. If the Levante wind blows from the east, the sea can become choppy with waves up to 1.5 metres, especially near the Strait. The skipper will check the forecast and may suggest a different route or a later departure time. In summer, the sea temperature reaches 24 degrees C, making swimming comfortable. In winter, wear a wetsuit if you plan to swim, as the water drops to 14 degrees C.

Combining a Speedboat Trip with a Visit to the Strait of Gibraltar

One of the best uses of a speedboat rental Estepona marina is a run to the Strait of Gibraltar, about 15 NM west. At 30 knots, you can get there in 30 minutes. The Strait is a migration corridor for marine life, and you have a high chance of seeing common dolphins, striped dolphins, and pilot whales. In late summer and early autumn, orcas pass through hunting tuna. The skipper will slow down and keep a safe distance, but the animals often approach the boat. You can also get a close view of the Rock of Gibraltar from the water. The current in the Strait can be strong, so the skipper will plan the route to avoid the main shipping lanes. This trip works best as a full-day rental, giving you time to stop for a swim at a quiet beach on the way back.

What to Do If the Weather Cancels Your Speedboat Rental

If the weather is bad, the skipper will cancel your speedboat rental Estepona marina for safety. This happens most often in winter or during strong Levante winds. On BoatHire24, you get a full refund if the operator cancels, or you can reschedule for another day. If you cancel yourself less than 48 hours before, you may lose your deposit. Check the forecast before you book. If you are flexible, choose a date with a few days of margin. The skipper will usually offer an alternative time the same day if the wind drops. In summer, cancellations are rare, maybe 2 or 3 days a month. If you are visiting for a short holiday, book early in your stay so you have time to reschedule if needed.

faqs.a: Yes. Common dolphins are frequently spotted in the Strait of Gibraltar area, about 15 NM from Estepona.

faqs.a: In summer, book at least a week ahead. In winter, a few days is usually enough. Last-minute bookings are possible on BoatHire24.

title: Luxury Catamaran Hire Puerto Banus Day: Premium Trip
metaDescription: Book luxury catamaran hire Puerto Banus day trip with skipper. Cruise Costa del Sol, spot dolphins, swim in Cala del Faro. From EUR 2,500 for 8 guests.
h1: Luxury Catamaran Hire Puerto Banus Day Trip
keyword: luxury catamaran hire Puerto Banus day
intro:
You want a day on the water that doesn't feel like a compromise. Luxury catamaran hire Puerto Banus day trips are the answer. You get a spacious, stable catamaran with a licensed skipper, leaving you free to sunbathe, eat, drink, and swim in the Mediterranean. No hidden fees, no driving, just the Costa del Sol at its best.

BoatHire24 connects you with the best catamarans in Puerto Banús, from 40-foot Lagoon models to 60-foot Sunreef yachts. Every booking includes a professional crew, fuel, and safety equipment. You just pick the boat, the date, and the itinerary. The skipper handles the rest.
bodyHtml:
What a Luxury Catamaran Day Trip Includes

When you book luxury catamaran hire Puerto Banus day, you get more than a boat. You get a full package. The catamaran comes with a licensed skipper who knows the local waters, fuel for the day, and all standard safety gear. Many boats also include a host or stewardess to serve drinks and food. You bring your own catering or order from a preferred supplier. Some catamarans offer a water trampoline, paddleboards, snorkelling gear, and even a BBQ on the back deck.

The typical day runs from 10am to 6pm. You depart from Puerto Banús, cruise south past the Marbella coastline, anchor for swimming and lunch, then return in time for sunset drinks. The skipper can adjust the route based on wind and your preferences. You don't need a boating licence or any experience. Just show up with your group.

Puerto Banús: The Starting Point

Puerto Banús is the hub for luxury catamaran hire Puerto Banus day trips. It is a purpose-built marina with designer shops, restaurants, and a constant buzz. Your catamaran will be moored in the main dock or the outer harbour. The marina has parking nearby, but book a spot in advance during peak season. You can also arrive by taxi from Marbella (10 minutes) or Estepona (20 minutes).

From Puerto Banús, you have direct access to the Mediterranean. The first stop is often the coast towards Cabopino or the dunes of Artola. You might spot the Rock of Gibraltar on a clear day. The skipper will point out the landmarks: the Sierra Blanca mountains behind Marbella, La Concha peak, and the exclusive villas of the Golden Mile.

Boat Options for Luxury Catamaran Hire Puerto Banus Day

The catamarans available for luxury catamaran hire Puerto Banus day range from 40 to 70 feet. The most common are Lagoon 450, Bali 4.5, and Fountaine Pajot Saba 50. These boats have 4 to 6 cabins, but you only use them for changing or siesta. The key spaces are the saloon, the flybridge, and the foredeck with sun pads.

For larger groups, consider a Sunreef 60 or a Privilege 585. These have more beam, meaning more deck space and a bigger trampoline. They also have a generator for air conditioning and a watermaker. Prices for a day trip start from around EUR 2,500 for an 8-person catamaran and go up to EUR 5,500 for a 12-person super-cat. Fuel and crew are included. Food and drinks are extra.

Browse our catamaran search to see current availability and pricing.

Typical Itinerary for a Day Trip

A standard luxury catamaran hire Puerto Banus day itinerary goes like this. Depart Puerto Banús at 10am. Cruise east along the coast past the Marbella Marina and the old town. The skipper will point out the Cala del Faro, a small cove with a lighthouse. You stop for a swim around midday. The water is typically 20 to 26 degrees Celsius from June to October.

Lunch is served on board. You can bring your own or order from a local catering service. Many clients choose a paella or a seafood platter. After lunch, you sail back towards Puerto Banús, possibly stopping for a second swim near the Río Verde beach. You return to the marina around 5pm, with time for a final drink on the flybridge.

If you want a longer day, some catamarans offer a 8-hour charter that goes as far as Estepona or even the Strait of Gibraltar. That is a 12-hour trip with a lunch stop in Estepona old town. Prices on request.

When to Book Luxury Catamaran Hire Puerto Banus Day

The best season for luxury catamaran hire Puerto Banus day is May to October. July and August are the hottest, with temperatures hitting 35 degrees Celsius. The sea is calmest in June and September. April and November can work if the weather is good, but expect cooler winds. The catamarans are in high demand during August and the Easter week. Book at least 2 weeks in advance for summer dates.

Winter charters are possible but less common. The catamarans are still available, but the water temperature drops to 15 degrees Celsius. The advantage is fewer crowds and lower prices. Some clients combine a day trip with a visit to the Nerja caves or a drive to Ronda.

Who Should Book a Luxury Catamaran Hire Puerto Banus Day

This is for groups of friends, families, or corporate events. The catamaran is stable, so it suits people who get seasick on monohulls. It is also great for families with children because the deck is flat and safe. The skipper and crew handle everything, so you can relax.

If you are celebrating a birthday, anniversary, or a wedding proposal, the crew can arrange champagne, flowers, and a photographer. Just let them know in advance. For bachelor or bachelorette parties, the catamaran can host up to 12 guests. Be aware that loud music and excessive alcohol are not allowed. The skipper has the final say on safety.

For smaller groups or a faster experience, consider a luxury RIB hire or a motor yacht. But for space, stability, and a true day out, the catamaran wins.

How to Book Your Luxury Catamaran Hire Puerto Banus Day

Booking with BoatHire24 is simple. Go to our homepage, select Puerto Banús as the port, choose a catamaran, and pick your date. You can filter by guest capacity, price, and amenities. Once you select a boat, you send a booking request. We confirm availability within 24 hours. A deposit of 50% secures the date. The balance is due 7 days before the charter.

We recommend you read our blog for tips on what to bring, how to tip the crew, and the best local restaurants for post-charter dinner. We also have a guide to the best swimming spots along the Costa del Sol.

If you have a specific catamaran in mind but it is not listed, contact us. We can source it. We work with all major charter companies in Puerto Banús.

Why Choose BoatHire24 for Your Day Charter

BoatHire24 has been in the Marbella charter market since 2015. We only list boats that are properly insured, maintained, and crewed. Every catamaran we offer has a valid commercial licence and a Spanish-flagged skipper. We do not list private boats that cut corners.

Our team speaks English, Spanish, and German. We can help you plan the itinerary, arrange catering, and even book a taxi from your hotel. We are based in Marbella, so we know the local conditions. If the wind is too strong for a catamaran, we will suggest a motor yacht instead. We want you to have a good day, not a wasted one.

Luxury catamaran hire Puerto Banus day trips are the best way to see the Costa del Sol from the water. Book yours today.

Detailed Breakdown of Costs: What You Actually Pay

Let's talk money. The headline price for luxury catamaran hire Puerto Banus day usually covers the boat, the skipper, and fuel for a standard 8-hour day. But there are extras. The base rate for a 40-foot catamaran starts from around EUR 2,500. A 50-foot model like the Lagoon 52 runs from EUR 3,800. For a 60-foot Sunreef, expect EUR 5,500 to EUR 7,000. These prices are for the boat hire only.

Food and drink are separate. You can bring your own, which costs whatever you spend at the supermarket. Or you can order from a catering company. A typical lunch menu with paella, salad, and drinks costs EUR 30 to EUR 50 per person. Some companies charge a corkage fee if you bring your own wine. Ask before you book. The crew tip is discretionary but standard at 10% to 15% of the charter fee. If you have a hostess, tip them separately, around EUR 50 to EUR 100.

Then there is the fuel surcharge. Most charters include fuel for a standard itinerary of 30 to 40 nautical miles. If you want to go further, say to the Strait of Gibraltar, the skipper will add a fuel supplement. That can be EUR 200 to EUR 500 extra. Always confirm what is included in the quoted price before you pay the deposit.

Ports and Anchorages: Where You Actually Go

From Puerto Banús, the most popular anchorage for luxury catamaran hire Puerto Banus day is the bay of Cabopino. It is a protected cove with sandy seabed, about 5 nautical miles east. The water is clear and shallow, perfect for swimming. You anchor near the dunes of Artola, a natural park. Another option is the coast off the Marbella Club Hotel, where the water is deeper and quieter.

If you want a longer cruise, head west towards Estepona. The journey is 12 nautical miles and takes about 1.5 hours at cruising speed. You can anchor off the Estepona old town beach or near the marina. The backdrop is the Sierra Bermeja mountains. For a truly memorable day, continue to the Strait of Gibraltar. That is 25 nautical miles from Puerto Banús. You will see dolphins, sometimes whales, and the African coast. This is a full-day trip, 10 hours minimum, and costs more due to fuel.

For a short hop, go to the Cala del Faro, a small cove with a lighthouse just east of Marbella. It is 3 nautical miles from Puerto Banús. The water is deep right up to the rocks, so you can anchor close. It is popular with locals and less crowded than the main beaches.

The Catamaran Fleet: Specific Models and Their Quirks

Not all catamarans are the same. The Lagoon 450 is a workhorse. It has a large flybridge, a forward cockpit with a trampoline, and a spacious saloon. The galley is down, which means the cook is separate from the guests. The Bali 4.5 has an open-plan saloon that opens fully to the aft deck. It feels like a floating terrace. The Fountaine Pajot Saba 50 has a huge flybridge and a hydraulic swim platform that makes boarding from the water easy.

For luxury catamaran hire Puerto Banus day, the Sunreef 60 is the top end. It has a full-beam master cabin, a jacuzzi on the flybridge, and a crew cabin separate from guests. The deck space is enormous. You can have 12 guests without feeling cramped. The Privilege 585 is similar but has a more traditional layout with a central helm station.

If you want a catamaran with a water trampoline, look for models like the Lagoon 42 or the Bali 4.0. These have a forward net that is safe for children. Some older models have a solid foredeck instead. Check the photos or ask us before booking.

Seasons and Weather: When to Go and What to Expect

The Mediterranean weather is predictable but not guaranteed. June and September are the sweet spots. The sea temperature is 22 to 24 degrees Celsius, the air is 28 to 30 degrees, and the wind is light. July and August are hotter, with air temperatures up to 35 degrees. The sea is calm in the morning but can develop a light chop by afternoon due to thermal winds. The skipper will adjust the route to stay comfortable.

May and October are shoulder months. The water is cooler, 18 to 20 degrees, and the air is 22 to 25 degrees. You can still swim, but you might want a wetsuit. The advantage is fewer boats on the water and lower prices. Some catamarans offer discounts of 15% to 20% in these months. November to March is low season. The catamarans are still available but you will be wearing a jacket. The water is 15 degrees. Only book if you are sure about the weather.

The wind in the Strait of Gibraltar is a factor. In summer, the Levante wind blows from the east and can reach 20 to 30 knots in the afternoon. The skipper will avoid the Strait if it is too windy. Instead, you stay close to the coast. Always trust the skipper's decision. Safety comes first.

Practical Tips for Your Day on the Water

Bring sunscreen with high SPF, a hat, and sunglasses. The sun reflects off the water and you will burn faster than on land. Bring a towel and a change of clothes. You will get wet from swimming or from the trampoline. Wear non-marking shoes. No black soles on the deck. The crew will ask you to remove shoes anyway.

If you are prone to seasickness, take a motion sickness pill an hour before departure. Catamarans are stable, but if you anchor in a swell, you will feel movement. Ginger biscuits and fresh air help. Avoid a heavy breakfast. The skipper can provide seasickness bands if you ask.

For photography, the best light is early morning or late afternoon. The golden hour from 6pm to 7pm gives you the best shots of the coastline. Bring a waterproof phone case or a GoPro for swimming shots. The crew can take group photos from the flybridge.

How to Choose the Right Catamaran for Your Group

For a group of 6 to 8 people, a 40 to 45-foot catamaran is enough. You get a saloon, a flybridge, and a foredeck. For 8 to 10 people, go for a 50-footer. For 10 to 12 people, you need a 55 to 60-footer. The number of cabins matters only if you plan to sleep on board. For a day trip, you only use the cabins for changing or a nap. Focus on deck space and amenities.

If you have children, choose a catamaran with a trampoline and a shallow swim platform. Avoid models with high freeboard that make boarding from the water difficult. Some catamarans have a barbecue grill on the aft deck. That is great for a casual lunch. Others have a full galley and a crew to cook. Decide what matters to you.

If you want a party atmosphere, look for a catamaran with a good sound system and a flybridge bar. Some boats have a Bluetooth speaker. Others have a built-in system with subwoofers. The crew can play your playlist. Just be respectful of other boats and the environment. No loud music after 8pm in the anchorage.

Combining Your Catamaran Day with Other Activities

You can extend your luxury catamaran hire Puerto Banus day by adding on a meal at a beach club. Some catamarans will dock at the Marbella Club or the Puente Romano beach club for lunch. You step off the boat and onto the sun loungers. This costs extra for the mooring and the food, but it is a popular option.

Another idea is to combine the day trip with a visit to the Nerja caves. The caves are 40 minutes by car from Puerto Banús. You can do the catamaran in the morning, drive to Nerja in the afternoon, and return for dinner. Or do the caves first and the boat after. We can help arrange the transport.

For a romantic day, the skipper can anchor in a quiet cove for a private lunch. You can have a chef on board to prepare a custom menu. This is popular for proposals and anniversaries. The crew can decorate the boat with flowers and set up a private dining area on the foredeck. Just give us a week's notice.

What Happens if the Weather Is Bad

The skipper decides if the trip goes ahead. If the wind is above 25 knots or there is a storm warning, the trip is cancelled. You get a full refund or a reschedule. If the weather is marginal, the skipper might suggest a different route, like staying in the marina or going to a closer anchorage. You can accept or cancel with a refund.

For luxury catamaran hire Puerto Banus day, the cancellation policy is standard. If you cancel more than 14 days before, you get a full refund minus a small admin fee. If you cancel within 7 days, you lose the deposit. If you cancel within 48 hours, you pay the full amount. Read the terms before you book. We recommend travel insurance that covers weather-related cancellations.

In summer, the chance of a cancellation is low. In May and October, it is higher. The skipper will monitor the forecast and inform you 24 hours before. If you are flexible, you can move the booking to the next day. We will help you find an alternative date.

Why Book Through BoatHire24 Instead of Directly

You can call a charter company directly and book the same catamaran. But you might pay more. We have negotiated rates with the fleet owners. We pass the savings to you. We also handle the paperwork, the deposit, and the communication. If something goes wrong, we are your single point of contact. We do not disappear after you pay.

We also vet every catamaran. We check the insurance, the safety equipment, and the skipper's licence. We have seen boats that are not properly maintained. We do not list them. You get peace of mind. Plus, we know the local scene. We can recommend the best caterer, the best photographer, and the best beach club for your group.

Luxury catamaran hire Puerto Banus day is a premium experience. You want it to go smoothly. Let us handle the details. You just show up and enjoy.
