faqs.a: Book at least a week ahead in summer. For other seasons, a few days is usually enough. Last-minute bookings are possible but limited.

title: Speedboat Hire Marbella Port Group: Up to 12 People
metaDescription: Book speedboat hire Marbella port group up to 12 people. RIBs and speedboats with skipper from EUR 450. Depart Puerto Banús or Marbella Marina. Instant online booking.
h1: Speedboat Hire Marbella Port Group: Up to 12 People
intro:
You want to take a group out on the water from Marbella port, and you need a speedboat that fits everyone. On BoatHire24, you can book a RIB or speedboat for up to 12 people, with a licensed skipper included. Depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, or other Costa del Sol ports. Prices start from around EUR 450 for a half-day trip. No licence required, just turn up and go.

Speedboat hire for a group is straightforward. You choose the boat size, the duration, and the departure port. The skipper handles navigation and safety, so you can focus on the coast views, the dolphins, and the stops for swimming. We list real boats with real availability, so you can book online with a deposit and pay the rest on the day.
bodyHtml:
What You Get with a Group Speedboat Hire

Every speedboat booking through BoatHire24 includes a professional skipper. That means you do not need a boating licence or any experience. The skipper knows the local waters, the best coves, and the weather patterns. You get a safety briefing, life jackets for everyone, and a fuel-included rate. Most boats have a Bluetooth sound system, a sun pad or seating area, and a small cabin for changing. Some newer RIBs have a fresh-water shower on the back deck. You can bring your own food and drink, or ask the skipper to stop at a chiringuito beach bar.

Departure Ports for Your Group

You can start your speedboat trip from several ports along the Costa del Sol. The most popular is Puerto Banús, the glitzy marina in Marbella. From there, you can head west to the Buenavista area or east toward Marbella town and the Río Verde beach. Another option is Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo), which is closer to the old town and the La Concha mountain backdrop. Cabopino is a quieter port near the Artola sand dunes, good for a more relaxed start. Estepona port gives you access to the Cala del Faro and the rugged coast toward Gibraltar. Sotogrande is a luxury residential marina, and Benalmádena offers quick access to the open sea. All these ports have easy parking and facilities.

Boat Options for Groups of 6 to 12

The boats you can hire range from 6-metre RIBs up to 11-metre speedboats. A typical 7-metre RIB seats 8 to 10 people comfortably. An 8.5-metre centre-console or bowrider fits 10 to 12. Some boats have a T-top with shade, others have a bimini top. All have a toilet on board. For a group of 12, you need a boat rated for that capacity, which usually means a larger RIB or a rigid inflatable with a high-horsepower outboard. We list the maximum passenger number on each boat page. You cannot exceed it for safety and insurance reasons.

Pricing Guidance for Group Speedboat Hire

Prices vary by boat size, age, and season. For a half-day (4 hours), expect from around EUR 450 to EUR 800 for a boat that holds 8 to 10 people. For a full day (8 hours), prices range from EUR 700 to EUR 1,500. A 10-person RIB with a 250 HP engine might cost EUR 600 for half a day. A newer 11-metre speedboat with a cabin could be EUR 1,200 for a full day. Peak summer months (July and August) are more expensive, and you may need to book two weeks ahead. Spring and autumn offer better availability and lower rates. You pay a deposit online (usually 30% to 50%), and the balance in cash or card on the day.

Best Times of Year for a Group Speedboat Trip

The Costa del Sol has a long season. From April to October, you can expect 20 to 30 degrees C and calm seas. July and August are the hottest, with sea temperatures around 24 degrees C. The water is warm enough for swimming from June to September. Spring and autumn have fewer crowds and more flexible booking slots. Winter trips are possible but the sea can be choppy, and some boats are laid up. The skipper will advise on the best time for your group size and itinerary.

What to Do on a Group Speedboat Trip

You can customise the route with the skipper. Popular stops include Cala del Faro near Estepona, a sheltered cove with clear water. The Río Verde area has a small beach and a cave. Puerto Banús itself is worth a slow cruise past the superyachts. If you head west, you might see dolphins in the Strait of Gibraltar area. For a longer trip, the Nerja caves are visible from the sea on the eastern side. Most groups do a mix of cruising, swimming, and stopping for lunch at a beach club. The skipper can recommend a chiringuito that takes boat bookings.

How to Book a Group Speedboat on BoatHire24

Go to the BoatHire24 homepage and use the search filters. Set the number of passengers to 12 or fewer. Choose your preferred port and date. The system shows available boats with prices and photos. Click on a boat to see the full details, including the cancellation policy and what is included. You can book directly online with a deposit. The confirmation email includes the skipper's contact details and meeting point instructions. For groups larger than 12, you might need two boats or a catamaran. Check the search page for all options.

Who This Is For

Speedboat hire for a group suits birthday parties, stag and hen dos, family reunions, corporate outings, and friend groups on holiday. It is not a party boat with a DJ or bar, but you can bring your own music and drinks. The skipper keeps things safe and legal. If you want a wild party, consider a larger catamaran or a yacht charter. For a fast, fun, and flexible day on the water with your own group, a speedboat is the right choice.

Frequently Asked Questions

We answer common questions about group speedboat hire in our blog. Here are the main ones.

Which Port Works Best for a Group of 10 to 12

If you are starting from Puerto Banús, you get the full Marbella glamour. The marina has a large car park (around EUR 25 per day) and plenty of cafés for a pre-departure coffee. A group of 12 can walk from the car to the pontoon in 5 minutes. Marbella Marina is more practical if your group is staying in the town centre. The port has a free drop-off zone and a public car park 200 metres away. Cabopino is a good choice for a quieter start. The marina is smaller, so you are on the water within 10 minutes of parking. Estepona port has a new promenade and public parking for around EUR 10 per day. Sotogrande is the most exclusive, but parking is limited and expensive (EUR 30 per day). Benalmádena has a huge car park and is close to the airport, which matters if your group is flying in that day.

Fuel Costs and Hidden Fees for Group Speedboat Hire

Most listings on BoatHire24 include fuel in the price. But you should confirm this before booking. Some operators charge extra for fuel based on engine hours, which can add EUR 100 to EUR 300 for a full day. Ask whether the price includes fuel for the whole trip or just a set number of nautical miles. There is no docking fee at the departure port, but if you stop at a beach club that has a jetty, the skipper may need to pay a small mooring fee (EUR 10 to EUR 20). You can avoid this by anchoring off the beach and swimming ashore. The skipper will explain the policy on the day. Always read the cancellation terms. Most operators offer a full refund if you cancel 48 hours in advance, but some have a 72-hour window for groups of 10 or more.

What a 7.5-Metre RIB Can Actually Do for 8 People

Take the example of a 7.5-metre RIB with a 200 HP outboard. It cruises at 25 knots and tops out at 40 knots. That means you can reach Cala del Faro from Puerto Banús in 20 minutes, or get to the dolphin-watching area near Gibraltar in 45 minutes. The boat has a T-top for shade, a small bow seating area, and a rear bench that fits three people. Eight adults can sit comfortably, but 10 starts to feel crowded. For 12 people, you need a minimum of 8.5 metres. A 9-metre RIB with twin 250 HP engines can carry 12 at 30 knots cruising speed. It has a larger deck area and a proper cabin with a toilet. That boat will cost around EUR 900 for half a day in high season.

How Weather Affects Group Speedboat Trips in Marbella

The Costa del Sol has a microclimate. Even on a cloudy morning, the sea can be flat calm by 11 am. But wind patterns change quickly, especially near the Strait of Gibraltar. A Levante wind (from the east) can create choppy conditions on the western side of Marbella, while the eastern side stays protected. The skipper will check the forecast the night before and may suggest a different route or departure time. For a group of 12, you want a sea state of 2 or less on the Beaufort scale. That means waves under 0.5 metres. In July and August, you get that 90% of the time. In April and October, it drops to 70%. If the sea is too rough, the operator may cancel or offer a larger, more stable boat. BoatHire24 listings include a weather clause in the terms.

What to Bring for a Group Speedboat Day

Each person needs a towel, sunscreen (SPF 50, water-resistant), a hat, and sunglasses. The sun reflects off the water, so you burn faster than on land. Bring a dry bag for phones and wallets. Most speedboats have a small dry storage compartment. For food, pack light things that do not melt. Sandwiches, fruit, crisps, and bottled water work well. Avoid glass bottles on board, as broken glass is a safety hazard. If you want to stop for lunch, the skipper can recommend a chiringuito that accepts boat arrivals. Popular ones include Chiringuito Lola on the beach near Río Verde and El Ancla in Estepona. They serve fresh seafood and cold drinks. You pay directly at the restaurant. The skipper will wait at the jetty or anchor offshore.

How to Split Costs Fairly Among 12 People

If you are organising the trip, collect the money upfront. A typical half-day for 12 people on an 8.5-metre speedboat costs around EUR 700 in mid-season. That works out at EUR 58 per person. Add EUR 10 per person for fuel if it is not included, plus EUR 15 each for lunch. Total per person is around EUR 83. For a full day at EUR 1,200, it is EUR 100 per person plus food and drink. That is cheaper than a day at a beach club with a sunbed and lunch. You can use a payment app like Revolut or PayPal to collect the money. Do not rely on cash on the day, as someone always forgets. The deposit you pay online (usually EUR 300) is refundable if no one cancels. You can split that equally too.

Speedboat Hire vs Catamaran for a Group of 12

A speedboat is faster and more exciting. You feel the wind and the spray. It is ideal for a group that wants to cover distance, see the coastline, and stop for quick swims. A catamaran is slower (8 to 12 knots) but has more deck space, a shaded area, and a larger cabin. For 12 people, a catamaran feels roomy, while a speedboat is cosy. If your group wants to party with music and dancing, a catamaran is better. If you want to explore multiple coves and get that adrenaline rush, choose a speedboat. The cost difference is not huge. A half-day catamaran for 12 starts at around EUR 800, while a speedboat is EUR 700. Both include a skipper. You can compare both types on the search page.

What Happens if Someone Is Late on the Day

The skipper arrives 15 minutes before the booked time. The group should be at the meeting point at least 10 minutes early. If someone is late, the skipper will call the lead booker. You can delay the departure by up to 30 minutes without losing time, but the return time stays the same. If someone is more than 30 minutes late, the trip may be shortened or cancelled. The operator keeps the deposit. For a group of 12, it is worth setting a group WhatsApp chat the day before. Share the exact meeting point, a Google Maps pin, and a photo of the boat. That way, everyone knows where to go. The marina addresses are on the BoatHire24 confirmation email.

How to Get the Best Photos on a Group Speedboat Trip

The light is best at 9 am to 11 am and 4 pm to 6 pm. The morning light hits the Marbella coastline from the east, so you get clear shots of La Concha mountain behind the boat. The afternoon light is warmer and works well for photos of the group on the bow. Ask the skipper to stop at a spot with a good backdrop, like the cliffs near Cala del Faro or the superyacht row in Puerto Banús. A drone is not allowed without permission from the marina authority. Some operators allow a small drone if you ask in advance. The most important thing is to take photos before anyone gets wet. Wet hair and sunglasses glare ruin group shots. Use a waterproof phone case or a GoPro for the swimming stops.

What to Do If You Have a Group Larger Than 12

BoatHire24 has options for groups up to 18 or 20. For larger groups, you can hire two speedboats that travel together. The skippers coordinate by radio. You can also consider a catamaran or a motor yacht that holds 18 to 30 guests. A 14-metre motor yacht with a flybridge can take 18 people comfortably and costs from EUR 1,500 for a half-day. A 12-metre catamaran can take 20 people and costs around EUR 1,200 for half a day. Check the capacity on each boat page. If you need more than 20, look at superyacht charters (price on request). The BoatHire24 team can help you find the right vessel for your group size.

Why a Skipper Makes Group Speedboat Hire Better

You might think you want to drive the boat yourself. But with 12 people on board, the skipper handles all the navigation, safety, and anchoring. They know the shallow spots near Cabopino and the rocks around the Punta de Calaburras lighthouse. They can spot dolphin pods from 500 metres away. They also handle the radio if the weather changes. A licence-free boat (up to 6 metres, 15 HP) is an option for a smaller group, but for 12 people, you need a larger boat that requires a licence. A skipper is included in every BoatHire24 charter. That means you can relax, drink a beer, and enjoy the ride. The skipper is not a tour guide but will point out landmarks and recommend the best swimming spots.

faqs.q: Do I need a licence to hire a speedboat for a group in Marbella?
faqs.a: No. All BoatHire24 speedboats include a licensed skipper. You just need to be 18 or older to book, and all passengers must follow the skipper's safety instructions.

faqs.q: Can we bring our own food and drink on board?
faqs.a: Yes. Most boats allow you to bring your own food and drink. Some skippers may have restrictions on glass bottles or red wine. Check the boat details or ask the skipper directly.

faqs.q: How far in advance should I book a group speedboat?
faqs.a: For summer weekends, book at least two weeks ahead. For weekdays or spring/autumn, a few days is usually fine. You can check real-time availability on the BoatHire24 search page.

faqs.q: What happens if the weather is bad on the day?
faqs.a: The skipper decides if it is safe to go out. If the trip is cancelled due to weather, you get a full refund or can reschedule. We do not charge extra for weather-related cancellations.

faqs.q: Can we choose the route or stops?
faqs.a: Yes. You can discuss the itinerary with the skipper at the start. They know the best spots for swimming, dolphin watching, and beach stops. You can change plans during the trip within reason.

faqs.q: Is there a toilet on board?
faqs.a: All boats listed for group hire have a toilet. On smaller RIBs it may be a portable unit in a small cabin. Larger speedboats have a proper marine toilet. Check the boat details for specifics.

faqs.q: What is the maximum group size for a speedboat?
faqs.a: The maximum is 12 passengers including children. The boat's licence limits the number. If you have more than 12, consider hiring two speedboats or a larger catamaran. Use the BoatHire24 search filters to find suitable options.

title: Luxury Yacht Charter Puerto Banus Day: All Inclusive
metaDescription: Book a luxury yacht charter Puerto Banus day all-inclusive with skipper. Motor yachts, catamarans, from around EUR 1,200. Compare boats on BoatHire24.
h1: Luxury Yacht Charter Puerto Banus Day: All Inclusive
keyword: luxury yacht charter Puerto Banus day
intro:
You want a day on the water in Marbella without surprises. A luxury yacht charter Puerto Banus day all-inclusive means one price for the boat, the skipper, fuel, taxes, and often food and drink. You turn up at the port, step aboard, and the crew handles the rest. No hidden extras, no haggling over fuel costs at the end. That is the deal.

Puerto Banus is the obvious starting point. The marina is packed with superyachts, designer shops, and bars that stay open late. But the real show is the sea. Within 30 minutes you can anchor off the sandbanks of the Río Verde, swim in the calm water at Cala del Faro, or cruise past the exclusive villas of the Golden Mile. With a skipper included, you can focus on the sun, the sangria, and the Instagram shots.

On BoatHire24 you compare boats from local operators, see exactly what is included, and book online. No middleman markups, just the boat and the crew. This page covers what you get, the ports you can leave from, the types of boats available, and how to pick the right one for your group.
bodyHtml:
What All-Inclusive Actually Means on a Luxury Yacht Charter Puerto Banus Day

All-inclusive on a luxury yacht charter Puerto Banus day typically covers the boat hire, the licensed skipper, fuel for the day, port taxes, and cleaning fees. Some operators throw in a welcome bottle of cava, soft drinks, and a basic snack platter. Others offer a full open bar and a gourmet lunch prepared on board. You need to read the listing carefully because the definition varies.

For example, a 12-metre motor yacht from Puerto Banus might include a skipper, fuel for up to 8 hours, a cooler with ice, and a Bluetooth speaker. A 20-metre catamaran might include a crew of two, a barbecue lunch, and unlimited drinks. The price difference reflects that. Expect to pay from around EUR 1,200 for a mid-size motor yacht and upwards of EUR 3,500 for a large catamaran with full catering. Check the inclusions list on each listing before you book.

Which Ports Can You Leave From

Puerto Banus is the most popular departure point for a luxury yacht charter Puerto Banus day, but you are not stuck there. The other ports on the Costa del Sol offer different advantages.

Puerto Banus: The glitziest option. You step off the yacht straight into the marina restaurants and boutiques. The port has 24-hour security, fuel docks, and plenty of parking. It is the closest to the Marbella Golden Mile and the Río Verde sandbanks.

Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo): A quieter, more local vibe. The marina is smaller than Banus but has good facilities and is closer to Marbella old town. The run to the swimming spots is about 10 minutes longer.

Cabopino: A small, sandy-beach marina near the Artola dunes. Good for a relaxed start, especially if you want to head east towards the Nerja caves or the Cala del Faro. The marina itself is less crowded.

Estepona: The western gateway. From Estepona you can cruise to the Strait of Gibraltar for dolphin watching or head to the exclusive Sotogrande area. The port is modern with a long promenade.

Sotogrande: A private, upmarket estate with a marina that feels like a resort. Ideal if you want to combine a yacht charter with a round at the Valderrama golf course. The surrounding coast is less developed.

Benalmádena: A large, family-friendly marina with a cable car and a sea-life centre. Good for groups staying in the Torremolinos or Fuengirola area. The run to Marbella takes about 40 minutes.

Each port has its own character. Choose based on where you are staying and what you want to see. Browse all available yachts by port on BoatHire24.

Boat Options for a Luxury Yacht Charter Puerto Banus Day

Not all luxury yachts are the same. The type of boat changes the experience, the space, and the price. Here are the main categories you will find on a luxury yacht charter Puerto Banus day.

Motor Yachts: The classic choice. Fast, sleek, and with plenty of deck space for sunbathing. A 15-metre motor yacht can hit 25 knots and cover the coast quickly. Good for groups who want to visit multiple spots in one day. The interior is usually air-conditioned with a saloon, a galley, and a few cabins. Prices from around EUR 1,500 for a half-day.

Catamarans: Stability is the big selling point. Catamarans do not roll as much as monohulls, so if anyone in your group gets seasick, this is the option. They also have a huge flybridge and a forward trampoline net for lounging. A 15-metre catamaran can carry 12 passengers comfortably. Prices from around EUR 2,000 for a full day.

Sailing Yachts: For a quieter, more traditional experience. Sailing yachts are slower than motor yachts but offer a different kind of luxury: the sound of the wind, the silence of the engine off, the sense of travel. Most sailing charters still use the engine for part of the day. Prices from around EUR 1,200.

RIBs and Speedboats: Not what most people picture as luxury, but a high-end RIB with a powerful outboard and comfortable seating can be a thrill. These are best for small groups who want to go fast, visit hidden coves, and not spend all day on the water. Prices from around EUR 600 for a half-day.

Fishing Boats: Some operators offer luxury fishing charters with a skipper who knows the best spots for sea bass, bream, and tuna. The boat is equipped with rods, reels, and a fish finder. Prices from around EUR 900 for a half-day.

Licence-Free Day Boats: These are smaller motorboats (up to 15 HP or 6 metres) that you can drive without a boating licence. The skipper is not included because you are the skipper. They are cheap but not really luxury. Not recommended for the all-inclusive luxury experience.

Superyachts: For the top end. A superyacht charter Puerto Banus day with a crew of 5, a jacuzzi, a jet ski, and a chef. Prices on request. Expect to pay upwards of EUR 10,000 for a day. These are listed on BoatHire24 but you need to contact the operator directly.

Read our blog post on choosing the right boat for your group for more detail.

Pricing Guidance for a Luxury Yacht Charter Puerto Banus Day

Prices vary by boat size, age, equipment, and inclusions. Here is a rough guide for a full day (8 hours) in high season (June to September).

Small motor yacht (10-13m): from around EUR 1,200. Includes skipper, fuel, taxes. Food and drink extra or bring your own.

Mid-size motor yacht (14-18m): from around EUR 2,500. Often includes a crew of 2, a basic lunch, and an open bar of beer, wine, and soft drinks.

Large motor yacht (19-24m): from around EUR 4,500. Full crew, gourmet catering, premium drinks, water toys (paddleboard, snorkel gear).

Catamaran (12-18m): from around EUR 2,000. Includes skipper, fuel, taxes. Food and drink usually extra.

Sailing yacht (12-18m): from around EUR 1,200. Skipper included, fuel for the engine included. Food and drink extra.

Low season (October to May) prices are 20-40% lower. Half-day (4 hours) is usually 60-70% of the full-day price. Always confirm the exact price on the listing page. Do not rely on third-party estimates.

Seasons and Weather for a Luxury Yacht Charter Puerto Banus Day

The Costa del Sol has a Mediterranean climate with over 300 sunny days a year. That means you can charter a yacht year-round, but the experience changes with the season.

High season (June-September): Air temperatures 28-35 degrees C, sea water 22-26 degrees C. The coast is busy, the ports are full, and the demand for yachts is high. Book at least 2 weeks in advance. The wind is usually light in the morning, picking up in the afternoon. The Levante wind (easterly) can bring some chop, but the sheltered bays are calm.

Shoulder season (April-May, October): Air temperatures 20-28 degrees C, sea water 17-22 degrees C. Fewer crowds, lower prices, and still pleasant for swimming in May and October. The wind is more variable. The Poniente (westerly) is warm and calm. The Levante can be stronger.

Low season (November-March): Air temperatures 15-20 degrees C, sea water 15-18 degrees C. Swimming is possible but bracing. The main appeal is whale and dolphin watching in the Strait of Gibraltar, or just cruising with a coat. Prices are lowest. Some boats are laid up for maintenance, so availability is limited.

Check the weather forecast before you go. The wind can shift quickly. Your skipper will make the call on whether to stay in sheltered waters or head to open sea.

How to Book a Luxury Yacht Charter Puerto Banus Day

Booking on BoatHire24 is straightforward. You search by port, date, and number of guests. Each listing shows the boat, the inclusions, the price, and the cancellation policy. You can message the operator directly through the platform to ask questions before you pay.

Once you book, you receive a confirmation with the meeting point (usually the port office or a specific dock), the skipper's contact number, and the departure time. Arrive 15 minutes early. The skipper will do a safety briefing and explain the itinerary. You can discuss where you want to go, but the skipper has the final say on safety grounds.

Payment is usually by credit card or bank transfer. Some operators take cash on the day, but that is less common for luxury charters. The platform holds your payment until after the charter, so you are protected if something goes wrong.

Search available yachts for your date on BoatHire24.

Who Is a Luxury Yacht Charter Puerto Banus Day For

This type of charter works for several groups.

Couples: A private yacht for two is romantic, especially at sunset. You get the attention of the crew and can choose a quiet itinerary. Small motor yachts or sailing yachts are best.

Families with children: Catamarans are stable and have plenty of space. The crew can prepare kid-friendly food. Many boats have paddleboards and snorkel gear. Children must be supervised at all times; the skipper is not a babysitter.

Groups of friends: A motor yacht with a flybridge is ideal for socialising. You can play music, swim, eat, and drink. The all-inclusive option means no arguments over the bill at the end.

Corporate events: A day charter can serve as a team-building event or a client entertainment day. Some operators offer catering, AV equipment, and branded materials. Check the maximum passenger capacity; most boats are licensed for 12 guests.

Special occasions: Birthdays, anniversaries, proposals. Let the operator know in advance. They can arrange decorations, a cake, or a photographer.

If you are not sure which boat suits your group, read our guide to choosing a charter boat.

What to Bring and What to Expect

Pack light. You need swimwear, a towel, sunscreen (reef-safe if possible), sunglasses, a hat, and a light jacket for the evening. Non-slip shoes are a good idea on a motor yacht. On a catamaran, barefoot is fine.

The skipper will provide life jackets for all passengers, but you must wear them if you are on the deck while the boat is moving at speed. The skipper will also have a first aid kit, a VHF radio, and a fire extinguisher. Do not touch the boat's equipment without permission.

Food and drink: If the charter is not all-inclusive, you can bring your own. Most operators allow you to bring a cooler with your own food and drink. Glass bottles are often banned on board for safety reasons. Use plastic or cans. The skipper will have a fridge and ice on request.

Toilets: Most luxury yachts have a marine toilet (heads). Do not flush anything other than toilet paper. The system is delicate and expensive to repair.

Seasickness: If you are prone to it, take medication an hour before departure. Ginger biscuits and fresh air help. Avoid heavy food and alcohol before the trip.

Local Highlights You Can See on a Luxury Yacht Charter Puerto Banus Day

The coast between Estepona and Marbella is packed with sights. Your skipper will know the best spots based on the wind and the crowds.

Río Verde sandbanks: A shallow area off the mouth of the Río Verde where the water is crystal clear and only a few metres deep. Popular for swimming and anchoring. The sandbanks shift with the currents, so the exact spot changes.

Cala del Faro: A small cove near Cabopino with a lighthouse. The water is deep enough for a swim and there is usually space to anchor. The cliffside walk is worth a photo.

La Concha mountain: The iconic peak behind Marbella. From the sea, you get a perfect view of its shell-shaped summit. The best angle is from the south, about a mile offshore.

Puerto Banus skyline: The marina is a sight from the water, especially at sunset when the lights come on. You can see the superyachts moored in the outer harbour.

Strait of Gibraltar: A longer trip (2 hours from Puerto Banus) but worth it for dolphin and whale watching. Common species are common dolphins, striped dolphins, and pilot whales. The strait is a migration route for whales in spring and autumn.

Nerja caves: A full-day trip east from Puerto Banus (about 1.5 hours by fast motor yacht). The caves are a natural wonder with stalactites and stalagmites. You can anchor near the Balcón de Europa and take a taxi to the caves.

Your skipper will suggest an itinerary based on your preferences. Be realistic about how far you can go in a day. A typical luxury yacht charter Puerto Banus day covers 20-30 nautical miles round trip.

faqs.a: From around EUR 1,200 for a small motor yacht to EUR 4,500 for a large catamaran with full catering. Superyachts are price on request. Prices drop 20-40% in low season.

title: Private Catamaran Hire Marbella Sunset: Romantic Trip
metaDescription: Book a private catamaran hire Marbella sunset trip for a romantic evening. Includes skipper, drinks, and stunning views. Check availability now.
h1: Private Catamaran Hire Marbella Sunset
intro:
You want a sunset that feels like it was made for just the two of you. A private catamaran hire Marbella sunset trip is exactly that: a spacious, stable catamaran, a licensed skipper who knows the best spots, and the sky turning shades of orange and pink over the Mediterranean. No crowds, no noise, just you, your partner, and the sea. This page tells you what to expect, which ports to depart from, how much it costs, and how to book it without any hassle.

BoatHire24 connects you with the best catamarans on the Costa del Sol, from Puerto Banús to Estepona. Every charter includes a professional skipper, so you just relax and watch the sunset. Let's get into the details.
bodyHtml:
What a Private Catamaran Sunset Charter Includes

A private catamaran hire Marbella sunset trip is not a shared excursion. It's your boat for the evening, with a crew that looks after you. Typically, the package includes a licensed skipper (mandatory for all charters over 6 metres), a welcome drink (cava or sangria often), and sometimes a small selection of snacks. The catamaran itself is spacious, with a large forward trampoline net where you can lie down, a shaded cockpit, and a cabin below if you need a bathroom or a quiet moment. Most catamarans on our platform are between 12 and 18 metres long, with capacity for 6 to 12 guests. The skipper will take you to a calm spot to anchor, usually near the coast, so you can swim or just float as the sun goes down. Some charters also offer a barbecue dinner or a DJ set, but that's extra and must be arranged in advance.

Why a Catamaran Is Perfect for a Sunset Trip

Catamarans have two hulls, which makes them incredibly stable. You won't feel seasick, even if the sea is a bit choppy. The deck space is massive compared to a monohull yacht of the same length, so you can move around freely, take photos, or dance if you feel like it. For a romantic sunset trip, that stability means you can enjoy your drink without spilling it, and the wide trampoline net is the best seat in the house for watching the sun dip into the sea. Plus, catamarans are quiet under motor, so you hear the water lapping and the seagulls, not a roaring engine. That matters when you're trying to set the mood.

Departure Ports for Your Sunset Catamaran

You have several options for departure ports along the Costa del Sol. Each offers a slightly different sunset experience. BoatHire24 works with all of them, so you can choose based on where you're staying.

Puerto Banús is the glitzy option. You'll depart from the marina full of superyachts, and the sunset view is over the western horizon, with the Sierra Blanca mountains behind you. The trip often includes a slow cruise past the celebrity mansions in the Golden Mile. Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo) is closer to the old town and less flashy. You'll see La Concha mountain from the water, and the sunset is just as good. Cabopino is quieter, with a small marina and the Artola dunes nearby. The sunset from there is more intimate, with fewer boats around. Estepona offers a view of the Strait of Gibraltar, and if you're lucky, you'll see dolphins on the way. Sotogrande is a bit further east, but the sunset over the Alboran Sea is stunning. Benalmádena

What You Actually See During a Marbella Sunset

The sun doesn't just drop into the sea here. It turns the sky into a gradient of orange, pink, and purple, with La Concha mountain providing a dramatic silhouette on the left. If you're departing from Puerto Banús, you'll also see the luxury apartment blocks of the Golden Mile reflecting the colours. The water goes glassy, and the wind usually dies down around 8pm, so your catamaran sits still. You might spot a pod of common dolphins feeding near the shore, especially between May and October. The whole thing lasts about 20 minutes, but it feels longer when you're on the water without any buildings blocking the view.

How Much a Private Catamaran Sunset Trip Costs

Pricing varies by boat size, season, and what's included. For a private catamaran hire Marbella sunset trip with a 12-metre catamaran for up to 8 guests, expect to pay from around EUR 600 to EUR 1,200 for a three-hour charter. Larger catamarans, 15-18 metres, with capacity for 12 guests, start at around EUR 1,500 for the same duration. If you want a full barbecue dinner with grilled fish and meat, add EUR 200-400. A DJ set or a saxophonist on board adds another EUR 300-500. Some operators include a bottle of cava and snacks in the base price, others charge extra. Always check the listing on our platform before booking. And remember, the skipper is included, so no extra cost there.

Best Time of Year for a Sunset Catamaran in Marbella

The sunset time changes dramatically. In June, the sun sets around 9:40pm, so you'd typically charter from 7pm to 10pm. In December, it's around 6pm, so a 4pm to 7pm slot works. The weather is reliable from April to October, with average temperatures of 22-30 degrees C. July and August are the hottest, but the sea breeze keeps it comfortable on the water. Winter sunsets are possible, but the sea can be rougher and the air temperature drops to 12-16 degrees C after dark. If you're booking in November to February, choose a catamaran with a fully enclosed cockpit or a heater. Most operators don't run sunset charters in winter, so availability is limited. Spring and autumn are the sweet spots: fewer crowds, lower prices, and still warm enough to swim.

What to Bring and What to Wear

You need a light jacket or a sweater. Even in summer, the wind picks up as the sun goes down, and you'll feel cold sitting still. Wear non-marking shoes on deck, or go barefoot. Bring a swimsuit if you plan to swim before the sunset, plus a towel. Sunscreen is essential until the sun actually dips, because the reflection off the water burns you faster than on land. A hat and sunglasses help. If you're bringing a camera, use a strap, because things fall overboard. For a romantic trip, consider a bottle of champagne if the charter doesn't include one, but check with the skipper first. Some catamarans have a cooler, others don't. Also bring cash for the crew tip, typically 10-15% of the charter fee if you're happy with the service.

How to Book a Private Catamaran for Sunset

Use the search on BoatHire24. Filter by boat type "catamaran", then by port. Select your date and the number of guests. Most listings show the duration options, typically 3 or 4 hours for sunset trips. Read the "what's included" section carefully. Some charters include a full bar, others only water and soft drinks. If you want a specific add-on like a photographer or a flower arrangement, message the owner through the platform before booking. Payment is typically 50% deposit upfront, balance on the day. You'll receive a confirmation email with the skipper's contact details. For a sunset trip, arrive at the marina 15 minutes early. The skipper will meet you at the specified berth. If you're late, you lose sunset time, and there's no refund.

Who Should Book a Private Catamaran Sunset Trip

This is not a party boat for 20 people. It's for couples, small groups of friends, or families with older children. The atmosphere is calm and intimate. If you're proposing, this is a solid choice. The skipper can help you arrange a moment, like playing a specific song or bringing out a cake. It's also good for a small wedding reception, a birthday celebration for up to 12 guests, or a corporate dinner with clients. If you want a loud party with a DJ and dancing, a motor yacht might suit you better. But for a relaxed evening where you talk, watch the sky, and maybe swim, a catamaran is the right fit. Children are welcome, but they must wear life jackets, and the skipper will insist on safety rules. No running on the trampoline net.

What Happens If the Weather Is Bad

The skipper has the final say. If the wind is above 25 knots or there's a storm forecast, the trip will be cancelled. You'll get a full refund or a reschedule. Check the forecast yourself on Windy or AEMET (Spanish met office) before the day. If the sea is a bit choppy but still safe, the skipper might offer a shorter route closer to the coast. You can also ask to postpone to a better day. Most operators are flexible with rescheduling if you give 24 hours notice. If you cancel on the day because you changed your mind, you lose the deposit. Weather cancellations are rare in summer, but possible in spring and autumn. The Costa del Sol has over 300 sunny days a year, so your odds are good.

Comparing Catamaran Sunset Trips to Other Boat Options

A private catamaran hire Marbella sunset trip offers stability and space. A sailing yacht is cheaper but less stable and has less deck space. A motor yacht is faster and can cover more distance, but you'll hear the engine and feel the motion. A RIB or speedboat is too small for a romantic dinner and the ride is bumpy. A fishing boat is not designed for comfort. Catamarans hit the sweet spot: they're quiet, spacious, and smooth. If you want to swim, the trampoline net is easy to climb back onto. If you want to eat, the cockpit table is big enough for a proper meal. If you want to dance, the deck is wide enough for a few couples. For a sunset trip, it's the most practical choice.

Local Tips for the Best Sunset Catamaran Experience

Book a 4-hour charter instead of 3. The extra hour lets you relax before the sunset, swim, and then watch the colours change without feeling rushed. Ask the skipper to anchor near Cala del Faro, a small cove west of Marbella. It's sheltered and less crowded than the main beaches. Bring a bluetooth speaker, but keep the volume low to respect other boats. If you're departing from Estepona, ask the skipper to head towards the Strait of Gibraltar. The sunset there is over the Atlantic, and you might see the lights of Africa after dark. Avoid booking on a Saturday in August if you want peace. The marinas are packed and the water is full of other boats. A Tuesday or Wednesday evening is much quieter. Check our blog for more detailed guides on specific ports and boat types.

What's Not Included in a Standard Sunset Catamaran Charter

The base price usually covers the boat, the skipper, fuel for the planned route, and basic insurance. It does not include food beyond a small snack, premium drinks, or decorations. If you want a full dinner, you need to arrange catering separately. Some catamarans have a galley, but the skipper is not a chef. You can bring your own food, but check if the charter allows it. Some operators provide a cooler with ice, others don't. If you want flowers, balloons, or a cake, order them from a local supplier and bring them on board. The skipper will not organise surprises unless you specifically ask and pay extra. Also, transport to the marina is not included. If you're staying in Marbella town, a taxi to Puerto Banús costs around EUR 15. Parking at the marina is EUR 20-30 for a few hours.

How to Make Your Sunset Trip More Romantic

Book a catamaran with a forward cabin. Some models have a double bed below deck, which gives you privacy if you want to change clothes or just have a quiet moment. Ask the skipper to anchor in a spot with no other boats nearby. The area between Marbella and Cabopino has several secluded coves. Time your departure so you're at the anchor point 30 minutes before sunset. That gives you time to settle, pour a drink, and take photos. If you're proposing, do it during the last 5 minutes of sunset, when the sky is most colourful. The skipper can take the photo for you. Avoid talking too much. Just watch. The silence on the water is the most romantic part.

Frequently Asked Questions About Private Catamaran Sunset Hire

Can I bring my own alcohol? Yes, most charters allow it, but check the listing. Some operators sell their own drinks and don't allow outside alcohol. If you bring glass bottles, be careful on deck. Plastic cups are safer.
Is there a bathroom on board? Yes, all catamarans over 10 metres have a marine toilet in a small cabin below deck. It's basic, but it works.
Can I swim during the sunset trip? Yes, the skipper will anchor in a calm area. Swim before the sun goes down, because the water gets cold quickly after dark. The skipper will provide life jackets if needed.
How many people can fit on a catamaran? Most catamarans on our platform take 6 to 12 guests. The legal limit is set by the boat's safety certificate. You cannot exceed it, even if you think there's space.
Do I need a licence? No, because the skipper is licensed and in charge. You are a passenger. Just enjoy the ride.
Can I bring my dog? Some operators allow small, well-behaved dogs. Ask the owner before booking. The dog must wear a life jacket, and you must clean up after it.
What happens if I'm seasick? Catamarans are very stable, but if you're prone to motion sickness, take a seasickness tablet an hour before departure. Ginger tea or crackers help. The skipper can also anchor in a sheltered spot to minimise movement.

  • Catamaran Charter Marbella Wedding: Vows at Sea

faqs.q: How much does a private catamaran hire Marbella sunset cost?
faqs.a: Prices start from around EUR 400 for a 2-hour trip on a smaller catamaran (10-12 metres) for up to 6 guests. Larger catamarans (15-18 metres) for 10-12 guests start from EUR 800. All prices include the skipper. Extras like catering or open bar are extra.

faqs.q: How long does a sunset catamaran trip last?
faqs.a: Most sunset charters are 2 to 3 hours, departing about an hour before sunset so you can anchor, swim, and watch the sun go down. Some operators offer longer trips if you want dinner on board.

faqs.q: Can we bring our own food and drink?
faqs.a: Yes, most catamarans allow you to bring your own food and drink, but check with the operator. Some have a corkage fee if you bring alcohol. The skipper will provide a cooler and ice.

faqs.q: Do we need to be experienced sailors?
faqs.a: No. The skipper handles everything. You just show up, relax, and enjoy the sunset. No sailing experience needed.

faqs.q: What happens if the weather is bad?
faqs.a: If the weather is unsafe, the operator will cancel and offer a full refund or reschedule. Usually, they decide 24-48 hours before departure. Contact us if you're unsure.

faqs.q: Can we get married on a catamaran at sunset?
faqs.a: Yes, several operators in Marbella offer wedding packages on catamarans. You'll need to book well in advance and arrange the paperwork with the local registry. Contact us for details.

faqs.q: Is it suitable for children or non-swimmers?
faqs.a: Yes, catamarans are stable and safe. Children are welcome, but life jackets are provided. Non-swimmers can stay on deck. The skipper will anchor in shallow water if you want to swim.

title: Motor Yacht Rental Estepona Day: Luxury on Water
metaDescription: Book a motor yacht rental Estepona day trip with a licensed skipper. Depart Puerto Banús, Marbella or Estepona. Compare prices and book online now.
h1: Motor Yacht Rental Estepona Day: Luxury on Water
intro:
You want a day on the Costa del Sol that feels like a private yacht club membership without the annual fees. A motor yacht rental Estepona day gives you exactly that: a sleek, powerful vessel with a skipper who knows the coastline better than your sat nav knows the roundabouts of Marbella. You don’t need a licence, you don’t need to navigate, and you definitely don’t need to clean up afterwards.

This is the shortcut to the Mediterranean life you see in Instagram ads. You step aboard at Puerto Banús or Estepona marina, the engine hums, and within minutes the coastline starts to shrink. The real question is not whether you can afford it (you can, for a day) but where you want the skipper to point the bow.
bodyHtml:
What a Motor Yacht Rental Estepona Day Actually Includes

You get the boat for a full day, typically 10:00 to 18:00 in summer, 10:00 to 17:00 in spring and autumn. The package includes a licensed skipper who handles everything from docking to anchoring. Fuel is usually extra, around EUR 150 to EUR 400 depending on the engine size and how far you go. Some operators include a basic welcome pack (water, soft drinks, ice), but you should confirm before you book.

Most motor yachts in the 30-50 foot range come with a bimini top for shade, a bow sunpad, a small galley, and a cabin with a toilet. You won’t be sleeping aboard on a day rental, but you can use the cabin to change or escape the midday sun. If you want a bigger boat with a flybridge, a crew member, or a jet ski towed behind, those are upgrades that push the price up but also the fun factor.

You do not get a party host, a chef, or a playlist curated by someone under 25. You get a boat, a skipper, and the freedom to do what you want. You bring your own food, your own drinks, and your own music. The skipper will usually have a Bluetooth speaker on board, or you can bring your own.

Departure Ports for Your Motor Yacht Rental Estepona Day

The most common departure ports for a motor yacht rental Estepona day are Estepona Marina (Puerto Deportivo de Estepona) and Puerto Banús. Estepona Marina is quieter, easier to park at, and closer to the town centre. Puerto Banús is busier, flashier, and surrounded by restaurants and shops. Both work well, but the choice affects your cruising route.

From Estepona Marina you can head west towards the Strait of Gibraltar, where the water gets deeper and the dolphin sightings more frequent. Or you can go east towards Marbella and the Marbella Marina, passing the sand dunes of Guadalmina and the golden beach of San Pedro Alcántara. The typical round trip from Estepona to Puerto Banús and back takes about four hours at cruising speed, leaving you plenty of time to anchor and swim.

From Puerto Banús you have the same options but with a longer run west to Estepona and a shorter run east to Marbella. Many charter companies based in Puerto Banús will also pick you up from Marbella Marina or Cabopino if you book in advance. Search all available motor yachts for Estepona day rentals to compare departure ports.

Boat Options for a Motor Yacht Rental Estepona Day

The term motor yacht covers a lot of ground. For a day rental you will typically find three categories: express cruisers (sporty, open, 30-40 feet), flybridge motor yachts (more space, upper deck, 40-60 feet), and luxury motor yachts (60 feet plus, crew included, price on request).

An express cruiser like a Sea Ray 350 Sundancer or a Jeanneau Prestige 36 is perfect for a couple or a small group of four. You get speed (25-30 knots), a shallow draft for anchoring close to shore, and enough space to lounge without feeling cramped. The downside is limited shade and a smaller galley.

A flybridge motor yacht like a Azimut 50 or a Sunseeker Predator 55 gives you a second steering station on the upper deck, more seating, and a better view. These boats handle groups of 8-12 people easily. The flybridge is where everyone ends up anyway, so it makes sense to start there. Fuel costs are higher, typically EUR 300-600 for a full day.

For larger groups or corporate events, a luxury motor yacht with a crew of two or three (skipper, steward, sometimes a chef) can be arranged. Prices start at around EUR 4,000 for the day and go up to EUR 15,000 for a 80-foot vessel with all the extras. These are best booked through BoatHire24 directly so we can match you with the right owner.

Pricing Guidance for Motor Yacht Rental Estepona Day

Prices vary by season, boat size, and duration. For a standard 35-foot express cruiser in June, expect to pay from around EUR 900 for a full day. A 45-foot flybridge motor yacht in August will be from around EUR 1,800. The same boat in November might be EUR 1,200. Always check if the price includes VAT (21% in Spain), fuel, and the skipper's fee. Some companies quote ex-VAT to look cheaper, then add it later.

Deposits are common, usually EUR 500 to EUR 1,000, refundable after the trip if there is no damage. You pay this by credit card or bank transfer before boarding. The deposit is returned within 48 hours after the charter ends, minus any fuel costs if they were not included upfront.

Extra costs to budget for: fuel (EUR 150-600), food and drink (EUR 50-200 depending on how fancy you go), dock fees if you stop at a restaurant with a marina (EUR 20-50), and a tip for the skipper (10-15% of the charter fee is standard). If you want water toys like a paddleboard, snorkel gear, or a jet ski, those are usually extra and cost EUR 50-150 per item per day.

Best Time of Year for a Motor Yacht Rental Estepona Day

The Costa del Sol has a long season. You can comfortably charter a motor yacht from April through October. July and August are peak season: hot (30-35 degrees C), crowded, and expensive. The water is warm (24-26 degrees C) and the sea is usually calm, but the marinas are busy and anchoring spots fill up early.

May, June, and September are the sweet spots. The weather is 25-30 degrees C, the water is pleasant (20-24 degrees C), and the crowds are thinner. You get more choice of boats and sometimes lower prices. October can still be good, especially early in the month, but the water starts to cool and the chance of wind increases.

Winter charters (November to March) are possible but less popular. The sea can be choppy, the air temperature drops to 15-20 degrees C, and many boats are in dry dock for maintenance. If you do find a motor yacht available in winter, the price will be lower, but you need to be flexible with the weather.

What to See on a Motor Yacht Rental Estepona Day

Your skipper will suggest a route based on the weather and your preferences. The most popular option is to head east from Estepona towards Marbella, passing the coast of San Pedro Alcántara and the dunes of Artola. You can anchor near the Cabopino sand dunes, which are protected and quiet, or continue to the Marbella Marina for a lunch stop.

If you go west from Estepona, you enter the waters of the Strait of Gibraltar. This area is famous for dolphin and whale sightings, especially in summer. The water is deeper and the current stronger, so it is less suitable for swimming but excellent for wildlife. You can anchor near the small beach of Cala del Faro, just west of Estepona, which is only accessible by boat.

Another option is to head south towards the African coast. On a clear day you can see the Rif Mountains of Morocco. Some skippers will take you across the strait to Ceuta or Tangier, but that requires a longer day (10-12 hours) and costs more in fuel. It is doable, but you need to clear customs, which adds paperwork. Most day charters stick to the Spanish side.

For a shorter trip, you can stay close to Estepona and anchor off the Estepona town beach, then walk into the old town for lunch. The old town has whitewashed buildings, flower pots, and plenty of seafood restaurants. Your skipper will wait at the marina or a nearby buoy.

How to Book a Motor Yacht Rental Estepona Day

Booking online is straightforward. You can use the BoatHire24 search page to filter by boat type, date, and number of guests. Each listing shows the boat, the skipper, the price, and the cancellation policy. You book and pay a deposit (usually 30-50%) online, and the balance is due on the day of the charter, either in cash or by card.

You will need to provide the names and passport numbers of all passengers at least 48 hours before departure. This is a legal requirement for all charter boats in Spain. The skipper will also do a safety briefing before you leave the dock. Life jackets are on board and must be worn by children under 12 and anyone who cannot swim.

If you have special requests (a specific route, a particular brand of drinks, a birthday cake), tell the charter company at least 24 hours in advance. Most are happy to accommodate, but they need time to prepare. Do not assume they will have champagne on ice unless you ask.

Who Is a Motor Yacht Rental Estepona Day For

This is for groups of friends who want a day of sun, sea, and speed. It is for couples who want a private escape without the crowds of the beach. It is for families with older children (the skipper can take you to shallow coves for swimming). It is for corporate teams who want to impress clients or reward staff.

It is not for people who get seasick easily (the Mediterranean is usually calm, but the Strait can be choppy). It is not for people who want a party boat with a DJ and a bar (hire a catamaran for that). It is not for people on a tight budget (a day on a motor yacht costs more than a day on a catamaran or a sailing yacht, but you get speed and luxury).

If you are unsure which boat type suits you, read our blog post comparing motor yachts, catamarans, and sailing yachts for a full breakdown. The short version: motor yachts are for speed and style, catamarans are for stability and space, sailing yachts are for the experience of sailing.

Safety and Legal Requirements for Motor Yacht Rental Estepona Day

All boats on BoatHire24 are licensed and insured. The skipper holds a valid Spanish or equivalent navigation licence. The boat has a safety certificate, a first aid kit, life jackets, and a VHF radio. You are not required to have any boating experience or a licence. The skipper is responsible for the vessel and the safety of everyone on board.

Alcohol is allowed on board, but the skipper cannot drink. If the skipper suspects anyone is dangerously intoxicated, they can terminate the charter early without a refund. Use common sense: enjoy a few beers or a bottle of wine, but do not get so drunk that you fall overboard.

Swimming is allowed from the boat when anchored. The skipper will choose a safe spot away from boat traffic. Do not swim near the propeller or when the engine is running. If you want to snorkel, bring your own gear or ask the charter company if they provide it.

What to Bring on a Motor Yacht Rental Estepona Day

Bring sunscreen (SPF 50, waterproof), a towel, sunglasses, a hat, and a light jacket or sweater for the evening. The sun on the water is intense, even in spring and autumn. Bring a swimsuit and a change of clothes. Bring your own food and drink unless the charter includes catering. Most boats have a cool box or a small fridge.

Do not bring glass bottles. They break, they cut feet, and they are banned on most charter boats. Use plastic or metal containers. Do not bring large suitcases or hard bags; there is limited storage on a day boat. A soft duffel bag or a beach bag works best.

If you plan to take photos, bring a waterproof case or a GoPro. The spray from the bow can ruin a phone. The skipper will usually take a photo of your group at the helm if you ask nicely.

  • Motor Yacht Charter Estepona Day: Coastal Cruise
