faqs.q: Is there real demand for sailing charter in Marbella?
faqs.a: Yes but it's a different market from motor yachts. Marbella sailing charter renters skew older (40–60), often experienced sailors looking for a slower-paced day. Demand concentrates on light-wind days (Levante 8–15 kn ideal) and weekday mornings when motor-yacht parties have departed. Total Marbella sail-charter market is ~250 charter days/year across our 4–6 active yachts — undersupplied vs demand.

faqs.q: How much will a 12–15m sailing yacht earn?
faqs.a: Day rates: €650–€950 low season, €850–€1,400 August. Realistic season: 18–28 charter days for a well-listed yacht, €14,000–€28,000 gross. Subtract 15–20% commission, skipper days (if not owner-skippered), insurance, marina, antifouling. Net: €9,000–€20,000 to owner. Less than a motor yacht of the same length but with lower wear costs.

faqs.q: Can I bareboat-rent to renters with PER?
faqs.a: Yes for renters with valid PER (Spanish) or RYA Day Skipper / ICC + restricted-to-sail endorsement. We verify the licence and prior sailing experience by phone. Bareboat charters run about 12% of our sail bookings — most renters prefer skippered because they're on holiday and the yacht is unfamiliar.

faqs.q: What about the Mediterranean bareboat charter market?
faqs.a: Mediterranean bareboat is dominated by Greek, Croatian, Turkish bases — longer multi-day flotillas where the renter takes the boat for 7–14 days. Marbella's bareboat market is mostly day charter, not week charter. If your yacht is set up for live-aboard week charter, you'll get more bookings basing it in Palma or Athens. We can advise.

faqs.q: Is the wear lower than on a motor yacht?
faqs.a: Generally yes. Sailing yachts under charter wear out sails (genoa 6–8 yrs, mainsail 8–10 yrs at charter intensity vs 12–15 private), running rigging every 3–5 yrs, antifouling annually. No big-engine maintenance, no fuel-burn-related wear, less interior abuse (sail-charter renters typically don't host party crowds). Hull insurance premium ~10–20% lower than motor yacht of same value.

faqs.q: What about catamarans — separate category?
faqs.a: Yes — see our [list your catamaran](/owners/list-your-catamaran-marbella/) page. Sailing catamarans earn 1.5–2x a monohull of the same length because of the day-charter party group market (10–14 guests on deck vs 6–8 on a monohull).

faqs.q: Best port for a sailing yacht in Marbella?
faqs.a: Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo) is best — quieter, better-suited to sailing yacht handling, easier to bring guests onto the dock. Puerto Banús is congested for sail manoeuvring with motor-yacht traffic. Cabopino and Sotogrande are excellent for cruising departures but lower charter walk-in.

faqs.q: Do I need a captain's licence to skipper my own yacht for paid charter?
faqs.a: Yes. Up to 12m / 30 NM from coast, PER is enough. 12–24m needs Patrón de Yate. Foreign equivalents: RYA Yachtmaster Offshore + ICC are accepted. We verify before listing.

title: Charter Out Your Catamaran in Marbella | BoatHire24
metaDescription: Charter out your catamaran in Marbella. 38–55ft cats earn €6,000–€18,000/month peak — only 1 active charter cat on the Costa del Sol. Undersupplied market.
h1: Charter Out Your Catamaran in Marbella: Owner Listing Guide
intro: The Costa del Sol charter market has exactly one active catamaran — a Lagoon 380 we run out of Marbella Marina. It turns away 15–25 enquiries a season it can't accept on date conflict, and the next-nearest charter cat is in Gibraltar. A second well-listed cat in Marbella would fill its calendar in Year 1. This page covers the economics and the practicalities of chartering out your catamaran through us — what makes the cat market different from monohull sail (covered on the sailboat page), and why undersupply is the dominant feature.
bodyHtml:
Why catamarans outperform monohulls on day charter

Three structural reasons:

  • Deck area. A 42 ft catamaran has 60–80% more usable space than a 42 ft monohull. Trampolines forward, hard-top flybridge, beam saloon, two transom benches. Hosts 10–14 guests comfortably; a monohull of the same length tops out at 6–8.
  • Stability at anchor. Cats don't roll. Lunch at anchor in 1.5 m swell is comfortable. On a monohull, half the guests are queasy by course two.
  • Visual product. Photos of 12 people on the trampoline of a 45 ft cat sell. The same charter on a monohull looks crowded.

Day rates per LOA-foot run 1.5–2× monohull. Marbella 42 ft cat day rate: €1,200–€1,800. 42 ft monohull: €650–€950.

Day rates and season earnings

CatamaranDay rate (low / Aug)Charter days/seasonGross revenue
Lagoon 38, Bali 4.0 (sail)€1,000–€1,50022–32€26,000–€44,000
Lagoon 42, Fountaine Pajot Astréa 42 (sail)€1,200–€1,80028–38€38,000–€68,000
Lagoon 46, FP Saona 47 (sail)€1,500–€2,20028–38€48,000–€85,000
Aquila 36, FP MY40 (power)€1,400–€2,00032–42€48,000–€80,000
Sunreef 50, Lagoon Sixty 7 (luxury)€3,200–€5,50020–32€72,000–€165,000

Net to owner after commission, crew, insurance, marina, maintenance: 55–70% of gross.

The cat-specific costs to budget

  • Berth fee +16–25%. Wider beam = wider slip = higher annual. Build into your day-rate pricing.
  • Antifouling 2 hulls. Almost double a monohull. €1,800–€3,200/yr.
  • Twin engines. Two service intervals, two impellers, two anodes. Roughly 1.6× monohull engine maintenance.
  • Sail wear on a 14m sail cat is higher. Larger square meterage. Mainsail every 7–9 charter seasons (€5,500–€8,000), genoa every 5–7 (€3,500–€5,000).
  • Insurance ~10–15% higher per €100k hull. See the insurance page.

Sailing cat vs power cat — for Marbella

Power cats edge ahead on Marbella day-charter economics. Reasons:

  1. No light-wind down days — the cat can still do the lunch-at-anchor cruise when there's 4 knots of breeze.
  2. Faster to anchor and back, so a 6-hour day fits comfortably (vs feeling rushed on a sail cat in a calm).
  3. Day rate runs slightly higher because the photos look like a 'yacht' rather than a 'sailing boat' — different renter pool.

Sailing cats win when the renter specifically asks for a sailing experience (about 22% of cat enquiries). Both markets exist; if you already own a sailing cat, list it. If you're buying, the power cat edges ahead for Marbella day-charter.

Marbella vs Palma — where to base

The standard catamaran ownership question. Two scenarios:

  • Week-long bareboat dominant. Palma. 18–24 one-week charters at €6,500–€9,500 per week for a 45 ft cat. Higher absolute gross, but boat is gone 5 months and you're chasing it for personal use.
  • Day charter + personal use. Marbella. Higher day rates than Palma (€1,500 vs €1,150), longer effective day-charter season (16 vs 14 weeks), boat available evenings and weekdays for owner use. Lower gross but higher net-of-personal-utility.

Our owner data: 45 ft sail cat in Marbella netted €36,500 in 2025 on 32 charter days, plus the owner used the boat 11 weeks personally. Same hull basing in Palma would have grossed ~€72,000 on 18 week-charters but with virtually zero owner use.

How to list your catamaran with us

  1. WhatsApp +358 400 406 194 with: make/model, year, hull length, beam, current sails/instruments inventory, lista status.
  2. Marina walkthrough — 45–60 minutes on a 38–45 ft cat. Drone footage included.
  3. Pricing meeting: review your rates if any, benchmark, set day rate + 4-hour sunset + multi-day tiers.
  4. Listing live in 5–10 days. Drone, sailing/cruising video, EN + ES copy.

Demand is the easy part — supply is the constraint. The first owner to list a 42–46 ft cat with us in 2026 will run at peak utilisation by mid-July.

Related: more for catamaran owners

  • Main owner-listing page
  • Monohull sailing yachts
  • Motor yachts 14m+
  • Charter insurance
  • Business setup
  • Do you need licence to rent boat spain
  • Marbella boat licence guide
  • Marbella boat licence types

faqs.q: Is the Marbella catamaran market really that undersupplied?
faqs.a: Yes. We've been the only operator running a charter catamaran (Lagoon 380) on the Costa del Sol consistently since 2022. The yacht runs 28–34 charter days a season and turns away 15–25 enquiries it can't accommodate. A second well-listed 40–45 ft cat would fill its calendar in Year 1.

faqs.q: How much does a 40–45 ft catamaran earn in Marbella?
faqs.a: Day rates: €1,200–€1,800 low season, €1,500–€2,400 August. Realistic 28–38 charter days/season at the higher end of demand. Gross €38,000–€72,000. Net to owner after 15–20% commission, skipper days, insurance, marina, cleaning: €25,000–€48,000. A 50–55 ft cat clears closer to €60,000–€90,000 net.

faqs.q: Sailing cat or power cat — which earns more?
faqs.a: Power cats earn ~20% more on day charter (slightly higher day rate, no light-wind down days, faster to anchor and back). Sailing cats earn more on multi-day (clientele who specifically want a sailing experience). Marbella is 92% day charter, so power cat economics edge ahead. Both viable.

faqs.q: Why do cats earn more than monohulls of the same length?
faqs.a: Geometry. A 42 ft cat has 60–80% more usable deck area than a 42 ft monohull, hosts 10–14 guests comfortably vs 6–8 on a mono, and the trampolines + flybridge are core to the party-group product. Day rate scales with usable group space, not LOA. Same crew cost on a cat as a mono — better unit economics.

faqs.q: Berth fees?
faqs.a: Wider beam = wider berth = higher fee. A 42 ft / 24 ft beam cat needs a 50 ft commercial berth at most Marbella marinas, paying 16–25% above a 42 ft monohull. Annual commercial berth at Marbella Marina: €20,000–€28,000 for a 42 ft cat vs €16,000–€22,000 for the monohull. Puerto Banús: €24,000–€36,000. Build it into pricing.

faqs.q: Captain — same as a monohull?
faqs.a: Same licence requirements (PER up to 12 m / 30 NM; Patrón de Yate above). Cats handle differently — twin engines, no keel, lots of windage. Owners new to cats should run 2–3 supervised charter days with our captain before bareboat-skippering paid charters.

faqs.q: Insurance load — higher than a monohull?
faqs.a: Marginally — ~10–15% higher premium per €100k hull value because of wider beam, twin-engine maintenance, and replacement-cost dynamics in a Med fire/sinking scenario. Charter cover for a 42 ft cat with €450k hull: €3,200–€4,800/yr in 2026. See our [insurance page](/owners/boat-rental-insurance-spain/) for context.

faqs.q: Should I base the cat in Marbella or Palma?
faqs.a: If you want week-long bareboat charter income, Palma earns more — €6,500–€9,500/wk for a 45 ft cat, 18–24 week charters. If you want day-charter and own the boat for personal use too, Marbella day charter is competitive on net economics because day rates are higher, the season is longer (16 weeks meaningful vs Palma's 14), and Marbella berth costs less.

title: Boat Rental Málaga 2026: Yacht Charter from Málaga Port | BoatHire24
metaDescription: Boat rental Málaga from €450. Yacht charters from Málaga Port — skipper, fuel, drinks included. 40-minute hop from Marbella fleet. WhatsApp booking.
h1: Boat Rental Málaga 2026: Yacht Charter from Málaga Port
intro: Málaga sits 40 nautical miles east of Marbella along the Costa del Sol — a 1h15 cruise on our 12.5 m motor yachts, or a 35-minute drive by road. Boat rental in Málaga works two ways through us: direct charters with Málaga-based yachts berthed at Muelle Uno, or repositioning our Marbella fleet (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39, Mangusta 80, Lagoon 380) to Málaga for the day. This page covers both, plus the three best day-charter routes from Málaga port.
bodyHtml:
Where charters depart from in Málaga

Muelle Uno (officially Marina del Puerto de Málaga) is the main charter departure. Built on the old fishing dock and pedestrianised in 2013, it runs along the south side of the harbour from the Pompidou Centre west to the lighthouse. Practical bits:

  • 5-minute walk from Calle Larios (Old Town) and Atarazanas market.
  • Public car park underneath the marina (€2.50/hr, 24/7 access).
  • Taxi rank at the marina gate; Cabify and Uber work in central Málaga.
  • Train station (Málaga María Zambrano) 1.5 km — €4 in a taxi, 18 minutes on foot.
  • Charter desks open from 9am; check-in for a 10am charter is 9:45.

Some larger yachts dock at Muelle 2 (the commercial / superyacht slips) — your booking confirmation will tell you which slip.

Pricing — Málaga vs Marbella

DurationMarbella baseMálaga direct fleetMarbella yacht repositioned to Málaga
2-hour sunset€749€450–€650€749 + €280 reposition = €1,029
4-hour half day€1,499€800–€1,200€1,499 + €380 = €1,879
8-hour full day€2,299€1,400–€1,800€2,299 + €450 = €2,749
License-free 5 m RIB (Marbella only)€220–€320—n/a (not viable to reposition)

If you want the Mangusta 80 (only 24 m sport yacht in Marbella's charter fleet) or the Lagoon 380 catamaran, repositioning makes sense — there's no equivalent yacht based in Málaga. For a generic 10–12 m motor yacht charter, the Málaga-direct fleet is cheaper and faster.

Three day-charter routes from Málaga port

East: Málaga → Nerja (Maro caves)

Best full-day route. 25 NM each way along the Axarquía coast. Pass Rincón de la Victoria, Vélez-Málaga, then the dramatic Maro cliffs and the Cueva del Tesoro caves. Swim stop at Cala del Cañuelo. Lunch at anchor off Nerja or stop at Marinas de Nerja. 6.5 hours afloat, back at Málaga by 5pm.

West: Málaga → Marbella (Puerto Banús)

40 NM each way, full day. Pass Torremolinos, Benalmádena, Fuengirola, Calahonda. Lunch in Puerto Banús — moor at the visitor dock, walk to Sinatra Bar or Astral Beach Club. Return mid-afternoon. Best for groups who want the 'see the Costa del Sol' day rather than a swim-heavy itinerary.

Bay loop (4 hours)

Half-day charter. Out to the Málaga lighthouse, west along Torremolinos coast, anchor at La Carihuela for a swim, return via the Pompidou Centre seafront. Comfortable for groups with children or non-sailors who don't want a long day on the water.

What's included on a Málaga charter

  • Licensed skipper (Patrón de Yate / Capitán de Yate).
  • Fuel within agreed cruising area.
  • Drinks: beer, wine, soft drinks, water, ice.
  • Snacks: chips, olives, charcuterie board.
  • Towels, snorkels (where appropriate), Bluetooth music.
  • Despacho de salida (sailing permit) filed with Málaga port authority.

Optional add-ons: catered lunch (€35–€55/pp), jet ski tow (€280 for 4h), paddle boards (€40/board/day), professional photographer (€450 half-day).

Booking and cancellation

WhatsApp +358 400 406 194 with date, group size, and which Málaga departure (direct fleet or repositioned Marbella yacht). We confirm within 60 minutes and send a booking link. 30% deposit secures the date; balance due 7 days before charter. Cancellation: full refund if 14+ days out, 50% if 7–14 days, no refund inside 7 days — though we'll reschedule on date changes where the yacht is otherwise unbooked.

Weather: skipper decision is final. If we cancel for weather, you get full refund or a free reschedule. If you cancel for weather inside 7 days, normal cancellation policy applies.

Related pages

  • Main Marbella boat rental hub — full fleet, all departure ports.
  • Yacht charter Marbella — Marbella-specific yacht options.
  • Boat rental Puerto Banús — Marbella's main marina.
  • Boat rental Estepona — the other Costa del Sol departure.

Related: more around the Costa del Sol

  • Boat rental Marbella
  • Boat rental Estepona
  • Boat rental Sotogrande
  • Puerto Banús yacht charter
  • Own a boat? List it on BoatHire24
  • Best time to charter boat marbella
  • What to pack boat day costa del sol
  • Dolphin watching boat trips marbella

faqs.q: Can I charter the same yacht in Málaga that you list in Marbella?
faqs.a: Yes — our 12.5 m Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 reposition to Málaga's Muelle Uno on 24-hour notice. Repositioning fee €280–€450 covers fuel + skipper time for the 40 NM each-way hop. Worth it for a half-day or full-day charter; not worth it for a 2-hour sunset cruise.

faqs.q: Where do charters depart from in Málaga?
faqs.a: Muelle Uno (Marina del Puerto de Málaga) — the urban marina next to the Pompidou Centre and Plaza de la Marina. 5-minute walk from the Old Town and Atarazanas market. Parking is at the public car park under the marina (€2.50/hr). Some larger yachts dock at Muelle 2; we direct you to the right slip 24 hours ahead.

faqs.q: How much for a day charter from Málaga?
faqs.a: Charter rates the same as Marbella: €749 for a 2h sunset cruise, €1,499 for a 4h half-day, €2,299 for an 8h full day on our 12.5 m motor yachts. Add €280–€450 repositioning if we bring the yacht from Marbella. Direct-from-Málaga operators we work with charge €450–€1,800 with no repositioning fee.

faqs.q: What can you see in a day from Málaga?
faqs.a: Three good routes. (1) East to Nerja: 25 NM each way, full day — Frigiliana cliffs, Maro caves, swim stop. (2) West to Marbella: 40 NM each way, full day — La Concha mountain backdrop, lunch in Puerto Banús. (3) Bay loop: 4 hours, Málaga lighthouse, Torremolinos coast, return via Muelle Uno. Pick when you book.

faqs.q: What's included?
faqs.a: Same inclusions as our Marbella charters: licensed skipper, fuel (within agreed area), drinks (beer, wine, soft drinks, water, ice), snacks, towels, music system. Extra catering, jet ski tow, paddle boards — add-on at booking.

faqs.q: Can I take the boat without a skipper?
faqs.a: Bareboat charter from Málaga is possible on smaller boats (license-free 5 m / 15 hp) and on yachts up to 12 m if you hold PER + sailing endorsement. For our 12 m+ motor yachts we provide the skipper. Booking confirms based on licence verification.

faqs.q: What's the booking process?
faqs.a: WhatsApp +358 400 406 194 with date, group size, departure preference (Marbella or Málaga). We confirm yacht availability and pricing within 60 minutes. 30% deposit holds the date; balance 7 days before charter. Cancellation policy: full refund if 14+ days out, 50% if 7–14, no refund inside 7.

faqs.q: Does Málaga have its own dedicated charter fleet, or am I getting a Marbella yacht?
faqs.a: Both. Málaga has 4–6 yachts based at Muelle Uno (medium-sized motor yachts and one Beneteau sailboat). For larger or specific yachts (Mangusta 80, Lagoon 380 catamaran, license-free boats), we reposition from Marbella. We tell you upfront which yacht and which marina the booking applies to.

title: Boat Rental Sotogrande — Charter from Marina | BoatHire24
metaDescription: Boat rental Sotogrande from €450. Charter from Puerto de Sotogrande — superyacht marina, polo set, quieter than Banús. Gibraltar 12 NM, Tarifa 28 NM.
h1: Boat Rental Sotogrande 2026: Charter from Puerto de Sotogrande
intro: Puerto de Sotogrande is the Costa del Sol's most exclusive marina — home to 600 berths, 30+ superyachts in season, polo grounds, La Reserva golf, and a clientele that skews wealthier and quieter than Puerto Banús. Boat rental from Sotogrande works for groups already staying in the area (Hotel Almenara, NH Sotogrande, La Reserva villas), for Gibraltar and Tarifa day-trip charters where Sotogrande is the natural departure, and for the quieter Marbella-coast experience away from Banús crowds.
bodyHtml:
Where Sotogrande sits — and why it matters for charter

Sotogrande is 28 NM west of Marbella, 12 NM east of Gibraltar, and 28 NM east of Tarifa. That puts it at the western edge of the Costa del Sol and at the eastern mouth of the Strait of Gibraltar. Practical consequences:

  • Gibraltar day-charter is faster from Sotogrande than from anywhere else on the Costa — 40 minutes each way.
  • Tarifa and the whale-watching routes are accessible without crossing the entire Costa del Sol coastline.
  • The marina itself is the largest superyacht facility in southern Spain — the 30 m+ yachts you'll see in August aren't tourists; they're Sotogrande residents.

The Sotogrande charter fleet

Smaller and more upmarket than Marbella:

  • 4–6 active 14–22 m motor yachts — Sunseeker Manhattan, Princess V58, Azimut 60.
  • One Jeanneau 44 sailing yacht — based at Marina Sotogrande, occasional charters.
  • Superyacht 30 m+ charters — usually privately managed; we can broker access on request.
  • No license-free RIB fleet at Sotogrande — closest is Estepona.
  • No beach-based jet ski operator at Playa de Sotogrande in 2026.

For specific Marbella-based yachts (Mangusta 80, Lagoon 380 catamaran, Astondoa 40, license-free boats), we reposition from Marbella. Repositioning fee €350–€500 covers fuel + skipper time for the 28 NM each-way hop.

Three routes from Sotogrande

Gibraltar (12 NM — full or half day)

The flagship Sotogrande day charter. 40 minutes each way. Pass La Línea, around Europa Point with the Rock looming, into British waters at Gibraltar. Moor at Marina Bay or Ocean Village for 2–3 hours ashore — lunch at The Tipsy Lounge or The Rock Hotel, walk up to Apes' Den, duty-free shopping. Return via Catalan Bay swim stop. 6.5 hours comfortable. See our full Gibraltar day-trip guide.

Tarifa and the Strait (28 NM — full day)

The most spectacular Costa route. 1h15 each way through the Strait of Gibraltar shipping lanes. Whale-watching mid-route — pilot whales, common dolphins, occasionally orcas (peak summer). Land at Tarifa marina for lunch ashore or anchor off Playa de los Lances. Return mid-afternoon before the Levante wind picks up. 9 hours afloat. Weather-dependent — we don't run if Levante exceeds 22 knots.

Estepona / Marbella coast (10 NM east, half day)

Reverse direction — east along the Costa to Estepona for lunch ashore, or continue to Puerto Banús (28 NM). Calm, scenic, less weather-exposed than the western routes. Good half-day option for groups not wanting to commit to a Gibraltar full day.

Pricing

DurationSotogrande-based fleetMarbella yacht repositioned
2-hour sunset€600–€850€749 + €350 = €1,099
4-hour half day€900–€1,500€1,499 + €420 = €1,919
8-hour full day (Gibraltar / Tarifa)€1,800–€2,800€2,299 + €500 = €2,799
Superyacht charter (30 m+, full day)€8,500–€18,000broker-arranged

All charters include licensed skipper, fuel within agreed area, drinks, snacks, towels.

Practical: marina access, parking, polo weeks

  • Marina access: Puerto de Sotogrande gate code or hotel pass required for some inner docks. We send the code 24 hours before.
  • Parking: free parking at the marina north car park; paid car park (€2/hr) closer to the inner dock. Either works.
  • Polo high season (mid-July to early September): marina is busy, charter availability tightens, book 2–3 weeks ahead for weekends.
  • Pre-charter lunch: Trocadero on the marina, Ke for Spanish small plates, Mara for seafood.
  • Post-charter: the Octagon for cocktails, or drive 10 min to La Reserva for sunset at the beach club.

Booking and weather

WhatsApp +358 400 406 194 with date, group size, route preference (Gibraltar, Tarifa, coastal). Confirm within 60 minutes. 30% deposit holds the date; balance 7 days out. Cancellation: full refund 14+ days, 50% 7–14 days, no refund inside 7. Weather-driven cancellations by skipper get full refund or free reschedule — especially relevant for Tarifa charters where Levante wind decides go/no-go.

Related

  • Main Marbella boat rental hub
  • Boat rental Estepona — the other western departure.
  • Tarifa day trip by boat
  • Gibraltar day trip — full route guide

Related: more around the western Costa del Sol

  • Boat rental Marbella
  • Boat rental Estepona
  • Boat rental Málaga
  • Bareboat charter
  • Own a boat? List it
  • Best time to charter boat marbella
  • What to pack boat day costa del sol
  • Dolphin watching boat trips marbella

faqs.q: Why charter from Sotogrande?
faqs.a: If you're already staying at the Hotel Almenara, NH Sotogrande, or one of the La Reserva villas, you avoid the 30-minute drive to Marbella. Sotogrande is also the closest serious marina to Gibraltar (12 NM, ~40 min) and Tarifa (28 NM, ~1h15) — both popular day-trip destinations harder to reach from Marbella.

faqs.q: What's the Puerto de Sotogrande charter fleet like?
faqs.a: Smaller and more upmarket than Banús. Roughly 4–6 active charter yachts based here — mostly 14–22 m motor yachts, one Jeanneau sailing yacht, occasional superyacht charters in the 30 m+ category. No license-free RIB fleet (closest is Estepona). For specific yachts or smaller boats we reposition from Marbella.

faqs.q: How long to Gibraltar from Sotogrande?
faqs.a: 12 NM direct — 40 minutes at 18 knots. Easiest Gibraltar day trip on the Costa. Comfortable full-day: 40 min out, 3 hours moored at Marina Bay or Ocean Village, 40 min back, plus a swim stop at Catalan Bay. Returns by 5pm. See our [Gibraltar by boat guide](/blog/gibraltar-day-trip-by-boat/) for the route detail.

faqs.q: Tarifa day trip — viable from Sotogrande?
faqs.a: Yes. 28 NM west — 1h15 at cruising speed. Pass Punta Carnero, the Strait of Gibraltar shipping lanes, Tarifa lighthouse. Lunch at anchor or moor at Tarifa marina (small, no superyacht space). Whale-watching mid-route if conditions allow. Full-day charter — 9 hours afloat — but probably the most spectacular Costa route. See our [Tarifa day trip page](/experiences/tarifa-day-trip-marbella/).

faqs.q: How much for a half-day charter from Sotogrande?
faqs.a: €900–€1,500 on a 14 m yacht. €1,400–€2,400 on a 16–18 m. Slightly higher than Marbella because Sotogrande berth costs are at the top of the Costa scale, but inclusions (skipper, drinks, snacks, fuel) the same.

faqs.q: Polo season — busier weeks?
faqs.a: Yes. Sotogrande polo high season runs July to early September with the International Tournament. Marina berths book up; some charter availability tightens. Worth booking 2–3 weeks ahead for any weekend charter in late July or August.

faqs.q: What about Tarifa wind?
faqs.a: Tarifa's famous Levante (east wind) can blow 25–35 knots even when Marbella is calm. We don't run Tarifa day-trip charters when forecast Levante exceeds 22 knots — uncomfortable and bordering unsafe in the strait. Skipper has final call; we reschedule with full refund.

faqs.q: Is there walk-in beach activity at Sotogrande?
faqs.a: Limited. Sotogrande's beach is quieter than Marbella's — fewer beach clubs, no jet-ski rental fleet, no beach-side cafés on Playa de Sotogrande. Walk-in charter demand is low; most Sotogrande bookings are pre-arranged with hotel guests or villa renters.

title: Mediterranean Bareboat Charter from Marbella | BoatHire24
metaDescription: Mediterranean bareboat charter from Marbella. Self-skipper yachts 10–15m if you hold PER or RYA Day Skipper. Day, multi-day to Gibraltar, Sotogrande, Tarifa.
h1: Mediterranean Bareboat Charter from Marbella: Self-Skippered Yachts
intro: Bareboat charter — taking the yacht out under your own command — is a small but real slice of the Marbella charter market. About 12% of our bookings go bareboat, almost all to renters with prior Greek, Croatian or Caribbean charter experience who want the same on the Costa del Sol. This page covers what bareboat looks like specifically from Marbella, the licence requirements, the routes available, and why Mediterranean bareboat charter from Marbella is a day-and-short-multi-day product rather than the 7–14 day flotilla model.
bodyHtml:
Why most 'Mediterranean bareboat' isn't from Marbella

When people search 'Mediterranean bareboat charter', they're usually picturing the Palma–Greece–Croatia–Turkey model: fly in, pick up a 12–15 m yacht for 7–14 days, sleep aboard, anchor in a different cove each night, return to base. That market is built on island chains and bays — the Balearics, the Cyclades, Dalmatia, the Aegean Turquoise Coast.

Marbella's coast is a single 90-mile line of beach with no island chain. There's nowhere to overnight at anchor in a sheltered cove that isn't a 50+ NM trip. The genuine week-charter Med bareboat is not Marbella's product — basing in Palma will serve that customer far better.

What Marbella offers instead: bareboat day charter (10am pick-up, 6pm return), and short multi-day to Gibraltar (24–48h), Sotogrande (24h), or Tarifa (48–72h whale-watching) for the brave / experienced.

What you can bareboat — Marbella fleet

YachtDay rate (bareboat)Licence requiredDeposit
5 m / 15 hp RIB (license-free)€220–€320None (over 18, briefing)€500
7–8 m motor boat€450–€650PER or ICC + powerboat€1,000
10 m motor yacht€650–€950PER or ICC + powerboat€1,500
12 m motor yacht€900–€1,400PER or RYA Day Skipper€2,500
10–12 m sailing yacht€550–€850PER + sail or RYA Day Skipper€2,000
14–15 m sailing yacht€800–€1,300Patrón de Yate or RYA Yachtmaster Coastal€3,500

Above 15 m, Marbella charter is almost entirely skippered — the licence requirements and insurance premiums make bareboat unviable.

Licences we accept

  • Spanish PER — Patrón de Embarcaciones de Recreo, the standard Spanish recreational skipper licence. Valid for vessels up to 12 m / 30 NM from coast.
  • RYA Day Skipper + ICC — UK / international, most common for British renters. Sail or motor endorsement determines what you can charter.
  • RYA Yachtmaster Coastal / Offshore — extends to 14 m+ vessels.
  • ASA 104 Bareboat Skipper / US Sailing Bareboat — accepted with prior Med charter experience.
  • German SBF-See, French Permis Côtier / Hauturier — both accepted with ICC top-up.

Verification: photo of front + back of licence, 10-minute video call confirming prior charter history (where, when, what yacht). For RYA / ASA we cross-check certificate numbers against the awarding body's register.

Three bareboat routes from Marbella

Marbella coast day-charter (Puerto Banús to Cabopino)

The standard. 10am pick-up at Puerto Banús or Marbella Marina, head east along the coast to Funny Beach for swim stop, anchor off Calahonda for lunch, return by 6pm. 12–16 NM total, well within sight of land. Best first bareboat for someone unfamiliar with Marbella waters.

Marbella → Sotogrande and back (28 NM each way, 1-day or overnight)

Head west past Estepona, into Sotogrande Bay. Lunch ashore at the marina or at anchor off the inner beach. Return same evening (long day) or overnight at Marina de Sotogrande (€80–€140 visitor berth) and return next morning. Despacho fine within Spanish waters.

Marbella → Gibraltar → Marbella (33 NM each way, 48 hours)

The flagship multi-day bareboat. Requires international despacho (we file). Day 1: Marbella to Gibraltar, lunch ashore, overnight at Marina Bay or Ocean Village (~€85/night for a 12 m yacht). Day 2: morning at Catalan Bay, return Marbella. Need clear weather forecast both directions — Levante wind closes the route. Best May, June, September.

Deposits, insurance, breakage

Pre-authorised on a card at handover, after licence verification. Released within 48 hours of return if no damage. Damage deductible €600–€1,500 depending on yacht — first €600 of any damage claim comes off your deposit, anything above goes to insurance. We carry full charter cover on every bareboat-eligible yacht (see insurance page for the policy structure if you're curious as an owner).

Tow-back if you ground the yacht: insurance covers, but expect your deposit to be deducted in full while assessment happens. Engine failure that's not your fault: covered by us, no deposit hit.

Booking

WhatsApp +358 400 406 194 with: licence type + number + photo, prior charter history (where + when + boats), date, group size, route preference. Verification within 24 hours. 30% deposit holds the booking; balance + damage deposit on handover. Cancellation: full refund 14+ days, 50% 7–14 days, no refund inside 7.

Related

  • Main Marbella boat rental hub
  • Boat rental no license — license-free 5 m / 15 hp option.
  • Yacht charter Marbella — skippered alternative.
  • Gibraltar day trip — full route guide
  • For owners: list your sailboat

Related: more around the Mediterranean

  • Boat rental Marbella
  • Sotogrande charters
  • Estepona charters
  • Own a sailing yacht? List it
  • Own a catamaran? List it
  • Best time to charter boat marbella
  • What to pack boat day costa del sol
  • Dolphin watching boat trips marbella

faqs.q: What's bareboat charter and how is it different from skippered?
faqs.a: Bareboat = you skipper the yacht yourself. Skippered = our captain drives. Bareboat costs less because you don't pay a captain (€180–€260/day saved), but you need a valid recreational skipper licence and prior charter experience. About 12% of Marbella charters are bareboat; most renters opt for skippered because they're on holiday.

faqs.q: What licence do I need?
faqs.a: For Marbella bareboat charter: Spanish PER (Patrón de Embarcaciones de Recreo) up to 12 m / 30 NM from coast, OR equivalent foreign certificate. We accept: RYA Day Skipper + ICC (most common for UK renters), Bareboat Skipper from ASA / US Sailing, German SBF-See, French Permis Côtier. Above 12 m: Patrón de Yate or RYA Yachtmaster Coastal.

faqs.q: Are there license-free options?
faqs.a: Yes — Spanish law allows boats up to 5 m / 15 hp / 2 NM from coast with no licence. See our [boat rental no license Marbella](/boat-rental-no-license-marbella/) page. Above that you need the certifications listed above.

faqs.q: Can I do a multi-day bareboat?
faqs.a: Yes, on the larger yachts (12 m+). Common multi-day routes from Marbella: Marbella–Gibraltar–Marbella (48 hours), Marbella–Sotogrande–Estepona round trip (3 days), or Marbella–Tarifa–Marbella (3 days with whale-watching). Multi-day rates €1,400–€2,800 for a 12 m yacht for a weekend, including 24-hour kit and despacho for international waters.

faqs.q: Why isn't Marbella the typical Mediterranean bareboat week-charter market?
faqs.a: Geography. Real Med bareboat is a 7–14 day flotilla market based in Palma, Athens, Trogir, Marmaris — renters fly in, take a 14 m yacht for a fortnight, sleep aboard, do a 250–400 NM route through islands. Marbella's coast is a single line with no island chain, so the week-charter product doesn't exist. Marbella is day-charter or short multi-day.

faqs.q: What deposit do I pay?
faqs.a: €1,500 on a 10 m yacht, €2,500 on a 12 m, €3,500 on a 15 m+. Pre-authorised on a card after licence verification. Released within 48 hours of return if no damage. Damage deductible €600–€1,500 depending on yacht.

faqs.q: How do you verify my licence?
faqs.a: Photo of front + back of your licence card, plus a 10-minute video call to confirm prior charter experience (where, when, what yacht). For RYA / ASA we cross-check the certificate number. Spanish PER is verified through the official Capitanía registry.

faqs.q: What if I'm rusty — can I do skippered with handover?
faqs.a: Yes. Many renters book skippered for day one, take the helm under captain supervision on day two, then bareboat from day three. Day-rate uplift only for the days with captain aboard. Works especially well for sailors who've chartered in Greece but never on the Costa del Sol.

title: Luxury Motor Yacht Hire Puerto Banus Day: Premium
metaDescription: Book luxury motor yacht hire Puerto Banus day trips with a skipper. See the Costa del Sol from the sea. Compare yachts, check prices, and reserve online.
h1: Luxury Motor Yacht Hire Puerto Banus Day
keyword: luxury motor yacht hire Puerto Banus day
intro:
You want a day on the water in Puerto Banus, and you want it done properly. A luxury motor yacht hire Puerto Banus day is the straightforward answer: a private, crewed motor yacht for the day, leaving from the glitziest marina on the Costa del Sol. No queues, no crowds, just you, your group, and a skipper who knows where the dolphins hang out.

This page tells you what to expect, what it costs, which boats are available, and how to book. We keep it direct because you have better things to do than read fluff.
bodyHtml:
What a Luxury Motor Yacht Hire Puerto Banus Day Includes

Every charter on BoatHire24 comes with a licensed skipper who handles the navigation, safety, and local knowledge. You do not need a licence. The yacht is yours for the agreed hours, typically 9am to 6pm or 10am to 7pm, but half-day options exist (4-5 hours). Fuel is included in the base price on most boats, but check the listing details because some charge extra for long distances. A basic welcome pack (water, soft drinks, ice) is standard. You can add catering, from a simple tapas platter to a full sit-down meal with champagne. Snorkelling gear, paddleboards, and floating mats are often available on request.

Puerto Banus: The Starting Point

Puerto Banus is the most famous marina in Marbella, stuffed with superyachts, designer shops, and restaurants that charge EUR 50 for a salad. Your luxury motor yacht hire Puerto Banus day begins at the main dock, usually near the Casino or the port authority office. The marina is easy to reach by taxi from Marbella (15 minutes, around EUR 20) or Malaga airport (45 minutes, around EUR 60). Parking is a nightmare, so get dropped off. Once you are aboard, the skipper will do a safety briefing, then you motor past the mega-yachts and out into the Mediterranean.

Boat Types for a Day Charter

The fleet for luxury motor yacht hire Puerto Banus day ranges from 40-foot Sunseekers to 80-foot Azimuts. You will find flybridge models with sun pads and a bimini top, open sport yachts for a faster ride, and enclosed salon boats for cooler days. Typical brands: Princess, Sunseeker, Azimut, Ferretti, Benetti. Most sleep 6-12 guests for a day trip, but the legal passenger limit is always displayed on the listing. If you want a hot tub, a swim platform with a shower, or a sound system, filter for those amenities on the search page.

Popular Day Routes from Puerto Banus

Your skipper will suggest a route based on sea conditions and your preferences. The classic run is west to the Cala del Faro and the dunes of Cabopino, about 10 NM from Puerto Banus. That takes 30 minutes at cruising speed. You anchor for a swim, lunch, and maybe a paddleboard session. Alternatively, head east to the Marbella coastline and the cliffs of the Sierra Blanca, with views of La Concha mountain. A longer trip goes to Estepona (15 NM), where you can dock for lunch in the old town. If you are lucky, you will see bottlenose dolphins off the Punta de la Mona headland. The Strait of Gibraltar is too far for a day trip from Puerto Banus (50+ NM), but you can book a dedicated dolphin-watching charter from Sotogrande if that is your priority.

Pricing Guidance for a Day Charter

Prices for luxury motor yacht hire Puerto Banus day start from around EUR 2,500 for a 40-foot motor yacht on a half-day basis in low season (November to February). A full day on a 50-foot flybridge in high season (June to September) runs from EUR 4,500 to EUR 7,000. Larger yachts (60-80 feet) cost EUR 8,000 to EUR 15,000 per day. Superyachts over 24 metres are price on request, typically EUR 20,000+. These prices include skipper, fuel, and basic amenities. Catering is extra: EUR 50-100 per person for a decent lunch. Security deposit (refundable) is usually EUR 500-2,000, depending on the yacht value. You can pay by bank transfer or credit card, and most operators require a 50% deposit at booking.

Best Season for a Luxury Motor Yacht Day

The Costa del Sol has over 300 sunny days a year, so you can charter almost any month. The peak season is July and August, when temperatures hit 30-35 degrees C and the sea is flat calm. That is also when prices are highest and availability lowest, so book at least two weeks ahead. May, June, September, and October are excellent: 25-30 degrees C, fewer crowds, and lower rates. April and November are marginal; you might get a good day, but expect cooler winds. December to March is quiet, but you can still get a day on the water if you are okay with 15-18 degrees C and possible rain. The water temperature in summer is 22-26 degrees C, so swimming is comfortable from June to October.

How to Book a Luxury Motor Yacht Hire Puerto Banus Day

Go to the search page on BoatHire24. Set the location to Puerto Banus, the date, and the number of guests. Filter by motor yacht and, if you want, by length or amenities. Each listing shows the price, guest capacity, and what is included. Click on a yacht to see photos, a full description, and the skipper's profile. Request a booking or send a message if you have questions. The operator will confirm availability and send a contract. Payment is handled securely through the platform. After the trip, leave a review to help other renters.

Who This Charter Is For

A luxury motor yacht hire Puerto Banus day works for couples, families, or groups of friends celebrating a birthday, anniversary, or corporate event. It is not a party boat, though you can bring your own music and drinks. If you want a raucous booze cruise, look at the RIBs or small speedboats. If you want absolute privacy and a professional crew, this is it. The skipper stays on board but stays out of your way. You can also book a photographer or a masseuse for an extra fee. Some yachts are available for wedding ceremonies or proposals, but confirm that with the operator in advance.

What to Bring on the Day

Bring swimwear, a towel, sunscreen (factor 50, the sun is strong), sunglasses, a hat, and a light jacket for the breeze. Shoes with non-marking soles are mandatory on most yachts. Leave high heels at home. A waterproof bag for your phone is smart. Cash for tips (10% of the charter fee is standard for the crew) and for any extras you buy at a beach club. You can bring your own food and drink, but check with the skipper first; some yachts have restrictions on red wine (stains) or glass bottles. The yacht will have a fridge and ice, so you can keep things cold.

Local Knowledge: Puerto Banus and Beyond

Puerto Banus was built in 1970 around a fishing port and is now the playground of the rich and famous. The marina has 915 berths, and the superyacht basin can hold vessels up to 50 metres. The port is named after its developer, Jose Banus, not the fish. Nearby, the Rio Verde is a river that runs through the golf valley of Nueva Andalucia. If you anchor off the coast, you will see the Marbella Club Hotel and the Golden Mile. The Costa del Sol blog has more details on activities and restaurants. For a longer trip, the Nerja caves are a 1.5-hour drive east, but you can also see them from the sea on a dedicated tour from Marbella.

Safety and Insurance

All yachts on BoatHire24 have valid insurance and safety equipment (life jackets, fire extinguishers, first aid kit). The skipper holds a Professional Skipper Licence (RYA or equivalent) and a radio operator certificate. The yacht is inspected before each charter. In case of bad weather, the operator will reschedule or refund. You should have your own travel insurance that covers water activities. The platform does not provide insurance.

Alternatives to a Motor Yacht

If you want a more relaxed day, consider a catamaran from Puerto Banus. Catamarans are more stable, have more deck space, and are better for groups with children. If you want speed, a RIB or speedboat is cheaper and faster (up to 50 knots). If you want to sail, a sailing yacht gives you the thrill of wind power, but it is slower and less spacious. Fishing enthusiasts can hire a fishing boat with tackle and a guide. For a licence-free option, there are small day boats (under 6 metres) that you can drive yourself without a licence, but those are not luxury motor yachts. Compare all options on the BoatHire24 homepage.

Final Word on Luxury Motor Yacht Hire Puerto Banus Day

A day on a luxury motor yacht from Puerto Banus is about enjoying the coast in comfort. The skipper does the work, you do the relaxing. Prices are clear, boats are real, and the sea is right there. Check the listings, pick your yacht, and go.

Why Puerto Banus is the Best Base for a Luxury Day Charter

Puerto Banus is not just a marina, it is a destination in itself. The port handles over 4,000 visiting yachts each year and has direct access to the Mediterranean without a lock or bridge. That means you are on open water within 10 minutes of leaving the dock. Compare that to Estepona or Sotogrande, where you spend extra time motoring out of the harbour. For a luxury motor yacht hire Puerto Banus day, the convenience of a quick exit matters because you maximise your time at anchor. The port also has a fuel dock, a chandlery, and several supermarkets within walking distance, so you can stock up before departure. The downside is the crowds in August, but the skipper will know the quiet departure times.

Specific Yacht Models You Might Find on BoatHire24

The listings for luxury motor yacht hire Puerto Banus day include specific models that are common in the local fleet. A Sunseeker Predator 55 is a sporty flybridge with a top speed of 36 knots, ideal for a fast run to Marbella. The Azimut 50 Fly has a more spacious salon and a large swim platform, better for families. The Princess V62 is a 62-footer with three cabins and a sun deck that seats 12. The Ferretti 780 is a 78-foot superyacht with a full galley and air conditioning throughout. Each model has different strengths, so look at the photos and the layout before booking. If you want a hot tub, check for a Ferretti or a Mangusta. If you want a sun pad for sunbathing, a Sunseeker or a Pershing works well.

How Sea Conditions Affect Your Day in Puerto Banus

The Costa del Sol is known for calm seas, but the wind can pick up in the afternoon, especially in the summer. The typical pattern is a light Levante wind from the east in the morning, which builds to 10-15 knots by 2pm. That is enough to make the water choppy outside the bay, but a luxury motor yacht handles it easily. The skipper will adjust the route to stay in sheltered areas, such as the lee of the Sierra Blanca cliffs or the Cabopino dunes. If the Poniente wind from the west blows, the sea is flat calm because the wind comes off the land. The worst conditions are in March and November, when storms from the Atlantic can bring 2-metre swells. The operator will cancel if the forecast is bad, so you are not stuck on a rough ride.

What to Eat and Drink on a Luxury Day Charter

Catering is a big part of the experience. Most operators offer a menu from a local caterer or partner restaurant. A typical lunch spread includes Spanish jamon, manchego cheese, olives, bread, and a salad, followed by grilled fish or paella. That costs around EUR 50 per person. A premium option includes lobster, steak, and champagne, at EUR 100-150 per person. You can also order from a beach club like Nikki Beach or Ocean Club, but the delivery fee is extra. Drinks are usually BYO, but some yachts have a bar package with beer, wine, and soft drinks for EUR 30-50 per person. Sparkling water and ice are always included. If you have dietary restrictions, tell the operator at booking, not on the day.

The Legal Side of a Day Charter in Spain

Spanish maritime law requires a licensed skipper for any vessel over 6 metres or with an engine over 15 horsepower. Your luxury motor yacht hire Puerto Banus day includes a skipper who holds a Professional Skipper Licence (RYA Yachtmaster or Spanish Patron de Embarcaciones). The yacht is registered as a commercial charter vessel (bandera de conveniencia or Spanish flag) and has a passenger certificate that limits the number of guests. That limit is legally binding, so do not try to squeeze in extra people. The operator will check ID and may ask for a passport copy. The charter contract is in Spanish and English, and you should read the cancellation policy carefully. Most operators offer a full refund if cancelled 14 days before, but high season may have stricter terms.

How to Save Money on a Luxury Motor Yacht Hire Puerto Banus Day

Prices are high in July and August, but you can save by booking a half-day instead of a full day. A half-day (4-5 hours) from 9am to 1pm or 2pm to 7pm costs roughly 60% of the full-day rate. You still get the same yacht, skipper, and fuel, but you skip the lunch stop. Another trick is to book in late May or early June, when the weather is good but the demand is low. You can also share the cost with a group; a 50-foot yacht for 10 people at EUR 5,000 works out at EUR 500 per person, which is cheaper than a beach club table. Avoid booking on a Saturday, which is the most expensive day. Weekdays are 10-20% cheaper. Finally, ask the operator if they have a last-minute deal; some offer 20% off for bookings within 48 hours.

What Happens in Bad Weather

The Costa del Sol has 300 sunny days, but the occasional storm or strong wind can force a cancellation. The skipper will check the weather forecast 24 hours before the charter. If the wind is over 20 knots or there is a yellow warning for rain, the operator will offer a reschedule or a full refund. You do not lose your deposit. If the weather is marginal, the skipper may suggest a shorter route or a different departure time. Do not insist on going out in bad conditions; it is not fun and the insurance may not cover it. The platform handles disputes, but most operators are flexible because they want repeat business. If you are visiting for a limited time, consider booking a flexible date or a backup activity.

Comparing Puerto Banus to Other Departure Ports

Puerto Banus is the premium choice, but other ports on the Costa del Sol offer different advantages. Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo) is closer to the town centre and has cheaper mooring fees, so the charter prices are slightly lower. Cabopino is a small port near the dunes, ideal for a quieter start. Estepona is 15 NM west and has a charming old town for a lunch stop. Sotogrande is further west, near the Strait of Gibraltar, and is better for dolphin watching. Benalmadena is east of Malaga and is closer to the Nerja caves. For a luxury motor yacht hire Puerto Banus day, you get the combination of glamour, convenience, and proximity to the best anchorages. If you want a different vibe, check the other ports on the search page.

Related guides

  • The Best Time to Charter a Boat in Marbella
  • Marbella vs Puerto Banús: Which Departure Port to Choose
  • How Much Does a Yacht Charter in Marbella Cost?
  • Dolphin Watching Boat Trips from Marbella: A Full Guide

faqs.q: What is included in a luxury motor yacht hire Puerto Banus day charter?
faqs.a: The charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, basic safety equipment, and a welcome pack with water and soft drinks. Catering, water toys, and extra crew can be added for an additional cost. The exact inclusions are listed on each yacht's page.

faqs.q: How much does a luxury motor yacht hire Puerto Banus day cost?
faqs.a: Prices start from around EUR 2,500 for a half-day on a 40-foot yacht in low season and go up to EUR 15,000 for a full day on a 60-80 foot yacht in high season. Superyachts are price on request. Catering is extra, typically EUR 50-100 per person.

faqs.q: Do I need a boating licence to charter a luxury motor yacht?
faqs.a: No. Every charter on BoatHire24 includes a licensed skipper who handles all navigation and safety. You do not need any licence or experience. Just show up and enjoy.

faqs.q: Can I bring my own food and drink on the yacht?

faqs.q: What is the best time of year for a day charter from Puerto Banus?
faqs.a: The best months are May, June, September, and October when temperatures are 25-30 degrees C, the sea is calm, and prices are lower than July and August. July and August are hot and crowded but still excellent. Winter charters are possible but cooler.

faqs.q: How far can we go on a day trip from Puerto Banus?
faqs.a: Typical day trips cover 10-20 NM from the marina. Popular destinations include Cala del Faro (10 NM west), Marbella coastline (east), and Estepona (15 NM west). The Strait of Gibraltar is too far for a day trip from Puerto Banus.

faqs.q: How do I book a luxury motor yacht hire Puerto Banus day?
faqs.a: Go to the BoatHire24 search page, set location to Puerto Banus, filter by motor yacht, and choose your date and group size. Send a booking request or message the operator. A 50% deposit is usually required to confirm.

title: Private Yacht Rental Marbella Hen: Girls' Celebration
metaDescription: Plan a private yacht rental Marbella hen party with a skipper included. Compare motor yachts, catamarans and RIBs from Puerto Banús. Book your girls' celebration now.
h1: Private Yacht Rental Marbella Hen Party
intro: If you are organising a hen party on the Costa del Sol, a private yacht rental in Marbella is the most straightforward way to give the bride-to-be a day she will actually remember. No queuing for tables, no overpriced bottle service, no dodging stag groups in a sweaty club. You get a dedicated skipper, a boat that fits your crowd, and the freedom to anchor off a quiet cove or cruise past the mansions of Puerto Banús. This page tells you exactly what to expect, which ports work best, and how much you should budget for a girls' celebration that does not involve inflatable penises or regret.
bodyHtml:
What a Private Yacht Rental for a Hen Party Includes

Every charter on BoatHire24 comes with a licensed skipper who handles navigation, mooring, and safety. You do not need a boating licence, and you do not have to steer anything. The skipper speaks English and Spanish, knows the local anchorages, and will usually help you set up the sound system or chill the cava. Most boats include a Bluetooth speaker, a sun deck with cushions, a shaded saloon, and a small galley with a fridge. You can bring your own food and drink, or arrange a catering package through the boat owner. What you will not get is a hostess, a DJ, or a photographer unless you add them as extras. The boat is yours for the agreed hours, and the skipper stays on board throughout.

Best Departure Ports for a Hen Party Charter

Puerto Banús is the obvious choice. It is the glitziest marina on the coast, full of superyachts, designer shops, and the sort of people who wear sunglasses indoors. Departing from here puts you right in the middle of the action. You can cruise past the superyacht row, wave at the tourists on the bridge, and head out to the sea in ten minutes. Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo) is a quieter alternative, closer to the old town and the beach clubs on the Golden Mile. Cabopino, Estepona, Sotogrande, and Benalmádena are also options, but they involve a longer drive from Marbella centre. For a hen party that wants to combine a boat trip with a night out, Puerto Banús is the most practical base.

Boat Options for a Girls' Celebration

Motor yachts are the most popular choice for hen parties. They have a large sundeck, a spacious bow area for sunbathing, and a cabin below deck where the bride can change into her second outfit. Typical sizes range from 8 metres (around EUR 400 for a half day) up to 15 metres (EUR 1,200 for a full day). Catamarans offer more deck space and stability, which matters if any of the guests are prone to seasickness. A 12-metre catamaran can seat 12 to 16 people comfortably and costs from around EUR 900 for a half day. RIBs and speedboats are cheaper and faster but have limited shade and seating; they work for a small group of four to six who want to pull a doughnut or visit a beach club quickly. Sailing yachts are quieter and more elegant, but the heeling can make high heels impractical. Licence-free day boats are a budget option for groups of six or fewer who want to self-drive, but then you lose the skipper.

Pricing Guidance for a Marbella Hen Party Boat

Prices vary by boat size, season, and duration. For a half day (4 hours) on a 10-metre motor yacht, expect to pay from around EUR 600 to EUR 900. A full day (8 hours) on the same boat runs from EUR 1,000 to EUR 1,500. Catamarans start at EUR 800 for a half day and go up to EUR 2,000 for a full day on a 15-metre model. RIBs cost from EUR 350 for a half day. Superyachts over 20 metres are price on request and usually require a minimum of three days. Fuel is typically included in the listed price on BoatHire24, but check the booking terms because some owners charge extra for long distances. The average hen group of 10 to 12 people can expect to pay between EUR 80 and EUR 150 per person for a half day, which is cheaper than a table at most beach clubs.

Best Time of Year for a Yacht Hen Party

The Costa del Sol has a Mediterranean climate with over 300 days of sunshine a year. The ideal months for a private yacht rental are May through October. In May and June, the sea temperature is around 18 to 20 degrees C, and the air temperature ranges from 22 to 28 degrees C. July and August are the hottest, with sea temps up to 25 degrees C and air temps above 30 degrees C. September and October are still warm, and the water stays pleasant until mid-October. If you book between November and April, you risk cooler winds and rougher seas, though there are calm days. The skipper will advise on the day. For a hen party that wants to swim, sunbathe, and wear minimal clothing, book for June, July, or September.

What to Do on the Boat

Most hen groups spend the first hour cruising along the coast, taking photos of the coastline and the superyachts. The skipper will anchor in a sheltered bay, typically Cala del Faro or near the Cabopino dunes. You can swim, use the paddleboard or inflatable toys if the boat has them, and eat lunch on deck. Some boats allow you to bring a portable speaker; others have a built-in system. If you want to stop at a beach club for lunch, the skipper can moor near one of the floating platforms at Nikki Beach or Ocean Club, but you need to book a table in advance. After lunch, you cruise back to port, usually with a round of cava and a playlist of the bride's favourite songs. The whole thing takes four to six hours, which is enough time for a proper celebration without anyone getting sunburned or bored.

How to Book a Private Yacht Rental for a Hen Party

Go to BoatHire24 and use the search filters to set your date, group size, and port. You will see a list of available boats with photos, specs, and prices. Each listing shows the boat capacity, included amenities, and cancellation policy. You can send a booking request directly through the site, and the owner will confirm within 24 hours. For a hen party, we recommend booking at least two weeks in advance during summer. If you want a specific boat or a large catamaran, book a month ahead. The payment is handled securely through the platform, and you can usually pay a deposit of 30 to 50 per cent to hold the date, with the balance due on the day. No hidden fees.

Who This Is For

This is for a group of women who want a private, classy day on the water without the hassle of organising a club night. It works for any age range, from a 21st birthday to a 50th celebration. The bride does not have to be a boat person; the skipper handles everything. You do not need to be a swimmer, because you can stay on deck the whole time. The only requirement is that you can handle a moderate amount of sun and a gentle sea breeze. If the group includes anyone with mobility issues, check the boat listing for accessibility; most yachts have a step from the dock to the deck that is about 50 cm high, and the toilet is usually down a steep ladder below deck. For a fully accessible charter, ask the owner before booking.

Local Attractions Near the Ports

If you have time before or after the charter, Puerto Banús has a row of seafood restaurants on the waterfront, a casino, and the usual luxury boutiques. Marbella old town is a 15-minute drive from the marina, with narrow cobblestone streets, tapas bars, and the Plaza de los Naranjos. Estepona old town has painted flower pots and a quieter vibe. Sotogrande is more residential, with a golf course and a polo club. Benalmádena has a cable car up to the mountain and a large marina with bars. None of these are essential for a hen party, but they make a good backup plan if the weather turns or someone wants a lie-in before the charter.

Frequently Asked Questions About Private Yacht Rental for Hen Parties

For more details on the booking process, read our blog about how to choose a boat for a group celebration. You can also search available boats directly and compare options.

How Many People Can You Fit on a Hen Party Yacht

Boat capacity is listed per vessel and is strictly enforced by the skipper and port authority. A typical 10-metre motor yacht seats 10 to 12 guests plus the skipper. A 12-metre catamaran can take up to 16. RIBs and speedboats usually fit 6 to 8. If your group is larger than 16, you need to charter two boats or look for a superyacht over 20 metres, which can carry 20 to 30 passengers. Do not try to squeeze extra people on board. The skipper will count heads before departure, and the boat will not leave the dock if the number exceeds the licence limit. For a hen party of 8 to 12 guests, a single motor yacht or catamaran is the sweet spot. For 14 or more, book two smaller boats that can raft up together in the bay, so you still spend time together.

What to Bring and What to Leave at Home

Bring swimwear, a towel, sunscreen (SPF 50, reef-safe if possible), sunglasses, a hat, and a light jacket or cover-up for the return trip when the wind picks up. Pack food and drink in a soft cooler bag, not a hard one, because storage space on a boat is limited. Most boats have a fridge, but it is small. Bring a reusable cup for each guest to avoid broken glass on deck. The bride can bring a veil, sash, or inflatable swan for photos, but check with the owner first because some skippers ban large inflatables. Leave behind high heels with thin stilettos; they damage the teak deck. Flat sandals or bare feet are fine. Do not bring glass bottles, red wine (it stains the upholstery), or a portable speaker that needs mains power. The boat will have a Bluetooth system or a 12-volt aux input. Leave the confetti and glitter at home; they are a nightmare to clean off a boat and some owners charge a cleaning fee.

Can You Swim and Snorkel During the Charter

Yes, the skipper will anchor in a calm bay where you can swim. Most boats have a swim ladder at the stern. The water clarity on the Costa del Sol is good, especially near Cabopino and Cala del Faro, where you can see the sandy bottom at 5 metres depth. Some boats include snorkel masks and fins as standard equipment. If not, you can rent them from a dive shop in Puerto Banús for around EUR 10 per set. The sea temperature in summer is 22 to 25 degrees C, so you do not need a wetsuit. If your hen party wants to do a group swim photo, ask the skipper to stop near a rocky outcrop or a buoy where the water is deeper and the background is more dramatic. Do not jump off the bow of the boat unless the skipper says it is safe. The anchor chain or the propeller can be a hazard in shallow water.

Can You Add a Photographer or a DJ

Yes, you can hire a photographer or a DJ as an extra service, but you need to arrange it separately. BoatHire24 does not offer these add-ons directly. You can search for a local event photographer in Marbella who specialises in boat shoots, or ask the boat owner for a recommendation. Expect to pay around EUR 200 to EUR 400 for a two-hour photo session on the water. A DJ with a portable PA system costs from EUR 300 for a half day. The boat has limited power outlets, so the DJ needs a battery-powered setup. Some owners do not allow a DJ because of noise restrictions in the marina and anchorages. If you just want background music, the boat's Bluetooth speaker is sufficient. For a hen party that wants a party vibe without the cost of a DJ, create a shared playlist on Spotify before the trip and let the skipper cue it up.

What Happens if Someone Gets Seasick

Seasickness on the Costa del Sol is rare because the Mediterranean is generally calm, especially in summer. The swell inside the bay is usually less than 0.5 metres. If any of your guests are prone to motion sickness, choose a catamaran instead of a motor yacht. Catamarans have a wider beam and do not roll as much. Book a morning charter, because the wind picks up in the afternoon and creates a slight chop. Advise the guest to take a seasickness tablet (such as Dramamine or Stugeron) the night before and again two hours before departure. Do not drink alcohol on an empty stomach; it worsens nausea. The skipper will have a bucket and a first aid kit on board, but they are not a medic. If someone vomits, the crew will clean it up. It happens. Do not let seasickness ruin the day for the whole group. The affected guest can lie down in the shaded saloon and usually feels better after 30 minutes.

Can You Stay Overnight on the Boat

Most boats on BoatHire24 are day charter vessels and are not equipped for overnight stays. They have a small cabin with a bed and a toilet, but no shower or cooking facilities that would make an overnight trip comfortable. If you want an overnight hen party, you need to charter a superyacht or a large catamaran with multiple cabins, a full galley, and a generator for air conditioning. These are available from around EUR 3,000 per night for a 15-metre catamaran up to EUR 10,000 per night for a 25-metre superyacht. The booking process is the same: use the search filters on BoatHire24 and look for boats listed with overnight capacity. You will need to pay a security deposit of EUR 500 to EUR 2,000, which is refunded if the boat is returned clean and undamaged. For a standard hen party, a day charter is more practical and cheaper.

What to Do If the Weather Turns Bad

The skipper monitors the weather forecast and will cancel the charter if the wind exceeds 20 knots or there is a thunderstorm warning. You will receive a full refund or the option to reschedule. If the weather is marginal, the skipper may suggest a shorter route inside the bay or a later departure time. The Costa del Sol is sheltered by the Sierra Blanca mountains, so sudden storms are rare in summer. In spring and autumn, you might get a morning cloud cover that clears by midday. The boat has a shaded cabin where you can sit if the sun is too strong or if a light rain passes. Do not argue with the skipper about weather decisions. They have the final say for safety reasons. If you are worried about losing your deposit, book a boat with a flexible cancellation policy, which BoatHire24 lists clearly on each boat page.

How to Get to the Port from Marbella

Puerto Banús is 6 km west of Marbella town centre, about a 10-minute taxi ride costing EUR 10 to EUR 15. Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo) is on the eastern edge of the town, a 5-minute walk from the old town. Estepona is 25 km west of Marbella, a 25-minute taxi ride costing EUR 30 to EUR 40. Sotogrande is 30 km further west, about 40 minutes by taxi. Benalmádena is 20 km east of Marbella, a 20-minute drive. For a hen party staying in Marbella, Puerto Banús is the most convenient departure port. If the group is staying in Estepona or Sotogrande, use the local port to save travel time. Taxis and Ubers are plentiful on the coast, but book a larger vehicle in advance if your group has more than 4 people. A minibus from Marbella to Puerto Banús costs around EUR 40 one way.

Can You Combine the Boat Trip with a Beach Club

Yes, you can stop at a beach club for lunch or drinks, but you need to book a table in advance and the skipper must moor at the club's buoy or dock. Popular beach clubs near Marbella include Nikki Beach (on the Golden Mile), Ocean Club (Marbella town), and Playa Padre (Puerto Banús). These clubs have a minimum spend per person, typically EUR 50 to EUR 100 for a sunbed and a drink. The skipper will drop you off at the club's floating platform, and you can spend 1 to 2 hours there before the boat picks you up again. This adds EUR 50 to EUR 100 to the charter cost for the extra fuel and time. Some clubs do not allow outside food or drink, so you cannot bring your own cava onto their premises. For a hen party that wants a full day of swimming, lunch, and drinks, this combination works well. Just make sure the club knows you are arriving by boat, because they reserve a specific mooring spot.
