faqs.q: Can we bring our own alcohol on a private boat charter Marbella stag?
faqs.a: Yes, most operators allow you to bring your own alcohol, but check first. Some charge a corkage fee for glass bottles or restrict certain types of alcohol. It's safer to bring cans and plastic cups. The skipper has the right to limit consumption if anyone gets too drunk.

faqs.q: What is the maximum group size for a stag boat charter?
faqs.a: It depends on the boat. Most motor yachts and catamarans for charter in Marbella have a maximum capacity of 10 to 20 passengers. This is strictly enforced for insurance and safety. Check the boat's listing for its maximum capacity, and don't try to exceed it.

faqs.q: Do we need a boating licence for a private charter?
faqs.a: No. Every charter on BoatHire24 includes a licensed skipper who handles all navigation. You just show up and enjoy. The only exception is licence-free day boats, but those are not suitable for a stag party.

faqs.q: What happens if the weather is bad on the day of our charter?
faqs.a: The skipper makes the final call on safety. If the weather is dangerous (high winds, storms), the charter may be cancelled or rescheduled. Most operators have a weather policy that allows a full refund or alternative date. Check the terms before booking.

faqs.q: Can we swim and use water toys during the charter?
faqs.a: Yes, swimming is usually allowed when the boat is anchored in a safe cove. Water toys like paddleboards and inflatables can be added for an extra fee. Confirm with the operator beforehand what toys are available and the cost.

faqs.q: Is it customary to tip the skipper and crew?
faqs.a: Yes, tipping is standard in the industry. A tip of 10-15% of the charter fee is appreciated if you're satisfied with the service. For a EUR 2,000 charter, that's EUR 200-300. Cash is preferred.

faqs.q: How far in advance should we book a stag boat charter?
faqs.a: For peak season (June-September), book at least 4-6 weeks in advance. For shoulder months, 2-3 weeks is usually enough. Popular boats and ports like Puerto Banús fill up quickly, so don't leave it to the last minute.

title: Motor Yacht Charter Fuengirola Day: Coastal Tour
metaDescription: Book a motor yacht charter Fuengirola day trip with a skipper. Explore the Costa del Sol coast from Puerto Banús. Prices from around EUR 1,200. Check availability.
h1: Motor Yacht Charter Fuengirola Day: Coastal Tour
intro:
You want a day on the water around Fuengirola, but you don't own a boat and you don't have a licence. That's fine. A motor yacht charter in Fuengirola for a day is exactly what you need. You get a private, insured motor yacht with a professional skipper who handles everything. You just turn up, relax, and decide where you want to go along the Costa del Sol coast.

BoatHire24 connects you with owners and charter companies based in Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, and other ports near Fuengirola. Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel for a standard day trip, and a safety briefing. You can choose from modern motor yachts ranging from 10 to 25 metres. Typical itineraries include cruising past the Marbella coastline, stopping for a swim near Cala del Faro, and lunch at a chiringuito in Estepona or Cabopino. Prices start from around EUR 1,200 for a day on a mid-range motor yacht, depending on the boat size, season, and extras.
bodyHtml:
What a Motor Yacht Charter Fuengirola Day Includes

When you book a motor yacht charter Fuengirola day through BoatHire24, the package is straightforward. You get a private boat for your group only. No sharing with strangers. The skipper is included in the price and has full insurance, a valid licence, and local knowledge of the coast from Fuengirola to Estepona. Fuel for up to 8 hours of cruising is usually included, but check with the owner because some high-performance yachts charge extra for fuel beyond a certain number of nautical miles. A typical day runs from 10:00 to 18:00, but you can negotiate different timings with the charter company. Most boats come with a sound system, a small fridge, a sun pad area at the bow, and a shaded cockpit. Some larger yachts have a cabin with a toilet and a shower. You bring your own food and drinks, or you can ask the skipper to arrange a catering package at an extra cost. Life jackets, a first aid kit, and a VHF radio are standard. The charter does not include mooring fees if you stop at a marina for lunch, so budget around EUR 20 to EUR 50 for that. Gratuity for the skipper is optional but customary at 10% to 15% of the charter fee.

Departure Ports Near Fuengirola

Fuengirola has a small marina called Puerto Deportivo de Fuengirola, but most motor yachts available for charter are based in larger ports nearby because they have deeper water and better facilities. The most common departure point is BoatHire24 listings from Puerto Banús, which is about 15 minutes by car from Fuengirola. Puerto Banús has a huge selection of motor yachts, from 12-metre day cruisers to 30-metre superyachts. Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo) is another option, slightly closer to Fuengirola and with a good range of boats. Cabopino, located between Marbella and Cala de Mijas, is a quieter port with a few motor yachts for charter. Estepona and Sotogrande are further west but offer larger yachts and less crowded waters. Benalmádena, east of Fuengirola, also has a marina with some charter options. When you search on our platform, you can filter by departure port to find the one most convenient for you. The skipper will meet you at the port 15 minutes before departure and handle all the docking procedures.

Types of Motor Yachts for a Day Charter

The motor yachts available for a day charter from Fuengirola vary in size, style, and amenities. The most common are flybridge motor yachts, which have an upper steering station and a large sun deck. These are good for groups of 6 to 12 people who want space to sunbathe and socialise. Sport motor yachts are faster and more streamlined, usually with a lower profile and a cockpit that opens to the water. They are better for smaller groups who want to reach a distant spot quickly. Some yachts are classified as motor sailers, but for a pure motor yacht experience, stick with the flybridge or sport models. You can also find small RIBs and speedboats on the platform, but those are not motor yachts. For a proper motor yacht charter Fuengirola day, expect a boat with at least two decks, a cabin, and a toilet. Larger yachts over 20 metres may have a crew of two (skipper and stewardess) and include extras like a jet ski or paddleboards. Check the exact specifications on the listing page before booking.

Pricing Guidance for a Day Charter

Pricing for a motor yacht charter Fuengirola day depends on the season, the boat size, and the duration. In the low season (October to April), you can find a 12-metre motor yacht for around EUR 1,200 to EUR 1,800 for a full day. In the high season (June to September), the same boat costs EUR 2,000 to EUR 3,000. A 15-metre flybridge yacht ranges from EUR 2,500 to EUR 4,500 in summer. Superyachts over 25 metres are price on request, but expect EUR 8,000 to EUR 15,000 per day. The price usually includes the skipper, fuel for up to 50 nautical miles, basic insurance, and the boat use. Extras like food, drinks, mooring fees, and additional crew are not included. Some charter companies ask for a security deposit, typically EUR 1,000 to EUR 3,000, which is refunded after the trip if there is no damage. You can pay by bank transfer or credit card. We recommend booking at least a week in advance in summer, as popular yachts go fast.

Best Time of Year for a Motor Yacht Charter Fuengirola Day

The Costa del Sol has a Mediterranean climate with mild winters and hot summers. The best time for a motor yacht charter Fuengirola day is from May to October. In May and June, the water temperature is around 18C to 22C, and the air temperature is 24C to 30C. July and August are the hottest months, with air temperatures reaching 35C and water temperatures up to 26C. September is still warm and less crowded. October can be pleasant but the water starts to cool down. Winter months (November to March) are possible for a day trip if you want a calm sea and lower prices, but you need to bring a jacket. The sea can be choppy in December and January due to the Levante wind. The skipper will advise you on the day if conditions are safe. For a guaranteed swimming and sunbathing experience, book between June and September.

Suggested Itineraries for a Day Trip from Fuengirola

You have several options for a day itinerary on a motor yacht charter Fuengirola day. The simplest is a coastal cruise west towards Marbella and Puerto Banús. You can anchor near the dunes of Artola or Cabopino for a swim. Another popular route is east towards Benalmádena and Torremolinos, passing the cliffs of Cala del Faro. If you want to see dolphins, head further west towards Estepona and the Strait of Gibraltar. The skipper knows the best spots. A typical day includes a morning cruise, a lunch stop at a beach club or chiringuito (like the one at Cabopino or Elviria), an afternoon swim, and a slow return. You can also request a stop at the island of Isla de las Palomas in Cabopino, but it is a protected area so you can only anchor nearby. For a longer trip, you can sail to Marbella Marina for lunch and then back. The skipper will suggest a route based on the weather and your preferences.

Who Is This Charter For

A motor yacht charter Fuengirola day suits families, groups of friends, couples, and corporate groups. Families with children enjoy the safe swimming stops and the space on the yacht. Groups of friends use it for a party day with music and drinks. Couples book it for a romantic day with a private skipper. Corporate groups use it for team building or client entertainment. The maximum capacity varies by boat, but most motor yachts carry between 6 and 12 passengers. Some larger yachts can take up to 20 people. Children are welcome, and life jackets are provided in all sizes. If you have a specific need, like a wheelchair-accessible yacht, mention it when you enquire. Not all boats are accessible, but some can accommodate limited mobility.

How to Book a Motor Yacht Charter Fuengirola Day on BoatHire24

Booking is simple. Go to our search page and set the location to Fuengirola or any nearby port. Choose the date, the number of guests, and the boat type as motor yacht. You will see a list of available yachts with photos, specs, and prices. Click on a listing to see the full details, including what is included and any extras. You can send an enquiry directly to the owner or charter company through the platform. They will confirm availability and send you a quote. Once you accept, you pay a deposit (usually 50%) to secure the booking. The balance is due on the day of the charter. You can also read our blog for tips on choosing the right yacht and what to expect on the day. If you have any questions, contact our support team via the website.

Local Attractions and Colour Along the Coast

The Costa del Sol coast between Fuengirola and Estepona has plenty of landmarks. You will see the silhouette of La Concha mountain behind Marbella. The Río Verde river mouth near Marbella is a good spot for birdwatching. Cala del Faro is a small cove with a lighthouse near Benalmádena, perfect for a photo stop. Estepona old town has whitewashed streets and flower pots. If you go further west, the Strait of Gibraltar offers a chance to see dolphins and even whales in summer. For a longer day, you can head east to Nerja and its famous caves, but that is a full-day trip of around 8 hours from Fuengirola. The skipper will tailor the route to your interests.

What to Bring and What to Expect on the Day

Bring sunscreen, a hat, sunglasses, a towel, swimwear, and a light jacket for the afternoon breeze. You can bring your own food and drinks, but glass bottles are often not allowed for safety reasons. Use plastic or cans. The boat has a cooler and ice. Arrive at the port 15 minutes before the departure time. The skipper will give a safety briefing and show you the facilities. During the trip, you can sit anywhere, swim, sunbathe, or chat. The skipper will handle navigation and anchoring. , you return to the same port. Tips are appreciated but not mandatory. If you have any complaints, report them to BoatHire24 within 24 hours.

Why Fuengirola Is Not the Best Departure Port for Motor Yachts

Fuengirola's marina, Puerto Deportivo de Fuengirola, is a decent place for small fishing boats and licence-free day boats up to 8 metres, but it has a draft limit of around 2.5 metres. Most motor yachts over 12 metres have a deeper draft and cannot enter safely. The marina also lacks the fuel dock and maintenance facilities that larger yachts need. This is why almost every motor yacht charter Fuengirola day on our platform departs from Puerto Banús or Marbella Marina. The drive from Fuengirola town centre to Puerto Banús is 12 minutes by car, or 20 minutes by taxi for around EUR 25. If you are staying in Fuengirola, you can book a transfer through the charter company or take the A-7 motorway. The extra travel time is worth it because you get access to a much wider selection of boats and a more glamorous departure experience. You can grab coffee at one of the portside cafes while waiting for the skipper to prepare the yacht.

Fuel Costs and Cruising Range for a Day Trip

The fuel consumption of a motor yacht varies dramatically by engine type and speed. A typical 12-metre flybridge with twin diesel engines burns about 60 to 80 litres per hour at cruising speed (around 20 knots). At EUR 1.30 per litre for marine diesel, that is EUR 78 to EUR 104 per hour. For an 8-hour day, fuel alone could cost EUR 624 to EUR 832. Most charter packages include fuel for up to 50 nautical miles, which is roughly 2.5 hours of cruising at 20 knots. If you want to go further, for example to Estepona or the Strait of Gibraltar, you will pay extra. The owner will estimate the additional fuel cost before departure. On a motor yacht charter Fuengirola day, you can save fuel by cruising at a slower speed of 10 to 12 knots, which halves the consumption. The skipper can advise on the most economical route. Ask the charter company for a fuel policy in writing before you book.

Comparing Motor Yachts with Catamarans for a Day Charter

If you are considering a motor yacht charter Fuengirola day, you might also wonder about catamarans. Motor yachts are faster and have a more traditional luxury feel with teak decks and plush seating. They are better for reaching distant spots quickly, such as the dolphin-watching areas near Estepona. Catamarans are slower but more stable, with more deck space and shallower draft, so they can anchor closer to beaches. For a group of 10 people who want to swim and sunbathe, a catamaran might be better. For a group of 6 who want speed and style, a motor yacht is the choice. On our platform, you can filter by boat type to compare both. Prices are similar for comparable sizes. A 14-metre motor yacht costs about the same as a 14-metre catamaran for a day charter. The main difference is the experience: motor yachts feel more like a private club, catamarans feel like a floating terrace.

Specific Motor Yacht Models Commonly Available

On a motor yacht charter Fuengirola day, you will often find models like the Sunseeker Portofino 40 or 46, the Azimut 43 or 50, the Princess V48 or V55, and the Sea Ray 40 or 45. These are all well-maintained boats with professional skippers. The Sunseeker Portofino 40 is a 12-metre sport cruiser with a spacious cockpit and a small cabin. It is ideal for a couple or a family of four. The Azimut 50 is a 15-metre flybridge with a large sun deck and a full galley. It suits groups of 8 to 10. The Princess V55 is a 17-metre performance yacht with a top speed of 35 knots. It is for people who want thrills and luxury. For a superyacht experience, you can charter a 25-metre Ferretti or Pershing, but those are price on request and require a crew of two. The listing page on BoatHire24 includes the exact model year, engine hours, and recent refit history. You can also see photos of the interior and exterior.

How the Skipper Enhances the Day

The skipper on a motor yacht charter Fuengirola day is not just a driver. They are a licensed professional with knowledge of the local coast, weather patterns, and anchorages. They will point out landmarks like the Castillo de Sohail in Fuengirola from the sea, the Torre del Mar lighthouse, and the cliffs of Cala del Faro. They know which beach clubs accept yacht drop-offs and which anchorages have the clearest water. For example, the anchorage at Cabopino has sandy bottom and calm water in the morning, while the area near Marbella's Río Verde can have a current. The skipper will also handle the mooring lines at lunch stops, so you do not have to do anything. If you want to learn about the boat or the area, they are happy to chat. If you want privacy, they will stay in the wheelhouse. You can tip the skipper directly in cash , but it is not required.

Insurance and Liability on a Day Charter

Every motor yacht charter Fuengirola day includes third-party liability insurance for the boat, which covers damage to other vessels or property. The owner also has hull insurance for the yacht itself. However, your personal belongings are not covered by the charter insurance. If you bring expensive camera equipment or jewellery, you should have your own travel insurance. The skipper is fully licensed and insured to operate the boat. If there is an accident due to skipper negligence, the owner's insurance covers it. If you damage the yacht intentionally or through reckless behaviour, you might be liable for the repair costs. The security deposit covers minor damage like scratches or broken glass. For major damage, the deposit might not be enough, and the owner could pursue additional costs. Always treat the yacht with care, and listen to the skipper's instructions about where to sit and what to touch.

Related guides

  • The Best Time to Charter a Boat in Marbella

  • How Much Does a Yacht Charter in Marbella Cost?

faqs.q: Do I need a boating licence for a motor yacht charter Fuengirola day?
faqs.a: No. Every charter includes a licensed skipper who operates the boat. You just enjoy the ride. This applies to all motor yachts, catamarans, and sailing yachts on BoatHire24.

faqs.q: How many people can join a motor yacht charter Fuengirola day?
faqs.a: Most motor yachts carry between 6 and 12 passengers. Larger yachts can take up to 20. Check the specific boat listing for the maximum capacity. Children count as passengers.

faqs.q: Can I choose my own itinerary for the day?
faqs.a: Yes. The skipper will suggest a route based on weather and your preferences, but you decide where to go. Popular stops include Cabopino, Marbella, and Estepona. Just let the skipper know.

faqs.q: Is food and drink included in the charter price?
faqs.a: No. You bring your own food and drinks, or you can ask the charter company to arrange catering for an extra fee. The boat usually has a fridge and ice.

faqs.q: What happens if the weather is bad on the day?
faqs.a: The skipper will assess conditions and may delay or cancel the trip for safety. If cancelled, you get a full refund or can reschedule. Check the cancellation policy on the listing.

faqs.q: Can I book a motor yacht charter Fuengirola day for a party or event?
faqs.a: Yes, you can. Some yachts allow parties with music and drinks. Check with the owner first, as some restrict loud music or large groups. Extra cleaning fees may apply.

faqs.q: How far in advance should I book?
faqs.a: In summer (June to September), book at least 7 to 14 days in advance. In low season, a few days is usually enough. Last-minute bookings are possible but options are limited.

title: Catamaran Hire Puerto Banus Group: Book for 40
metaDescription: Book catamaran hire Puerto Banus group for up to 40 guests. Compare prices, ports & boats. Instant online booking with licensed skipper. Start your trip.
h1: Catamaran Hire Puerto Banus Group: Book for 40
intro: You want to take a group of 40 people out on a catamaran from Puerto Banus. That is a big ask, but it is doable. Most catamarans on the Costa del Sol cap out at 12 to 20 guests, but a few larger vessels can handle 40 comfortably. You will need a proper charter catamaran, not a standard rental. And you will need a licensed skipper, which every BoatHire24 charter includes. So yes, you can book catamaran hire for a group of 40 in Puerto Banus. Here is exactly what to expect, what it costs, and how to secure the date.
bodyHtml:
What a Catamaran Hire for 40 Includes

When you book a catamaran for 40 people, you are chartering a vessel typically 20 to 30 metres long. Think of it as a floating party platform with two hulls, so it is stable and has loads of deck space. You get a skipper who knows the coast from Puerto Banus to Estepona and beyond. You get fuel for the trip (usually included in the price for a half-day or full-day charter). You get insurance, life jackets, and safety briefing. What you do not get automatically is food and drink. Most charters offer catering as an add-on: a paella lunch, a BBQ, or a tapas spread. Soft drinks and beer are often available on board at extra cost. Some catamarans have a sound system, a sunbathing trampoline at the bow, and a small galley. For 40 people, you will likely need a crew of two or three: a skipper plus a steward or host.

Puerto Banus: The Departure Port

Puerto Banus is the obvious starting point for a group catamaran trip. It is the glitzy marina in Marbella, packed with superyachts, designer shops, and bars. You meet your skipper at the port office or a designated berth. The marina has parking (paid, about EUR 20-30 per day) and is easy to reach from Marbella town, Estepona, or Malaga airport. From Puerto Banus, you head south along the coast towards the Marbella beaches, or west towards the Cala del Faro and the Cabopino dunes. The water is calm, the weather is warm (sea temperature 18-26°C depending on the month), and the views of La Concha mountain are a constant backdrop.

Boat Options for a Group of 40

Standard catamarans like a Lagoon 450 or Fountaine Pajot hold 12 to 20 guests. For 40, you need a larger commercial catamaran. Options include the Sunreef 70 or a similar custom-built charter catamaran. These have a beam of 10-12 metres, giving you 300+ square metres of deck space. Expect a flybridge with seating, a large aft deck, and a saloon with air conditioning. Some catamarans in this size range have a hydraulic platform for swimming and tenders. You can also consider a motor yacht if you prefer speed, but for a group of 40, the catamaran is better for stability and socialising. Check the BoatHire24 search for catamarans with a capacity of 40. Not all listings show the exact guest limit, so filter by length (over 20m) and contact the owner.

Pricing Guidance for a 40-Guest Catamaran

You are looking at EUR 3,000 to 6,000 for a half-day (4 hours) and EUR 5,000 to 10,000 for a full day (8 hours). These are rough numbers. The price depends on the boat, the season, and the extras. High season (June to September) is the most expensive. Low season (November to March) can be 30-40% cheaper. Fuel is typically included for a standard itinerary (e.g. Puerto Banus to Cabopino and back). If you want to go further, like to Estepona or Sotogrande, expect a fuel surcharge. Catering for 40 people adds EUR 500-1,500 depending on the menu. Drinks packages are extra. You pay a deposit upfront (usually 50%) and the balance on the day. All prices on BoatHire24 are in euros and include VAT.

Seasonal Considerations for Group Charters

The best months for a catamaran hire in Puerto Banus are May through October. The sea is calm, the sun is strong, and the evenings are long. July and August are peak season, so book two to three months in advance. April and November can be pleasant but the water is cooler (around 16-18°C) and there is a risk of wind. December to March is quiet; you might get a good deal but the weather is unpredictable. For a group of 40, you need a guaranteed date, so aim for a Saturday in June or September. The Strait of Gibraltar area can have strong winds (Levante) in spring, but Puerto Banus is sheltered. If the forecast is bad, your skipper may suggest an alternative route or postpone.

Itineraries for a Group of 40

A typical half-day itinerary from Puerto Banus: depart at 11:00, cruise west along the coast past the Marbella Club and Puente Romano, anchor off Cabopino beach for swimming and lunch, return by 15:00. For a full day, you can go further west to the Cala del Faro (a rocky cove near Estepona) or east to the Nerja caves area (about 2.5 hours one way, so you would need a full day plus fuel). Another option is to head south towards the Strait of Gibraltar, where you can see dolphins and maybe whales. Your skipper knows the best spots for swimming, snorkelling, and avoiding crowds. You can also stay in the bay and just cruise, with music and drinks. The choice is yours.

How to Book a Catamaran for 40 on BoatHire24

Go to the BoatHire24 search page, set the guest count to 40, the port to Puerto Banus, and the date. The search will show available catamarans. If none appear, try reducing the guest count to 20 and look for boats that say "up to 40" in the description. You can also contact the owner directly through the platform. We recommend messaging at least two or three owners to compare prices and availability. Once you agree on a date and price, you pay the deposit via credit card or bank transfer. The owner will confirm and send you the boarding details. Cancellation policies vary: most allow free cancellation up to 14 days before, then a 50% refund up to 7 days before.

Who Is This For?

Catamaran hire for 40 people is ideal for corporate events, stag or hen parties, milestone birthdays, family reunions, or team-building trips. It is also popular for weddings: you can hold the ceremony on board or just the reception. The boat is your private venue for the day. You get a skipper, so no one needs a licence. You can bring your own food and drink if the charter allows (check with the owner). Some catamarans have a DJ setup or you can bring a Bluetooth speaker. For a group of 40, you will need to coordinate logistics: parking, boarding time, and any dietary restrictions. The crew will help with that.

Other Ports Near Puerto Banus for Group Catamarans

If Puerto Banus is fully booked, consider departing from Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo), which is a 10-minute drive east. Or Cabopino, which is smaller but closer to the dunes. Estepona port is 20 minutes west and has a few large catamarans. Sotogrande is further west (30 minutes by car) and is quieter. Benalmádena is east of Malaga, about 45 minutes from Puerto Banus. The advantage of Puerto Banus is the glamour factor and the proximity to restaurants and nightlife. For a group of 40, the port matters because you need space to gather and board. Puerto Banus has a wide pier and plenty of room.

What to Bring and What to Expect

Bring sunscreen, sunglasses, a hat, a towel, and swimwear. The boat will have a first aid kit and life jackets. You can bring your own food and drink unless the charter includes catering. Some boats have a fridge and ice box. Expect the crew to be professional and friendly. They will give a safety briefing at the start. You can help with the ropes if you want, but it is not required. The skipper will decide the itinerary based on weather and sea conditions. If the sea is rough, they may avoid open water and stay close to the coast. For a group of 40, the boat will have two toilets and possibly a shower. Plan for a maximum of 8 hours at sea; any longer and you will need a different charter type.

Frequently Asked Questions About Catamaran Hire for 40 in Puerto Banus

Read on for common questions. If you have a specific query, check the BoatHire24 blog for more detailed guides on group charters, or contact us.

Why a Catamaran Works Better Than a Motor Yacht for 40 Guests

You might think a motor yacht is the obvious choice for a group of 40. It is not. A catamaran has two hulls, which means it does not heel over like a monohull. Your guests can walk around without holding on. The deck space is roughly double that of a motor yacht of the same length. For 40 people, you need that space. A motor yacht of 25 metres might have a main deck and a flybridge, but the interior saloon is cramped for 40. On a catamaran, the saloon opens directly to the aft deck and the bow trampoline. You can have 20 people on the trampoline, 10 on the aft deck, and 10 on the flybridge. The boat does not feel crowded. Also, catamarans have a shallower draft (around 1.5 metres), so you can anchor closer to beaches like Cabopino or Cala del Faro. Motor yachts need deeper water.

Specific Catamaran Models That Hold 40 Guests

Not every catamaran can take 40. The Lagoon 620 holds 20. The Fountaine Pajot 67 holds 24. For 40, you need a commercial-grade vessel. Look for the Sunreef 70, which has a beam of 11.5 metres and a length of 21.3 metres. It can carry up to 50 guests with a crew of 3. Another option is the Lagoon 77, which has a capacity of 40 with a crew of 2. The Nautitech 80 is a performance catamaran that can take 40, but it is rarer on the Costa del Sol. The Bali 5.4 is a catamaran with a hard-top flybridge and a capacity of 30, so it is a bit tight for 40. Always confirm the exact guest limit with the owner. On BoatHire24, you can filter by length and then message the owner to ask about capacity. Do not rely on the listing alone.

Cost Breakdown for Catering and Extras

The base price of EUR 5,000 to 10,000 for a full day does not include food. For 40 people, a paella lunch costs around EUR 25-35 per person, so EUR 1,000 to 1,400. A BBQ with meat, fish, and salad is EUR 30-45 per person, so EUR 1,200 to 1,800. Tapas are cheaper at EUR 15-25 per person. Drinks packages: soft drinks and water EUR 5-10 per person, beer EUR 10-15 per person, wine EUR 15-20 per person. A full open bar (beer, wine, spirits, mixers) costs EUR 30-50 per person. If you bring your own food and drink, there is often a corkage fee of EUR 200-500. Some catamarans have a minimum spend on catering. Ask the owner before booking. For a group of 40, the total with catering and drinks can reach EUR 8,000 to 15,000 for a full day in high season.

How the Weather Affects a Group of 40 on a Catamaran

The Costa del Sol has over 300 sunny days a year, but wind can be a problem. The Levante wind blows from the east and can reach 30 knots in spring. A catamaran handles wind better than a motor yacht, but 40 guests on a windy day is not fun. The skipper will choose a sheltered route, like staying close to the coast between Puerto Banus and Cabopino. The Poniente wind from the west is lighter and more common in summer. Sea temperature in July and August is 24-26°C, so swimming is pleasant. In June and September, it is 20-22°C. In April and October, it is 16-18°C. For a group of 40, you want the water warm enough for swimming. If the weather is bad, most charters allow a free reschedule up to 48 hours before. Check the policy on BoatHire24.

Parking and Logistics for a Group of 40 at Puerto Banus

Puerto Banus has a large car park near the port entrance. It costs about EUR 25 per day. For 40 people, you need 10-12 cars. There is also a taxi rank and a bus stop. The boarding point is usually at the end of the main pier, near the statue. You need to arrive 15 minutes before departure. The crew will help with boarding. If you have guests with mobility issues, tell the owner in advance. Catamarans have a gangway, but it can be steep. The boat has a toilet, but for 40 people, two toilets can get busy. Plan for a rotation. The crew will manage the flow. For a stag or hen party, the port security may ask you to keep noise down while boarding. Once you are out at sea, you can turn up the music.

Legal Requirements for a Group Charter of 40

In Spain, a commercial charter for 40 passengers requires the boat to have a Passenger Certificate issued by the Spanish Maritime Authority. The skipper must have a Professional Skipper Licence (PATRON PROFESIONAL DE EMBARCACIONES DE RECREO or similar). The boat must have life jackets for all guests, a liferaft with capacity for 40, and a first aid kit. The owner is responsible for compliance. You do not need a licence as a guest. The crew will give a safety briefing. For a group of 40, the boat must have a public address system for announcements. If the boat does not have a Passenger Certificate, it cannot legally take 40 guests. Always ask the owner for proof. On BoatHire24, all listed boats are verified for commercial use.

Best Itinerary for a Full Day with 40 Guests

Depart Puerto Banus at 10:00. Cruise west past the Marbella Club and the Golden Mile. Anchor off Cabopino beach at 11:00. Swim, snorkel, and have lunch on board. At 13:00, sail further west to Cala del Faro, a rocky cove near Estepona. Anchor again for more swimming. At 15:00, head back east, passing Puerto Banus and continuing to the Marbella Marina area. At 16:00, anchor off the beach of La Bajadilla for a final swim. Return to Puerto Banus at 17:00. This itinerary covers 15 nautical miles. Fuel is included. If you want to see dolphins, ask the skipper to head south towards the Strait of Gibraltar. The dolphin pods are common near the Punta de la Mona. That adds 10 nautical miles each way, so you need a full day and a fuel surcharge of around EUR 200-400.

How to Split Costs for a Group of 40

If you are organising the trip, collect money from each guest in advance. For a full day at EUR 8,000 (including catering and drinks), each person pays EUR 200. For a half day at EUR 5,000, each pays EUR 125. You can use a payment app like Bizum or PayPal. Some owners on BoatHire24 accept group payments via a single invoice. You pay the deposit (50%) and then collect the rest from guests. If someone cancels, you need a backup. Set a deadline for payment, say 14 days before the trip. If you have 40 people, expect a few no-shows. Overbook by 2-3 guests to account for cancellations. The boat capacity is a hard limit, so do not exceed 40.

What to Do if the Boat You Want Is Not Available

If no catamaran for 40 is available on your date, consider booking two smaller catamarans. Two catamarans for 20 each cost around EUR 3,000-5,000 each, so total EUR 6,000-10,000. You can sail together and anchor at the same spot. The group can split between the two boats. This works well for corporate events or weddings. Another option is a motor yacht with a capacity of 40, but check the deck space. A motor yacht of 30 metres can take 40, but it is less stable. You can also look at departing from Estepona or Sotogrande, which have fewer bookings. Use the BoatHire24 blog for tips on finding alternative boats.

Insurance and Liability for a Group of 40

The boat owner has insurance that covers the boat and crew. Your personal belongings are not covered. If you bring expensive items (cameras, phones, jewellery), keep them in a dry bag. The crew is not liable for lost or damaged items. For a group of 40, consider taking out a group travel insurance policy that covers cancellation, medical emergencies, and personal liability. Some policies cover water sports. The cost is about EUR 10-20 per person. If someone gets seasick, the crew has medication on board. For a group of 40, expect 2-3 people to feel sick. The catamaran is stable, but it still moves. Advise guests to take seasickness tablets an hour before departure.

Related guides

  • Marbella vs Puerto Banús: Which Departure Port to Choose
  • How Much Does a Yacht Charter in Marbella Cost?
  • Dolphin Watching Boat Trips from Marbella: A Full Guide
