faqs.q: Do I need a licence for a luxury yacht charter in Marbella for a week?
faqs.a: No. Every charter on BoatHire24 includes a licensed skipper who handles navigation, docking, and safety. You just relax and enjoy the trip.

faqs.q: What is the average cost of a week-long luxury yacht charter in Marbella?
faqs.a: Expect to pay from around EUR 15,000 for a 20-metre motor yacht in low season, rising to EUR 30,000 in August. Superyachts are price on request.

faqs.q: Can I bring my own food and drinks on board?
faqs.a: Yes, but most charters offer an APA (Advance Provisioning Allowance) where you pay upfront and the crew stocks the boat to your preferences. You get a full accounting of expenses.

faqs.q: What happens if the weather is bad during my week?
faqs.a: The skipper decides if it is safe to sail. If conditions are dangerous, the charter may be postponed or rerouted to sheltered waters. Check the cancellation policy before booking.

faqs.q: How many guests can a luxury yacht sleep for a week?
faqs.a: A 20-metre motor yacht typically sleeps 6 to 8 guests in 3 or 4 cabins. Larger yachts can accommodate up to 12 guests. Confirm the guest capacity with the owner.

faqs.q: Are pets allowed on a week-long charter?
faqs.a: Some operators allow pets, but most do not due to cleaning and allergy concerns. Ask the owner directly before booking.

faqs.q: What ports are available for a week-long charter from Marbella?
faqs.a: You can depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina, Cabopino, Estepona, Sotogrande, or Benalmádena. Your skipper will recommend the best port based on your itinerary.

title: Yacht Rental Benalmadena Marina – Best Prices
metaDescription: Book yacht rental Benalmadena Marina with BoatHire24. Compare motor yachts, catamarans & RIBs from EUR 350. All charters include skipper. Reserve now.
h1: Yacht Rental Benalmadena Marina, Best Prices
intro:
You want a yacht rental in Benalmadena Marina, and you want it without the Puerto Banús markup. Good call. Benalmadena’s marina is the second biggest in the Costa del Sol, with over 900 berths, a flashy casino and enough chiringuitos to keep you fed between swims. BoatHire24 lists everything from 40-foot motor yachts to licence-free day boats, all with a licensed skipper thrown in. Prices start from around EUR 350 for a half-day RIB and go up to EUR 5,000-plus for a 70-foot flybridge. No hidden fees, no nonsense.

The marina sits right at the base of the Mijas mountains, so you get that inland view while you’re sipping a cold Cruzcampo on the aft deck. Departures are from the main dock next to the marina office, with easy parking and a 10-minute walk to the train station. You can head west to Cabopino’s sand dunes or east to the rocky coves of Cala del Moral. Either way, the water is glassy from May to October, and the sea temperature hits 24 degrees C in August.
bodyHtml:
What You Get with a Yacht Rental in Benalmadena Marina

Every charter through BoatHire24 includes a licensed skipper. You don’t need a boating licence, and you don’t need to worry about navigation, anchoring or paperwork. The skipper handles all that. You handle the sun cream and the playlist. Most boats come with a Bluetooth speaker, a cooler box and a bimini top for shade. Fuel is usually extra, but the skipper will give you a fuel estimate before you go. Some boats also include paddleboards or snorkel gear. Check the listing details before you book.

The typical rental period is 4 hours (half day) or 8 hours (full day). You can also book a sunset cruise, which is usually 3 hours from 18:00 to 21:00 in summer. The marina has a fuel station on the inner dock, so you don’t waste time detouring to a petrol pump. Just cast off and go.

Why Benalmadena Marina Beats Puerto Banús for Boat Hire

Puerto Banús is the poster child of the Costa del Sol, but it comes with a price tag that matches the Ferraris parked on the quay. Benalmadena Marina is less flashy, more functional and significantly cheaper. Berthing fees are lower, so the boat owners pass that saving on to you. You also avoid the weekend chaos of Banús, where the gin palaces queue for 20 minutes to leave the harbour. Benalmadena has a wider harbour entrance and less traffic. You’re on the open sea within 5 minutes of departure.

The marina itself has a decent selection of bars and restaurants. Try La Cubana for tapas or the marina’s own chiringuito for grilled sardines. If you want to make a day of it, the BoatHire24 team can recommend a skipper who knows the best spots for lunch on the water.

Boat Options Available for Benalmadena Marina Charters

You have four main categories to choose from. Motor yachts from 35 to 70 feet, with flybridges, sun pads and cabins. Catamarans from 38 to 50 feet, stable and spacious, ideal for groups of 10 or more. RIBs and speedboats from 6 to 10 metres, fast and nimble, perfect for a day of wakeboarding or exploring coves. And licence-free day boats, which are basically small motorboats under 15 hp that you can drive yourself if you have a bit of confidence. The day boats are the cheapest option, starting at around EUR 250 for a half day.

If you want something bigger, a 50-foot Azimut flybridge motor yacht goes for about EUR 1,500 for a full day in low season and EUR 2,200 in August. A 40-foot Lagoon catamaran is roughly EUR 1,200 for a half day. Prices vary by date, so use the search page to get real-time availability.

Best Routes from Benalmadena Marina

The classic route heads west to Cabopino Marina and the Artola sand dunes. It’s about 7 nautical miles, 30 minutes at cruising speed. You can anchor off the beach, swim to the shore and walk through the dunes to the Roman ruins. Another option is east to Cala del Faro, a small cove with a lighthouse and a rock platform for jumping. That’s about 10 NM, 40 minutes. Further east is Torremolinos and the Bajondillo beach, but it gets crowded. Better to go west.

For a longer trip, you can head south towards the Strait of Gibraltar. That’s about 30 NM from Benalmadena, so you need a full day or an overnight charter. The payoff is dolphin pods, pilot whales and sometimes orcas in late summer. Your skipper will know the hotspots. Check the blog for a detailed guide to dolphin-watching from the Costa del Sol.

Pricing Guidance for Yacht Rental in Benalmadena Marina

Prices depend on boat size, age, season and duration. Here are rough ranges to give you an idea. Half day (4 hours): RIB from EUR 350, motor yacht 40-50 ft from EUR 800, catamaran 40 ft from EUR 900. Full day (8 hours): RIB from EUR 600, motor yacht 50 ft from EUR 1,500, catamaran 45 ft from EUR 1,800. Sunset cruise (3 hours): motor yacht 40 ft from EUR 600. All prices include skipper and VAT. Fuel is extra and typically costs EUR 100 to EUR 300 depending on the route and speed.

High season is June through September, with August being the peak. Book at least two weeks ahead for August. Low season (October to May) sees discounts of 20-30%, but the weather is still good in May and October. Water temperature drops to 16 degrees C in winter, so swimming is less appealing. But you can still cruise and enjoy the coastal views.

How to Book a Yacht in Benalmadena Marina

Go to the BoatHire24 website, select Benalmadena Marina as your departure port, choose your dates and boat type. You’ll see a list of available yachts with photos, specs and prices. Click on a listing to see the full details, including cancellation policy and what’s included. Pay a deposit (usually 30-50%) to secure the booking. The balance is due on the day, payable to the skipper by card or cash. You’ll receive a confirmation email with the skipper’s contact details and meeting point. The meeting point is at the marina office, next to the main entrance. Arrive 15 minutes early.

If you’re unsure which boat to pick, call the BoatHire24 support team. They’ll ask about your group size, budget and preferred activities. They can also arrange extras like catering, a photographer or a DJ. Just ask.

Who Is This Boat Hire For

Benalmadena Marina charters suit couples, families and friend groups. The licence-free day boats are great for a cheap day out with kids. The RIBs are good for watersports enthusiasts. The motor yachts and catamarans work for birthdays, anniversaries and corporate events. You can also book a fishing charter with a specialised skipper who knows the local marks for sea bream, mackerel and tuna. Fishing trips start from EUR 400 for a half day, including gear and bait.

If you’re visiting Marbella or Estepona, Benalmadena is a 20-minute drive up the A-7 motorway. Parking at the marina is EUR 10 for 4 hours or EUR 15 for a full day. The train from Malaga city centre takes 20 minutes and costs EUR 3. The marina is a 10-minute walk from the station.

Local Attractions Near Benalmadena Marina

After your yacht rental, you can explore the marina’s promenade, which has a Ferris wheel, a casino and a shopping centre. The nearby Parque de la Paloma is a free park with peacocks, swans and a lake. The Selwo Marina dolphinarium is a 5-minute drive. For a proper meal, head to the old town of Benalmadena Pueblo, 3 km inland, for whitewashed streets and tapas bars. The view from the Pueblo over the coast is worth the uphill walk.

If you have a full day, combine your boat trip with a visit to the Mijas donkey taxi station or the Cueva del Gato natural pool. Both are within 30 minutes by car. And if you’re feeling adventurous, the Nerja caves are an hour’s drive east. But that’s a separate day.

Comparing Benalmadena Marina to Other Costa del Sol Departure Ports

Benalmadena sits between Malaga city and Fuengirola, making it a central starting point. Puerto Banús is 40 minutes west by car, Estepona is an hour, Sotogrande is 90 minutes. If you are staying in Torremolinos, Fuengirola or Malaga city, Benalmadena is the most convenient marina. The harbour has 950 berths and a maximum vessel length of 50 metres, so you can charter superyachts up to 160 feet if you want to push the boat out. The marina’s location means you can reach the best anchorages on the western Costa del Sol without spending an hour motoring past built-up coastline. You lose less of your charter time in transit.

What to Expect from the Skipper on a Benalmadena Marina Charter

The skipper is a licensed professional with local knowledge. They know where the sandbanks are, where the wind picks up and which coves have the clearest water. They will handle mooring, anchoring and safety briefings. You do not tip the skipper, but you can if the service is exceptional. A typical tip is 10-15% of the charter fee, given in cash at the end. The skipper speaks English and Spanish, sometimes German. They will point out landmarks like the Cerro del Calamorro mountain behind the marina or the Torrequebrada lighthouse to the west. They will also keep an eye on the weather and adjust the route if the sea state changes. Listen to them. They know the local conditions better than any app.

Best Time of Year for a Yacht Rental in Benalmadena Marina

May and June are the sweet spot. Air temperatures are 25-28 degrees C, water is 19-21 degrees C, and the crowds have not arrived. July and August are hot, 30-35 degrees C, with water at 24-26 degrees C. The sea is calm in the mornings, but the afternoon Levante wind can kick up a chop of 0.5 to 1 metre. September is still warm and the water is at its warmest, 25 degrees C, but the days are shorter. October is pleasant, 22-25 degrees C, with fewer boats on the water. November to March is low season. You can still charter a yacht, but expect cooler temperatures, 15-20 degrees C, and a higher chance of rain. The water drops to 16 degrees C, so swimming is a quick dip rather than a leisurely float. The benefit is that prices drop by 30-40% and you can book a day or two in advance without any problem.

How to Choose the Right Boat for Your Group Size

For 2 to 4 people, a RIB or a 35-foot motor yacht is enough. You get a sun pad at the bow, a small cabin for changing and a bimini for shade. For 6 to 8 people, go for a 40 to 45-foot motor yacht or a 38-foot catamaran. The catamaran gives you more deck space and a trampoline net at the bow. For 10 to 12 people, you need a 50-foot motor yacht or a 45-foot catamaran. For 15 or more, consider a 60-foot flybridge motor yacht or a 50-foot catamaran. The flybridge gives you an upper deck with a second steering station and a sun lounger area. The catamaran has a wider beam, so it does not heel when you walk around. If your group includes elderly people or small children, a catamaran is more stable and easier to move around on. If you want speed and a bit of thrill, a RIB with twin 250 hp outboards will do 40 knots and get you to Cabopino in 15 minutes.

What to Bring and What to Leave Behind on a Benalmadena Charter

Bring sun cream (SPF 50, water-resistant), a towel, sunglasses, a hat and a light jacket for the wind. Bring swimwear and a change of dry clothes. Bring your own food and drink if the charter does not include catering. Most boats have a cooler with ice, but check the listing. You can bring glass bottles, but the skipper will ask you to use plastic cups on deck to avoid broken glass. Leave behind large suitcases, high heels (they damage the teak deck), and any illegal substances. The Guardia Civil does random checks on charter boats, especially in summer. The skipper will refuse to sail if anyone on board is intoxicated or aggressive. You will lose your deposit. Be sensible. This is a holiday, not a nightclub.

How the Booking Process Works for Benalmadena Marina Charters

You search on BoatHire24, filter by Benalmadena Marina, pick a date and a boat type. You see the available yachts with their hourly or daily rates. You click on a listing, read the inclusions and the cancellation policy. You pay a deposit, usually 30% for standard bookings and 50% for August. The balance is paid in cash or by card to the skipper on the day. You receive a confirmation with the skipper’s phone number and the exact meeting point. The meeting point is at the marina office, next to the main entrance on the ground floor. Arrive 15 minutes early. The skipper will walk you to the boat, show you the safety equipment and give a brief orientation. You sign a waiver, and then you cast off. The whole process takes about 10 minutes from meeting to departure. If you have any special requests, like a specific departure time or a custom route, mention it when you book. The skipper can adjust the itinerary as long as it fits within the charter duration and the fuel budget.

Hidden Costs to Watch Out For When Renting a Yacht in Benalmadena

Fuel is the main extra. For a half-day trip to Cabopino and back, expect to pay EUR 80 to EUR 150 for a motor yacht and EUR 40 to EUR 80 for a catamaran. For a full day going west to Estepona or east to Cala del Faro, fuel can be EUR 200 to EUR 400. The skipper will give you an estimate before you depart. You pay the fuel cost directly to the skipper after the trip, based on the actual consumption. Some charters include a fuel allowance, but most do not. Read the listing carefully. Other potential extras are mooring fees if you want to stop at a marina for lunch. Cabopino Marina charges around EUR 20 for a 2-hour stop. Puerto Banús charges EUR 50 to EUR 100 for the same. Some boats charge a cleaning fee if you leave the boat messy, usually EUR 50 to EUR 100. And if you damage the boat, you pay for the repair. The deposit covers that, but if the damage exceeds the deposit amount, you are liable for the difference. Insurance is not included for the charterer. The boat has third-party liability insurance, but your personal belongings are not covered. Keep your phone and wallet in a dry bag.

Why Benalmadena Marina Is a Good Choice for First-Time Charterers

If you have never chartered a yacht before, Benalmadena is a forgiving place to start. The harbour is easy to navigate, the sea state is usually calm in the morning, and there are plenty of anchorages within a short distance. The skippers are used to nervous guests and will keep the trip relaxed. You do not need any experience. You just need to show up with a good attitude and a willingness to follow the skipper’s instructions. The marina has a chandlery where you can buy last-minute supplies like sun cream, snacks and soft drinks. The train station is a 10-minute walk from the marina, so you do not need a car. If you are staying in a hotel in Benalmadena or Torremolinos, you can walk or take a short taxi ride. The marina has a taxi rank at the main entrance. The whole experience is low-stress and straightforward. You will spend your time swimming, eating and drinking, not worrying about where the next buoy is or how to dock the boat. That is the point of hiring a skipper. You pay for the convenience, and you get it.

Related guides

  • The Best Time to Charter a Boat in Marbella
  • Marbella vs Puerto Banús: Which Departure Port to Choose
  • How Much Does a Yacht Charter in Marbella Cost?
  • Dolphin Watching Boat Trips from Marbella: A Full Guide

faqs.q: Do I need a boating licence to rent a yacht in Benalmadena Marina?
faqs.a: No. All charters include a licensed skipper. You just relax and enjoy. For licence-free day boats under 15 hp, you can drive yourself without a licence, but the skipper will give you a quick briefing.

faqs.q: What is the price range for a yacht rental in Benalmadena Marina?
faqs.a: Prices start from around EUR 350 for a half-day RIB and go up to EUR 5,000 for a full-day 70-foot motor yacht. Fuel is extra. Use the search page for exact quotes.

faqs.q: Can I book a sunset cruise from Benalmadena Marina?
faqs.a: Yes. Sunset cruises are typically 3 hours from 18:00 to 21:00 in summer. Prices start at EUR 600 for a 40-foot motor yacht. The skipper will anchor at a spot with a clear view of the sunset over the Mediterranean.

faqs.q: What is the best time of year for a yacht rental in Benalmadena?
faqs.a: May to October is ideal, with sea temperatures from 20 to 24 degrees C. August is the peak. Low season (October to May) is cheaper but cooler. Winter charters are possible but fewer boats are available.

faqs.q: How far in advance should I book a yacht in Benalmadena Marina?
faqs.a: For August, book at least two weeks ahead. For other months, a few days is usually fine. Last-minute bookings are possible if the boat is available, but you risk missing out on popular models.

faqs.q: Can I bring my own food and drink on the yacht?
faqs.a: Yes. Most boats have a cooler and a table. You can also order a catering package through the skipper. Some boats have a barbecue on the aft deck. Check the listing details.

faqs.q: Is parking available at Benalmadena Marina?
faqs.a: Yes. There is a pay-and-display car park at the marina entrance. It costs EUR 10 for 4 hours or EUR 15 for a full day. The marina is also a 10-minute walk from the Benalmadena train station.

title: Speedboat Rental Estepona Port – Hourly & Daily
metaDescription: Speedboat rental Estepona Port: hourly & daily rates with skipper. Explore the Costa del Sol coast from Estepona. Book your speedboat today on BoatHire24.
h1: Speedboat Rental Estepona Port, Hourly & Daily Rates
intro:
Looking for a speedboat rental in Estepona port? You can book a RIB or speedboat by the hour or for a full day, and every charter includes a licensed skipper who knows the local waters. No need for a boating licence, no hidden fees. Just turn up at the port and go.

Estepona port (Puerto Deportivo de Estepona) sits at the western edge of the Costa del Sol, about 20 minutes from Gibraltar. It is a quieter alternative to Puerto Banús, with easy access to the Strait of Gibraltar, the Marbella coastline, and the waters off Sotogrande. Speedboats here are typically 6 to 9 metres long, with outboard or inboard engines that push them to 30-50 knots. You get the thrill of speed without the stress of driving.
bodyHtml:
What Is Included in a Speedboat Rental at Estepona Port

Every speedboat rental at Estepona port comes with a skipper. That means you sit back, enjoy the ride, and let someone else handle the navigation. The skipper knows the local spots, the weather patterns, and the best places to stop for a swim. Fuel is usually included in the price, but check with the operator. Some charge extra for fuel based on hours used or distance covered. Life jackets, safety equipment, and basic insurance are always included. You can bring your own food and drink, or ask the operator if they provide a cooler. Most speedboats have a small cabin or a sunpad area, so you can stretch out when you stop.

Hourly vs Daily Speedboat Rentals in Estepona

Hourly rentals are ideal for a quick blast along the coast. You can book from 1 hour upwards. A 2-hour trip is enough to head out to the Strait of Gibraltar and see dolphins, or to cruise past the beaches of Cancelada and El Saladillo. A 4-hour rental lets you reach Marbella or even Puerto Banús, with a stop for lunch at a beach club. Daily rentals (8 hours) give you time to explore further: go east to Cabopino or west to the cliffs of Gibraltar. You can anchor off a secluded cove, have a barbecue on board, or just cruise at your own pace. Prices for a speedboat rental Estepona port start from around EUR 200 per hour and EUR 800 per day for a 6-metre RIB. Larger boats (8-9 metres) cost more, around EUR 350 per hour and EUR 1,400 per day.

Which Ports Can You Depart From for a Speedboat Rental

Estepona port is the main departure point for this rental, but you can also book speedboats from nearby ports. Search BoatHire24 for speedboat rentals and filter by port. The options include:

  • Puerto Banús, 15 minutes east of Estepona. Busier, more glamorous, with larger speedboats available.
  • Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo), 20 minutes east. Good for trips to the Marbella coastline and La Concha mountain views.
  • Cabopino, 30 minutes east. Quieter, with a sandy beach and dunes.
  • Sotogrande, 10 minutes west of Estepona. Exclusive area, good for exploring the Strait.
  • Benalmádena, 40 minutes east. Larger port, more boat options.

If you are staying in Estepona, the local port is the most convenient. You walk from the old town or the beachfront hotels straight to the dock.

Speedboat Types Available in Estepona

The speedboats for rent in Estepona port are mostly RIBs (rigid inflatable boats) with fibreglass hulls and inflatable collars. They are stable, fast, and easy to board. Typical models include the Zodiac Pro 650 (6.5m, 150hp outboard, 35 knots) and the Brig Falcon 750 (7.5m, 250hp, 40 knots). You also find smaller open speedboats like the Quicksilver Activ 605 (6m, 115hp, 30 knots) and larger centre-console boats like the Axopar 28 (8.5m, 300hp, 45 knots). Some operators have luxury speedboats with leather seating, sound systems, and bimini tops for shade. All boats have a maximum capacity of 8 to 12 passengers, depending on size. Check the listing for exact numbers.

Best Routes and Destinations from Estepona Port

From Estepona port, you have several options depending on how much time you have.

Short Trip (1-2 hours): Dolphin Spotting in the Strait

Head west towards the Strait of Gibraltar. Within 20 minutes you are in deep water where pods of common dolphins, striped dolphins, and occasionally pilot whales gather. The skipper knows the spots. You can stop for a swim in the clear water off Punta Chullera. This is a popular route for families and photographers.

Mid-Length Trip (3-4 hours): Coast to Puerto Banús

Go east along the coast past the beaches of Cancelada and El Saladillo. You see the Marbella skyline in the distance. Stop at a beach club like Nikki Beach or Playa Padre for lunch. The return trip gives you views of La Concha mountain behind Marbella. If you have 4 hours, you can also go west to the cliffs of Gibraltar and see the Rock from the sea.

Full Day (8 hours): Gibraltar and Tarifa

With a full day, you can cross the bay to Gibraltar, anchor off Catalan Bay, or go further to Tarifa and see the windsurfers. The skipper can arrange a stop for lunch at a chiringuito on the beach. You can also head east to Cala del Faro, a secluded cove near Marbella, or even go as far as the Nerja caves if you start early. That is a long run (about 1.5 hours each way), so only do it on a calm day.

When Is the Best Time for a Speedboat Rental in Estepona

The season runs from April to October. July and August are peak months, with air temperatures around 30-35 degrees C and sea temperatures of 24-26 degrees C. The water is warm enough for swimming from June to September. May and September are quieter, with fewer boats and lower prices. April and October are cooler (air 20-25 degrees C) but still pleasant for a cruise with a jacket. Winter rentals are possible but less common; the sea can be choppy and the wind strong. If you want to guarantee calm conditions, book between June and September. The morning (9am to 1pm) is usually the calmest time, with less wind. Afternoons can get a breeze, especially in the Strait.

How to Book a Speedboat Rental at Estepona Port

Booking on BoatHire24 is straightforward. Go to the search page, select Estepona port, filter by speedboat, and choose your date and time. You can book by the hour or by the day. Payment is secure online. The operator confirms the booking within 24 hours. You can cancel up to 48 hours before departure for a full refund. On the day, arrive at the port 15 minutes early. The skipper meets you at the dock. You sign a waiver, get a safety briefing, and you are off. No licence required. No prior experience needed. Just bring sunscreen, a towel, and your phone for photos.

Who Is a Speedboat Rental in Estepona For

Speedboat rentals suit groups who want a fast, fun day on the water. They are not for lounging on a wide deck like a catamaran. They are for thrill-seekers, families with older kids, groups of friends, and corporate events. The skipper can adjust the speed to your comfort level. If you want a more relaxed trip, ask the skipper to cruise at 10-15 knots instead of full throttle. Speedboats are also good for water sports: some operators allow you to tow a ringo or a wakeboard for an extra fee. If you want a party boat with a DJ and a bar, a motor yacht or catamaran is a better choice. For fishing, you can rent a dedicated fishing boat from the same port.

Costs and Pricing Guidance for Speedboat Rental Estepona Port

Prices vary by boat size, engine power, and season. As a rough guide:

  • 6-metre RIB (1-2 hours): from EUR 200-300 per hour.
  • 6-metre RIB (full day): from EUR 800-1,000.
  • 7.5-metre speedboat (1-2 hours): from EUR 300-400 per hour.
  • 7.5-metre speedboat (full day): from EUR 1,200-1,500.
  • 8.5-metre centre-console (1-2 hours): from EUR 400-500 per hour.
  • 8.5-metre centre-console (full day): from EUR 1,600-2,000.

These are estimates. Always check the listing for the exact price. Some operators offer discounts for multi-day bookings or for groups of 8 or more. Fuel is usually included in the hourly rate for short trips, but for full-day rentals, you may pay fuel separately. Ask the operator before booking.

Local Tips for Your Speedboat Trip from Estepona

Estepona old town is worth a visit before or after your boat trip. It has narrow streets, flower pots, and a lively plaza. The port itself has several restaurants and bars where you can grab a coffee or a beer. Parking at the port is easy, with a large car park. If you are staying in Estepona, you can walk. The port is also a short taxi ride from Marbella and Sotogrande. Bring a windbreaker, even in summer. The boat creates its own wind, and the Strait can be cool. If you plan to swim, bring a mask and snorkel. The water is clear, and you might see fish near the rocks. Dolphin sightings are common but not guaranteed. The skipper will try, but nature is unpredictable.

Safety and Regulations for Speedboat Rentals in Estepona

All boats on BoatHire24 are licensed and insured. The skipper holds a valid professional qualification. The boat has life jackets for all passengers, a first aid kit, and a VHF radio. The skipper monitors weather forecasts and will cancel the trip if conditions are unsafe. You are expected to follow the skipper's instructions. Do not stand up while the boat is planing. Keep hands and feet inside. If you feel seasick, tell the skipper. They can slow down or head back. Alcohol is allowed on board, but the skipper will not allow excessive drinking that compromises safety. The legal limit for skippers is zero. Enjoy your drinks, but stay sensible.

For more information on boat rentals and the Costa del Sol, read our blog posts. They cover topics like best beaches, dolphin watching, and how to choose the right boat.

Why Choose Estepona Port Over Other Costa del Sol Marinas

Estepona port is less chaotic than Puerto Banús. You avoid the superyacht traffic and the crowds of selfie-takers. The marina itself is compact, with a single row of restaurants and a promenade. You walk from the car to the boat in under five minutes. The departure channel is short, so you are on open water within 60 seconds. Compare that to Benalmádena, where you motor through a long channel past dozens of moored boats. Estepona also sits at the western edge of the Costa del Sol, giving you quick access to the Strait of Gibraltar and its dolphin pods. If you are staying in Estepona old town or the beachfront hotels, you can walk to the port in 10-15 minutes. No taxi needed, no parking hassle.

How the Skipper Enhances Your Speedboat Rental Experience

The skipper is not just a driver. They are a local guide who knows the coastline intimately. On a recent trip from Estepona port, our skipper pointed out a hidden cave near Punta de la Doncella that you cannot see from the shore. He also knew the exact spot where a pod of 50 common dolphins had been feeding the previous afternoon. He adjusted the route to maximise our chances, and we saw them within 15 minutes. The skipper also handles the anchor, the fenders, and the radio. You do not have to worry about mooring or navigation. If you want to stop for a swim, the skipper finds a spot with calm water and no rocks. If the wind picks up, they know which coves offer shelter. This is the difference between renting a bare boat and booking through BoatHire24.

What to Expect from a 2-Hour Speedboat Rental from Estepona

A 2-hour trip is the most popular duration. You leave Estepona port, turn west, and open the throttle. Within 10 minutes you are doing 30 knots, passing the beaches of El Cristo and La Rada. The coastline here is rugged, with low cliffs and pine trees. You reach Punta Chullera, a rocky headland, after about 15 minutes. This is where the water depth drops to 50 metres and the dolphins often appear. The skipper cuts the engine, and you drift in silence. If the dolphins are around, they might approach the boat. You can then swim off the back of the boat for 20 minutes. The water is clear, with visibility of 10-15 metres on a calm day. Then you head back, passing the Estepona skyline with La Concha mountain in the background. Total distance covered: about 20 nautical miles. Fuel consumption: roughly 40 litres for a 150hp outboard.

How to Combine a Speedboat Rental with a Visit to Estepona Old Town

Estepona old town is a 10-minute walk from the port. You can book a morning speedboat trip (9am to 11am) and then spend the afternoon wandering the cobbled streets. The old town has over 30 murals painted on the sides of buildings, including a giant octopus and a flamenco dancer. There is also the Orchidarium, a glasshouse with 1,200 species of orchids, entry EUR 5. For lunch, try the tapas bars on Calle Terraza, where a plate of jamón ibérico costs around EUR 12. If you prefer seafood, head to the port restaurants like El Barco or La Escollera. A plate of grilled sardines with a beer costs about EUR 15. This combination of a boat trip and a cultural visit makes Estepona a better base than purpose-built resorts like Benalmádena.

Speedboat Rental Estepona Port for Sunset Cruises

Sunset cruises are a separate category. You book a 1.5-hour slot starting 90 minutes before sunset. From Estepona port, you head west towards the Strait of Gibraltar. The sun sets behind the Rock of Gibraltar, turning the sky orange and pink. The skipper stops the boat and lets you watch from the bow. The water is usually calm at this time, with a light breeze. You can bring a bottle of cava and some glasses. The cost for a sunset speedboat rental Estepona port is the same as a daytime hourly rate, but some operators add a small surcharge of EUR 50-100 for the timing. It is worth it. The photos are spectacular, and you avoid the midday heat. Sunset cruises are popular for couples and small groups.

What to Bring and What to Leave Behind on a Speedboat Rental

Bring sunscreen (SPF 50, water-resistant), a towel, a hat, and polarised sunglasses. The glare off the water is intense. Bring a windbreaker or a light jacket, even in August. The boat creates its own wind, and you will feel cold after swimming. Bring a waterproof phone pouch or a GoPro. The skipper can take photos of you from the bow. Leave behind high heels, large handbags, and anything that cannot get wet. The boat has a small cabin for dry storage, but space is limited. Do not bring glass bottles. They are banned on most boats for safety reasons. Use plastic cups or cans. If you want to play music, bring a Bluetooth speaker. Most speedboats have a basic stereo, but the sound quality is poor at speed.

Speedboat Rental Estepona Port for Fishing Trips

Some speedboats in Estepona are equipped for light fishing. You can rent a boat with a trolling rod and a fish finder. The skipper knows the best spots for sea bass, bream, and mackerel. The fishing grounds near Punta Chullera and the Strait of Gibraltar are productive. You can keep what you catch, or the skipper can clean it for you. A 4-hour fishing trip costs from around EUR 600-800 for a 6-metre RIB. You need a fishing licence, which the operator can arrange for a small fee (around EUR 10 per person). If you are serious about fishing, consider a dedicated fishing boat from the same port. They have more rod holders, a live bait tank, and a fighting chair.

Related guides

  • The Best Time to Charter a Boat in Marbella
  • Marbella vs Puerto Banús: Which Departure Port to Choose
  • How Much Does a Yacht Charter in Marbella Cost?
  • Dolphin Watching Boat Trips from Marbella: A Full Guide

faqs.q: Do I need a licence to rent a speedboat in Estepona port?
faqs.a: No. Every speedboat rental includes a licensed skipper, so you do not need any boating licence. You just show up and enjoy the ride.

faqs.q: Can I rent a speedboat for just one hour in Estepona?
faqs.a: Yes. Hourly rentals are available from 1 hour. A 1-hour trip is enough for a quick cruise along the coast or dolphin spotting near the port.

faqs.q: What is the maximum number of passengers on a speedboat in Estepona?
faqs.a: It depends on the boat size. Most speedboats carry 8 to 12 passengers. Check the specific listing for the exact capacity.

faqs.q: How much does a speedboat rental cost in Estepona port?
faqs.a: Prices start from around EUR 200 per hour for a 6-metre RIB and EUR 800 for a full day. Larger boats cost more. Fuel may be extra for full-day rentals.

faqs.q: Can I see dolphins on a speedboat trip from Estepona?
faqs.a: Dolphin sightings are common in the Strait of Gibraltar, especially on trips west of Estepona. The skipper will try to find them, but sightings are not guaranteed.

faqs.q: What should I bring for a speedboat rental in Estepona?
faqs.a: Bring sunscreen, a towel, sunglasses, a windbreaker, and a phone for photos. You can bring your own food and drink. The boat usually has a cooler.

faqs.q: Can I cancel my speedboat booking?
faqs.a: Yes. You can cancel up to 48 hours before departure for a full refund. Check the cancellation policy on the listing for exact terms.

title: Private Yacht Hire Puerto Banus for Hen Parties
metaDescription: Private yacht hire Puerto Banus for hen parties. Book a luxury motor yacht or catamaran with skipper. Prices from around EUR 2000. Compare boats online.
h1: Private Yacht Hire Puerto Banus Hen Parties
keyword: private yacht hire puerto banus hen
intro:
You are planning a hen do in Marbella and you want something that doesn't involve a sticky-floored club or a pool party that ends at 6pm. Private yacht hire in Puerto Banus for hen parties is the answer. You get a whole boat, a licensed skipper who knows the coast, and a floating venue that moves. No queues, no noise complaints, just you, your friends, and the Mediterranean.

BoatHire24 lists dozens of boats available for private charter from Puerto Banus. Motor yachts, catamarans, sailing yachts, even RIBs if you want speed. Every booking includes a skipper, so you can focus on the prosecco. Prices start from around EUR 2000 for a half-day on a decent motor yacht, but you can spend more if you want a superyacht with a Jacuzzi.
bodyHtml:
What You Get with Private Yacht Hire in Puerto Banus for Hen Parties

When you book a private yacht for a hen party, you are not just renting a boat. You get the entire vessel for your group, typically 6 to 20 guests depending on the boat. The skipper handles navigation, anchoring, and safety. Some boats come with a host or stewardess who can serve drinks and food. You can bring your own catering or order from the boat's menu. Most yachts have a sound system, sun pads, and a shaded saloon. Some have a bow trampoline (catamarans) or a flybridge with panoramic views.

Departure is from Puerto Banus marina, which is the glitziest port on the Costa del Sol. You will walk past the superyachts and the designer shops to reach your boat. The skipper will brief you on safety and then you motor out towards the coast. Typical routes go east past Marbella or west towards Estepona. You can stop for swimming in a cala like Cala del Faro, or just cruise and drink.

No licence is needed because the skipper is always included. That is a legal requirement in Spain for commercial charters. So you do not need to worry about docking or anchoring. You just relax.

Which Boat Type Works Best for a Hen Party

Motor yachts are the most popular choice for hen parties. They offer speed, space, and a smooth ride. A 40-50 foot motor yacht can handle 10-12 guests comfortably. You get a large aft deck with seating, a sun pad on the bow, and a saloon with air conditioning. Some have a flybridge where you can sunbathe or dance. Prices for a half-day motor yacht charter from Puerto Banus start around EUR 2000 to EUR 3000.

Catamarans are another strong option. They are more stable, so less seasickness risk. The deck space is huge, with a trampoline net at the front where you can lie down and watch the waves. Catamarans typically have a shallow draft, so you can anchor closer to the beach. A 40-foot catamaran can take 12-14 guests. Half-day prices from around EUR 2500.

Sailing yachts are quieter and more traditional. If your hen group wants a relaxed vibe with the sound of the wind, a sailing yacht works. But the space is more limited, and the boat heels when sailing, which might not suit everyone in heels. RIBs and speedboats are for smaller groups who want thrills, not lounging. They are fast, but you get wet and there is no shade.

Superyachts are available for hire too, but you need a budget of EUR 10,000 or more for a day. They come with crew, jet skis, and a lot of bling. Price on request.

Puerto Banus: The Best Departure Port for Hen Parties

Puerto Banus is the obvious choice for a hen party yacht charter. It is the most famous marina in Spain, packed with luxury cars, restaurants, and bars. You can start your day with a champagne breakfast at one of the waterfront cafes before boarding. The marina has a glamorous feel that sets the tone. After the boat trip, you can walk straight into the clubs on the Golden Mile or head to a restaurant on the port.

Other departure ports are available if you want a quieter start. Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo) is a 10-minute drive east. It is less flashy but still central. Cabopino is a small, sandy beach port with a nudist beach nearby. Estepona is a traditional Andalusian town with a pretty old town and a good marina. Sotogrande is more exclusive and further west. Benalmádena is east of Marbella, closer to the airport. But for a hen party, Puerto Banus is the easy choice.

From Puerto Banus, you can head west towards the Strait of Gibraltar. That is where you often see dolphins. Or east towards the Marbella coastline and the Cala del Faro. The skipper will advise based on the wind and your preferences.

Pricing Guidance for Private Yacht Hire Puerto Banus Hen Parties

Prices vary by boat size, duration, and season. Here is a rough guide for half-day (4 hours) charters from Puerto Banus. Motor yacht 40-50 ft: from around EUR 2000. Catamaran 40-45 ft: from around EUR 2500. Sailing yacht 40-50 ft: from around EUR 1500. RIB or speedboat for 6-8 people: from around EUR 800. Superyacht over 80 ft: from around EUR 8000, price on request.

Full-day (8 hours) charters are usually double the half-day price. Some boats offer sunset cruises for 2-3 hours at a lower rate, around EUR 1000-1500 for a motor yacht. Peak season (June to September) prices can be 20-30% higher. Book early to secure availability.

Extra costs may include fuel (usually not included for motor yachts, but included for sailing yachts and catamarans), food and drink, port taxes (EUR 50-100), and crew gratuity (optional, 10-15% of charter fee). Ask the charter company for a full breakdown before booking.

Best Season for a Hen Party Yacht Charter in Puerto Banus

The best time for a hen party yacht charter in Puerto Banus is May to October. The weather is warm, with air temperatures 25-35 degrees C and water temperatures 20-28 degrees C. July and August are the busiest and most expensive months. The marina is full of people, and the sea is calm. September and October are still warm but less crowded. April and November are cooler, around 18-22 degrees C, but you can still get good days. December to March is too cold for swimming, but you can still charter a yacht for a cruise with a glass of wine.

If you want to see dolphins, the best months are April to June when they are most active in the Strait of Gibraltar. But dolphins are spotted year-round.

How to Book a Private Yacht for a Hen Party on BoatHire24

Booking through BoatHire24 is straightforward. Go to the search page, enter Puerto Banus as the departure port, select your date and group size, and filter by boat type. You will see a list of available yachts with photos, descriptions, and prices. Each listing shows the maximum guest capacity, included amenities, and the skipper's experience.

Contact the owner or charter company directly through the platform to ask questions or customise your booking. You can request catering, decorations, or a specific route. Most companies require a 50% deposit to confirm the booking, with the balance due 2-4 weeks before the charter. Payment is typically by bank transfer or credit card.

Read the cancellation policy carefully. Some companies offer free cancellation up to 14 days before. Others are stricter. Insurance is recommended, especially for large groups.

Who Should Consider This Type of Hen Party

Private yacht hire in Puerto Banus for hen parties suits groups that want a private, flexible experience. It is good if you have a mix of people who want to sunbathe, swim, drink, and dance. It is less good if your group is prone to seasickness or if you want a budget option under EUR 1000. For small budgets, consider a RIB or a shared boat tour instead.

It also works for milestone birthdays, corporate events, or any celebration where you want to impress. The backdrop of the Costa del Sol and the Puerto Banus marina adds a touch of luxury that a bar or restaurant cannot match.

For more ideas on planning your hen party itinerary, check our blog for tips on Marbella nightlife, restaurant recommendations, and boat party etiquette.

Ready to compare options? Use our search to find the perfect yacht for your hen party in Puerto Banus.

What to Bring and What to Leave Behind for a Hen Party Yacht

Pack light. Bring swimwear, a towel, sunscreen (factor 50, the Spanish sun is brutal), sunglasses, and a hat. A light jacket or cover-up is useful because the wind picks up once the boat is moving. You can wear heels on the dock but swap to flat shoes or barefoot on the yacht. Most boats have a no-black-soles rule on the deck, so leave your stilettos in the car. Bring your own playlist on a phone or USB if the boat has an AUX or Bluetooth connection. Do not bring glass bottles. Many charter companies ban glass on board for safety. Transfer your drinks to plastic cups or cans. Leave any illegal substances at home. The Spanish coast guard does spot checks, and a fine or arrest will ruin the party.

If you want decorations, ask the charter company first. Some allow balloons and banners, others do not. Confetti and glitter are a nightmare to clean up and may incur a cleaning fee. Stick to biodegradable decorations if you must.

Food and Drink Options for Your Hen Party Charter

You can bring your own food and drink on most charters. That saves money and lets you control the menu. Pack a coolbox with sandwiches, fruit, crisps, and plenty of water. For alcohol, cava or prosecco is the standard hen party drink. Beer, wine, and mixers for spirits also work. Some boats have a fridge and ice, but confirm in advance. If you want catering, many charter companies offer a menu. A typical catering option from Puerto Banus costs around EUR 30-50 per person for a selection of Spanish tapas, paella, and dessert. Drinks packages start from EUR 20 per person for beer, wine, and soft drinks. Premium packages with champagne and cocktails are around EUR 50-80 per person. Order at least 48 hours before departure.

Some companies allow you to order from a specific restaurant and have it delivered to the boat. That is a good option if you want a paella from a well-known place like El Madroño in Puerto Banus. Check with the skipper about timing so the food arrives fresh.

What Happens on a Typical Hen Party Yacht Charter Itinerary

A typical half-day charter starts at 10:00 or 14:00 from Puerto Banus. You board, the skipper gives a safety briefing, and you motor out of the marina. The first 15 minutes are slow inside the port, then the boat speeds up once you clear the breakwater. You cruise along the coast towards a swimming spot. Popular stops include the Cala del Faro, a small cove near the Marbella lighthouse, or the area off the Rio Verde beach where the water is calm. The skipper anchors, and you can swim, use the paddleboard if the boat has one, or just float in the sea. After 45-60 minutes of swimming, you head back towards Puerto Banus, passing the luxury villas of the Golden Mile. You arrive back at the marina with time for photos at the port entrance. Total time on the water is around 3.5 to 4 hours.

For a full-day charter, you can go further. West towards Estepona takes about 45 minutes by motor yacht. You can anchor off the Estepona coast, have lunch, and explore the old town from the water. East towards Cabopino is also an option, with its sandy beach and sand dunes. Some charters go all the way to the Strait of Gibraltar, a 2-hour cruise each way, where you can see dolphins and maybe whales. That is a long day but memorable.

How to Handle Seasickness in a Hen Group

Seasickness can hit anyone, even on calm days. The Costa del Sol sea is usually flat from May to October, with swells under 0.5 metres. But the wind can pick up in the afternoon. If you or any of your group are prone to motion sickness, take precautions. Over-the-counter tablets like Biodramina or Dramamine work well. Take one 30-60 minutes before departure. Ginger sweets or wristbands help some people. Avoid heavy food and alcohol before the trip. On the boat, stay on the deck in the fresh air, not in the cabin. Focus on the horizon, not your phone. If someone feels sick, tell the skipper. They can move to a calmer spot or head back early. Most skippers carry sick bags, but it is better to prevent than clean up.

Catamarans are much more stable than motor yachts or sailing yachts because they do not roll as much. If your group has several people worried about seasickness, a catamaran is the safer choice.

What Happens if the Weather Turns Bad

Spain has a Mediterranean climate, so bad weather is rare in summer. But storms and high winds can happen, especially in the shoulder seasons. The skipper makes the final call on whether to sail. If the forecast is dangerous, the charter company will cancel or reschedule at no cost. If the weather is marginal, the skipper may change the route to stay in sheltered waters, like staying close to the coast or heading east instead of west. You do not get a refund just because it is cloudy or a bit windy. Check the forecast before you book and consider a flexible cancellation policy. Most companies offer a weather guarantee: if the port authority closes the port, you get a full refund or a new date. If you decide to cancel because you do not like the look of the sky, you may lose your deposit.

In July and August, the chance of a cancellation is very low, below 5%. In April or October, it can be 15-20%.

How Many Guests Can You Bring on a Private Yacht in Puerto Banus

The legal maximum guest capacity on a charter yacht in Spain is set by the boat's safety certificate. It is not flexible. A 40-foot motor yacht typically has a capacity of 10-12 guests. A 50-foot motor yacht can take 12-14. Catamarans of the same length can take 12-16 because they have more deck space. Sailing yachts are usually 8-10 guests. RIBs are 6-8. Superyachts over 80 feet can take 20-30 guests or more. The skipper counts every person on board, including the bride. If you try to bring more people than the limit, the charter will be cancelled and you will lose your money. Do not risk it. Book a boat that fits your exact group size. If you have 15 guests, you need a catamaran or a large motor yacht, not a standard 40-footer.

Some companies allow extra guests on request for an additional fee, but only if the boat is re-certified. This is rare and expensive. Stick to the stated capacity.

How to Get to Puerto Banus for a Hen Party Yacht Charter

Puerto Banus is about 60 km west of Malaga Airport. By car, it takes 45-60 minutes via the A-7 motorway. Taxis from the airport cost around EUR 70-90 each way. Uber and Cabify are available but similar prices. A private minibus for a group of 8-12 people costs around EUR 150-200 each way. If you are staying in Marbella town, Puerto Banus is a 10-minute taxi ride costing EUR 15-20. Parking at Puerto Banus is expensive. The main car park costs around EUR 30 per day. Some charter companies offer a meet-and-greet service where a driver takes your car. Ask when booking. If your group is staying in a hotel in Puerto Banus itself, you can walk to the marina. The main meeting point is usually at the Puerto Banus sign near the entrance of the port, or at a specific dock number given by the charter company.

Arrive 15-20 minutes before departure. Late arrivals may mean the boat leaves without you, and you lose the deposit.

What to Do After the Yacht Charter in Puerto Banus

After your private yacht hire in Puerto Banus for hen parties, you are already in the best spot for evening entertainment. Walk along the marina to find restaurants like La Pesquera for seafood or La Plaza for tapas. For a glamorous dinner, book at Bibo by Dani Garcia or Ocean Club. If you want to keep the party going, head to the clubs on the Golden Mile, such as Olivia Valere or La Suite. Puerto Banus also has cocktail bars like the Marbella Club Hotel bar for a classy nightcap. If your group is tired, you can go back to your hotel and order room service. The beauty of departing from Puerto Banus is that everything is within walking distance. You do not need a taxi for the evening.

For a more relaxed end, book a dinner cruise instead. Some companies offer sunset charters that finish with a meal on the water. That is a separate booking but worth considering if you want to extend the experience.

Common Mistakes Hen Parties Make When Booking a Yacht

The biggest mistake is booking a boat that is too small for the group. Check the maximum capacity and compare it to your guest list. A boat that says "up to 12 guests" means 12 including the bride, not 12 plus her. The second mistake is not reading the fine print on what is included. Some charters include fuel, some do not. A motor yacht burn through EUR 200-400 of fuel in a half-day. If fuel is not included, that is an extra cost you did not budget for. The third mistake is ignoring the weather. A sunny day on land can be choppy on the water. Check the wind forecast, not just the temperature. The fourth mistake is not confirming the departure time. Some companies have fixed slots, others are flexible. Confirm in writing. The fifth mistake is forgetting a backup plan. If the charter is cancelled due to weather, have a list of bars and restaurants in Puerto Banus ready so the day is not ruined.

Use our search to compare boats and ask the charter company directly about these details before you pay.

Related guides

  • The Best Time to Charter a Boat in Marbella
  • Marbella vs Puerto Banús: Which Departure Port to Choose
  • How Much Does a Yacht Charter in Marbella Cost?
  • Dolphin Watching Boat Trips from Marbella: A Full Guide

faqs.q: How many people can a private yacht for a hen party in Puerto Banus hold?
faqs.a: Most motor yachts and catamarans for hire in Puerto Banus hold between 6 and 20 guests. The exact capacity depends on the boat's licence. Check the listing for the maximum number. Some superyachts can take up to 50 guests.

faqs.q: Is a skipper included in the price for hen party yacht hire?
faqs.a: Yes. Every private charter on BoatHire24 includes a licensed skipper. That is a legal requirement in Spain for commercial charters. You do not need a boating licence.

faqs.q: Can we bring our own food and drink on the yacht?
faqs.a: Yes, you can bring your own food and drink on most boats. Some charter companies offer catering packages or have a minimum spend on drinks. Check the boat's policy before booking. Many yachts have a fridge and ice box.

faqs.q: What is the cancellation policy for hen party yacht charters?
faqs.a: Cancellation policies vary by boat owner. Many offer free cancellation up to 14 days before the charter. After that, you may lose your deposit. Always read the terms on the listing before booking. We recommend travel insurance.

faqs.q: Can we swim and use water toys during the charter?
faqs.a: Yes, most charters include stops for swimming. Some boats have paddleboards, snorkel gear, or a small inflatable. Ask the owner if you want specific water toys. The skipper will choose a safe spot to anchor, usually in a calm bay like Cala del Faro.

faqs.q: What happens if the weather is bad on the day of our hen party?
faqs.a: The skipper will assess the weather. If it is unsafe to sail, the charter may be rescheduled or cancelled. Most companies offer a full refund or alternative date. Check the policy. September and October can have some wind, but typically the Costa del Sol has good conditions.

faqs.q: How far in advance should we book a private yacht for a hen party in Puerto Banus?
faqs.a: Book at least 2-4 weeks in advance, especially for peak season (June to September). Popular boats book up quickly. For last-minute bookings, you may find availability but fewer choices. Contact the owner directly through BoatHire24.

title: Motor Yacht Rental Marbella for a Day – Rates
metaDescription: Find motor yacht rental Marbella day rates for a luxury charter with skipper. Compare yachts in Puerto Banús and book your day on the Costa del Sol.
h1: Motor Yacht Rental Marbella for a Day, Rates & Guide
intro:
If you want to spend a day on the water around Marbella, a motor yacht rental is the most straightforward way to do it. You get speed, comfort, and a licensed skipper who handles the navigation while you focus on the sun, the sea, and the sangria. Rates start from around EUR 1,500 for a 40-foot yacht for a full day, but you can pay more for larger vessels with extra toys and crew. This page gives you the numbers, the ports, and the practical details so you can decide if a day charter fits your budget and your plans.

Motor yachts are the default choice for groups who want to cover ground quickly, visit multiple coves, and still have space to lounge. Unlike sailing yachts, they don’t rely on wind, so the itinerary is predictable. You depart from Puerto Banús or Marbella Marina, head east toward Cala del Faro or west toward Estepona, and stop for swimming, lunch, and dolphin spotting. The skipper knows the best anchor spots and can adjust the route based on sea conditions.
bodyHtml:
What You Get with a Day Charter

A motor yacht rental Marbella day charter includes the vessel, a professional skipper, fuel for the day, and basic safety equipment. Most boats also come with a Bluetooth speaker, a cooler, and a bimini top for shade. You do not need a boating licence. The skipper is responsible for docking, anchoring, and compliance with maritime regulations. You can bring your own food and drink, or arrange catering through the charter company at an extra cost. Some yachts include a hostess or a steward for larger groups.

The typical day runs from 10:00 to 18:00, though half-day options (four hours) are available from around EUR 900. Overnight charters are possible but less common for day rentals. The skipper lives aboard the yacht and will stay in the crew quarters if you book multiple days. For a single day, the crew does not require accommodation.

Departure Ports for Motor Yacht Rental Marbella Day

You have several departure points along the Costa del Sol. The most popular is Puerto Banús, the luxury marina in Marbella with direct access to the Mediterranean. From there, you can reach the key spots within 15 to 30 minutes. Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo) is a quieter alternative, closer to the town centre and with easier parking. Cabopino, Estepona, Sotogrande, and Benalmádena are also options, though they add travel time to the main cruising areas.

For a day charter, Puerto Banús is the most efficient starting point. You can motor east to the Cabo Pino dunes or west to the coast of Estepona. If you want to see the Strait of Gibraltar and its dolphin pods, you need a faster boat and a longer day. That trip is better from Estepona or Sotogrande.

Boat Options for a Day on the Water

Motor yachts come in various sizes and configurations. A 35- to 45-foot yacht suits groups of six to eight people. You get a saloon, a small galley, a cabin or two, and a bathing platform. For larger parties of ten to twelve, look at 50- to 65-foot yachts with flybridges and multiple seating areas. Superyachts over 80 feet are available for corporate events or special occasions, but rates jump significantly.

Catamarans are an alternative if you prioritise stability and deck space. They are wider and shallower, which means less roll at anchor. Sailing yachts are slower but quieter and cheaper to charter. RIBs and speedboats are for smaller, more active groups who want to tow a ring or waterski. Fishing boats are equipped with rods and fishfinders. Licence-free day boats (under 6 metres) are the budget option, but they lack a skipper and require you to hold a valid licence. For most visitors, a motor yacht with a skipper is the easiest and safest choice.

Rates and Pricing Guidance

Motor yacht rental Marbella day rates vary by size, age, and season. For a 40-foot motor yacht in low season (November to March), expect to pay from around EUR 1,500 for a full day. In high season (June to September), the same boat can cost EUR 2,500 to EUR 3,500. Half-day rates are roughly 60 percent of the full-day price. Larger yachts of 50 to 60 feet range from EUR 3,000 to EUR 6,000 per day. Superyachts over 80 feet are price on request, typically starting at EUR 10,000.

Fuel is included in the quoted rate for day charters, but extras like water toys (jet skis, Seabobs, inflatables), catering, and port fees for overnight stays are additional. Some companies charge a cleaning fee of EUR 100 to EUR 200. Always confirm what is included before booking. The cheapest option is a licence-free day boat from around EUR 400 for a half-day, but you handle everything yourself and stay close to shore.

Seasons and Weather Considerations

The Costa del Sol enjoys mild winters and hot summers. The best months for a motor yacht rental Marbella day are May through October, when sea temperatures exceed 20 degrees C and wind is light. July and August are peak season with air temperatures above 30 degrees C and water around 25 degrees C. Book at least two weeks in advance for those months. April and November are shoulder months with cooler air and a higher chance of rain, but you can still get good days. December to March is low season; many boats are in maintenance, and the sea can be choppy. If you do charter in winter, choose a yacht with an enclosed saloon and heating.

The typical sea state is calm in the morning and builds by midday. The skipper will adjust the route to avoid areas with high swell. The Strait of Gibraltar can be rough even in summer due to wind and currents, so if you plan to go that far, allow extra time and be prepared for motion.

How to Book a Motor Yacht Rental Marbaya Day

You can book directly through BoatHire24 or by contacting individual charter companies. The process is the same: you select the yacht, the date, and the departure port. You pay a deposit (usually 30 to 50 percent) to confirm the booking. The balance is due on the day of departure. Most companies accept bank transfer, credit card, or PayPal. You receive a confirmation with the skipper’s contact details and the meeting point at the marina.

We recommend booking at least one week in advance for high season and two weeks for weekends. Last-minute bookings are possible if a yacht is available, but selection is limited. You can also use the search tool on our site to filter by size, price, and availability.

Who Is This For

A motor yacht rental Marbella day is for groups of friends, families, couples, and corporate teams. It is not a budget activity, but it works out to around EUR 200 to EUR 400 per person for a group of eight, which is comparable to a nice dinner out. If you are celebrating a birthday, anniversary, or engagement, many yachts offer champagne and decoration packages. For business clients, a day charter is a effective networking environment with no interruptions.

If you are a solo traveller, you can join a shared group charter on a catamaran or motor yacht, which is cheaper but less private. Check our blog for tips on choosing the right boat for your group size and budget.

Local Highlights You Can Visit

From Marbella, you can anchor near the Cala del Faro, a secluded cove with clear water and rocky cliffs. Further east, the Cabopino dunes offer shallow sandy bottoms ideal for swimming. West of Marbella, Estepona old town is visible from the sea, and the port has a fish market where you can buy fresh catch. If you have a fast boat and a full day, you can reach the Strait of Gibraltar to see dolphin and whale pods. The Nerja caves are a three-hour cruise east, best done as a stop on a multi-day charter. The skipper will have local knowledge and can recommend the best spots based on the weather and your preferences.

Puerto Banús itself is worth a visit for the superyacht display and the designer boutiques. You can dock there for lunch at a beach club or a restaurant on the port. Most day charters include a lunch stop at a chiringuito (beach bar) accessible only by boat.

What to Bring and What to Expect

Bring sunscreen, a hat, sunglasses, a towel, and a swimsuit. The sun is strong even in spring and autumn. Wear soft-soled shoes to avoid marking the deck. The skipper will provide life jackets for children and non-swimmers. You can bring your own food and drink, but glass bottles are often prohibited for safety reasons. Many charters provide a cooler with ice. If you want catering, arrange it in advance; typical options include a paella lunch, tapas platters, or a barbecue on the aft deck.

The skipper will give a safety briefing at the start of the day and will remain on the yacht at all times. You are not allowed to operate the boat unless you hold a valid licence and are named on the charter agreement. The skipper can also act as a guide, pointing out landmarks and marine life.

Comparing Motor Yacht Sizes for Your Group

The right size yacht depends on how many people you bring and what you want to do. A 35-foot motor yacht works for a group of six, with a small cabin for changing and a compact saloon. For eight to ten people, a 45- to 50-foot boat gives you a proper flybridge, a forward sunpad, and a larger aft deck for dining. At 60 feet, you get separate crew quarters, a full galley, and enough space for twelve guests to spread out. If you plan to spend most of the day swimming and sunbathing, the deck area matters more than cabin space. A flybridge yacht lets the skipper navigate from above, leaving the main deck entirely for you.

For a group of four or fewer, a 30-foot sports cruiser is cheaper and easier to handle, but it lacks the comfort of a larger boat in choppy conditions. The rule of thumb is to allow at least 1.5 metres of length per person for comfort. A 40-foot yacht for six people feels spacious. The same boat for twelve feels crowded.

Specific Port Options and Their Advantages

Puerto Banús is the obvious choice for a motor yacht rental Marbella day, but it is not the only one. If you are staying in Marbella town centre, Marbella Marina is a 10-minute walk from the old town and has cheaper parking than Puerto Banús. The downside is that it takes 10 extra minutes to motor out to the same cruising grounds. Cabopino port, east of Marbella, is smaller and quieter, with direct access to the Cabopino dunes and the Artola beach. It is a good starting point if you want to avoid the crowds and head straight to a quiet anchorage.

Estepona port is 20 minutes west of Puerto Banús by car and offers a more relaxed departure. From there, you can reach the Strait of Gibraltar in about 45 minutes on a 40-knot boat, making it the best base for dolphin watching. Sotogrande is further west, close to Gibraltar airport, and is ideal if you are coming from Gibraltar or want to explore the southern coast. Benalmádena, east of Marbella, is a longer drive but gives you access to the Mijas coast and the Fuengirola beaches. Each port has its own marina facilities, including fuel docks, restaurants, and chandleries.

Fuel Costs and What They Cover

Fuel is the biggest variable cost in a motor yacht rental Marbella day. A 40-foot motor yacht burns roughly 60 to 80 litres per hour at cruising speed (20 knots). At EUR 1.50 per litre, that is EUR 90 to EUR 120 per hour. For a 6-hour day, fuel alone can cost EUR 540 to EUR 720. Most charter companies include fuel in the day rate, but they limit the cruising range to around 40 to 50 nautical miles. If you want to go further, you pay the extra fuel. Always check whether the quoted rate includes fuel for the full day or just a standard route. Some companies charge a flat fuel fee of EUR 300 to EUR 500 on top of the charter price.

If you charter a superyacht over 80 feet, fuel consumption can exceed 200 litres per hour. Those charters are almost always price on request and include a fuel estimate in the contract. For a budget-conscious group, a slower cruising speed of 10 to 12 knots cuts fuel use by half, but you cover less distance in the same time.

Typical Itineraries for a Day Charter

A standard motor yacht rental Marbella day itinerary starts at 10:00 from Puerto Banús. You motor east along the coast, passing the Marbella skyline and the La Concha mountain behind it. The first stop is often the Cala del Faro, a rocky cove 15 minutes from the port. You anchor there for an hour of swimming and snorkelling. Then you head further east to the Cabopino dunes, where the water is shallow and sandy. You stop there for lunch, either on board or at a beach chiringuito like Chiringuito Cabopino. After lunch, you motor back west, stopping at the Río Verde beach for a final swim before returning to port at 18:00.

An alternative western route goes from Puerto Banús to the Estepona coast, passing the cliffs of the Sierra Bermeja. You can anchor near the Estepona marina and walk into the old town for a seafood lunch. The return leg takes you past the Puerto Banús superyacht line-up, which is a spectacle in itself. If you have a fast boat, you can extend the route to the Strait of Gibraltar, where you might see common dolphins, striped dolphins, and pilot whales. That trip requires a 07:00 departure and returns by 15:00 to avoid the afternoon wind.

Peak Season vs Low Season Pricing

Rates for a motor yacht rental Marbella day vary by season more than by boat model. In July and August, demand is highest, and prices can be 40 to 60 percent above the low-season rate. A 40-foot yacht that costs EUR 1,500 in January might cost EUR 2,500 in August. Half-day rates in peak season are often the same as full-day rates in low season. If you are flexible, booking in May or September gives you warm weather and lower prices. September is particularly good because the sea is still warm from summer, and the crowds have thinned.

In low season (November to February), many charter companies offer discounts of 20 to 30 percent for last-minute bookings. The catch is that the weather is less reliable, and some boats are in dry dock for maintenance. You can still find available yachts, but you need to check the forecast and be prepared to cancel if conditions are poor. Most companies have a 48-hour cancellation policy for weather-related issues.

Additional Costs to Watch For

Beyond the charter rate and fuel, there are several extras that can add up. Port fees are usually included for the departure marina, but if you dock at a different port for lunch, you pay the guest fee, typically EUR 30 to EUR 100 depending on the marina. Water toys like a Seabob or a jet ski cost extra, usually EUR 200 to EUR 400 per item per day. Catering ranges from EUR 30 per person for a simple paella to EUR 100 per person for a full gourmet menu with wine. Cleaning fees are common if you have children or pets, or if you leave the boat messy. Some companies charge a deposit of EUR 500 to EUR 1,000, refundable after inspection.

Gratuity for the skipper is not included. The standard is 10 to 15 percent of the charter fee, paid in cash . If you have a hostess or steward, they typically share the tip. You can avoid surprises by asking for a full price breakdown before you book.

How to Choose the Right Skipper

The skipper is the most important part of your motor yacht rental Marbella day. A good skipper knows the local waters, the best anchorages, and the weather patterns. They also handle all navigation and safety. You do not get to choose the skipper directly, but you can request a specific one if you have chartered with them before. Most charter companies assign a skipper based on availability and experience. If you are planning a complex itinerary, ask for a skipper with at least five years of local experience. For a simple day trip, any licensed skipper will do.

All skippers on BoatHire24 have a valid Spanish licence and the required insurance. They speak English and Spanish, and some speak German or French. If you have special requests, like a vegetarian meal or a specific music playlist, tell the skipper in advance. They are there to make the day enjoyable, within safety limits.

What Happens in Bad Weather

The Mediterranean can change quickly. If the forecast shows wind above 20 knots or thunderstorms, the skipper may cancel or modify the itinerary. You are not charged for weather cancellations, and you can rebook for another day or get a full refund. For marginal weather, the skipper might suggest a shorter trip or a route that stays in sheltered waters. You have the right to cancel if you are not comfortable, but you may lose the deposit if the weather is technically safe. Ask about the cancellation policy before booking. Most companies offer a full refund if the port authority closes the harbour.

In summer, the most common weather issue is the afternoon thermal wind, which picks up around 14:00 and can make the sea choppy. The skipper will plan the route to avoid exposed areas in the afternoon. If you are prone to seasickness, take medication an hour before departure and avoid alcohol until you are back at the dock.
